**HIGH severity, 7yr retention**; HMAC-chained; emitter: devops-lead (IC) via `emit-audit-event` Worker at T+0 of every R-35 activation (P1 and P0); Zod v2 payload schema: `z.object({ incident_id: z.string().uuid(), confirmed_stale_since: z.string().datetime(), stale_hours: z.number().positive(), missed_runs: z.number().int().nonneg(), at_risk_domain_count: z.number().int().nonneg(), earliest_expiry_at: z.string().datetime().nullable(), trigger: z.enum(['pagerduty_alert', 'manual_discovery']), initial_severity: z.enum(['P1', 'P0']) })`; `at_risk_domain_count = 0` and `earliest_expiry_at = null` permitted at P1 (before R-35-C1 domain sweep completes); `at_risk_domain_count > 0` requires `earliest_expiry_at` non-null (earliest cert expiry among at-risk domains); `initial_severity = 'P0'` when R-35-C1 confirms ≥ 1 white-label domain expiring within 7 days during the stale window; **privacy invariant:** no `user_id`, no `tenant_id`, no domain names, no hostname values — operational incident declaration only; `at_risk_domain_count` is aggregate; **WL-CERT-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant:** this event must precede `system.wl_cert_check_restored` for the same `incident_id` — inversion triggers R-05; `emit-audit-event` Worker returns HTTP 422 `WL_CERT_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` on ordering violation; 7yr retention required — CC7.2/A1.2 monitoring control degradation record; `peer_jobs_stale` context: co-stale with job 37 (`caep_reregister_sweep`) or job 38 (`sso_fleet_health_check`) indicates shared pg_cron infrastructure failure (H2/H4 root cause hypothesis); cross-ref: INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-35 §R-35.3 (immediate actions — IC emits this event at T+0), INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-35 §R-35.7 (DEC-030 chain spec — canonical Zod schemas and WL-CERT-CHAIN-01 full spec), OBSERVABILITY §12.6 job 40 (canonical job registration; `wl-cert-check-stale` PagerDuty dedup; `form-devops` P1 routing; 26h freshness window), OBSERVABILITY §29 (WL-SLO-02/WL-SLO-03 alert coverage — detection gap scope during stale window), DEC-030; **closes INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-35.11 item 1 (P0/M10 — 🟢 2026-06-22)**
- `system.wl_cert_check_restored` — devops-lead confirmed that pg_cron job 40 (`white_label_cert_check`) was re-enabled and successfully executed its first run following a stale incident; closes the WL-CERT-CHAIN-01 evidence chain for the incident; **STANDARD severity, 3yr retention**; HMAC-chained; emitter: devops-lead (human, via `emit-audit-event` Worker) after the next successful `pg_cron.job_run_details` entry for job 40 is confirmed post-fix; Zod v2 payload schema: `z.object({ incident_id: z.string().uuid(), restored_at: z.string().datetime(), root_cause: z.enum(['H1_job_deleted', 'H2_cf_token_invalid', 'H3_worker_proxy_failure', 'H4_cloudflare_api_outage', 'H5_supabase_outage']), fix_deployed_at: z.string().datetime(), domains_verified: z.number().int().nonneg() })`; `domains_verified` is the count of white-label domains confirmed active (`cloudflare_custom_hostnames.status = 'active'`) on the first successful restored run (0 if no white-label tenants are provisioned); **privacy invariant:** no `user_id`, no `tenant_id`, no domain names, no hostname values — `domains_verified` is aggregate count; `root_cause` is a structured enum; **WL-CERT-CHAIN-01:** must be preceded by `system.wl_cert_check_failure_declared` for the same `incident_id` — inversion triggers R-05; 3yr retention sufficient — operational restoration record (SOC 2 CC7.2/A1.2 confirmation); cross-ref: INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-35 §R-35.5 Step 4, INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-35 §R-35.7 (DEC-030 chain spec), INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-35 §R-35.9 (SOC 2 evidence mapping — CC7.2/A1.2), OBSERVABILITY §12.6 job 40, DEC-030

#### Evidence collection automation events (SOC2_READINESS.md §81)

> Emitted by the Cloudflare Cron Worker (§81.4–§81.6) and pg_cron job 33 (`evidence_cron_freshness_check`, OBSERVABILITY.md §12.6). **Privacy floor (all three events):** `actor_id = NULL`, `actor_type = 'system'`, `tenant_id = '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000'` (platform-level sentinel — no individual tenant). HR never sees these events; `compliance_reviewer` role reads all; `form_api` REVOKED from these rows. DEC-030 HMAC-chained.

- `system.evidence_collection_automated` — Cloudflare Cron Worker completed a successful monthly evidence collection run (01:00 UTC, 1st of month); **STANDARD severity, 7yr retention**; SOC 2 evidence artefact AUTO-E-001 (CC4.2/CC7.1); **EVD-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant:** this event must be preceded in the audit chain by `system.evidence_vault_configured` for the same calendar year — `emit-audit-event` Worker returns HTTP 422 on violation; payload: `{period: /^\d{4}-\d{2}$/, artifacts_written: string[] (each must start with 'compliance/evidence/'), chain_integrity_ok: true (literal — Worker aborts if false), slo_all_met: boolean, master_index_hash: string (SHA-256 hex, 64 chars), run_duration_ms: number (positive int)}`; cross-ref: SOC2_READINESS §81.5, SOC2_READINESS §81.7, DEC-030

- `system.evidence_cron_stale` — pg_cron job 33 (`evidence_cron_freshness_check`, 03:05 UTC 2nd of month) detected no `system.evidence_collection_automated` event for the prior period within 48 h; **HIGH severity, 3yr retention**; dead-man's switch signal that triggers AL-EVD-01 (P2 Slack `#compliance-alerts`); payload: `{period: string (YYYY-MM — the period for which evidence was expected), checked_at: string (ISO-8601), window_hours: 48 (literal)}`; cross-ref: SOC2_READINESS §81.9 AL-EVD-01, OBSERVABILITY §12.6 job 33, DEC-030

- `system.evidence_cron_conflict` — Cloudflare Cron Worker detected R2 `If-Match` ETag conflict during MASTER-INDEX reconciliation; Worker retries once; if second attempt also fails, this event is emitted and the run is aborted (no `system.evidence_collection_automated` is emitted for that run); **MEDIUM severity, 1yr retention**; triggers AL-EVD-04 (P2 Slack); payload: `{period: string (YYYY-MM), r2_key: string (MASTER-INDEX path), attempt_number: 1 | 2}`; cross-ref: SOC2_READINESS §81.6, DEC-030

### Admin (key management)

> HIGH severity · 7yr retention · HMAC-chained. Triggers PagerDuty P2 alert on unexpected rotation (outside scheduled window). Emitted synchronously inside KMS transaction — failure aborts rotation.

- `admin.encryption_key_rotated` — master encryption key rotated in KMS; payload: `{key_id, key_version_old, key_version_new, rotation_trigger: 'scheduled'|'incident'|'compromise', rotated_by, kms_provider}`; HIGH severity, 7yr retention; cross-ref: SOC2_READINESS §56, OBSERVABILITY §30.10 item 10, DEC-030
- `admin.signing_key_rotated` — HMAC chain signing key rotated; payload: `{key_id, rotation_trigger, rotated_by}`; HIGH severity, 7yr retention; DEC-030 chain continuity preserved via `key_transition` metadata
- `admin.key_rotation_initiated` — PAM session opened and both engineers present; **CRITICAL severity, 7yr retention**; MUST be the first chain event in any rotation — `emit-audit-event` Worker rejects subsequent rotation events if no matching `key_rotation_initiated` for the same `key_name` within 24h; payload: `{key_name, rotation_type: 'scheduled'|'emergency'|'infrastructure_repair', pam_session_id, second_engineer_user_id, reason, pre_rotation_hmac_chain_status: 'intact', estimated_window_minutes}`; cross-ref: SOC2_READINESS §57.4.1
- `admin.key_rotation_announced` — Dev-env rotation notice sent to #engineering Slack; **HIGH severity, 7yr retention**; emitter: `security-engineer` (manual); payload: `{key_name, announcement_channel: 'slack:#engineering', local_env_rotation_deadline_iso}`; cross-ref: SOC2_READINESS §57.4.2
- `admin.emergency_key_rotation` — Unplanned P0 rotation triggered by compromise signal; **CRITICAL severity, 7yr retention**; triggers GDPR Art. 33 72h clock via `gdpr_art33_clock_started_iso` field; payload: `{key_name, trigger: 'trufflehog_detection'|'log_leak'|'anomalous_query'|'third_party_report'|'pitr_post_rotation', incident_id, pam_session_id, second_person_user_id, gdpr_art33_clock_started_iso, gdpr_art33_deadline_iso, scope_assessment_status: 'in_progress'}`; **privacy invariant: JWT value never stored — `iat` Unix timestamp only**; cross-ref: SOC2_READINESS §57.5.2, INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-16
- `admin.key_rotation_reminder` — Automated cadence alert (14d, 7d, 3d before scheduled rotation); **LOW severity, 3yr retention**; emitter: `workers/key-rotation-monitor` Cron (daily 09:00 UTC per KEY_ROTATION_KV); payload: `{key_name, days_until_expiry, next_rotation_due_iso, rotation_type: 'scheduled'}`; no PagerDuty — Slack `#security-ops` only; cross-ref: SOC2_READINESS §57.7, OBSERVABILITY §30 AL-KEY-01
- `admin.key_rotation_overdue` — Scheduled rotation window missed; **HIGH severity, 7yr retention**; fires when `days_until_expiry ≤ 0`; triggers AL-KEY-02 (P0 PagerDuty, no auto-resolve); payload: `{key_name, days_overdue, last_rotation_iso, rotation_period_days}`; cross-ref: SOC2_READINESS §57.7, OBSERVABILITY §30 AL-KEY-02
- `admin.key_rotation_incident_opened` — IC opened before any R-21 key rotation or forensic action; **HIGH severity, 7yr retention**; MUST be the first DEC-030 event in the R-21 step sequence for any key type — `emit-audit-event` gate: this event must appear in the DEC-030 chain before proceeding to any rotation action; **KEY-IC-CHAIN-01 anchor** — `admin.key_rotation_incident_closed` MUST follow for the same `incident_id` (HTTP 422 `KEY_IC_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` on inversion); payload: `{incident_id: UUID (IC correlation key bridging PagerDuty + DEC-030 chain + IR-KEY-E-001..E-006 artefact files), key_type: enum('HMAC_AUDIT_CHAIN_KEY'|'KEYPOINTS_ENC_KEY'|'SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_JWT'|'CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_SECRET'|'ANTHROPIC_API_KEY'|'SENTRY_DSN'|'POSTHOG_API_KEY'|'SUPABASE_ANON_KEY'), trigger_alert_id: 'AL-KEY-01'|'AL-KEY-02'|'AL-KEY-03'|'AL-KEY-04'|'manual', key_age_days: int ≥ 0 (0 if age unknown), rotation_due_by: ISO 8601 (scheduled deadline per R-21.3 cadence table), overdue: bool (true if key_age_days exceeds scheduled cadence), two_person_auth_required: bool (true for HMAC_AUDIT_CHAIN_KEY + KEYPOINTS_ENC_KEY per R-21.4; false for remaining six key types), ic_user_id: UUID (FORM staff auth UUID of IC opener — FORM-internal identity only)}`; **privacy floor: `ic_user_id` is a FORM-internal auth UUID — not an enterprise employee identifier; no enterprise tenant `user_id`, name, email, health value, coaching content, or GDPR Art. 9 data; `key_type` enum values are system component names, not personal data**; SOC 2 CC6.8 (cryptographic key protection lifecycle — IC opened before any rotation action), CC7.2 (system event monitoring — IC linked to AL-KEY-01/02/03/04 trigger), CC7.3 (anomaly evaluation — IC opened in response to anomalous key age or missing verification event); cross-ref: INCIDENT_RESPONSE §R-54.3–§R-54.5 (canonical payload spec + Zod schema), SOC2_READINESS §58.16 (KEY-IC-CHAIN-01 registration in HMAC chain integrity controls), OBSERVABILITY §30 (AL-KEY-01..04 trigger alert IDs)
- `admin.key_rotation_incident_closed` — IC closed after key rotation + verification complete (or after `deferred_approved` / `false_positive` resolution); **HIGH severity, 7yr retention**; **KEY-IC-CHAIN-01 terminal** — requires preceding `admin.key_rotation_incident_opened` for same `incident_id`; `emit-audit-event` Worker returns HTTP 422 `KEY_IC_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` if no matching opened event found; max window: 48h for HMAC_AUDIT_CHAIN_KEY/KEYPOINTS_ENC_KEY, 72h for remaining six key types; payload: `{incident_id: UUID (same as paired opened event), key_type: enum (same eight-value set — redundant for auditor query convenience), resolution: 'rotated_on_schedule'|'rotated_emergency'|'deferred_approved'|'false_positive', rotation_completed_at: ISO 8601|null (null when resolution = 'false_positive' or 'deferred_approved'), verification_completed: bool (false only for false_positive or deferred_approved resolutions), deferred_reason: string|null (conditional — required if resolution = 'deferred_approved'; max 200 chars; operational context only — no employee name, health value, or GDPR Art. 9 data permitted), pir_committed: bool (true if IC severity ≥ P1 — PIR required within 5 business days of close; false for P2/P3), ic_user_id: UUID (FORM staff auth UUID of IC closer — may differ from opener; both captured in chain)}`; SOC 2 CC6.8 (rotation completion evidence — this event confirms IC closure after `admin.encryption_key_rotation_verified`), CC5.3 (control activities performed as designed — IC lifecycle closed after verification; no rotation left open), CC7.5 (post-incident review — `pir_committed` field flags P0/P1 closure requiring PIR); cross-ref: INCIDENT_RESPONSE §R-54.3–§R-54.5 (canonical payload spec + Zod schema), SOC2_READINESS §58.16 (KEY-IC-CHAIN-01 registration)

**Zod v2 schemas (`admin.key_rotation_incident_opened` / `admin.key_rotation_incident_closed` — canonical source: `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-54.5`):**

```typescript
import { z } from 'zod';

const KeyTypeEnum = z.enum([
  'HMAC_AUDIT_CHAIN_KEY',
  'KEYPOINTS_ENC_KEY',
  'SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_JWT',
  'CLOUDFLARE_WORKERS_SECRET',
  'ANTHROPIC_API_KEY',
  'SENTRY_DSN',
  'POSTHOG_API_KEY',
  'SUPABASE_ANON_KEY',
]);

export const KeyRotationIncidentOpenedPayload = z.object({
  incident_id:              z.string().uuid(),
  key_type:                 KeyTypeEnum,
  trigger_alert_id:         z.enum(['AL-KEY-01', 'AL-KEY-02', 'AL-KEY-03', 'AL-KEY-04', 'manual']),
  key_age_days:             z.number().int().min(0),
  rotation_due_by:          z.string().datetime(),
  overdue:                  z.boolean(),
  two_person_auth_required: z.boolean(),
  ic_user_id:               z.string().uuid(),
});

export const KeyRotationIncidentClosedPayload = z.object({
  incident_id:          z.string().uuid(),
  key_type:             KeyTypeEnum,
  resolution:           z.enum(['rotated_on_schedule', 'rotated_emergency', 'deferred_approved', 'false_positive']),
  rotation_completed_at: z.string().datetime().nullable(),
  verification_completed: z.boolean(),
  deferred_reason:      z.string().max(200).nullable(),
  pir_committed:        z.boolean(),
  ic_user_id:           z.string().uuid(),
});
```

**HMAC chain requirement (KEY-IC-CHAIN-01):** `admin.key_rotation_incident_opened` is the five-event chain anchor: `admin.key_rotation_incident_opened` → `admin.encryption_key_rotation_initiated` → `admin.encryption_key_rotated` → `admin.encryption_key_rotation_verified` → `admin.key_rotation_incident_closed`, all keyed on `incident_id`. `emit-audit-event` Worker enforces KEY-IC-CHAIN-01: `admin.key_rotation_incident_closed` for an `incident_id` with no preceding `admin.key_rotation_incident_opened` returns HTTP 422 `KEY_IC_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION`. Both events include `prev_hash`. Stale IC monitoring: absence of `admin.key_rotation_incident_closed` within 48h of `admin.key_rotation_incident_opened` (HMAC_AUDIT_CHAIN_KEY / KEYPOINTS_ENC_KEY) or 72h (other key types) triggers a devops-lead alert. **Auditor CC6.8 query** (returns exactly 5 rows per completed rotation): `SELECT event_type, created_at, metadata->>'incident_id' AS iid FROM audit_log_events WHERE metadata->>'incident_id' = '<uuid>' ORDER BY created_at`. Cross-ref: SOC2_READINESS §58.16 (KEY-IC-CHAIN-01 full invariant spec + five-event chain diagram + CC6.8 mapping table); INCIDENT_RESPONSE §R-54.4 (invariant rationale); INCIDENT_RESPONSE §R-54.6 (R-21 step sequence integration).

**Emitter assignment:** `admin.key_rotation_incident_opened` — IC (manual, PAM-elevated `form_admin`); `admin.key_rotation_incident_closed` — IC (manual, PAM-elevated `form_admin`; closer may differ from opener). Both events require `emit-audit-event` Worker with PAM elevation — `form_api` and `form_system` roles cannot emit directly.

### Admin (dashboard sessions)

> LOW severity · 1yr retention · HMAC-chained · DEC-030. Emitted by Admin Dashboard backend (Workers layer) on initial session start for `enterprise_admin` and `tenant_manager` roles. Used as the signal source for `champion_login_monitor` (pg_cron job 50, `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §59`) to evaluate T0-Beta pilot health (COST_MODEL §46.2). **Two-layer de-duplication invariant (OQ-CHAMP-OBS-01 resolution — OBSERVABILITY §61.4):** (1) client-side `sessionStorage.getItem('form-admin-sess-emitted')` gate — prevents duplicate emissions across page navigations within the same browser tab; (2) server-side KV `admin-sess-start-{actor_id}:{tenant_id}` with 4 h TTL — `emit-audit-event` Worker returns HTTP 200 `{"deduplicated": true}` on KV hit with no HMAC chain entry. A second genuine login after ≥ 4 h emits a new event (consistent with COST_MODEL §46.2 T0-Beta "≥ 2 admin logins"). **Privacy invariant:** no actor `user_id`, `actor_name`, `actor_email`, coaching content, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category data; `tenant_id_slug` is an opaque human-safe slug — not a UUID.

| Event type | Severity | Retention | Trigger | Key payload fields |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `admin.dashboard_session_started` | LOW | 1 yr | First Admin Dashboard page load in a new browser session (post de-dup) | `tenant_id_slug` (string, max 64), `actor_role` (enum: `enterprise_admin`\|`tenant_manager`), `pilot_day` (INT\|NULL — null if tenant not on pilot), `is_pilot_active` (BOOL) |

**Zod v2 schema (canonical source: this section, v2.58):**

```typescript
const AdminDashboardSessionStartedSchema = z.object({
  tenant_id_slug:  z.string().min(1).max(64),
  actor_role:      z.enum(['enterprise_admin', 'tenant_manager']),
  pilot_day:       z.number().int().min(0).max(180).nullable(),
  is_pilot_active: z.boolean(),
});
```

**Emitter:** `emit-audit-event` Worker (after Layer 2 KV gate passes); called by Admin Dashboard backend on initial page load. `form_api` and `form_system` roles cannot directly emit — requires Admin Dashboard auth context.

**SOC 2 auditor narrative (CC3.2):** `admin.dashboard_session_started` events provide continuous evidence that the IT admin champion is actively engaging with the Admin Dashboard during the 45-day pilot window. `champion_login_monitor` (job 50) queries these events to evaluate the T0-Beta criterion. The de-dup invariant ensures event count equals genuine session count — no inflation from page navigation. Absence of events for a pilot in the Day-45 window is the direct T0-Beta signal. The HMAC chain provides tamper-evident evidence of the monitoring control's operation over the pilot window.

**Cross-references:** `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §59` (job 50 `champion_login_monitor` — primary consumer); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §61.4` (OQ-CHAMP-OBS-01 resolution — de-dup mechanism definition); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §46.2` (T0-Beta trigger: "Day-45 champion logins < 2"); `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §17` (enterprise pilot runbook — SSO setup timing may affect when admin champion first logs in). Closes `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §59.10` item 1 documentation portion (P0/M6).

---

### Enterprise Admin Programme Health events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · COST_MODEL §54 · C1.2 / CC2.2)

> Defined in `docs/COST_MODEL.md §54` (v2.27.0, 2026-07-01 — DEC-092 OQ-CS-02 resolution). One LOW-severity event emitted when the tenant admin loads the Programme Health card in the Admin Dashboard Overview tab and receives a non-`insufficient_data` label. This event proves the PHI label (Healthy / Needs Attention / At Risk) — not the raw FEHS score — was the surface disclosed to the tenant admin, satisfying SOC 2 C1.2 (confidential information — confirming what was exposed) and CC2.2 (external communication of programme health). **Emitter:** Admin Dashboard backend (`emit-audit-event` Worker) on HTTP 200 from `GET /api/admin/tenants/:tenantId/programme-health` with `phi_label ≠ 'insufficient_data'` in the response. **PHI-VIEW-01 chain ordering:** `admin.programme_health_label_viewed` must only fire after `enterprise.health_score_updated` for the same `tenant_id` and `snapshot_date` — guaranteed by read-after-write model (Worker reads from `tenant_engagement_snapshots`, populated by the `fehs_compute` pg_cron job that emits `enterprise.health_score_updated`); not a Worker HTTP 422 invariant. **Deduplication key:** `phi-label-viewed-{tenant_id}-{admin_user_id}-{snapshot_date}` — `emit-audit-event` Worker returns HTTP 200 `{"deduplicated": true}` on key hit; prevents event flooding on page refresh within the same snapshot period. **Privacy floor:** no raw FEHS score (0–100); no `activation_rate_pct` or `wau_rate_pct` aggregate metrics; no individual employee `user_id`, name, email, health value, coaching content, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category data; `admin_user_id` is a FORM-internal auth UUID only — never exposed to employer HR (per `hr_never_sees_individual_data` invariant, `docs/ENTERPRISE.md`); `form_api` REVOKED from `tenant_engagement_snapshots` direct access; `insufficient_data` state (k < 5 activated members — QBR-K-ANON-01 Tier 1 floor, §51) does NOT emit this event.

| Event type | Severity | Retention | Trigger | Key payload fields |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `admin.programme_health_label_viewed` | LOW | 1 yr | Admin Dashboard `GET /api/admin/tenants/:tenantId/programme-health` returns HTTP 200 with `phi_label ≠ 'insufficient_data'`; deduplicated per snapshot period | `phi_label` (enum: `'healthy'`\|`'needs_attention'`\|`'at_risk'`), `snapshot_date` (ISO 8601 date — underlying monthly snapshot), `trend` (`'improved'`\|`'stable'`\|`'declined'`\|`null`), `admin_user_id` (pseudonymous UUID — FORM-internal auth UUID per §29.12 pattern; **never** employer HR identifier) |

**Zod v2 schema (canonical source: `docs/COST_MODEL.md §54.7`):**

```typescript
// admin.programme_health_label_viewed (LOW · 1 yr · C1.2 / CC2.2)
// PHI-VIEW-01: must fire after enterprise.health_score_updated for same tenant_id + snapshot_date
// Dedup key: phi-label-viewed-{tenant_id}-{admin_user_id}-{snapshot_date}
// Privacy invariant: NO fehs_score, NO activation_rate_pct, NO individual employee user_id
const ProgrammeHealthLabelViewedSchema = z.object({
  phi_label:       z.enum(['healthy', 'needs_attention', 'at_risk']),
  snapshot_date:   z.string().regex(/^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/),     // ISO 8601 date only
  trend:           z.enum(['improved', 'stable', 'declined']).nullable(),
  admin_user_id:   z.string().uuid(),                             // FORM-internal auth UUID only
});
```

**Emitter:** Admin Dashboard backend (Workers layer) after PHI Worker endpoint returns HTTP 200. `form_api` and `form_system` roles cannot directly emit — requires `tenant_admin` RLS auth context. `form_admin` cross-tenant read allowed (for CSM tooling) but also subject to dedup invariant. Emit is skipped entirely when `phi_label = 'insufficient_data'` (k < 5 gate — no event, no dedup key write).

**SOC 2 auditor narrative (C1.2):** `admin.programme_health_label_viewed` provides the tamper-evident, auditor-inspectable proof that the PHI label string — not the raw FEHS score — was the only signal disclosed to the tenant admin at each card load. The `phi_label` field in the chain event confirms what was exposed; the absence of `fehs_score`, `activation_rate_pct`, and `wau_rate_pct` in the payload schema confirms what was withheld. The annual PHI-E-001 artefact (`docs/SOC2_READINESS.md` — pending P1/M8) cross-references this event stream as the primary C1.2 evidence that FORM's confidential FEHS methodology was not disclosed to enterprise customers.

**SOC 2 auditor narrative (CC2.2):** Each `admin.programme_health_label_viewed` event with a non-null `trend` field constitutes an auditor-inspectable record of what programme health communication was sent externally (to the tenant admin) and in what form (3-band label + trend direction). CC2.2 requires external communication of commitments — the PHI card is the approved communication surface; this event chain proves the communication happened and its content was bounded.

**Cross-references:** `docs/COST_MODEL.md §54` (full PHI design — DEC-092, API spec, band mapping, CSM efficiency economics, privacy floor, implementation checklist; §54.7 = canonical Zod schema and DEC-030 event spec; §54.10 item 3 — this registration closes P1/M7 obligation); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §26.9` (FEHS 6-signal model — PHI band mapping input; raw FEHS withheld from this event per §33.3 policy); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §33.3` (CHS "internal only" — rationale for withholding raw FEHS; §33.3 PHI exception note — 🟢 Done v5.12.4, 2026-07-01); `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §17` (aggregate-only admin reporting schema — PHI consistent with existing exposure model); `docs/DECISION_LOG.md DEC-092` (formal decision record — 2026-07-01); `enterprise.health_score_updated` (PHI-VIEW-01 predecessor event — confirms FEHS snapshot exists before PHI label is computed); `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md` (PHI-E-001 C1.2/CC2.2 evidence — pending P1/M8 registration). **Closes `docs/COST_MODEL.md §54.10` item 3 (P1/M7 — 🟢 2026-07-01).** Owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer.
