```typescript
  error: z.string().optional(), // raw error — excluded from PagerDuty payload
});
```

#### BCL-CHAIN-01 Ordering Invariant

| Invariant ID | Rule | HTTP error code | Enforcement point | Exemptions |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| BCL-CHAIN-01 | `backchannel_logout.revoked` MUST be preceded by `backchannel_logout.received` with the same `{tenant_id, bcl_request_id}` — anchor lookup window: 60 seconds (TTL in audit KV index) | `BCL_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` (HTTP 422) | `emit-audit-event` Worker — pending `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §46.8` item 7 (P0/M8); co-activates R-05 on violation | `backchannel_logout.failed` is **exempt** — must be writable even when `received` was never persisted |

#### BCL SOC 2 Evidence Artefacts

| ID | SOC 2 criteria | Description | Retention | R2 path |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **BCL-E-001** | CC6.1, CC6.3 | 30-day `backchannel_logout.*` DEC-030 HMAC-chain export — all tenants with `backchannel_logout_enabled = true`; confirms every `.received` anchor is followed by `.validated` + `.revoked` (or `.failed`); automated monthly export via `compliance/scripts/export-bcl-events.sh`; signed JSON Lines | 7 yr | `compliance/evidence/oidc-bcl/bcl-e-001-30day-export.jsonl` |
| **BCL-E-002** | CC6.1 | Integration test results — Okta developer sandbox (BCL-I-001) + Entra ID developer tenant (BCL-I-003); CI run artefact collected at §46.8 P0 checklist completion; compliance-officer files after BCL-I pass | 7 yr | `compliance/evidence/oidc-bcl/bcl-e-002-integration-test.json` |
| **BCL-E-003** | CC8.1 | Migration 0101 (`oidc_sub_hash` column on `enterprise_sessions`) apply timestamp + devops-lead sign-off | 7 yr | `compliance/evidence/oidc-bcl/bcl-e-003-migration-0101-log.json` |

**Collection responsibility:** compliance-officer files BCL-E-001 at T+30 days post M8 go-live. platform-engineer files BCL-E-002 at P0 checklist completion. devops-lead files BCL-E-003 at migration apply. BCL-E-001 through BCL-E-003 registered in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §163` (count 132 → 135, per §46.8 item 10 P1).

**Auditor narrative for CC6.1:** FORM's OIDC back-channel logout Worker (`oidc-backchannel-logout.ts`, §46.4) ensures that when an IdP (Okta, Azure AD) initiates session revocation — typically on IdP-side user logout, device deregistration, or admin revocation — all matching FORM `enterprise_sessions` rows are revoked within the 2-second SLA. Without BCL, a user revoked at the IdP would retain a valid FORM session token until natural expiry. `backchannel_logout.received` → `.validated` → `.revoked` HMAC triples (BCL-E-001 export) prove the termination control was exercised. `backchannel_logout.revoked` with `sessions_revoked_count: 0` documents the no-match path (IdP session unknown to FORM) — a bounded, expected non-failure condition, not a control gap.

**Auditor narrative for CC6.3:** The BCL lifecycle events provide an immutable audit trail of OIDC session revocation at both the FORM layer (`enterprise_sessions.revoked_at` set by `revokeOidcSessions()`) and the federation layer (IdP-initiated BCL round-trip). `revocation_method` in `.validated` and `.revoked` distinguishes `sid`-path (single-session precision) from `sub`-path (all-sessions-for-sub revocation) — both meet CC6.3 session lifecycle completeness. BCL-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant prevents phantom `.revoked` events that could obscure incomplete revocation.

**Retention table additions:** `backchannel_logout.received` / `backchannel_logout.validated` / `backchannel_logout.revoked` STANDARD 7yr CC6.1/CC6.3; `backchannel_logout.failed` HIGH 7yr CC6.1.

---

### BCL Chain Integrity Monitor events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-74 · OBSERVABILITY §70.9 · SOC 2 CC7.2/CC7.3/CC8.1)

> Three DEC-030 HMAC-chained events emitted by the hourly BCL-CHAIN-01 integrity check job (`docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §70.9 item 4` — pg_cron job 59 (`bcl_chain_integrity_check`, `form_audit` role, hourly `0 * * * *`; DEC-098, 2026-07-04)) to provide continuous monitoring of the BCL-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant defined in `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §46.11`. The check job runs the R-74-C2 retrospective SQL against `audit_log_events` hourly and emits `security.bcl_chain_01_violation` (CRITICAL) per orphaned `{tenant_id, bcl_request_id}` pair — defined as a `backchannel_logout.revoked` row with no corresponding `backchannel_logout.received` anchor within the 60-second TTL window — or `system.bcl_chain_check_passed` (LOW) on a clean run. `security.bcl_chain_01_violation_closed` (HIGH) is emitted by IC under PAM elevation when R-74 is resolved and compliance-officer has signed off on BCL-CHN-E-001. **Privacy floor (all three events):** `tenant_id` and `bcl_request_id` are FORM-internal UUIDs. No `user_id`, no `oidc_sub_hash`, no employee name, email, coaching content, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category data in any payload. `oidc_sub_hash` is present in underlying `audit_log_events` rows — it is never surfaced in violation event payloads. **BCL-VIO-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant:** `security.bcl_chain_01_violation_closed` MUST be preceded by a `security.bcl_chain_01_violation` with the same `{tenant_id, bcl_request_id, incident_id}` in the HMAC chain — `emit-audit-event` Worker returns HTTP 422 `BCL_VIO_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` on inversion; implementation pending R-74.12 item 1 (P1/M8). Cross-ref: `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-74` (BCL-CHAIN-01 Integrity Violation P0 runbook — R-74.7 canonical event spec); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §70.4 AL-BCL-03` (P0 PagerDuty firing rule); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §70.9 item 4` (check job spec; OQ-BCL-OBS-01 resolved — pg_cron job 59 adopted per DEC-098, 2026-07-04); `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §46.11` (BCL-CHAIN-01 invariant + `BCL_ANCHOR_KV` 60s TTL definition). **Closes `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-74.12` item 1 (P1/M8) and `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §70.9` item 11 (P1/M8).** Owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer + devops-lead.

| Event type | Severity | Retention | Trigger | Key payload fields |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `security.bcl_chain_01_violation` | CRITICAL | 7 yr | Hourly BCL-CHAIN-01 check job (§70.9 item 4) finds ≥ 1 orphaned `backchannel_logout.revoked` row via R-74-C2 SQL; emits one event per `{tenant_id, bcl_request_id}` orphan; P0 PagerDuty `form-security`; no auto-resolve; no cooldown; co-activates R-74; **self-referential note:** if `emit-audit-event` is the source of the H4/H5 bug and cannot emit this event, the check job retries via `compliance_reviewer` PAM-elevated direct Supabase INSERT per R-74.7 | `tenant_id` (UUID — affected tenant), `bcl_request_id` (UUID — the orphaned `backchannel_logout.revoked` event's correlation key), `incident_id` (UUID — FORM-generated per violation instance; BCL-VIO-CHAIN-01 anchor; bridges this event and its `violation_closed` terminal), `violation_type` (enum `retrospective_sql` — hourly job found orphan in persisted chain; distinguish from live `BCL_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` HTTP 422 handled by §46.11 without emitting this event), `detected_at` (ISO 8601 UTC — check job run timestamp), `revoked_event_at` (ISO 8601 UTC — `created_at` of the orphaned `backchannel_logout.revoked` event in the HMAC chain) |
| `security.bcl_chain_01_violation_closed` | HIGH | 7 yr | IC PAM-elevated (compliance-officer sign-off required per R-74); emitted at R-74 resolution after: (1) R-74-C2 SQL returns 0 rows, (2) BCL-CHN-E-001 artefact filed to R2, (3) compliance-officer approves via T-74-C template; BCL-VIO-CHAIN-01 terminal event; `emit-audit-event` Worker returns HTTP 422 `BCL_VIO_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` if no prior `security.bcl_chain_01_violation` with same `incident_id` exists in the chain | `tenant_id` (UUID — affected tenant), `bcl_request_id` (UUID — original orphaned request), `incident_id` (UUID — BCL-VIO-CHAIN-01 terminal; MUST match `security.bcl_chain_01_violation`), `pam_request_id` (UUID — IC PAM session; two-person auth §19.4 required), `root_cause_code` (enum: `H1` \| `H2` \| `H3` \| `H4` \| `H5` — per R-74.5 root cause taxonomy: H1 TTL expiry in retry window, H2 `writeBclAnchor()` silent failure, H3 IdP duplicate replay, H4 code bug, H5 malicious manipulation), `incident_reference` (string — R-74 PagerDuty incident URL or FORM IR ticket; non-empty), `artefact_path` (string — R2 object path to BCL-CHN-E-001 package), `r74_c2_zero_row_confirmed` (`true` literal — HTTP 422 if absent or `false`; precondition that R-74-C2 SQL returned zero rows at time of closure) |
| `system.bcl_chain_check_passed` | LOW | 1 yr | Hourly BCL-CHAIN-01 check job all-clear path: R-74-C2 SQL returns 0 rows — no orphaned `backchannel_logout.revoked` events detected in the HMAC chain; emitted fleet-wide once per run (not per tenant — privacy floor: zero-orphan signal reveals no per-tenant information); no alert; surfaces in BCL-OBS-E-001 quarterly report (§70.8) as continuous attestation log | `bcl_pairs_checked` (int ≥ 0 — count of `backchannel_logout.revoked` events scanned by R-74-C2 SQL in this run; `bcl_pairs_checked = 0` is valid if no BCL activity in the period, emitted as explicit attestation), `check_run_at` (ISO 8601 UTC — check job execution timestamp) |

```typescript
// AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §BCL-Chain-Monitor-Events — Zod v2 schemas
// Canonical source: docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-74.7 + docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §70.9 item 4

import { z } from 'zod/v2';

// ── security.bcl_chain_01_violation ──────────────────────────────────────────
export const BclChain01ViolationPayload = z.object({
  tenant_id:        z.string().uuid(),
  bcl_request_id:   z.string().uuid(),
  incident_id:      z.string().uuid(),           // BCL-VIO-CHAIN-01 anchor
  violation_type:   z.literal('retrospective_sql'),
  detected_at:      z.string().datetime(),
  revoked_event_at: z.string().datetime(),       // created_at of orphaned .revoked event
});

// ── security.bcl_chain_01_violation_closed ───────────────────────────────────
export const BclChain01ViolationClosedPayload = z.object({
  tenant_id:                 z.string().uuid(),
  bcl_request_id:            z.string().uuid(),
  incident_id:               z.string().uuid(), // BCL-VIO-CHAIN-01 terminal; must match violation
  pam_request_id:            z.string().uuid(),
  root_cause_code:           z.enum(['H1', 'H2', 'H3', 'H4', 'H5']),
  incident_reference:        z.string().min(1),
  artefact_path:             z.string().min(1), // R2 path to BCL-CHN-E-001
  r74_c2_zero_row_confirmed: z.literal(true),
});

// ── system.bcl_chain_check_passed ────────────────────────────────────────────
export const BclChainCheckPassedPayload = z.object({
  bcl_pairs_checked: z.number().int().min(0),
  check_run_at:      z.string().datetime(),
});
```

#### BCL-VIO-CHAIN-01 Ordering Invariant

| Invariant ID | Rule | HTTP error code | Enforcement point | Exemptions |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| BCL-VIO-CHAIN-01 | `security.bcl_chain_01_violation_closed` MUST be preceded by `security.bcl_chain_01_violation` with the same `{tenant_id, bcl_request_id, incident_id}` in the HMAC chain | `BCL_VIO_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` (HTTP 422) | `emit-audit-event` Worker — implementation pending R-74.12 item 1 (P1/M8) | None — no `violation_closed` event may be emitted without a prior violation anchor for the same `incident_id` |

#### BCL Chain Integrity Monitor SOC 2 Evidence Artefact

| ID | SOC 2 criteria | Description | Retention | R2 path |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **BCL-CHN-E-001** | CC7.2, CC7.3, CC8.1 | Per-activation R-74 evidence package. Required files: `incident-timeline.md`, `r74-c1-output.txt` (audit log extraction), `r74-c2-output.txt` (must show 0 rows at closure), `r74-c3-raw-output.txt` (CF KV TTL metadata), `r74-c4-pam-session-state.txt`, `chain-segment.json` (raw HMAC chain entries for affected `bcl_request_id` — restricted to IC + compliance-officer + outside counsel for H5; `oidc_sub_hash` MUST be redacted before auditor sharing), `root-cause-analysis.md` (compliance-officer signature required), `bcl_chain_01_violation_closed-event-id.txt`. Compliance-officer sign-off required before R-74 closure. | 7 yr | `compliance/evidence/oidc-bcl/chain-violations/bcl-chn-e-001-{incident_id}-{YYYY-MM-DD}/` |

**Collection responsibility:** IC files BCL-CHN-E-001 at R-74 resolution; compliance-officer co-signs. `security.bcl_chain_01_violation_closed` `artefact_path` field records the R2 location. Registration in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4` master evidence table pending R-74.12 item 4 (P1/M8).

**Auditor narrative for CC7.2:** The hourly BCL-CHAIN-01 check job provides continuous retroactive detection of BCL ordering violations beyond the 60-second live enforcement window. Each run of R-74-C2 SQL constitutes an auditor-verifiable monitoring sweep: `system.bcl_chain_check_passed` with `bcl_pairs_checked > 0` affirmatively attests that all `backchannel_logout.revoked` events in the HMAC chain had valid `backchannel_logout.received` anchors. Combined with the `system.bcl_chain_check_passed` 2-hour freshness window (to be configured per §70.9 item 4), this provides a continuous CC7.2 audit record with no gap longer than 2 hours.

**Auditor narrative for CC7.3:** When `security.bcl_chain_01_violation` fires, IC is paged P0 via AL-BCL-03 (PagerDuty `form-security`); IC-declare within 15 minutes; R-74 response timeline documented in BCL-CHN-E-001 `incident-timeline.md`. `security.bcl_chain_01_violation_closed` with `r74_c2_zero_row_confirmed: true` provides tamper-evident proof that IC confirmed zero remaining violations before declaring the incident resolved. Compliance-officer sign-off (T-74-C template + `violation_closed` event) closes the formal audit trail.

**Auditor narrative for CC8.1:** For root cause H4 (code bug) or H5 (malicious manipulation), BCL-CHN-E-001 `root-cause-analysis.md` documents the specific change that broke or violated the BCL-CHAIN-01 invariant. The R-74 resolution playbook requires a tabletop exercise before M8 deploy (R-74.12 item 5), demonstrating that FORM treats BCL-CHAIN-01 as a change-management control gate.

**Retention table additions:** `security.bcl_chain_01_violation` CRITICAL 7yr CC7.2/CC7.3/CC8.1; `security.bcl_chain_01_violation_closed` HIGH 7yr CC7.2/CC7.3/CC8.1; `system.bcl_chain_check_passed` LOW 1yr CC7.2.

---

### BCL Admin Session Bulk Revocation events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-73 · SOC 2 CC6.3/CC7.3)

> One DEC-030 HMAC-chained event emitted by IC under PAM elevation (two-person auth §19.4) via `POST /internal/tenant/{slug}/revoke-all-sessions` Admin API endpoint during R-73 (BCL REVOCATION_QUEUE Exhausted) resolution. This event provides tamper-evident proof that FORM exercised an administrative control to revoke all active enterprise sessions for the affected tenant when the BCL REVOCATION_QUEUE three-retry budget (5s/10s/20s, 35s total) was exhausted — closing the CC6.3 access revocation gap created by the R-73 incident. **Not part of the BCL-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant** — this is a post-incident remediation action, not a BCL lifecycle event. **Privacy floor:** `sessions_revoked_count` is an aggregate integer; `bcl_request_ids` are FORM-internal UUIDs; no `user_id`, no individual session UUIDs, no `oidc_sub_hash`, no employee name, email, coaching content, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category data. Cross-ref: `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-73` (BCL REVOCATION_QUEUE Exhausted P1 runbook — R-73.7 canonical event spec + BCL-REV-E-001); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §70.4 AL-BCL-02` (P1 PagerDuty trigger); `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §46.4.3` (REVOCATION_QUEUE retry spec). **Closes `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-73.12` item 1 (P1/M8).** Owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer.

| Event type | Severity | Retention | Trigger | Key payload fields |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `enterprise.admin_sessions_bulk_revoked` | HIGH | 7 yr | IC PAM-elevated (two-person auth §19.4 required) via `POST /internal/tenant/{slug}/revoke-all-sessions` Admin API during R-73 resolution; emitted by endpoint after `enterprise_sessions` bulk update (`revoked_at = NOW()` on all rows where `tenant_id = slug AND revoked_at IS NULL`); not BCL-CHAIN-01 chained; routes to CSM for §4.3 SLA notification (T-73-B within 15 min); P1 evidence that CC6.3 access revocation gap was closed | `tenant_id` (UUID — affected tenant; same slug used in API path), `pam_request_id` (UUID — IC PAM session; two-person auth co-approver identity not in event payload — in `pam_approvals` table), `bcl_request_ids` (string[] of UUIDs — `bcl_request_id` values from the triggering `backchannel_logout.failed` events identified by R-73-C1 scope query; may be empty `[]` if REVOCATION_QUEUE retries exhausted without a traceable `bcl_request_id`), `sessions_revoked_count` (int ≥ 0 — aggregate count of `enterprise_sessions` rows updated; no individual `user_id` or session UUID in payload), `incident_reference` (string — R-73 PagerDuty incident URL or FORM IR ticket; non-empty) |

```typescript
// AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §BCL-Admin-Revocation-Events — Zod v2 schemas
// Canonical source: docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-73.7 + R-73.12 item 1

import { z } from 'zod/v2';

// ── enterprise.admin_sessions_bulk_revoked ────────────────────────────────────
export const AdminSessionsBulkRevokedPayload = z.object({
  tenant_id:              z.string().uuid(),
  pam_request_id:         z.string().uuid(),
  bcl_request_ids:        z.array(z.string().uuid()),   // may be [] if unresolvable
  sessions_revoked_count: z.number().int().min(0),
  incident_reference:     z.string().min(1),
});
```

#### BCL Admin Session Bulk Revocation SOC 2 Evidence Artefact

| ID | SOC 2 criteria | Description | Retention | R2 path |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **BCL-REV-E-001** | CC6.3, CC7.3 | Per-activation R-73 evidence package. Required files: `incident-timeline.md`, `r73-c1-output.txt` (`backchannel_logout.failed` identification), `r73-c2-output.txt` (active session detection — must show 0 active sessions at closure, per privacy floor: aggregate count only, no `user_id`), `r73-c3-output.txt` (post-resolution zero-session verification), `csm-notification.txt` (T-73-B timestamp, tenant slug, CSM acknowledgement; no employee identifiers), `admin_sessions_bulk_revoked-event-id.txt` (`enterprise.admin_sessions_bulk_revoked` DEC-030 chain record ID). Filed within T+60 min of R-73 resolution. | 7 yr | `compliance/evidence/oidc-bcl/bcl-rev-e-001-{YYYY-MM-DD}/` |

**Collection responsibility:** IC files BCL-REV-E-001 within T+60 min of R-73 resolution. compliance-officer reviews within 24 hours. Registration in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4` master evidence table pending R-73.12 item 3 (P1/M8). Provision of `compliance/evidence/oidc-bcl/` R2 subfolder pending R-73.12 item 2 (P1/M8 — devops-lead).

**Auditor narrative for CC6.3:** CC6.3 requires that access is revoked in a timely manner following a revocation event. When REVOCATION_QUEUE exhaustion leaves enterprise employee FORM sessions active after an IdP BCL signal, `enterprise.admin_sessions_bulk_revoked` with `sessions_revoked_count > 0` proves that FORM detected the revocation gap (via AL-BCL-02 P1 PagerDuty) and closed it through a PAM-elevated administrative action within the R-73 response window. The `incident_reference` field links this event to the R-73 IR record for cross-audit traceability.

**Auditor narrative for CC7.3:** BCL-REV-E-001 `csm-notification.txt` documents that the affected tenant's CSM was notified within 15 minutes (§4.3 SLA) of the confirmed P1 incident. `incident-timeline.md` documents the full T+0..T+60 IC response sequence. The two-person auth requirement for `POST /internal/tenant/{slug}/revoke-all-sessions` (§19.4) proves that FORM's remediation action itself was subject to access control and cannot be performed unilaterally.

**Retention table additions:** `enterprise.admin_sessions_bulk_revoked` HIGH 7yr CC6.3/CC7.3.

---

### Enterprise Adoption Monitoring events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · COST_MODEL §40.8 · CC4.1/CC2.2/CC7.3/CC4.2/A1.1)

> Defined in `docs/COST_MODEL.md §40.8` (v2.6, 2026-06-18 — §40 Enterprise Customer Adoption Economics & Seat Utilization Health Model). Five DEC-030 HMAC-chained events covering the monthly adoption snapshot lifecycle, customer milestone detection, health band degradation, QBR completion governance, and CSM follow-up advisory monitoring. **Scope:** Enterprise tier — all active Starter/Growth/Enterprise tenants. **ADO-CHAIN-01 monitoring invariant:** If `enterprise.adoption_health_downgraded` is emitted for a tenant, the `evidence-collection-cron` Worker checks for a corresponding `enterprise.qbr_completed` or CRM CSM check-in within 10 business days; if neither is present, it emits `system.csm_followup_overdue` (LOW advisory, non-blocking). **QBR-PRIV-01 chain invariant:** `enterprise.qbr_completed` carries `privacy_floor_verified: z.literal(true)` — the `emit-audit-event` Worker returns HTTP 422 if the field is absent or `false`, preventing the QBR completion event from being chained without explicit CSM attestation that no individual employee data was shared. **Privacy invariant (all five events):** no `user_id`, no individual health values, no coaching content, no employee names or email addresses — tenant-aggregate or contract-level metadata only. `enterprise_adoption_snapshots` table: `form_api` REVOKED; `compliance_reviewer` SELECT-only; `form_system` ALL. `coaching_engaged_seats < 5` suppressed at API query layer (k-anonymity floor — see `docs/COST_MODEL.md §40.7.2`). Cross-ref: `docs/COST_MODEL.md §40` (full adoption economics model — S1/S2/S3 funnel, health band matrix, QBR financial framework, `enterprise_adoption_snapshots` DDL migration 0078, four DEC-030 event Zod schemas, CSM intervention playbook); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §40.9` (ADO-E-001/002/003 SOC 2 evidence artefacts); `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §90` (ADO-E-001 CC4.1/A1.1, ADO-E-002 CC2.2/CC4.1, ADO-E-003 CC7.3/CC4.2 registered in master evidence table v3.15.0). **Closes `docs/COST_MODEL.md §40.10` checklist item 1 (P0/M10 — register `enterprise.adoption_snapshot_filed`, `enterprise.adoption_milestone_reached`, `enterprise.adoption_health_downgraded`, `enterprise.qbr_completed`, and `system.csm_followup_overdue` in `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §Enterprise + §System` before first enterprise pilot go-live).**

| Event type | Severity | Retention | Trigger | Key payload fields |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `enterprise.adoption_snapshot_filed` | STANDARD | 3 yr | Monthly `evidence-collection-cron` Worker (automated) or CSM manual trigger via Admin Console; fires after INSERT into `enterprise_adoption_snapshots` and health band comparison vs. prior month | `tenant_id` (UUID), `snapshot_month` (ISO date `YYYY-MM-01`), `contracted_seats` (int), `activated_seats` (int), `activation_rate_pct` (float 0–100), `wau_count` (int), `wau_rate_pct` (float 0–100), `habitual_seat_count` (int), `habitual_rate_pct` (float 0–100), `wau_health_band` (enum: `green`\|`amber`\|`red`), `coaching_sessions_total` (int), `filed_by` (enum: `evidence-collection-cron`\|`csm-manual`) |
| `enterprise.adoption_milestone_reached` | STANDARD | 3 yr | Emitted once per milestone per tenant when a snapshot crosses a threshold for the first time: S1 ≥ 40% activated seats by Day 28, S2 WAU ≥ 30% at M3, S3 Habitual ≥ 20% at M6, or WAU crosses Green band (≥ 40%) | `tenant_id` (UUID), `milestone` (enum: `s1_activation_40pct`\|`s2_wau_30pct`\|`s3_habitual_20pct`\|`s2_wau_40pct_green`), `rate_pct` (float 0–100 — actual rate at milestone crossing), `contracted_seats` (int), `days_since_contract_start` (int ≥ 0) |
| `enterprise.adoption_health_downgraded` | HIGH | 3 yr | Monthly snapshot job detects `wau_health_band` is worse than the prior month: Green → Amber, Green → Red, or Amber → Red; also triggers ADO-CHAIN-01 monitoring: `system.csm_followup_overdue` emitted if no CSM follow-up recorded within 10 business days | `tenant_id` (UUID), `snapshot_month` (ISO date `YYYY-MM-01`), `prior_band` (enum: `green`\|`amber`\|`red`), `current_band` (enum: `green`\|`amber`\|`red`), `wau_rate_pct` (float 0–100), `contracted_seats` (int), `acv_usd` (int — aggregate contract value; no individual employee data) |
| `enterprise.qbr_completed` | STANDARD | 3 yr | CSM triggers via Admin Console "Complete QBR" modal after QBR call; cannot be back-dated more than 7 days; **QBR-PRIV-01 chain invariant:** `privacy_floor_verified: true` required or HTTP 422 | `tenant_id` (UUID), `qbr_date` (ISO date `YYYY-MM-DD`), `tier` (enum: `starter`\|`growth`\|`enterprise`), `contracted_seats` (int), `wau_rate_pct_at_qbr` (float 0–100 — from latest snapshot), `wau_health_band_at_qbr` (enum: `green`\|`amber`\|`red`), `renewal_date` (ISO date `YYYY-MM-DD` — date only, no time), `expansion_discussed` (bool), `privacy_floor_verified` (`true` literal — `z.literal(true)`; HTTP 422 if absent or `false`) |
| `system.csm_followup_overdue` | LOW | 1 yr | Advisory: `evidence-collection-cron` weekly sweep emits this when a tenant has `enterprise.adoption_health_downgraded` with no `enterprise.qbr_completed` or CRM check-in task within 10 business days; non-blocking; routes to compliance-officer SIEM review queue, not PagerDuty | `tenant_id` (UUID), `downgrade_event_id` (UUID — DEC-030 chain record ID of the triggering `adoption_health_downgraded` event), `days_since_downgrade` (int ≥ 10), `current_band` (enum: `amber`\|`red`), `acv_usd` (int — contract-level; for CSM triage priority) |

**Zod v2 schemas (canonical source: `docs/COST_MODEL.md §40.8` — registered here for `emit-audit-event` Worker validation):**

```typescript
import { z } from 'zod/v4';

// enterprise.adoption_snapshot_filed — COST_MODEL §40.8.1
export const AdoptionSnapshotFiledPayload = z.object({
  tenant_id:               z.string().uuid(),
  snapshot_month:          z.string().regex(/^\d{4}-\d{2}-01$/),    // ISO date, first of month
  contracted_seats:        z.number().int().positive(),
  activated_seats:         z.number().int().nonnegative(),
  activation_rate_pct:     z.number().min(0).max(100),
  wau_count:               z.number().int().nonnegative(),
  wau_rate_pct:            z.number().min(0).max(100),
  habitual_seat_count:     z.number().int().nonnegative(),
  habitual_rate_pct:       z.number().min(0).max(100),
  wau_health_band:         z.enum(['green', 'amber', 'red']),
  coaching_sessions_total: z.number().int().nonnegative(),
  filed_by:                z.enum(['evidence-collection-cron', 'csm-manual']),
  // Privacy floor: no user_id, no per-user data, no coaching content
});

// enterprise.adoption_milestone_reached — COST_MODEL §40.8.2
export const AdoptionMilestoneReachedPayload = z.object({
  tenant_id:                  z.string().uuid(),
  milestone:                  z.enum([
    's1_activation_40pct',    // 40% seats activated — Day 28/60 milestone
    's2_wau_30pct',           // WAU ≥ 30% at M3
    's3_habitual_20pct',      // Habitual ≥ 20% at M6
    's2_wau_40pct_green',     // WAU crosses Green threshold (≥ 40%)
  ]),
  rate_pct:                   z.number().min(0).max(100),
  contracted_seats:           z.number().int().positive(),
  days_since_contract_start:  z.number().int().nonnegative(),
  // Emitted once per milestone per tenant — not on every snapshot
  // No user_id, no individual data
});

// enterprise.adoption_health_downgraded — COST_MODEL §40.8.3
export const AdoptionHealthDowngradedPayload = z.object({
  tenant_id:          z.string().uuid(),
  snapshot_month:     z.string().regex(/^\d{4}-\d{2}-01$/),
  prior_band:         z.enum(['green', 'amber', 'red']),
  current_band:       z.enum(['green', 'amber', 'red']),
  wau_rate_pct:       z.number().min(0).max(100),
  contracted_seats:   z.number().int().positive(),
  acv_usd:            z.number().positive(),   // aggregate contract value — not individual-level data
  // No user_id, no employee names, no health values
});

// enterprise.qbr_completed — COST_MODEL §40.8.4 + §52 (DEC-089)
export const QbrCompletedPayload = z.object({
  tenant_id:                z.string().uuid(),
  qbr_date:                 z.string().regex(/^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/),
  tier:                     z.enum(['starter', 'growth', 'enterprise']),
  contracted_seats:         z.number().int().positive(),
  wau_rate_pct_at_qbr:      z.number().min(0).max(100),
  wau_health_band_at_qbr:   z.enum(['green', 'amber', 'red']),
  renewal_date:             z.string().regex(/^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/), // date only — no time component
  expansion_discussed:      z.boolean(),
  privacy_floor_verified:   z.literal(true),   // QBR-PRIV-01 chain invariant — HTTP 422 if absent or false
  // No attendee names, no per-user engagement metrics, no coaching content
  //
  // FEHS breakdown — added v2.66 / DEC-089 / COST_MODEL §52
  // Both fields optional now; required at M10 Admin Console modal launch.
  // FEHS-CHAIN-01: if either is present, the other must also be present (HTTP 422 on violation).
  // FEHS-CHAIN-01: |weighted_sum(fehs_breakdown) − fehs_score_at_qbr| must be ≤ 0.5 (HTTP 422 FEHS_CHAIN_01_SCORE_MISMATCH).
  // Worker check order: QBR-PRIV-01 → FEHS-CHAIN-01 → Zod validation.
  fehs_score_at_qbr: z.number().min(0).max(100).optional(),
  // Total FEHS composite score (0–100) at QBR time. Formula: see COST_MODEL §26.9.
  fehs_breakdown: z.object({
    activation_pct:         z.number().min(0).max(100), // signal 1: activated/contracted × 100 (weight 25%)
    wau_pct:                z.number().min(0).max(100), // signal 2: WAU/contracted × 100 (weight 25%)
    seat_utilisation_pct:   z.number().min(0).max(100), // signal 3: habitual/contracted × 100 (weight 20%)
    exec_engagement_score:  z.number().min(0).max(100), // signal 4: exec attendance cadence (weight 15%)
    support_volume_inverse: z.number().min(0).max(100), // signal 5: 100 − min(tickets_30d/floor,100) (weight 10%)
    renewal_distance_score: z.number().min(0).max(100), // signal 6: decay from days_until_renewal (weight 5%)
    // Privacy floor: all six signals are tenant-aggregate only — no user_id, name, email, or health value.
    // k-anonymity: inherits QBR-K-ANON-01 Tier 1 (k ≥ 5) from source health_score_updated aggregates.
  }).optional(),
});

// system.csm_followup_overdue — ADO-CHAIN-01 advisory (LOW, non-blocking)
export const CsmFollowupOverduePayload = z.object({
  tenant_id:             z.string().uuid(),
  downgrade_event_id:    z.string().uuid(),        // DEC-030 chain record ID of adoption_health_downgraded
  days_since_downgrade:  z.number().int().min(10), // advisory fires at ≥ 10 business days
  current_band:          z.enum(['amber', 'red']),
  acv_usd:               z.number().positive(),    // contract-level; for CSM triage priority
  // Advisory only — does not block the chain; no PagerDuty
});
```

**ADO-CHAIN-01 monitoring invariant:** When `enterprise.adoption_health_downgraded` is emitted for a tenant, a CSM check-in task must be created in Linear within 2 business days. The `evidence-collection-cron` Worker (§81) runs a weekly advisory sweep checking whether any `adoption_health_downgraded` event from the past 10 business days lacks a corresponding `enterprise.qbr_completed` event or a CRM-linked CSM task for the same `tenant_id`. If the 10-business-day window is exceeded, the Worker emits `system.csm_followup_overdue` (LOW, 1yr, advisory). This is an operational control, not a chain gate — it does not block any subsequent event and does not return HTTP 422. It routes to the compliance-officer weekly SIEM review queue, not PagerDuty.

**QBR-PRIV-01 chain invariant:** For any `enterprise.qbr_completed` event, `privacy_floor_verified` must be `true`. This field is typed as `z.literal(true)` in the Zod v2 schema — the Admin Console "Complete QBR" modal's submit button is disabled until the CSM checks the attestation checkbox (*"I confirm no individual employee data was shared in this QBR"*). The `emit-audit-event` Worker independently re-validates the literal before chaining the event; if `privacy_floor_verified` is absent or `false`, the Worker returns HTTP 422 and rolls back the Admin Console action. This converts the privacy floor for QBR communications from a procedural policy into an architectural enforcement that creates a tamper-evident chain record of the CSM's attestation.

**Emitter assignments:**
- `enterprise.adoption_snapshot_filed` — `evidence-collection-cron` Cloudflare Worker (§81, automated monthly run) or `admin-dashboard-api` (CSM manual trigger, `form_system` role). Fail-closed: if the Worker returns non-2xx, the `enterprise_adoption_snapshots` INSERT is rolled back.
- `enterprise.adoption_milestone_reached` — `evidence-collection-cron` (same monthly run as `adoption_snapshot_filed`; emitted only once per milestone per `tenant_id`; idempotency enforced via milestone history check before emission).
- `enterprise.adoption_health_downgraded` — `evidence-collection-cron` (same monthly run; emitted after snapshot row is committed and band comparison detects a downgrade; HIGH severity — treated as a potential revenue risk signal).
- `enterprise.qbr_completed` — `admin-dashboard-api` (CSM manual trigger via Admin Console "Complete QBR" modal; `form_system` role; QBR-PRIV-01 chain invariant enforced at Worker layer, not only UI layer).
- `system.csm_followup_overdue` — `evidence-collection-cron` (weekly advisory sweep; LOW severity; no PagerDuty; routes to compliance-officer SIEM review queue).

**SOC 2 evidence artefacts (registered in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §90`, v3.15.0, 2026-06-19):**

| Artefact | SOC 2 criteria | Description | Cadence | Retention | Path |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **ADO-E-001** | CC4.1, A1.1 | Quarterly export of `enterprise_adoption_snapshots` for all active tenants; `wau_health_band` distribution (Green/Amber/Red counts + ACV at each band — Q3 fleet summary); k-anonymity attestation for `coaching_engaged_seats`; zero-row quarters filed as affirmative health attestation JSON | Quarterly from M11 | 3 yr | `compliance/evidence/adoption/ADO-E-001_<YYYY-QN>.csv` |
| **ADO-E-002** | CC2.2, CC4.1 | Quarterly export of all `enterprise.qbr_completed` DEC-030 events; `privacy_floor_verified: true` in every row verified at export; zero-event Starter quarters documented as expected (semi-annual cadence) | Quarterly from M11 | 3 yr | `compliance/evidence/adoption/ADO-E-002_<YYYY-QN>.csv` |
| **ADO-E-003** | CC7.3, CC4.2 | Quarterly export of `enterprise.adoption_health_downgraded` events paired with evidence of CSM follow-up within 10 business days (`system.csm_followup_overdue` absence or resolution confirmed); zero-event quarters filed as affirmative attestation JSON | Quarterly from M11 | 3 yr | `compliance/evidence/adoption/ADO-E-003_<YYYY-QN>.csv` |

**Auditor narrative for CC4.1:** FORM monitors enterprise customer adoption health on a monthly cadence via `enterprise_adoption_snapshots`. The DEC-030 chain records the snapshot (ADO-E-001), milestone achievement, and health band degradation (ADO-E-003). The QBR cadence (ADO-E-002) is a formally documented performance review with each tenant. Together, these three artefacts demonstrate that FORM evaluates system performance against defined thresholds and acts when those thresholds are not met — satisfying CC4.1's requirement to monitor performance against commitments on an ongoing basis.

**Auditor narrative for CC2.2:** `enterprise.qbr_completed` with `privacy_floor_verified: true` is an immutable DEC-030 chain record that FORM communicated aggregate product performance to the enterprise tenant on a documented date. QBR-PRIV-01 ensures the communication did not include prohibited individual-level data — a chain enforcement, not a procedural check. ADO-E-002 provides quarterly evidence of this communication cadence across the fleet, satisfying CC2.2's requirement to communicate externally about matters affecting the security and performance of the system.

**Auditor narrative for CC7.3:** `enterprise.adoption_health_downgraded` (HIGH) is the anomaly-detection event — it signals that a tenant's adoption trajectory has worsened, which is a leading indicator of churn risk and, at Red band, a deficiency in FORM's customer success controls. ADO-E-003 pairs downgrade events with evidence of CSM follow-up within 10 business days, demonstrating that FORM evaluates identified anomalies and responds within a defined timeframe. `system.csm_followup_overdue` ensures unanswered downgrade events surface to compliance-officer review — converting a potential process gap into a chain-recorded advisory signal.

**Auditor narrative for CC4.2 and A1.1:** Monthly snapshots, health band downgrade detection, CSM follow-up monitoring, and quarterly QBR cadence create a continuous performance monitoring loop demonstrating that FORM evaluates deficiencies (Red-band accounts) and tracks resolution. A1.1 mapping: adoption health degradation is a capacity-adjacent signal — insufficient utilisation creates churn risk, which threatens service continuity for the affected tenant's contract; ADO-CHAIN-01 CSM follow-up monitoring with `system.csm_followup_overdue` ensures FORM responds to utilisation threats proactively.

---

### Enterprise Pilot Save Protocol events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · COST_MODEL §46.7 · CC3.2/CC7.2/CC9.2)

> Defined in `docs/COST_MODEL.md §46.7` (v2.20.0, 2026-06-27). Four DEC-030 HMAC-chained events covering the Pilot CSM Save Protocol lifecycle: T0-Alpha trigger record, weekly checkpoint, save outcome, and abandon outcome. **PILOT-SAVE-CHAIN-01 chain invariant:** For any save-protocol cluster sharing a `pilot_id`, `enterprise.pilot_save_protocol_triggered` must precede `enterprise.pilot_saved` or `enterprise.pilot_abandoned` within 45 calendar days; the `emit-audit-event` Worker validates predecessor presence before appending the terminal event; HTTP 422 `PILOT_SAVE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` on violation → R-05. **Privacy floor (all four events):** `pilot_id` is a FORM-internal UUID — no employee `user_id`, name, email, health metric, coaching content, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category data in any payload; `activation_rate` and `wau_rate` are aggregate NUMERIC (0.00–1.00) across all enrolled seats. `form_api` REVOKED on `pilot_programs`, `session_completed`, and `audit_log_events`. **SOC 2 evidence source:** SAVE-E-001 annual chain export (CC3.2/CC7.2/CC9.2) — primary definition in `docs/COST_MODEL.md §46.8`; registered in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §126.1`. **Registered v2.56 (2026-06-29) — closes `docs/COST_MODEL.md §46.9` item 1 (P0/M4) and jointly satisfies `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-49.12` item 1 (P0/M5).**

| Event type | Severity | Retention | Trigger | Key payload fields |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `enterprise.pilot_save_protocol_triggered` | HIGH | 7 yr | T0-Alpha condition detected by job 49 (`pilot_activation_monitor`): Day-14 cohort activation rate < 30% threshold; IC (CSM or compliance-officer, PAM-elevated) emits at R-49 Step 1 after R-49-C2 confirms active Day-14 cohort; PILOT-SAVE-CHAIN-01 anchor | `pilot_id` (UUID), `trigger_type` (enum: `T0A`\|`T0B`\|`T0G`), `activation_rate` (float 0–1 — aggregate Day-14 rate at trigger), `trigger_day` (int > 0 — day within pilot where T0-Alpha fired), `triggered_by_pam_session` (UUID — IC PAM session) |
| `enterprise.pilot_save_protocol_updated` | STANDARD | 3 yr | CSM emits weekly progress checkpoint during active save-protocol window; records current trajectory; no chain ordering constraint (advisory cadence record) | `pilot_id` (UUID), `day` (int > 0 — current day within pilot), `activation_rate` (float 0–1), `wau_rate` (float 0–1 — weekly active user rate), `intervention_applied` (enum: `kickoff_recall`\|`coaching_demo_workshop`\|`it_sprint`\|`content_reseeding`\|`pilot_extension`\|`none`) |
| `enterprise.pilot_saved` | HIGH | 7 yr | CSM emits when pilot reaches target activation rate within T+30 window; PILOT-SAVE-CHAIN-01 successor — HTTP 422 `PILOT_SAVE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` if no prior `enterprise.pilot_save_protocol_triggered` for same `pilot_id` within 45 days | `pilot_id` (UUID), `final_activation_rate` (float 0–1), `days_to_save` (int > 0 — calendar days from trigger to save), `seat_count` (int > 0 — enrolled seats at close) |
| `enterprise.pilot_abandoned` | HIGH | 7 yr | Founder emits on formal abandon decision (COST_MODEL §46.4); PILOT-SAVE-CHAIN-01 successor — HTTP 422 `PILOT_SAVE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` if no prior `enterprise.pilot_save_protocol_triggered` for same `pilot_id` within 45 days; `founder_approved: true` literal required | `pilot_id` (UUID), `final_activation_rate` (float 0–1), `intervention_count` (int ≥ 0 — total interventions attempted during save window), `founder_approved` (`true` literal — HTTP 422 if absent or false) |

**Zod v2 schemas (canonical source: `docs/COST_MODEL.md §46.7` — registered here for `emit-audit-event` Worker validation):**

```typescript
export const PilotSaveProtocolTriggeredPayload = z.object({
  pilot_id:                 z.string().uuid(),
  trigger_type:             z.enum(['T0A', 'T0B', 'T0G']),
  activation_rate:          z.number().min(0).max(1),
  trigger_day:              z.number().int().positive(),
  triggered_by_pam_session: z.string().uuid(),
});
export const PilotSaveProtocolUpdatedPayload = z.object({
  pilot_id:             z.string().uuid(),
  day:                  z.number().int().positive(),
  activation_rate:      z.number().min(0).max(1),
  wau_rate:             z.number().min(0).max(1),
  intervention_applied: z.enum(['kickoff_recall','coaching_demo_workshop','it_sprint','content_reseeding','pilot_extension','none']),
});
export const PilotSavedPayload = z.object({
  pilot_id:              z.string().uuid(),
  final_activation_rate: z.number().min(0).max(1),
  days_to_save:          z.number().int().positive(),
  seat_count:            z.number().int().positive(),
});
export const PilotAbandonedPayload = z.object({
  pilot_id:               z.string().uuid(),
  final_activation_rate:  z.number().min(0).max(1),
  intervention_count:     z.number().int().nonnegative(),
  founder_approved:       z.literal(true),
});
```

**PILOT-SAVE-CHAIN-01 — Ordering invariant:** For any `pilot_id`, `enterprise.pilot_save_protocol_triggered` must precede `enterprise.pilot_saved` or `enterprise.pilot_abandoned` in the HMAC chain within 45 calendar days; `emit-audit-event` Worker returns HTTP 422 `PILOT_SAVE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` if the predecessor is absent or older than 45 days → R-05 co-activated. `enterprise.pilot_save_protocol_updated` is advisory — no chain ordering invariant.

**Emitters:** `enterprise.pilot_save_protocol_triggered` — IC (CSM or compliance-officer, PAM-elevated) at R-49 Step 1 when R-49-C2 positive (active Day-14 cohort detected); `enterprise.pilot_save_protocol_updated` — CSM weekly cadence during active save-protocol window; `enterprise.pilot_saved` — CSM at save close; `enterprise.pilot_abandoned` — founder (PAM-elevated) at formal abandon decision.

**CC3.2 auditor narrative:** The `enterprise.pilot_save_protocol_triggered` → `enterprise.pilot_saved` / `enterprise.pilot_abandoned` PILOT-SAVE-CHAIN-01 chain is an immutable DEC-030 record that FORM responded to a detected deficiency (sub-30% Day-14 activation rate) with a defined corrective action (Pilot CSM Save Protocol). CC3.2 requires FORM to implement procedures to identify and respond to identified risks; this chain provides tamper-evident, auditor-verifiable evidence of the detection-to-resolution lifecycle. SAVE-E-001 annual chain export (CC3.2/CC7.2/CC9.2) is the primary SOC 2 evidence artefact — registered in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §126.1`.

**CC7.2 auditor narrative:** `enterprise.pilot_save_protocol_triggered` carries `activation_rate` (aggregate NUMERIC) and `trigger_day` — auditor-inspectable IC attestation that job 49 (`pilot_activation_monitor`) detected the T0-Alpha condition within the 26h freshness window. The HMAC chain proves detection timing is tamper-evident. `enterprise.pilot_saved` or `enterprise.pilot_abandoned` terminal event closes the corrective action loop, satisfying CC7.2 monitoring-to-remediation evidence. `enterprise.pilot_save_protocol_updated` weekly checkpoints provide continuous CSM progress documentation throughout the T+30 intervention window.

**CC9.2 auditor narrative:** The save-protocol chain — triggered, updated, saved/abandoned — is the primary record of FORM's vendor/customer risk response for pilots at risk of non-adoption. `enterprise.pilot_saved` provides the positive outcome record; `enterprise.pilot_abandoned` (with `founder_approved: true` literal) provides the formal governance decision record with founder sign-off attestation, satisfying CC9.2 contract-level decision accountability.

---

### Pilot Activation Monitor Stale events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-49 · CC7.2/A1.1/CC3.2)

> Defined in `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-49` (v1.0, 2026-06-29) and `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §58` (v5.5.0, 2026-06-29). Two DEC-030 HMAC-chained events anchoring the R-49 incident lifecycle for pg_cron job 49 (`pilot_activation_monitor`, `0 10 * * *`, 26h freshness window) staleness. **PILOT-ACT-STALE-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant:** `system.pilot_activation_monitor_restored` is blocked (HTTP 422 `PILOT_ACT_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION`) if no prior `system.pilot_activation_monitor_stale_declared` exists in the HMAC chain for the same `incident_id`; additionally `restored` is blocked after it has already been emitted for the same `incident_id` (terminal enforcement) → R-05 co-activated on violation. **Privacy floor (both events):** `incident_id` is a FORM-internal UUID; all other fields are booleans, positive numbers, enums, or datetimes — no employee `user_id`, no `activation_rate` value, no `pilot_id`, no tenant name, or GDPR Art. 9 health data. R-49-C2/C3 scope queries surface only `pilot_id` UUIDs and aggregate rates within the IC's `#security-restricted` session — never in the chain. **SOC 2 evidence source:** PILOT-ACT-STALE-E-001 per-incident IC narrative (CC7.2/A1.1/CC3.2) — registered in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §126.6`. **Registered v2.56 (2026-06-29) — closes `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-49.12` item 1 (P0/M5).**

| Event type | Severity | Retention | Trigger | Key payload fields |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `system.pilot_activation_monitor_stale_declared` | HIGH | 7 yr | IC (devops-lead, PAM-elevated) declares job 49 stale at R-49 Step 1 after PagerDuty fires `system.cron_job_stale` for `pilot_activation_monitor`; PILOT-ACT-STALE-CHAIN-01 anchor; `day14_cohort_active = true` gates R-49-C2 customer-success page; `save_protocol_gap = true` governs PILOT-ACT-STALE-E-001 retention tier (7yr) | `incident_id` (UUID), `stale_hours` (float > 0 — hours since last successful `pilot_activation_monitor` run), `day14_cohort_active` (bool — true if R-49-C2 finds ≥ 1 active Day-14 pilot cohort during stale window; triggers customer-success page), `save_protocol_gap` (bool — true if R-49-C3 finds T0-Alpha condition that would have fired but didn't during stale window) |
| `system.pilot_activation_monitor_restored` | STANDARD | 3 yr | IC confirms job 49 restored and first successful post-fix run confirmed; PILOT-ACT-STALE-CHAIN-01 terminal event; HTTP 422 `PILOT_ACT_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` if no prior `stale_declared` for same `incident_id` → R-05; `save_protocol_gap_resolved = true` required before P1 incident closure when gap found | `incident_id` (UUID), `root_cause` (enum: `H1_deleted`\|`H1_disabled`\|`H2_permissions_revoked`\|`H3_pg_net_degraded`\|`H4_supabase_outage`), `stale_hours` (float > 0 — total stale duration from declaration to restoration), `day14_cohort_affected` (bool — matches `day14_cohort_active` at declaration), `save_protocol_gap_found` (bool — true if R-49-C3 confirmed T0-Alpha miss), `save_protocol_gap_resolved` (bool — true if CSM manually ran R-49-C3 detection and triggered save protocol; required `true` when `save_protocol_gap_found = true` before P1 closure), `fix_deployed_at` (ISO 8601 datetime) |

**Zod v2 schemas (canonical source: `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-49` §R-49.9 — registered here for `emit-audit-event` Worker validation):**

```typescript
export const PilotActivationMonitorStaleDeclaredSchema = z.object({
  incident_id:          z.string().uuid(),
  stale_hours:          z.number().positive(),
  day14_cohort_active:  z.boolean(),
  save_protocol_gap:    z.boolean(),
});
export const PilotActivationMonitorRestoredSchema = z.object({
  incident_id:                z.string().uuid(),
  root_cause:                 z.enum(['H1_deleted','H1_disabled','H2_permissions_revoked','H3_pg_net_degraded','H4_supabase_outage']),
  stale_hours:                z.number().positive(),
  day14_cohort_affected:      z.boolean(),
  save_protocol_gap_found:    z.boolean(),
  save_protocol_gap_resolved: z.boolean(),
  fix_deployed_at:            z.string().datetime(),
});
```

**PILOT-ACT-STALE-CHAIN-01 — Ordering invariant:** `system.pilot_activation_monitor_restored` is blocked (HTTP 422 `PILOT_ACT_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION`) if no prior `system.pilot_activation_monitor_stale_declared` exists in the HMAC chain for the same `incident_id`; additionally, `restored` is blocked after it has already been emitted for the same `incident_id` (terminal enforcement — incident is closed). Violation → R-05 co-activated. Structural peers: AMEND-STALE-CHAIN-01 (R-48 / v2.54); SCIM-PROV-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 (R-47 / v2.52); ETF-CRON-CHAIN-01 (R-29 / v2.55).

**Emitters:** Both events emitted by IC (devops-lead, PAM-elevated) via `POST /internal/audit/emit` (DEC-030 HMAC-chained). `stale_declared` at R-49 Step 1 after PagerDuty `pilot-act-check-stale` P1 fires; `restored` at R-49 Step 6 after job 49 confirmed healthy and first post-fix `system.pilot_activation_check_passed` LOW emitted. No automated emission path — both events require IC PAM elevation.

**PagerDuty alert integration:** `system.pilot_activation_monitor_stale_declared` resolves PagerDuty `pilot-act-check-stale` P1 (`form-devops` service; dedup key `pilot-act-check-stale`) — the parent `system.cron_job_stale` event fires the P1; `stale_declared` is the IC acknowledgement. `system.pilot_activation_monitor_restored` resolves the PagerDuty incident. No additional alert fires on `stale_declared` emission (parent already firing). If R-49-C2 confirms `day14_cohort_active = true`, customer-success is paged separately per R-49 Step 2.

**CC7.2 auditor narrative:** `system.pilot_activation_monitor_stale_declared` (HIGH, 7yr) is an immutable DEC-030 record that FORM detected and declared a monitoring control degradation — job 49 staleness creates a T0-Alpha detection blind spot for active Day-14 pilot cohorts. The `stale_hours` field provides auditor-verifiable detection timing; `day14_cohort_active` distinguishes normal from escalated P1 severity; `save_protocol_gap` is the IC's auditor-inspectable Boolean attestation of save-protocol impact during the stale window. CC7.2 requires FORM to demonstrate that it evaluates and responds to identified anomalies; the PILOT-ACT-STALE-CHAIN-01 declared → restored terminal sequence satisfies this requirement. Retention 7yr for `stale_declared` (primary IC monitoring-control attestation spanning multi-cycle SOC 2 observation); 3yr for `restored` (operational closure record — primary attestations already captured in 7yr `stale_declared`).

**A1.1 auditor narrative:** The `stale_hours` field in both events quantifies the availability gap for the T0-Alpha monitoring service. For the SOC 2 A1.1 criterion (FORM's monitoring services meet stated availability commitments), the `stale_declared` event documents the gap scope and the `restored` event confirms closure. `save_protocol_gap_found = false` in `restored` is the auditor's affirmative attestation that no save-protocol trigger was missed during the stale window; `save_protocol_gap_found = true` + `save_protocol_gap_resolved = true` is the compensating-control record for any gap — satisfying A1.1 evidence that FORM responded to the availability failure.

**CC3.2 auditor narrative:** `save_protocol_gap` in `stale_declared` and `save_protocol_gap_found` / `save_protocol_gap_resolved` in `restored` together form the IC attestation chain for CC3.2 — FORM's risk response to a monitoring deficiency that could have delayed T0-Alpha detection and the Pilot CSM Save Protocol. `save_protocol_gap_resolved = true` is the direct evidence that FORM corrected the risk response gap (CSM manually ran the R-49-C3 detection query and triggered save protocol where required). PILOT-ACT-STALE-E-001 per-incident IC narrative artefact (registered in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §126.6`) provides the durable SOC 2 evidence record.

---

### Enterprise Pilot Graduation events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · COST_MODEL §47.6 · CC3.2/CC7.2/CC9.2/A1.1)

> Defined in `docs/COST_MODEL.md §47.6` (v2.21.0, 2026-06-29). Two DEC-030 HMAC-chained events covering the pilot graduation lifecycle: standard-path graduation decision record and contract activation trigger. **GRAD-CHAIN-01 chain invariant:** `enterprise.pilot_graduated` is blocked (HTTP 422 `GRAD_CHAIN_01_NO_PILOT_STARTED`) if no prior `enterprise.pilot_started` exists in the HMAC chain for the same `pilot_id`. **GRAD-CHAIN-02 chain invariant:** `enterprise.contract_activated` with non-null `pilot_id` is blocked (HTTP 422 `GRAD_CHAIN_02_NO_GRADUATION_EVENT`) if no prior `enterprise.pilot_graduated` or `enterprise.pilot_saved` for the same `pilot_id` exists within 30 days in the HMAC chain. **Privacy floor (both events):** `pilot_id` and `tenant_id` are FORM-internal UUIDs — no employee `user_id`, name, email, health metric, coaching content, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category data in any payload; `activation_rate_at_graduation` and all financial fields are aggregate contract-level values only. `form_api` REVOKED on `pilot_programs`, `enterprise_contracts`, and `audit_log_events`. **SOC 2 evidence source:** GRAD-E-001 annual chain export (CC3.2/CC7.2/A1.1) — registered in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §129`. **Registered v2.59 (2026-06-29) — closes `docs/COST_MODEL.md §47.8` item 1 (P0/M5).**

| Event type | Severity | Retention | Trigger | Key payload fields |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `enterprise.pilot_graduated` | HIGH | 7 yr | CSM (bucket A — self-authorize), CS Lead (bucket B — countersign), or Founder (bucket C — sign-off) emits on formal graduation decision; GRAD-CHAIN-01 anchor — HTTP 422 `GRAD_CHAIN_01_NO_PILOT_STARTED` if no prior `enterprise.pilot_started` for same `pilot_id`; not emitted for save-protocol graduations (those use `enterprise.pilot_saved` per §46.7) | `pilot_id` (UUID), `tenant_id` (UUID), `graduation_date` (TIMESTAMPTZ — ISO 8601 graduation decision timestamp), `activation_rate_at_graduation` (NUMERIC(5,4) — e.g. 0.7 = 70%), `seat_count_graduated` (INT — contracted seats entering paid term), `acv_at_graduation_usd` (NUMERIC(12,2)), `graduation_tier` (enum: `'standard'\|'conditional'` — conditional requires Founder sign-off), `activation_bucket` (enum: `'A'\|'B'\|'C'` — A ≥ 70%, B 50–70%, C 30–50%), `approved_by_role` (enum: `'csm'\|'cs_lead'\|'founder'` — per §47.2.2 authority table) |
| `enterprise.contract_activated` | STANDARD | 3 yr | compliance-officer (PAM-elevated) emits on first billing cycle activation for pilot-graduated, save-protocol-graduated, and direct-to-enterprise deals; GRAD-CHAIN-02 prerequisite when `pilot_id` non-null — HTTP 422 `GRAD_CHAIN_02_NO_GRADUATION_EVENT` if no prior `enterprise.pilot_graduated` or `enterprise.pilot_saved` for same `pilot_id` within 30 days; `pilot_id = null` bypasses chain check (direct deals) | `tenant_id` (UUID), `contract_id` (UUID — FK to `enterprise_contracts.id`), `pilot_id` (UUID\|null — null for direct-to-enterprise deals with no prior pilot), `activation_date` (TIMESTAMPTZ — matches `enterprise_contracts.contract_start_date`), `billing_cycle` (enum: `'monthly'\|'annual'\|'multi_year'`), `first_invoice_date` (TIMESTAMPTZ), `total_acv_usd` (NUMERIC(12,2)), `graduation_path` (enum: `'standard'\|'post_save_protocol'\|'direct'` — `'direct'` = no prior pilot) |

**Zod v2 schemas (canonical source: `docs/COST_MODEL.md §47.6` — registered here for `emit-audit-event` Worker validation):**

```typescript
export const PilotGraduatedSchema = z.object({
  pilot_id:                      z.string().uuid(),
  tenant_id:                     z.string().uuid(),
  graduation_date:               z.string().datetime(),
  activation_rate_at_graduation: z.number().min(0).max(1),
  seat_count_graduated:          z.number().int().positive(),
  acv_at_graduation_usd:         z.number().positive(),
  graduation_tier:               z.enum(['standard', 'conditional']),
  activation_bucket:             z.enum(['A', 'B', 'C']),
  approved_by_role:              z.enum(['csm', 'cs_lead', 'founder']),
});
export const ContractActivatedSchema = z.object({
  tenant_id:          z.string().uuid(),
  contract_id:        z.string().uuid(),
  pilot_id:           z.string().uuid().nullable(),
  activation_date:    z.string().datetime(),
  billing_cycle:      z.enum(['monthly', 'annual', 'multi_year']),
  first_invoice_date: z.string().datetime(),
  total_acv_usd:      z.number().positive(),
  graduation_path:    z.enum(['standard', 'post_save_protocol', 'direct']),
});
```

**GRAD-CHAIN-01 — Ordering invariant:** `enterprise.pilot_graduated` is blocked (HTTP 422 `GRAD_CHAIN_01_NO_PILOT_STARTED`) if no `enterprise.pilot_started` exists in the HMAC chain for the same `pilot_id`. Pilots below 30% activation at Day 90 without completing the §46 CSM Save Protocol cannot emit `enterprise.pilot_graduated` — save protocol must produce `enterprise.pilot_saved` first (PILOT-SAVE-CHAIN-01 successor). Structural peers: PILOT-SAVE-CHAIN-01 (§46 / v2.56); EXP-CHAIN-01 (§41 / v2.22).

**GRAD-CHAIN-02 — Ordering invariant:** `enterprise.contract_activated` with non-null `pilot_id` is blocked (HTTP 422 `GRAD_CHAIN_02_NO_GRADUATION_EVENT`) if no `enterprise.pilot_graduated` or `enterprise.pilot_saved` for the same `pilot_id` exists in the HMAC chain within 30 days. For direct-to-enterprise deals (`pilot_id = null`), no chain check is applied. Structural peers: PILOT-SAVE-CHAIN-01 (§46 / v2.56); EXP-CHAIN-02 (§41 / v2.22).

**Emitters:** `enterprise.pilot_graduated` — CSM (bucket A, self-authorize), CS Lead (bucket B countersign), or Founder (bucket C sign-off), all via IC PAM-elevated `POST /internal/audit/emit`; `enterprise.contract_activated` — compliance-officer via IC PAM-elevated call on first billing cycle confirmation.

**CC3.2 auditor narrative:** The `enterprise.pilot_graduated` event is an immutable, HMAC-chained record of FORM's formal graduation decision, including the approval authority tier (`approved_by_role`) and the activation threshold met (`activation_bucket`). CC3.2 requires FORM to implement procedures to identify and respond to identified risks during service delivery; the GRAD-CHAIN-01 → GRAD-CHAIN-02 sequence proves that no contract was activated without a traceable pilot graduation decision. GRAD-E-001 annual chain export (CC3.2/CC7.2/A1.1, registered in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §129`) enables auditors to verify that every `enterprise.contract_activated` with a `pilot_id` links to a prior `enterprise.pilot_graduated` or `enterprise.pilot_saved` — closed-loop evidence of the pilot-to-paid lifecycle.

**CC7.2 auditor narrative:** `enterprise.pilot_graduated` carries `activation_rate_at_graduation` (aggregate NUMERIC, 0–1) and `activation_bucket` (A/B/C) — auditor-inspectable evidence that FORM evaluated pilot adoption health before committing to a paid contract. The `approved_by_role` field proves the correct approval authority tier was engaged for the activation level achieved (CSM / CS Lead / Founder per §47.2.2). GRAD-CHAIN-01 guarantees the graduation event is preceded by a `pilot_started` event, providing a complete lifecycle timeline for CC7.2 monitoring-to-decision evidence.

**CC9.2 auditor narrative:** `enterprise.contract_activated` is the immutable record that a paid service commitment was made following a traceable governance process (GRAD-CHAIN-02). For save-protocol paths, `graduation_path = 'post_save_protocol'` links to the PILOT-SAVE-CHAIN-01 sequence (§46), satisfying CC9.2 contract-level decision accountability for both the standard graduation and the distressed-pilot recovery paths.

**A1.1 auditor narrative:** `enterprise.contract_activated` provides a timestamped record of when paid service availability begins for each tenant (`activation_date` matches `enterprise_contracts.contract_start_date`). For A1.1 (FORM's monitoring services meet stated availability commitments), the event establishes the audit baseline from which SLA monitoring obligations begin — linking to OBSERVABILITY §23 (enterprise SLA events) and the `enterprise_sla_events` table in DATA_MODEL §22.

---

### Enterprise Seat Expansion & Tier Upgrade events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · COST_MODEL §41.7 · CC5.2/CC1.4/A1.1)

> Defined in `docs/COST_MODEL.md §41.7` (v2.7, 2026-06-20 — §41 Enterprise Seat Expansion Economics: Mid-Contract Upsell, Tier Upgrade Pricing & Expansion Governance). Four DEC-030 HMAC-chained events covering the seat expansion lifecycle: formal approval anchor, billing record (with DATA_MODEL §24 baseline fields plus §41.7.2 financial extension), tier-upgrade companion, and automatic price-floor enforcement record. **First AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA registration of `billing.seats_expanded`** — previously defined in `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §24.5.1` (baseline fields only); this registration adds the §41.7.2 financial extension fields as a compatible superset schema. **EXP-CHAIN-01 chain invariant:** For any expansion event cluster sharing an `amendment_id`, `billing.seats_expanded` must follow `enterprise.expansion_initiated` in chain order — the `emit-audit-event` Worker validates predecessor presence before appending; HTTP 422 if absent. **EXP-CHAIN-02 chain invariant:** If `enterprise.expansion_initiated` carries `tier_crossing: true`, then `enterprise.tier_upgraded` must follow for the same `amendment_id` — the Worker validates this at the tier-upgrade emission point; HTTP 422 if the initiating event is absent or `tier_crossing: false`. **FLOOR-CHAIN-01 ordering convention:** If `enterprise.expansion_floor_enforced` is emitted for an amendment, it must precede `enterprise.expansion_initiated` in the chain (floor is auto-enforced at the Admin Console pricing preview step, before the amendment is counter-signed). **`floor_respected` chain invariant (all applicable events):** `enterprise.expansion_initiated` and `billing.seats_expanded` both carry `floor_respected: z.literal(true)` — the Worker returns HTTP 422 if the field is absent or `false`; a below-floor expansion may not proceed without this invariant. **Privacy invariant (all four events):** no `user_id`, no employee name, no email address, no health metric value, no coaching transcript content, no biometric identifier. All seat counts and financial figures are aggregate integers or floats at the contract level; no per-employee data in any payload. `enterprise_contracts` financial rate columns: `form_api` REVOKED (per DATA_MODEL §24.5.1); `compliance_reviewer` SELECT-only; `form_system` ALL. Cross-ref: `docs/COST_MODEL.md §41` (full expansion economics — trigger framework §41.2, billing model §41.3, tier upgrade pricing §41.4, discount authority matrix §41.5, NRR decomposition §41.8, SOC 2 evidence artefacts §41.9); `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §24.5.1` (`enterprise_seat_allocations` DDL — seat expansion via `effective_from`/`effective_to` history pattern; `billing.seats_expanded` baseline schema); `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §24` (migration 0083 — `initial_seats`, `current_seats`, `expansion_count`, `last_expansion_date` on `enterprise_contracts`); `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §35` (BDG `contracted_seats` source — `billing.seats_expanded` post-event hook triggers Cloudflare KV DELETE for `bDG:${tenant_id}:guardConfig`); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §31.5` (price floor rates: Starter $12, Growth $9, Enterprise $6 1yr; $4.50/$4.50/$4.00 3yr); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §41.9` (EXP-E-001/EXP-E-002 SOC 2 evidence artefacts — registration in SOC2_READINESS.md §80.3/§79.4 pending P1/M11 COST_MODEL §41.10 item 7); `docs/ENTERPRISE.md` (no-go customers; privacy floor). **Closes `docs/COST_MODEL.md §41.10` checklist item 1 (P0/M10 — register all four §41.7 DEC-030 events in AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §Enterprise before first expansion event is processed — 🟢 2026-06-20).**

| Event type | Severity | Retention | Trigger | Key payload fields |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `enterprise.expansion_initiated` | STANDARD | 7 yr | CSM or `tenant_admin` counter-signs seat expansion amendment via Admin Console "Expansion Amendment" modal on DocuSign countersignature callback; anchor event for the expansion sub-chain; `amendment_id` uniquely identifies the expansion cluster across all four event types | `tenant_id` (UUID), `contract_id` (UUID), `amendment_id` (UUID — DocuSign/amendment ref; correlates all expansion events for the same amendment), `trigger_type` (enum: `csm_initiated`\|`tenant_initiated`\|`milestone_driven`), `seats_before` (int), `seats_added` (int ≥ 1; ≥ 25 per §41.2 minimum unless founder-approved sub-minimum waiver), `seats_after` (int), `tier_before` (enum: `starter`\|`growth`\|`enterprise`), `tier_after` (enum), `tier_crossing` (bool — `true` when `tier_before ≠ tier_after`; activates EXP-CHAIN-02), `rate_per_seat_usd` (float — effective rate post-expansion), `expansion_invoice_usd` (float ≥ 0 — pro-rata invoice), `tier_crossing_credit_usd` (float ≥ 0 — credit for existing seats crossing to lower rate), `net_invoice_usd` (float ≥ 0), `billing_variant` (enum: `immediate`\|`anniversary_rollup`), `expansion_effective_date` (ISO 8601 date), `contract_anniversary_date` (ISO 8601 date), `months_remaining` (int ≥ 1), `discount_pct` (float 0–100), `floor_respected` (`true` literal — **HTTP 422 if absent**; EXP-CHAIN invariant), `approved_by` (enum: `csm`\|`cs_lead`\|`founder`\|`founder_board`), `decision_log_ref` (string or null — **required** when `discount_pct > 15` or below-floor waiver) |
| `billing.seats_expanded` | STANDARD | 7 yr | Emitted by billing Worker after `enterprise_seat_allocations` history rows are written (new row: updated `total_seats`, new `effective_from`); EXP-CHAIN-01: must immediately follow `enterprise.expansion_initiated` for the same `amendment_id` (HTTP 422 on predecessor absence); post-event hook triggers BDG KV cache invalidation per SSO_SCIM §35.7 | **DATA_MODEL §24 baseline fields:** `tenant_id` (UUID), `previous_seats` (int ≥ 0), `new_seats` (int > 0), `price_per_seat_usd_cents` (int — per-seat rate in minor currency units); **COST_MODEL §41.7.2 financial extension fields:** `expansion_arr_usd` (float ≥ 0 — annualised value of new seats at effective rate: `seats_added × rate_per_seat_usd × 12`), `net_invoice_usd` (float ≥ 0 — pro-rata invoice for remainder of term), `billing_variant` (enum: `immediate`\|`anniversary_rollup`), `amendment_id` (UUID — EXP-CHAIN-01 correlation field linking to `enterprise.expansion_initiated`), `floor_respected` (`true` literal — **HTTP 422 if absent**) |
| `enterprise.tier_upgraded` | STANDARD | 7 yr | Emitted only when `enterprise.expansion_initiated.tier_crossing = true` (EXP-CHAIN-02); correlated via `amendment_id`; emitted after `billing.seats_expanded` in the same amendment transaction | `tenant_id` (UUID), `contract_id` (UUID), `amendment_id` (UUID — EXP-CHAIN-02 correlation field), `tier_before` (enum), `tier_after` (enum), `seats_at_upgrade` (int — `seats_after` from `expansion_initiated`), `old_rate_per_seat_usd` (float), `new_rate_per_seat_usd` (float — lower rate from tier upgrade), `tier_crossing_credit_usd` (float ≥ 0 — credit applied to net invoice for rate differential on existing seats), `upgrade_effective_date` (ISO 8601 date), `multi_year_commitment` (bool — `true` if upgrade conditioned on term extension per §41.4), `decision_log_ref` (string or null), `floor_respected` (`true` literal) |
| `enterprise.expansion_floor_enforced` | HIGH | 7 yr | System auto-cap: Admin Console pricing calculator detects `proposed_rate < floor` for the target tier (§31.5); emitted synchronously at pricing preview step, before the amendment is presented for countersignature; FLOOR-CHAIN-01: must precede `enterprise.expansion_initiated` for the same `amendment_id`; zero-fire expected for on-floor or above-floor proposals — any emission signals a below-floor attempt | `tenant_id` (UUID), `contract_id` (UUID), `amendment_id` (UUID — FLOOR-CHAIN-01 correlation field), `tier_after` (enum — the target tier after expansion), `requested_rate_usd` (float — original proposed per-seat rate), `floor_rate_usd` (float — §31.5 minimum for this tier and contract term), `applied_rate_usd` (float — equals `floor_rate_usd`; system-applied cap), `floor_source` (literal `'COST_MODEL_§31.5'`), `csm_notified` (bool), `founder_approval_required` (bool — `true` when requested rate is below the deepest 3yr floor, requiring founder sign-off per §41.5) |

**Zod v2 schemas (canonical source: `docs/COST_MODEL.md §41.7` — registered here for `emit-audit-event` Worker validation):**

```typescript
import { z } from 'zod/v4';

// enterprise.expansion_initiated — COST_MODEL §41.7.1
// Severity: STANDARD · Retention: 7 years · EXP-CHAIN-01/02 anchor
export const ExpansionInitiatedPayload = z.object({
  tenant_id:                   z.string().uuid(),
  contract_id:                 z.string().uuid(),
  amendment_id:                z.string().uuid(),
  trigger_type:                z.enum(['csm_initiated', 'tenant_initiated', 'milestone_driven']),
  seats_before:                z.number().int().positive(),
  seats_added:                 z.number().int().min(1),
  seats_after:                 z.number().int().positive(),
  tier_before:                 z.enum(['starter', 'growth', 'enterprise']),
  tier_after:                  z.enum(['starter', 'growth', 'enterprise']),
  tier_crossing:               z.boolean(),
  rate_per_seat_usd:           z.number().positive(),
  expansion_invoice_usd:       z.number().nonnegative(),
  tier_crossing_credit_usd:    z.number().nonnegative().default(0),
  net_invoice_usd:             z.number().nonnegative(),
  billing_variant:             z.enum(['immediate', 'anniversary_rollup']),
  expansion_effective_date:    z.string().date(),
  contract_anniversary_date:   z.string().date(),
  months_remaining:            z.number().int().min(1),
  discount_pct:                z.number().min(0).max(100),
  floor_respected:             z.literal(true),   // HTTP 422 if absent — EXP-CHAIN invariant
  approved_by:                 z.enum(['csm', 'cs_lead', 'founder', 'founder_board']),
  decision_log_ref:            z.string().nullable(),
  // No user_id, no employee name, no health data, no Art. 9 special category.
});

// billing.seats_expanded — DATA_MODEL §24 baseline + COST_MODEL §41.7.2 financial extension
// Severity: STANDARD · Retention: 7 years · First full AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA registration
export const SeatsExpandedPayload = z.object({
  // DATA_MODEL §24.5.1 baseline fields
  tenant_id:                z.string().uuid(),
  previous_seats:           z.number().int().nonnegative(),
  new_seats:                z.number().int().positive(),
  price_per_seat_usd_cents: z.number().int().positive(),
  // COST_MODEL §41.7.2 financial extension fields
  expansion_arr_usd:        z.number().nonnegative(),
  net_invoice_usd:          z.number().nonnegative(),
  billing_variant:          z.enum(['immediate', 'anniversary_rollup']),
  amendment_id:             z.string().uuid(),     // EXP-CHAIN-01 correlation with expansion_initiated
  floor_respected:          z.literal(true),       // HTTP 422 if absent
  // No user_id, no individual-level data.
});

// enterprise.tier_upgraded — COST_MODEL §41.7.3
// Severity: STANDARD · Retention: 7 years · Emitted only when tier_crossing = true (EXP-CHAIN-02)
export const TierUpgradedPayload = z.object({
  tenant_id:                 z.string().uuid(),
  contract_id:               z.string().uuid(),
  amendment_id:              z.string().uuid(),
  tier_before:               z.enum(['starter', 'growth', 'enterprise']),
  tier_after:                z.enum(['starter', 'growth', 'enterprise']),
  seats_at_upgrade:          z.number().int().positive(),
  old_rate_per_seat_usd:     z.number().positive(),
  new_rate_per_seat_usd:     z.number().positive(),
  tier_crossing_credit_usd:  z.number().nonnegative(),
  upgrade_effective_date:    z.string().date(),
  multi_year_commitment:     z.boolean(),
  decision_log_ref:          z.string().nullable(),
  floor_respected:           z.literal(true),
  // No user_id, no individual-level data.
});

// enterprise.expansion_floor_enforced — COST_MODEL §41.7.4
// Severity: HIGH · Retention: 7 years · FLOOR-CHAIN-01 predecessor to expansion_initiated
export const ExpansionFloorEnforcedPayload = z.object({
  tenant_id:                  z.string().uuid(),
  contract_id:                z.string().uuid(),
  amendment_id:               z.string().uuid(),
  tier_after:                 z.enum(['starter', 'growth', 'enterprise']),
  requested_rate_usd:         z.number().positive(),
  floor_rate_usd:             z.number().positive(),
  applied_rate_usd:           z.number().positive(),   // always equals floor_rate_usd
  floor_source:               z.literal('COST_MODEL_§31.5'),
  csm_notified:               z.boolean(),
  founder_approval_required:  z.boolean(),
  // No user_id, no individual-level data.
});
```

**Chain ordering protocol:**

1. **(Optional) `enterprise.expansion_floor_enforced`** — emitted synchronously when the Admin Console pricing calculator detects `proposed_rate < floor`. Precedes step 2 for the same `amendment_id`. FLOOR-CHAIN-01 convention: presence of this event confirms the floor was applied *before* countersignature, not retroactively. HIGH severity: emitted even for below-floor requests that are subsequently redirected to founder approval.
2. **`enterprise.expansion_initiated`** — anchor event; emitted on DocuSign/amendment countersignature callback. Carries `floor_respected: z.literal(true)` — the Worker returns HTTP 422 if `floor_respected` is absent or `false`. `amendment_id` uniquely identifies the expansion cluster.
3. **`billing.seats_expanded`** — emitted by billing Worker after `enterprise_seat_allocations` INSERT. EXP-CHAIN-01: Worker validates `enterprise.expansion_initiated` predecessor for the same `amendment_id` before appending; HTTP 422 if absent. Carries `floor_respected: z.literal(true)`. Post-event hook triggers Cloudflare KV DELETE for `bDG:${tenant_id}:guardConfig` (BDG cache invalidation — SSO_SCIM §35.7).
4. **(Conditional) `enterprise.tier_upgraded`** — emitted only if `enterprise.expansion_initiated.tier_crossing = true`. EXP-CHAIN-02: Worker validates `enterprise.expansion_initiated` with `tier_crossing: true` for the same `amendment_id`; HTTP 422 if absent or `tier_crossing: false`. Emitted in the same Admin Console session as `enterprise.expansion_initiated`.

**Emitter assignments:**
- `enterprise.expansion_floor_enforced` — `admin-dashboard-api` (synchronous at pricing preview step; `form_system` role; emitted before amendment is presented for countersignature when `proposed_rate < floor`).
- `enterprise.expansion_initiated` — `admin-dashboard-api` (CSM or `tenant_admin` via Admin Console on DocuSign countersignature callback; `form_system` role).
- `billing.seats_expanded` — `billing-worker` (automated; `form_system` role; emitted after `enterprise_seat_allocations` INSERT within the same amendment transaction; post-event hook triggers BDG KV cache invalidation per SSO_SCIM §35.7).
- `enterprise.tier_upgraded` — `admin-dashboard-api` (emitted automatically when `tier_crossing: true`; same Admin Console session as `enterprise.expansion_initiated`).

**SOC 2 evidence artefacts (canonical source: `docs/COST_MODEL.md §41.9`; registration in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4` and `§80.3` pending P1/M11 — COST_MODEL §41.10 item 7):**

| Artefact | SOC 2 criteria | Description | Cadence | Retention | Path |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **EXP-E-001** | CC5.2, CC1.4 | Annual export of all `billing.seats_expanded` events from the DEC-030 HMAC chain; `floor_respected: true` on every row verified at export; `expansion_arr_usd`, `net_invoice_usd`, `discount_pct` ≤ authority matrix threshold for `approved_by` level; attestation: "No expansion event in the audit period had `floor_respected: false` or a discount exceeding the authority matrix in §41.5" | Annual (post-contract-anniversary sweep) | 7 years | `compliance/evidence/expansion/EXP-E-001_<YYYY>.csv` |
| **EXP-E-002** | CC4.1 | Quarterly cross-reference: adoption health band distribution from `enterprise_adoption_snapshots` vs. expansion pipeline in `enterprise_contracts` (`expansion_count > 0` in the quarter); confirms CSM expansion outreach is correlated with Green-band accounts; no expansion initiated for a Red-band account without a documented recovery plan | Quarterly | 3 years | `compliance/evidence/expansion/EXP-E-002_<YYYY>-Q<N>.csv` |

**Auditor narrative for CC5.2:** FORM enforces price floors for seat expansions through two independent controls: (1) system-level auto-cap at the Admin Console pricing preview step (documented by `enterprise.expansion_floor_enforced` HIGH, 7yr), and (2) `floor_respected: z.literal(true)` chain invariants on both `enterprise.expansion_initiated` and `billing.seats_expanded` — the `emit-audit-event` Worker returns HTTP 422 if the field is absent or `false`. EXP-E-001 provides period-level attestation that `floor_respected: true` holds on every expansion event in the annual audit window. Together, these two layers demonstrate that pricing controls over expansion discounts are both technically enforced at the point of entry and periodically verified through evidence export — satisfying CC5.2's requirement to select and develop control activities that mitigate pricing-governance risks.

**Auditor narrative for CC1.4:** `enterprise.expansion_floor_enforced` (HIGH, 7yr) is an immutable record that a below-floor rate request was identified and rejected by the system — the integrity of the pricing commitment is enforced architecturally, not procedurally. `enterprise.expansion_initiated.approved_by` records the authority level that approved the expansion, and `decision_log_ref` links to the DECISION_LOG entry for discounts exceeding 15%. This creates a complete, tamper-evident chain from floor enforcement through approval to billing, satisfying CC1.4's requirement to demonstrate a commitment to integrity and ethical values in commercial operations.

**Auditor narrative for A1.1:** Seat expansion events (`enterprise.expansion_initiated`, `billing.seats_expanded`) record `seats_after`, `tier_after`, and `rate_per_seat_usd` — the post-expansion contracted capacity. These events are the primary operational evidence that FORM's contracted capacity matches the billing record for each enterprise tenant, satisfying A1.1's requirement to demonstrate that current processing capacity meets performance commitments.

---

### Enterprise Pure Tier Upgrade events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · COST_MODEL §45.5 · CC5.2/CC6.1)

> Defined in `docs/COST_MODEL.md §45.5` (v2.15, 2026-06-26 — §45 Pure Tier Upgrade: Amendment vs. New Contract Governance, OQ-EXP-03 Resolution, DEC-082). One DEC-030 HMAC-chained billing event covering the rate-change step of a pure tier upgrade (same seat count, lower per-seat rate).
