Distinct from the §41.7 seat expansion lifecycle: no `amendment_id` multi-event correlation, no `enterprise.expansion_initiated` prerequisite — `billing.rate_updated` is emitted as the second event in a two-event pair (`enterprise.contract_amended` → `billing.rate_updated`) by the `amend_contract_tier()` Supabase RPC in a single atomic transaction. **TU-CHAIN-01 chain invariant:** `emit-audit-event` Worker returns HTTP 422 `TU_CHAIN_01_FLOOR_VIOLATION` if `new_rate_per_seat_usd` < §31.5 price floor for the target tier (Starter $6.00 / Growth $4.50 / Enterprise $4.00 — 1yr floors; $4.50/$4.50/$4.00 — 3yr). **Privacy invariant:** no `user_id`, no employee name, no email address, no health metric, no coaching transcript, no biometric identifier. `tenant_id` and `contract_id` are FORM-internal UUIDs; `prior_rate_per_seat_usd` and `new_rate_per_seat_usd` are commercial-metadata-only aggregate values at the contract level. **`form_api` REVOKED** from `enterprise_contracts` amendment columns and from `audit_log_events` INSERT — `amend_contract_tier()` SECURITY DEFINER runs as `form_system`. Cross-ref: `docs/COST_MODEL.md §45` (governance — option analysis §45.2, ASC 606 mechanics §45.3, migration 0088 §45.4, DEC-030 events §45.5, Admin Console §45.6, SOC 2 evidence §45.7, checklist §45.8); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §31.5` (price floors — TU-CHAIN-01 source constants); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §35.6` (`enterprise.contract_amended` baseline event — §45.5.1 extends with `tier_upgrade` sub-object); `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §35` (BDG — `contracted_seats` unchanged; KV cache invalidated by RPC); DEC-082 (governance decision); DEC-030. **Closes `docs/COST_MODEL.md §45.8` item 5 (P0/M12 — register `billing.rate_updated` in `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md` — 🟢 2026-06-26).**

| Event type | Severity | Retention | Trigger | Key payload fields |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `billing.rate_updated` | HIGH | 7 yr | Emitted by `amend_contract_tier()` Supabase RPC (SECURITY DEFINER, `form_system` role) as the second event in the two-event pair (`enterprise.contract_amended` → `billing.rate_updated`) within a single atomic transaction; `enterprise.contract_amended` with `amendment_type = 'tier_upgrade'` must precede in the same RPC call; TU-CHAIN-01: Worker returns HTTP 422 `TU_CHAIN_01_FLOOR_VIOLATION` if `new_rate_per_seat_usd` < §31.5 floor for target tier; post-emit: RPC invalidates BDG KV cache key `scim:guard_cfg:{tenant_id}` within 60s (BDG seat count unchanged; tier field updated); ADM-E-002 annual cross-reference source | `tenant_id` (UUID — FORM-internal), `contract_id` (UUID — **same as pre-amendment**; Option A invariant per DEC-082; new contract would require new `tenant_id` per DEC-079 + DEL-E-001 deletion certificate), `prior_rate_per_seat_usd` (number, four decimal places — rate in force immediately before amendment), `new_rate_per_seat_usd` (number, four decimal places — rate after amendment; TU-CHAIN-01 enforces ≥ §31.5 floor), `effective_date` (ISO 8601 date-only — amendment effective date, same as `enterprise_contracts.amendment_date`), `amendment_event_id` (UUID — soft-ref to the `enterprise.contract_amended` event UUID that anchors this rate change; enables ADM-E-002 cross-reference without requiring strict HMAC predecessor enforcement), `floor_respected` (`true` literal — **TU-CHAIN-01: HTTP 422 `TU_CHAIN_01_FLOOR_VIOLATION` if absent or `false`**) |

**Zod v2 schema (canonical source: `docs/COST_MODEL.md §45.5.2` — registered here for `emit-audit-event` Worker validation):**

```typescript
import { z } from 'zod/v4';

// billing.rate_updated — COST_MODEL §45.5.2
// Severity: HIGH · Retention: 7 years
// TU-CHAIN-01 terminal event for pure tier upgrade (no seat count change)
// Privacy: tenant_id + contract_id are FORM-internal UUIDs; rate figures are
//          aggregate commercial metadata only — no employee user_id, name, email,
//          health value, coaching content, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category data.
export const RateUpdatedPayload = z.object({
  tenant_id:               z.string().uuid(),
  contract_id:             z.string().uuid(),
  prior_rate_per_seat_usd: z.number().positive(),   // four decimal places, e.g. 9.0000
  new_rate_per_seat_usd:   z.number().positive(),   // four decimal places; TU-CHAIN-01 floor enforced at Worker
  effective_date:          z.string().date(),        // ISO 8601 date-only, e.g. "2026-06-26"
  amendment_event_id:      z.string().uuid(),        // soft-ref to enterprise.contract_amended event UUID
  floor_respected:         z.literal(true),          // HTTP 422 TU_CHAIN_01_FLOOR_VIOLATION if absent or false
});
```

**TU-CHAIN-01 invariant:**

```
TU-CHAIN-01 (pure tier upgrade rate floor — emit-audit-event Worker)

CONDITION:   billing.rate_updated where new_rate_per_seat_usd < §31.5 floor for target tier
             (1yr: Starter $6.00 / Growth $4.50 / Enterprise $4.00;
              3yr: Starter $4.50 / Growth $4.50 / Enterprise $4.00)
REJECTION:   emit-audit-event Worker returns HTTP 422 TU_CHAIN_01_FLOOR_VIOLATION
PATTERN:     Structural peer to REENTRY-CHAIN-01 (COST_MODEL §44.5) and RENEW-CHAIN-01
             (COST_MODEL §42.7) — all three are write-path HTTP 422 floor enforcement
             gates in the emit-audit-event Worker
NOTE:        HTTP 422 is NOT emitted for the monitoring-layer (no monitoring sentinel
             for TU-CHAIN-01 needed — violation is physically blocked at write time)
```

**SOC 2 auditor narratives:**

**CC5.2 (commitments enforcement):** `billing.rate_updated` with `floor_respected: z.literal(true)` is a tamper-evident, HMAC-chained record that every per-seat rate reduction under a pure tier upgrade remained at or above the COGS-anchored §31.5 price floor. ADM-E-001 (annual export of `enterprise.contract_amended` events with `tier_upgrade` sub-object — `floor_respected: true`, `asc_606_treatment` documented) and ADM-E-002 (annual cross-reference confirming no `billing.rate_updated` without a prior `enterprise.contract_amended` for the same `tenant_id` within 24 hours) together provide two independent evidence paths for CC5.2 pricing commitment compliance — the amendment authorisation record and the billing application record.

**CC6.1 (logical access authorisation):** `billing.rate_updated` is exclusively emitted by the `amend_contract_tier()` SECURITY DEFINER RPC (`form_system` role); `form_api` has no INSERT access to `audit_log_events` or to `enterprise_contracts` amendment columns. The dual countersign requirement (CSM + compliance-officer for below-list-rate amendments), `floor_respected: z.literal(true)` chain invariant, and `amendment_event_id` soft-reference to the prior `enterprise.contract_amended` event create a three-anchor authorization chain: (1) written countersign on record, (2) floor check enforced at write time, (3) billing rate change recorded in the HMAC chain. This satisfies CC6.1's requirement that changes to billing terms are authorized, recorded, and auditor-inspectable.

---

### Enterprise Contract Renewal events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · COST_MODEL §42.7 · CC5.2/CC1.4/CC4.1/CC2.2/A1.1)

> Defined in `docs/COST_MODEL.md §42.7` (v2.8, 2026-06-20 — §42 Enterprise Contract Renewal Economics). Three DEC-030 HMAC-chained events covering the full annual renewal lifecycle: 90-day notice anchor (`enterprise.renewal_notice_sent`), CPI escalation calculation audit trail (`enterprise.renewal_escalation_calculated`), and contract confirmation terminal event (`enterprise.contract_renewed`). **RENEW-CHAIN-01 invariant:** `enterprise.contract_renewed` is blocked (HTTP 422 `RENEW_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION`) unless a prior `enterprise.renewal_notice_sent` for the same `tenant_id` exists in the HMAC chain within 120 days of the renewal date. **ESCALATION-CHAIN-01 invariant:** `enterprise.contract_renewed` with `rate_basis = 'escalation'` is blocked (HTTP 422 `ESCALATION_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION`) unless a prior `enterprise.renewal_escalation_calculated` for the same `tenant_id` exists within 150 days. **RENEW-NOTICE-01 monitoring invariant:** `evidence-collection-cron` fires P1 PagerDuty `form-enterprise` (compliance-officer, 5-day resolution SLA) if no `enterprise.renewal_notice_sent` is found for a tenant whose `enterprise_contracts.renewal_date` falls within 85–95 days. **`floor_respected` chain invariant:** `enterprise.renewal_escalation_calculated` and `enterprise.contract_renewed` both carry `floor_respected: z.literal(true)` — the `emit-audit-event` Worker returns HTTP 422 if the field is absent or `false`; a below-floor renewal cannot proceed without overriding both controls. **Privacy invariant (all three events):** no `user_id`, no employee name, no email address, no health metric, no coaching transcript, no biometric identifier in any payload. `sent_by` / `calculated_by` / `confirmed_by` are FORM-internal compliance-officer team UUIDs — never tenant employee identifiers. `bls_report_date` is a date-only string (ISO 8601) — never a raw BLS URL. Cross-ref: `docs/COST_MODEL.md §42` (renewal economics — notice protocol §42.3, multi-year commitment §42.4, price escalation §42.5, `enterprise_renewals` DDL §42.6, Zod schemas §42.7, NRR waterfall §42.8, SOC 2 evidence artefacts §42.9); `docs/ENTERPRISE.md` (tier pricing $6–12/seat, multi-year discounts −15%/−25%; no-go customers); `docs/MSA_TEMPLATE.md §6.1` (auto-renewal; 90-day notice obligation; §8.6 price escalation clause); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §31.5` (COGS-anchored price floor — applies at renewal); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §32` (Pricing Exception Approval — `pricing_exception_event_id` required for `retention_discount` path); `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §43` (`enterprise_renewals` DDL — migration 0084; DDL-layer `CONSTRAINT chk_floor_always_respected CHECK (floor_respected = true)` backstop); `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §100` (REN-E-001/002/003 cross-reference closure; DDL-layer invariant narratives). **Closes `docs/COST_MODEL.md §42.10` checklist item 1 (P0/M12 — register all three §42.7 DEC-030 events in `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §Enterprise` before first renewal event is processed — 🟢 2026-06-20).**

| Event type | Severity | Retention | Trigger | Key payload fields |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `enterprise.renewal_notice_sent` | STANDARD | 7 yr | Compliance-officer emits via Admin Console "Renewal Workflow" Step 1 "Send Notice"; must be emitted ≥ 90 days before `renewal_date`; `days_until_renewal` Zod range (int 85–100) enforces the 90±5 window; RENEW-NOTICE-01 monitoring invariant fires P1 PagerDuty `form-enterprise` when no notice found 85–95 days before `renewal_date`; RENEW-CHAIN-01 anchor — `enterprise.contract_renewed` requires this event within 120 days via `notice_event_id` FK | `tenant_id` (UUID), `renewal_date` (date-only string `/^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/`), `days_until_renewal` (int 85–100 — 90±5 window), `current_seats` (int positive), `current_acv_usd` (float positive), `notice_type` (enum: `standard`\|`price_escalation`\|`non_renewal`), `escalation_preview_pct` (float 0–0.05 optional — present only when `notice_type = 'price_escalation'`), `sent_by` (UUID — FORM compliance-officer identity; **never** a tenant employee UUID) |
| `enterprise.renewal_escalation_calculated` | HIGH | 7 yr | Compliance-officer emits via Admin Console "Renewal Workflow" Step 2 "Calculate Escalation" when price escalation applies (Year 2+ multi-year); emitter pre-populates `prior_rate_per_seat_usd` from `enterprise_contracts` and `cpi_rate_pct` from BLS CPI-U release; compliance-officer reviews, confirms, and triggers emission; ESCALATION-CHAIN-01 prerequisite — `enterprise.contract_renewed` with `rate_basis = 'escalation'` requires this event within 150 days → HTTP 422 `ESCALATION_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` if absent | `tenant_id` (UUID), `prior_rate_per_seat_usd` (float positive), `cpi_rate_pct` (float 0–0.15 — BLS CPI-U annual rate), `applied_escalation_pct` (float 0–0.05 — formula: `min(cpi_rate_pct + 0.01, 0.05)`; hard-capped at 5%), `cap_triggered` (bool — `true` when CPI+1% > 5%), `new_rate_per_seat_usd` (float positive — escalated rate; must satisfy §31.5 floor), `floor_respected` (`true` literal — **HTTP 422 if absent**), `bls_report_date` (date-only string — BLS CPI-U publication date; **never** a raw URL), `calculated_by` (UUID — FORM compliance-officer identity; **never** a tenant employee UUID) |
| `enterprise.contract_renewed` | STANDARD | 7 yr | Compliance-officer emits via Admin Console "Renewal Workflow" Step 3 "Confirm Renewal" after DocuSign Order Form counter-signature (or auto-renewal under MSA §6.1); triggers `enterprise_contracts` UPDATE + `enterprise_renewals` INSERT in a single `SERIALIZABLE` transaction (migration 0084); RENEW-CHAIN-01 enforced: HTTP 422 `RENEW_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` if no prior `enterprise.renewal_notice_sent` within 120 days; ESCALATION-CHAIN-01 enforced when `rate_basis = 'escalation'` | `tenant_id` (UUID), `renewal_date` (date-only string), `renewal_type` (enum: `standard_annual`\|`multi_year_2`\|`multi_year_3`\|`retention_discount`\|`non_renewal`), `prior_seats` (int positive), `renewed_seats` (int ≥ 0 — 0 only for `non_renewal`), `prior_rate_per_seat_usd` (float positive), `new_rate_per_seat_usd` (float ≥ 0 — 0 only for `non_renewal`), `new_acv_usd` (float ≥ 0), `new_contract_years` (1\|2\|3 union), `rate_basis` (enum: `escalation`\|`current_list`\|`retention_discount`\|`rate_lock_continuation`), `escalation_pct` (float 0–0.05 optional — present when `rate_basis = 'escalation'`), `multi_year_discount_pct` (float 0–0.25 optional — present when `renewal_type` multi-year), `floor_respected` (`true` literal — **HTTP 422 if absent**), `pricing_exception_event_id` (UUID optional — **required** when `rate_basis = 'retention_discount'`; FK to prior `enterprise.pricing_exception_approved` per §32), `notice_event_id` (UUID — RENEW-CHAIN-01 FK to prior `enterprise.renewal_notice_sent`), `confirmed_by` (UUID — FORM compliance-officer identity; **never** a tenant employee UUID) |

**Zod v2 schemas (canonical source: `docs/COST_MODEL.md §42.7` — registered here for `emit-audit-event` Worker validation):**

```typescript
import { z } from 'zod/v4';

// enterprise.renewal_notice_sent — COST_MODEL §42.7.1
// Severity: STANDARD · Retention: 7 years · RENEW-CHAIN-01 anchor
export const RenewalNoticeSentPayload = z.object({
  tenant_id:              z.string().uuid(),
  renewal_date:           z.string().regex(/^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/),  // date-only
  days_until_renewal:     z.number().int().min(85).max(100),          // 90±5 window
  current_seats:          z.number().int().positive(),
  current_acv_usd:        z.number().positive(),
  notice_type:            z.enum(['standard', 'price_escalation', 'non_renewal']),
  escalation_preview_pct: z.number().min(0).max(0.05).optional(),    // present if price_escalation
  sent_by:                z.string().uuid(),                          // compliance-officer UUID
  // No user_id, no employee name, no health data, no Art. 9 special category.
});

// enterprise.renewal_escalation_calculated — COST_MODEL §42.7.2
// Severity: HIGH · Retention: 7 years · ESCALATION-CHAIN-01 prerequisite
export const RenewalEscalationCalculatedPayload = z.object({
  tenant_id:               z.string().uuid(),
  prior_rate_per_seat_usd: z.number().positive(),
  cpi_rate_pct:            z.number().min(0).max(0.15),              // BLS CPI-U annual rate
  applied_escalation_pct:  z.number().min(0).max(0.05),              // min(cpi_rate_pct + 0.01, 0.05)
  cap_triggered:           z.boolean(),                               // true if CPI+1% > 5%
  new_rate_per_seat_usd:   z.number().positive(),
  floor_respected:         z.literal(true),                           // HTTP 422 if absent
  bls_report_date:         z.string().regex(/^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/), // date-only, never raw URL
  calculated_by:           z.string().uuid(),                         // compliance-officer UUID
  // No user_id, no employee name, no health data, no Art. 9 special category.
});

// enterprise.contract_renewed — COST_MODEL §42.7.3
// Severity: STANDARD · Retention: 7 years · RENEW-CHAIN-01 + ESCALATION-CHAIN-01 terminal event
export const ContractRenewedPayload = z.object({
  tenant_id:                  z.string().uuid(),
  renewal_date:               z.string().regex(/^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/),
  renewal_type:               z.enum(['standard_annual', 'multi_year_2', 'multi_year_3', 'retention_discount', 'non_renewal']),
  prior_seats:                z.number().int().positive(),
  renewed_seats:              z.number().int().min(0),                // 0 only for non_renewal
  prior_rate_per_seat_usd:    z.number().positive(),
  new_rate_per_seat_usd:      z.number().min(0),                      // 0 only for non_renewal
  new_acv_usd:                z.number().min(0),
  new_contract_years:         z.union([z.literal(1), z.literal(2), z.literal(3)]),
  rate_basis:                 z.enum(['escalation', 'current_list', 'retention_discount', 'rate_lock_continuation']),
  escalation_pct:             z.number().min(0).max(0.05).optional(), // present if rate_basis = 'escalation'
  multi_year_discount_pct:    z.number().min(0).max(0.25).optional(), // present if multi_year renewal_type
  floor_respected:            z.literal(true),                        // HTTP 422 if absent
  pricing_exception_event_id: z.string().uuid().optional(),           // required if retention_discount
  notice_event_id:            z.string().uuid(),                      // RENEW-CHAIN-01 FK anchor
  confirmed_by:               z.string().uuid(),                      // compliance-officer UUID
  // No user_id, no employee name, no health data, no Art. 9 special category.
});
```

**Chain ordering protocol:**

1. **`enterprise.renewal_notice_sent`** — anchor event; compliance-officer emits ≥ 90 days before `renewal_date`. `days_until_renewal` Zod range (85–100) enforces the contractual 90±5-day window. RENEW-NOTICE-01 pg_cron monitoring fires P1 PagerDuty `form-enterprise` if no notice exists 85–95 days before `renewal_date`; compliance-officer has a 5-day SLA to emit. `notice_event_id` in `enterprise.contract_renewed` creates the durable RENEW-CHAIN-01 FK — a missing notice makes the renewal a system-enforced HTTP 422, not a missed procedural control.
2. **(Conditional) `enterprise.renewal_escalation_calculated`** — emitted alongside Step 1 when price escalation applies (Year 2+ multi-year). Compliance-officer reviews the pre-populated CPI calculation before emitting. `floor_respected: z.literal(true)` validates that `new_rate_per_seat_usd ≥ §31.5 floor` at calculation time. ESCALATION-CHAIN-01: `enterprise.contract_renewed` with `rate_basis = 'escalation'` requires this event within 150 days → HTTP 422 `ESCALATION_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` if absent.
3. **`enterprise.contract_renewed`** — terminal event; emitted after DocuSign counter-signature. RENEW-CHAIN-01 enforced: HTTP 422 `RENEW_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` if no prior `enterprise.renewal_notice_sent` within 120 days. ESCALATION-CHAIN-01 enforced when `rate_basis = 'escalation'`. `floor_respected: z.literal(true)` invariant: HTTP 422 if absent (Zod Worker-layer) and `CONSTRAINT chk_floor_always_respected CHECK (floor_respected = true)` (DDL-layer backstop per DATA_MODEL §43). `pricing_exception_event_id` required when `rate_basis = 'retention_discount'`. Triggers `enterprise_contracts` UPDATE + `enterprise_renewals` INSERT in a single `SERIALIZABLE` transaction.

**Emitter assignments:**
- `enterprise.renewal_notice_sent` — `admin-dashboard-api` (compliance-officer via Admin Console "Renewal Workflow" Step 1 "Send Notice"; `form_system` role; manual — no automated emitter path).
- `enterprise.renewal_escalation_calculated` — `admin-dashboard-api` (compliance-officer via Admin Console Step 2 "Calculate Escalation" when applicable; rates auto-populated from contract + BLS CPI-U input; compliance-officer reviews and confirms before emission; `form_system` role).
- `enterprise.contract_renewed` — `admin-dashboard-api` (compliance-officer via Admin Console Step 3 "Confirm Renewal" on DocuSign countersignature callback; triggers `enterprise_contracts` UPDATE + `enterprise_renewals` INSERT in single `SERIALIZABLE` transaction; `form_api` REVOKED on `enterprise_renewals`; `form_system` role).

**SOC 2 evidence artefacts (canonical definitions: `docs/COST_MODEL.md §42.9`; DDL-layer supplements: `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §100`; registration in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4` pending P1/M13 — COST_MODEL §42.10 item 5):**

| Artefact | SOC 2 criteria | Description | Cadence | Retention | Path |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **REN-E-001** | CC5.2 / CC1.4 | Annual export of all `enterprise.contract_renewed` events from the DEC-030 HMAC chain; `floor_respected: true` verified on every row; `rate_basis` distribution and `notice_event_id` cross-reference confirming RENEW-CHAIN-01 compliance for every renewal in the observation year; attestation: "No `enterprise.contract_renewed` event had `floor_respected: false` or lacked a prior notice event within 120 days" | Annual | 7 years | `compliance/evidence/renewals/REN-E-001_<YYYY>.csv` |
| **REN-E-002** | CC4.1 / CC2.2 | Quarterly renewal notice compliance export: all `enterprise.renewal_notice_sent` events in the quarter with `days_until_renewal` distribution and `notice_type` breakdown; cross-check that every account with `renewal_date` in the following 90 days has a notice on file; zero-notice accounts flagged as exceptions requiring compliance-officer explanation | Quarterly | 3 years | `compliance/evidence/renewals/REN-E-002_<YYYY-QN>.csv` |
| **REN-E-003** | CC4.1 / A1.1 | Annual renewal conversion rate analysis: blended retention rate by WAU health band vs. §42.8.2 model; escalation uplift realised vs. modelled; multi-year commitment conversion rate; board-level ARR waterfall (Retained + Escalation Uplift − Contraction − Churn); fleet-level aggregate only — no individual tenant names or employee data | Annual | 3 years | `compliance/evidence/renewals/REN-E-003_<YYYY>.csv` |

**Auditor narrative for CC5.2:** FORM enforces price floors at renewal through two independent controls: (1) `floor_respected: z.literal(true)` on `enterprise.renewal_escalation_calculated` — the Worker rejects any emission where `new_rate_per_seat_usd` would fall below the §31.5 floor; (2) `floor_respected: z.literal(true)` on `enterprise.contract_renewed` — the Worker returns HTTP 422 if absent, making it structurally impossible to commit a below-floor renewal without overriding both controls. A third DDL-layer backstop (`CONSTRAINT chk_floor_always_respected CHECK (floor_respected = true)` on `enterprise_renewals` — DATA_MODEL §43) rejects any INSERT that bypasses the Worker. REN-E-001 provides period-level attestation that `floor_respected: true` holds on every `enterprise.contract_renewed` event in the annual observation window, satisfying CC5.2's requirement to select and develop control activities that mitigate pricing-governance risks.

**Auditor narrative for CC1.4:** `enterprise.contract_renewed.confirmed_by` records the compliance-officer UUID accountable for the renewal confirmation. `notice_event_id` creates a durable FK to the RENEW-CHAIN-01 notice event — any out-of-sequence renewal is a system-enforced HTTP 422, not a human-dependent control. `enterprise.renewal_escalation_calculated.calculated_by` + `bls_report_date` (date-only, never raw URL) provide a tamper-evident record of the CPI calculation methodology and source, satisfying CC1.4's requirement to demonstrate individual accountability in commercial operations.

**Auditor narrative for CC4.1 / CC2.2 (REN-E-002):** CC4.1 requires FORM to evaluate performance against established commitments. CC2.2 requires communication to external parties about processing commitments. The 90-day renewal notice is a contractual commitment per MSA §6.1. REN-E-002's quarterly `days_until_renewal` distribution proves FORM honored this commitment on a per-account basis — as a DEC-030 chain record, not a policy claim. RENEW-NOTICE-01 pg_cron monitoring closes the control loop by firing P1 PagerDuty 85–95 days before each `renewal_date`.

**Auditor narrative for A1.1 (REN-E-003):** `enterprise.contract_renewed.renewed_seats` records the post-renewal contracted seat count — the processing capacity FORM commits to serve in the next contract term. REN-E-003's annual conversion rate analysis provides evidence that FORM's renewal mechanics are operating as designed and that the NRR target of ≥ 120% (COST_MODEL §42.8.3) is being tracked against actuals, satisfying A1.1's capacity-monitoring requirement.

---

### Enterprise Contract Renewal Monitoring events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · OBSERVABILITY §51 · CC4.1/CC2.2/A1.1)

> Defined in `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §51` (v4.8.0, 2026-06-21). Two DEC-030 HMAC-chained events covering the RENEW-NOTICE-01 pg_cron job 39 monitoring control (`renewal_notice_check`, daily 09:00 UTC): a per-tenant alert event when a contract enters the 85–95-day notice-obligation window without a notice on file, and an all-clear event when the daily sweep completes with zero gaps detected. **Relationship to Enterprise Contract Renewal events (§42.7):** those three events (`enterprise.renewal_notice_sent`, `enterprise.renewal_escalation_calculated`, `enterprise.contract_renewed`) document the *commercial* renewal lifecycle; these two events document the *operational monitoring* of that lifecycle. Together they close the CC4.1 control loop: COST_MODEL §42.7 events are the evidence that the obligation was fulfilled; OBSERVABILITY §51 events are the evidence that the control was detecting any failure in real time. **RENEW-NOTICE-01 monitoring invariant:** `renewal_notice_check` job 39 fires `enterprise.renewal_notice_overdue` HIGH/7yr per flagged tenant when `enterprise_contracts.renewal_date BETWEEN CURRENT_DATE + 85 AND CURRENT_DATE + 95` AND no `enterprise.renewal_notice_sent` within 100 days; fires `system.renewal_notice_check_passed` LOW/1yr when zero gaps detected. **Privacy invariant (both events):** no `user_id`, no employee name, no email, no health value, no GDPR Art. 9 special category; `enterprise.renewal_notice_overdue` carries only `tenant_id` (FORM-internal UUID), `renewal_date` (DATE), `days_until_renewal` (int 85–95), `acv_usd` (aggregate contract value), `check_run_at` (UTC timestamp), `alert_dedup_key` (string); `system.renewal_notice_check_passed` carries only `contracts_checked` (int) and `check_run_at` (UTC timestamp) — no `tenant_id`. **Closes `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §51.7` item 1 (P0/M11 — register both events in `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md` — 🟢 2026-06-21).**

| Event type | Severity | Retention | Trigger | Key payload fields |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `enterprise.renewal_notice_overdue` | HIGH | 7 yr | `renewal_notice_check` pg_cron job 39 (daily 09:00 UTC) detects an active `enterprise_contracts` row with `renewal_date BETWEEN CURRENT_DATE + 85 AND CURRENT_DATE + 95` and no `enterprise.renewal_notice_sent` DEC-030 event for the same `tenant_id` within the prior 100 days; emitted once per flagged tenant per daily run (PagerDuty 72h dedup key `renew-notice-missing-{tenant_id}` suppresses alert storms but does NOT suppress the DEC-030 event — full audit trail preserved without PagerDuty storm); fires AL-RENEW-01 P1 PagerDuty `form-enterprise` → compliance-officer; 5-day SLA to emit notice; auto-resolves when `enterprise.renewal_notice_sent` on file at next 09:00 UTC run | `tenant_id` (UUID — FORM-internal), `renewal_date` (date-only `/^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/`), `days_until_renewal` (int 85–95), `acv_usd` (float positive — aggregate contract value; no per-user financial data), `check_run_at` (ISO 8601 UTC — job execution timestamp), `alert_dedup_key` (string — `renew-notice-missing-{tenant_id}`) |
| `system.renewal_notice_check_passed` | LOW | 1 yr | `renewal_notice_check` pg_cron job 39 (daily 09:00 UTC) completes RENEW-NOTICE-01 SQL with zero rows returned — no active contract in the 85–95-day window lacks a notice event; operational all-clear signal; emitted once per successful job run that finds zero gaps; absence of this event for > 26 h triggers `system.cron_job_stale` (HIGH, OBSERVABILITY §12.6) and routes to PagerDuty P1 `form-compliance` → INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-28 | `contracts_checked` (int ≥ 0 — count of active contracts scanned), `check_run_at` (ISO 8601 UTC) |

**Zod v2 schemas (canonical source: `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §51.4` — registered here for `emit-audit-event` Worker validation):**

```typescript
import { z } from 'zod/v4';

// enterprise.renewal_notice_overdue — OBSERVABILITY §51.4.1
// Severity: HIGH · Retention: 7 years · RENEW-NOTICE-01 per-tenant gap alert
export const RenewalNoticeOverduePayload = z.object({
  tenant_id:          z.string().uuid(),
  renewal_date:       z.string().regex(/^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/),   // ISO 8601 date-only
  days_until_renewal: z.number().int().min(85).max(95),
  acv_usd:           z.number().positive(),
  check_run_at:       z.string().datetime(),                       // ISO 8601 UTC
  alert_dedup_key:    z.string(),                                  // "renew-notice-missing-{tenant_id}"
  // No user_id, no employee name, no health data, no Art. 9 special category.
  // acv_usd is aggregate contract-level; no per-employee financial data.
});

// system.renewal_notice_check_passed — OBSERVABILITY §51.4.2
// Severity: LOW · Retention: 1 year · RENEW-NOTICE-01 all-clear health signal
export const RenewalNoticeCheckPassedPayload = z.object({
  contracts_checked: z.number().int().nonnegative(),
  check_run_at:      z.string().datetime(),
  // No tenant_id — privacy floor: safe to stream to SIEM without redaction.
});
```

**Emitter assignments:**
- `enterprise.renewal_notice_overdue` — `renewal_notice_check` pg_cron job 39 (`form_system`, `service_role`, automated daily — REVOKE on `enterprise_contracts` prevents `form_api` access; `service_role` elevation required for RENEW-NOTICE-01 SQL; emitted once per flagged tenant per run).
- `system.renewal_notice_check_passed` — `renewal_notice_check` pg_cron job 39 (`form_system`, `service_role`, automated daily — emitted once per run when zero gaps detected).

**Alert routing:**
- `enterprise.renewal_notice_overdue` → AL-RENEW-01 P1 PagerDuty `form-enterprise` → compliance-officer; dedup key `renew-notice-missing-{tenant_id}` 72h; 5-day resolution SLA; auto-resolves on next run once `enterprise.renewal_notice_sent` is on file. Runbook: `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §51.3.1`.
- `system.renewal_notice_check_passed` → no alert (all-clear signal). Absence triggers `system.cron_job_stale` HIGH (§12.6); INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-28 is the recovery runbook.

**SOC 2 evidence artefacts:**

| Artefact | SOC 2 criteria | Description | Cadence | Retention | Path |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **REN-E-002** | CC4.1 / CC2.2 | Quarterly `enterprise.renewal_notice_sent` compliance export — cross-check every active account; `enterprise.renewal_notice_overdue` events in the quarter confirm detection gaps were caught and paged; defined in `docs/COST_MODEL.md §42.9` and registered in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4` | Quarterly | 3 yr | `compliance/evidence/renewals/REN-E-002_<YYYY-QN>.csv` |
| **REN-OBS-E-001** | CC4.1 / A1.1 | Quarterly `renewal_notice_check` job 39 run history from `pg_cron.job_run_details`; confirms zero stale gaps > 26 h — proving RENEW-NOTICE-01 detection control was continuously operational; `system.renewal_notice_check_passed` all-clear events corroborate the pg_cron run history; defined in `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §51.5` and registered in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4` | Quarterly | 3 yr | `compliance/evidence/renewals/REN-OBS-E-001_<YYYY-QN>.md` |

**CC4.1 auditor narrative:** CC4.1 requires FORM to evaluate and communicate performance against defined thresholds. `enterprise.renewal_notice_overdue` HIGH/7yr proves that when the RENEW-NOTICE-01 threshold was missed, FORM's monitoring control detected it within one daily check cycle and notified the compliance-officer via AL-RENEW-01. `system.renewal_notice_check_passed` LOW/1yr proves the monitoring control was running on days where no gap was detected. Together with REN-OBS-E-001 (zero stale-job gaps in the quarter), they close the CC4.1 loop: the threshold is defined, monitored in automated daily sweeps, and any breach is detected and paged within 26 hours.

**CC2.2 auditor narrative:** CC2.2 requires communication to external parties about processing commitments. The 90-day renewal notice is a contractual commitment per MSA §6.1. `enterprise.renewal_notice_overdue` is the system's own evidence that it detected a potential failure to honor that commitment and alerted the compliance-officer within the 85–95-day detection window — before the MSA §6.1 breach could occur.

**A1.1 auditor narrative (REN-OBS-E-001):** A1.1 requires monitoring for threats to the availability of enterprise commitments. `renewal_notice_check` job 39 is the availability control for FORM's contractual renewal notice obligation. `system.renewal_notice_check_passed` and the absence of job 39 stale gaps in REN-OBS-E-001 together prove that the availability control was continuously operational throughout the SOC 2 observation quarter.

---

### Enterprise Post-Churn & Deletion SLA events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · COST_MODEL §43.7 + OBSERVABILITY §12.6 + §53 · C1.2/CC4.1/CC5.2/CC6.1/CC7.2)

> Defined in `docs/COST_MODEL.md §43.7` (v1.0, 2026-06-23 — §43 Enterprise Post-Churn Economics, Offboarding Cost Model & Logo Winback Analytics) and `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §12.6` (v1.4 patch, 2026-06-23 — pg_cron job 43 `deletion_sla_monitor`). Five DEC-030 HMAC-chained events covering the full enterprise post-churn lifecycle: CSM-initiated winback outreach anchor (`enterprise.winback_initiated`), winback conversion terminal event (`enterprise.winback_converted`), compliance-officer deletion certificate issuance (`enterprise.deletion_certificate_issued`), and pg_cron job 43 DELETION-SLA-01 monitoring early-warning and escalation events (`enterprise.deletion_sla_warning`, `enterprise.deletion_sla_critical`). **WINBACK-CHAIN-01 invariant (amended DEC-079):** `enterprise.winback_converted` is blocked (HTTP 422 `WINBACK_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION`) unless the event identified by `winback_initiated_event_id` exists in the HMAC chain, is an `enterprise.winback_initiated` event, was created within 365 days, and has `tenant_id` = `prior_tenant_id` of the converted event. Lookup is by direct event UUID rather than `tenant_id` because winback always provisions a new `tenant_id` UUID (DEC-079 Option B) — the prior tenant UUID is carried as `prior_tenant_id` in the payload for self-contained chain auditability. No retroactive winback recognition is architecturally possible. **DELETION-CHAIN-01 invariant:** `enterprise.deletion_certificate_issued` is blocked (HTTP 422 `DELETION_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION`) unless `days_since_trigger ≤ 35`; the `emit-audit-event` Worker escalates to P0 PagerDuty `form-enterprise` if `trigger_type = 'gdpr_art17_request'` and `days_since_trigger > 30` (GDPR Art. 17 30-day SLA breach risk). **DELETION-SLA-01 monitoring invariant (pg_cron job 43):** `deletion_sla_monitor` (`0 */6 * * *`, 7h freshness window) queries `enterprise_churn_events WHERE deletion_requested_at IS NOT NULL AND deletion_completed_at IS NULL`; emits `enterprise.deletion_sla_warning` HIGH/7yr at day 25 (5-day pre-breach warning → AL-DEL-01 P1 PagerDuty `form-enterprise`) and `enterprise.deletion_sla_critical` CRITICAL/7yr at day 29 (1-day pre-breach → P0 escalation → founder + compliance-officer); PagerDuty uses 6h dedup key `deletion-sla-warning-{tenant_id}` (alert storm suppressed; DEC-030 trail emitted on every 6h cycle — full audit chain preserved without PagerDuty storm). **Privacy floor (all five events):** No `user_id`, no employee name, no email address, no health value, no GDPR Art. 9 special-category data — `tenant_id` is a FORM-internal UUID only. `enterprise_churn_events` table: `form_api` REVOKED; `tenant_manager` role excluded (no RLS policy — access denied by default per DATA_MODEL §44). **Closes `docs/COST_MODEL.md §43.10` item 1 (P0/M10 — register `enterprise.winback_initiated`, `enterprise.winback_converted`, `enterprise.deletion_certificate_issued` in AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §Enterprise — 🟢 2026-06-23)** and the AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md obligation noted in §43.10 item 3 (`enterprise.deletion_sla_warning` / `enterprise.deletion_sla_critical` — 🟢 2026-06-23). Cross-ref: `docs/COST_MODEL.md §43.2` (post-churn state machine; OFFBOARD-CHAIN-01 / WINBACK-CHAIN-01 / DELETION-CHAIN-01 invariants); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §43.7.2` (Zod schemas — canonical source for winback and certificate events); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §43.7.3` (chain invariants summary table); `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §44` (canonical `enterprise_churn_events` DDL — migration 0085; `ece_deletion_sla_respected` CHECK DDL backstop at 35 days); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §12.6` (pg_cron job 43 `deletion_sla_monitor` — canonical registry; DELETION-SLA-01); `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §102` (DEL-E-001 / WBK-E-001 / CHN-E-001 registered in §79.4 master evidence table — v3.27.0, 2026-06-23); `docs/ENTERPRISE.md` (no-go criteria; privacy floor — no individual employee data); DEC-030.

| Event type | Severity | Retention | Trigger | Key payload fields |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `enterprise.winback_initiated` | HIGH | 7 yr | CSM (`form_admin` role) logs first authorised outreach attempt to a churned tenant in the Admin Console "Winback Pipeline" (§43.10 item 6, P1/M12 — pending build); emitter: CSM (manual, PAM-elevated); WINBACK-CHAIN-01 prerequisite — must precede `enterprise.winback_converted` within 365 days; emitted once per outreach sequence initiation (subsequent touches update `enterprise_churn_events.winback_status` only, no additional DEC-030 event) | `tenant_id` (UUID — FORM-internal), `prior_contract_id` (UUID — FK to churned `enterprise_contracts`), `churn_date` (date-only YYYY-MM-DD), `months_since_churn` (int 1–60), `churn_reason` (`ChurnReasonEnum` from `enterprise_churn_events.churn_reason` — no free text), `wau_band_at_churn` (enum: `green`\|`amber`\|`red`), `prior_acv_usd` (float positive — aggregate contract-level ACV; no per-employee financial data), `initiated_by` (UUID — CSM `user_id`, `form_admin` role; never a tenant employee), `outreach_sequence` (enum: `month_3`\|`month_6`\|`month_12`\|`month_18_plus`\|`ad_hoc`) |
| `enterprise.winback_converted` | CRITICAL | 7 yr | New enterprise contract counter-signed by churned tenant; emitter: compliance-officer (manual, PAM-elevated) via Admin Console "Winback Pipeline" counter-sign step (§43.10 item 6, P1/M12 — pending build); `emit-audit-event` Worker blocks HTTP 422 `WINBACK_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` if the event identified by `winback_initiated_event_id` does not exist in the HMAC chain, is not of type `enterprise.winback_initiated`, was created more than 365 days ago, or has `tenant_id` ≠ `prior_tenant_id` of this event — DEC-079: lookup is by event UUID, not `tenant_id`, because winback always provisions a new `tenant_id` | `tenant_id` (UUID — new provisioned UUID per DEC-079 Option B), `prior_tenant_id` (UUID — churned tenant UUID; enables HMAC chain self-audit without joining `enterprise_churn_events`), `winback_contract_id` (UUID — FK to new `enterprise_contracts` row; new `contract_id` required per §43.6.3 — prevents zero-denominator NRR inflation), `prior_contract_id` (UUID), `winback_initiated_event_id` (UUID — WINBACK-CHAIN-01 predecessor; direct event-UUID lookup per DEC-079), `months_since_churn` (int 1–60), `prior_acv_usd` (float positive), `new_acv_usd` (float positive), `acv_premium_pct` (float 0–200; +15–30% typical per §43.6.1 model), `new_seats` (int positive), `new_contract_years` (literal 1\|2\|3), `confirmed_by` (UUID — compliance-officer `user_id`) |
| `enterprise.deletion_certificate_issued` | CRITICAL | 7 yr | Compliance-officer countersigns and uploads deletion certificate PDF to R2 after verifying two-phase hard-delete completion in Admin Console "Offboarding Workflow" Step 3 (§43.10 item 4, P0/M11 — pending build); emitter: compliance-officer (manual, PAM-elevated); DELETION-CHAIN-01 terminal event; `emit-audit-event` Worker rejects HTTP 422 `DELETION_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` if `days_since_trigger > 35`; P0 PagerDuty `form-enterprise` escalation if `trigger_type = 'gdpr_art17_request'` and `days_since_trigger > 30` | `tenant_id` (UUID), `contract_id` (UUID), `deletion_request_event_id` (UUID optional — present if `trigger_type = 'gdpr_art17_request'`), `trigger_type` (enum: `gdpr_art17_request`\|`standard_90_day`\|`early_termination`), `deletion_date` (YYYY-MM-DD), `classes_deleted` (string[] — subset of: `user_pii`\|`health_data_art9`\|`nutrition_logs`\|`workout_sessions`\|`coaching_content`\|`sso_personal_attributes`), `classes_retained` (string[] — subset of: `audit_log_chain`\|`financial_records`\|`contract_records`\|`aggregate_metrics`; basis GDPR Art. 17(3)(e)), `retained_legal_basis` (literal `'gdpr_art17_3e_legal_obligation'`), `certificate_r2_path` (string regex `^compliance/evidence/deletions/.+\.pdf$`), `compliance_officer_id` (UUID), `days_since_trigger` (int 0–35) |
| `enterprise.deletion_sla_warning` | HIGH | 7 yr | pg_cron job 43 (`deletion_sla_monitor`, `0 */6 * * *`) detects `enterprise_churn_events` row where `now() - deletion_requested_at > INTERVAL '25 days'` AND `deletion_completed_at IS NULL` — 5-day pre-breach warning before GDPR Art. 17 30-day SLA; emitter: `form_system` (`service_role` — `form_api` REVOKED from `enterprise_churn_events`); DEC-030 event emitted on every 6h cycle until `deletion_completed_at` is populated (full audit trail preserved); PagerDuty uses 6h dedup key (alert storm suppressed); fires AL-DEL-01 P1 PagerDuty `form-enterprise` → compliance-officer + enterprise-architect | `tenant_id` (UUID — FORM-internal only; no company name, employee name, or health data), `days_since_deletion_requested` (int ≥ 0), `alert_tier` (literal `'warning'`), `check_run_at` (ISO 8601 UTC — job execution timestamp), `alert_dedup_key` (string — `deletion-sla-warning-{tenant_id}`) |
| `enterprise.deletion_sla_critical` | CRITICAL | 7 yr | pg_cron job 43 (`deletion_sla_monitor`, `0 */6 * * *`) detects `enterprise_churn_events` row where `now() - deletion_requested_at > INTERVAL '29 days'` AND `deletion_completed_at IS NULL` — 1-day pre-breach GDPR Art. 17 SLA escalation; emitter: `form_system` (`service_role`); if `deletion_completed_at` remains NULL at day 30, GDPR Art. 17 SLA is breached — trigger INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-09 GDPR breach assessment; fires AL-DEL-01 P0 escalation PagerDuty `form-enterprise` → founder + compliance-officer | `tenant_id` (UUID — FORM-internal only; no company name, employee name, or health data), `days_since_deletion_requested` (int ≥ 0), `alert_tier` (literal `'critical'`), `check_run_at` (ISO 8601 UTC — job execution timestamp), `alert_dedup_key` (string — `deletion-sla-warning-{tenant_id}`) |
| `enterprise.litigation_hold_declared` | HIGH | 7 yr | Compliance-officer (manual, PAM-elevated) activates a litigation hold under MSA §11.6.1 for a churned or active tenant; GDPR Art. 17(3)(e) basis; suspends §12.3 deletion timeline for non-health data only; emitter: compliance-officer (Admin Console; PAM session required); outside counsel countersign required before emission; Art. 9 health data is never subject to hold — §12.2 zero-grace-period (DEC-036) is absolute; fires no PagerDuty alert on normal activation (compliance-officer authored) | `tenant_id` (UUID — FORM-internal), `hold_reason_category` (enum: `billing_dispute`\|`legal_claim`\|`regulatory_direction`\|`anticipated_litigation`), `held_data_categories` (string[] — category names only: `billing_records`\|`contract_records`\|`audit_log_entries`\|`sla_performance_data`), `activation_date` (YYYY-MM-DD), `target_review_date` (YYYY-MM-DD — 6 months from activation), `outside_counsel_ref` (string non-null — outside counsel hold-instruction reference), `compliance_officer_id` (UUID) |
| `enterprise.litigation_hold_released` | HIGH | 7 yr | Compliance-officer (manual, PAM-elevated) releases, withdraws, or expires a litigation hold under MSA §11.6.6; deletion of held data must complete within 10 business days; deletion certificate provided within 5 business days thereafter; emitter: compliance-officer (Admin Console; PAM session required) | `tenant_id` (UUID — FORM-internal), `hold_reason_category` (enum: `billing_dispute`\|`legal_claim`\|`regulatory_direction`\|`anticipated_litigation`), `release_reason` (enum: `dispute_resolved`\|`legal_claim_concluded`\|`regulatory_obligation_expired`\|`court_order_vacated`\|`max_duration_reached`), `activation_date` (YYYY-MM-DD — from the corresponding `enterprise.litigation_hold_declared` event), `release_date` (YYYY-MM-DD), `held_data_categories` (string[] — same categories as on declaration), `deletion_target_date` (YYYY-MM-DD — 10 business days from `release_date`), `compliance_officer_id` (UUID) |
| `system.deletion_sla_monitor_stale_declared` | HIGH | 7 yr | IC-emitted at R-41 T+0 when `pg-cron-health-monitor` pages with `job_name = 'deletion_sla_monitor'` or manual discovery confirms job 43 exceeds 7h freshness; emitter: compliance-officer (manual, PAM-elevated, `emit-audit-event` Worker); DELETION-SLA-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 prerequisite — must precede `system.deletion_sla_monitor_restored` for same `incident_id`; `danger_window_tenants_affected > 0` triggers immediate P0 escalation to founder | `incident_id` (UUID), `confirmed_stale_since` (ISO 8601 UTC — last successful run), `stale_minutes` (positive int), `missed_runs` (nonneg int), `trigger` (enum: `pagerduty_alert`\|`manual_discovery`\|`co_active_r03`), `initial_severity` (enum: `P1`\|`P0` — P0 if danger_window_tenants_affected > 0), `danger_window_tenants_affected` (nonneg int — count from R-41-C2; 0 = no active danger) |
| `system.deletion_sla_monitor_restored` | STANDARD | 3 yr | IC-emitted at R-41 Step 6 when job 43 confirmed running after restoration; DELETION-SLA-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 terminal event; emitter: compliance-officer (manual, `emit-audit-event` Worker); PagerDuty P1/P0 `deletion-sla-monitor-stale` resolved on emission; `art17_sla_breached: true` triggers INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-09 (GDPR Art. 33 72h window starts from `confirmed_stale_since`) | `incident_id` (UUID), `restored_at` (ISO 8601 UTC), `root_cause` (enum: `H1`\|`H2`\|`H3`\|`H4`), `fix_deployed_at` (ISO 8601 UTC), `stale_window_hours` (positive float), `danger_window_tenants_affected` (nonneg int), `art17_sla_breached` (bool — true = R-09 activated; false = no Art. 17 breach during stale window) |
| `enterprise.offboard_chain_sla_breach` | HIGH | 7 yr | pg_cron job 44 (`offboard_chain_monitor`, `0 * * * *`) detects `enterprise_churn_events` row where `offboarding_initiated_at IS NULL AND churn_date::timestamptz < now() - INTERVAL '24 hours'` — OFFBOARD-CHAIN-01 SLA breach detected; emitter: `form_system` (`service_role` — `form_api` REVOKED from `enterprise_churn_events`); emitted once per flagged tenant per hourly run; OFFL-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant — audit event HTTP 200 confirmed before AL-OFFL-01 PagerDuty fires (§53.5.2 SQL enforces ordering within loop iteration); dedup `offboard-chain-breach-{tenant_id}` 4h cooldown; auto-resolves in PagerDuty when `offboarding_initiated_at` populated at next hourly run; fires AL-OFFL-01 P1 PagerDuty `form-enterprise` → compliance-officer + enterprise-architect; INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-44 is the job 44 stale recovery runbook (§53.10 item 6 — to be authored) | `tenant_id` (UUID — FORM-internal only; no company name, employee name, or health data), `churn_date` (string regex `^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$` — ISO 8601 date of confirmed churn), `hours_since_churn` (int ≥ 0 — hours elapsed since `churn_date`), `check_run_at` (ISO 8601 UTC — job 44 run timestamp), `alert_dedup_key` (string — `offboard-chain-breach-{tenant_id}`) |
| `system.offboard_chain_check_passed` | LOW | 1 yr | pg_cron job 44 (`offboard_chain_monitor`, `0 * * * *`) all-clear path: emitted once per run when `breach_count = 0` (no `enterprise_churn_events` rows with `offboarding_initiated_at IS NULL` past 24h) — fleet-level attestation that all churned tenants have `offboarding_initiated_at` populated within OFFBOARD-CHAIN-01 SLA; emitter: `form_system` (`service_role`); no `tenant_id` in payload (privacy floor — a zero-breach signal reveals no per-tenant information); 1yr retention consistent with peer system-level health attestation events (`system.sca_sla_check_passed`, OBSERVABILITY §52) | `tenants_checked` (int ≥ 0 — total `enterprise_churn_events` rows checked; no individual identifiers), `check_run_at` (ISO 8601 UTC — job 44 run timestamp) |
| `system.offboard_chain_monitor_stale_declared` | HIGH | 7 yr | IC-emitted at R-44 T+0 when `pg-cron-health-monitor` pages with `job_name = 'offboard_chain_monitor'` or manual discovery confirms job 44 exceeds 2h freshness; emitter: compliance-officer (manual, PAM-elevated, `emit-audit-event` Worker); OFFBOARD-CHAIN-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 prerequisite — must precede `system.offboard_chain_monitor_restored` for same `incident_id`; `offboard_chain_breaches_at_declared > 0` triggers immediate P0 escalation to founder; registered per R-44.11 item 1 (INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-44 v1.0, 2026-06-25) | `incident_id` (UUID), `confirmed_stale_since` (ISO 8601 UTC — last successful run), `stale_minutes` (positive int), `missed_runs` (nonneg int), `trigger` (enum: `pagerduty_alert`\|`manual_discovery`\|`co_active_r03`), `initial_severity` (enum: `P1`\|`P0` — P0 if offboard_chain_breaches_at_declared > 0), `offboard_chain_breaches_at_declared` (nonneg int — count from R-44-C2 at declaration; 0 = no active breach) |
| `system.offboard_chain_monitor_restored` | STANDARD | 3 yr | IC-emitted at R-44 Step 6 when job 44 confirmed running after restoration and R-44-C2 re-run passes (zero active OFFBOARD-CHAIN-01 breaches or all breached tenants have `offboarding_initiated_at` populated); OFFBOARD-CHAIN-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 terminal event; emitter: compliance-officer (manual, `emit-audit-event` Worker); PagerDuty P1/P0 `offboard-chain-monitor-stale` resolved on emission; registered per R-44.11 item 1 (INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-44 v1.0, 2026-06-25) | `incident_id` (UUID — must match stale_declared), `restored_at` (ISO 8601 UTC), `root_cause` (enum: `H1`\|`H2`\|`H3`\|`H4`), `fix_deployed_at` (ISO 8601 UTC), `stale_window_hours` (positive float), `offboard_chain_breaches_at_declared` (nonneg int), `offboard_chain_breaches_at_restored` (nonneg int — 0 = no active breach at restoration, required before PagerDuty resolve) |

**Zod v2 schemas (canonical source: `docs/COST_MODEL.md §43.7.2` for winback and certificate events; `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §12.6` job 43 for deletion SLA events — registered here for `emit-audit-event` Worker validation):**

```typescript
import { z } from 'zod/v4';

// ChurnReasonEnum — from enterprise_churn_events (DATA_MODEL §44 · COST_MODEL §43.8)
const ChurnReasonEnum = z.enum([
  'low_utilization', 'champion_left', 'budget_cut',
  'competitor', 'platform_mismatch', 'compliance_requirement',
  'acquisition', 'other',
]);

// enterprise.winback_initiated — COST_MODEL §43.7.2
// Severity: HIGH · Retention: 7 yr · WINBACK-CHAIN-01 prerequisite
export const WinbackInitiatedPayload = z.object({
  tenant_id:           z.string().uuid(),
  prior_contract_id:   z.string().uuid(),
  churn_date:          z.string().date(),
  months_since_churn:  z.number().int().min(1).max(60),
  churn_reason:        ChurnReasonEnum,
  wau_band_at_churn:   z.enum(['green', 'amber', 'red']),
  prior_acv_usd:       z.number().positive(),
  initiated_by:        z.string().uuid(),   // CSM (form_admin role)
  outreach_sequence:   z.enum(['month_3', 'month_6', 'month_12', 'month_18_plus', 'ad_hoc']),
  // Privacy floor: no employee user_id, name, email, health data, or coaching content.
});

// enterprise.winback_converted — COST_MODEL §43.7.2
// Severity: CRITICAL · Retention: 7 yr · WINBACK-CHAIN-01 terminal event
export const WinbackConvertedPayload = z.object({
  tenant_id:                  z.string().uuid(),   // new UUID — Option B (DEC-079)
  prior_tenant_id:            z.string().uuid(),   // churned tenant UUID — DEC-079 self-audit field
  winback_contract_id:        z.string().uuid(),   // new contract_id required (§43.6.3)
  prior_contract_id:          z.string().uuid(),
  winback_initiated_event_id: z.string().uuid(),   // WINBACK-CHAIN-01 predecessor (direct event lookup)
  months_since_churn:         z.number().int().min(1).max(60),
  prior_acv_usd:              z.number().positive(),
  new_acv_usd:                z.number().positive(),
  acv_premium_pct:            z.number().min(0).max(200),
  new_seats:                  z.number().int().positive(),
  new_contract_years:         z.union([z.literal(1), z.literal(2), z.literal(3)]),
  confirmed_by:               z.string().uuid(),   // compliance-officer
  // Privacy floor: tenant_id and prior_tenant_id are FORM-internal UUIDs only; no employee data.
});

// enterprise.deletion_certificate_issued — COST_MODEL §43.7.2
// Severity: CRITICAL · Retention: 7 yr · DELETION-CHAIN-01 terminal event
export const DeletionCertificateIssuedPayload = z.object({
  tenant_id:                 z.string().uuid(),
  contract_id:               z.string().uuid(),
  deletion_request_event_id: z.string().uuid().optional(),   // present if gdpr_art17_request
  trigger_type:              z.enum(['gdpr_art17_request', 'standard_90_day', 'early_termination']),
  deletion_date:             z.string().date(),
  classes_deleted:           z.array(z.enum([
    'user_pii', 'health_data_art9', 'nutrition_logs',
    'workout_sessions', 'coaching_content', 'sso_personal_attributes',
  ])),
  classes_retained:          z.array(z.enum([
    'audit_log_chain', 'financial_records', 'contract_records', 'aggregate_metrics',
  ])),
  retained_legal_basis:      z.literal('gdpr_art17_3e_legal_obligation'),
  certificate_r2_path:       z.string().regex(/^compliance\/evidence\/deletions\/.+\.pdf$/),
  compliance_officer_id:     z.string().uuid(),
  days_since_trigger:        z.number().int().min(0).max(35),   // DELETION-CHAIN-01 enforced
  // Privacy floor: tenant_id is FORM-internal UUID; no company name, employee names, or health data.
});

// enterprise.deletion_sla_warning — OBSERVABILITY §12.6 pg_cron job 43 (day 25)
// Severity: HIGH · Retention: 7 yr · DELETION-SLA-01 pre-breach warning
export const DeletionSlaWarningPayload = z.object({
  tenant_id:                     z.string().uuid(),
  days_since_deletion_requested: z.number().int().nonnegative(),
  alert_tier:                    z.literal('warning'),
  check_run_at:                  z.string().datetime(),   // ISO 8601 UTC
  alert_dedup_key:               z.string(),              // "deletion-sla-warning-{tenant_id}"
  // Privacy floor: no employee user_id, name, email, health value, or Art. 9 special-category data.
});

// enterprise.deletion_sla_critical — OBSERVABILITY §12.6 pg_cron job 43 (day 29)
// Severity: CRITICAL · Retention: 7 yr · DELETION-SLA-01 P0 pre-breach escalation
export const DeletionSlaCriticalPayload = z.object({
  tenant_id:                     z.string().uuid(),
  days_since_deletion_requested: z.number().int().nonnegative(),
  alert_tier:                    z.literal('critical'),
  check_run_at:                  z.string().datetime(),   // ISO 8601 UTC
  alert_dedup_key:               z.string(),              // "deletion-sla-warning-{tenant_id}"
  // Privacy floor: no employee user_id, name, email, health value, or Art. 9 special-category data.
});

// enterprise.litigation_hold_declared — MSA §11.6 (DEC-080, 2026-06-23)
// Severity: HIGH · Retention: 7 yr · GDPR Art. 17(3)(e) legal-claim retention basis
const LitigationHoldReasonEnum = z.enum([
  'billing_dispute', 'legal_claim', 'regulatory_direction', 'anticipated_litigation',
]);
const HeldDataCategoryEnum = z.enum([
  'billing_records', 'contract_records', 'audit_log_entries', 'sla_performance_data',
]);
export const LitigationHoldDeclaredPayload = z.object({
  tenant_id:              z.string().uuid(),
  hold_reason_category:   LitigationHoldReasonEnum,
  held_data_categories:   z.array(HeldDataCategoryEnum).min(1),
  activation_date:        z.string().date(),
  target_review_date:     z.string().date(),   // 6 months from activation_date
  outside_counsel_ref:    z.string().min(1),   // non-null: outside counsel hold-instruction reference
  compliance_officer_id:  z.string().uuid(),
  // Privacy floor: no employee user_id, health values, coaching content, or Art. 9 data.
  // Art. 9 health data is categorically excluded from all litigation holds (MSA §11.6.2 absolute exclusion).
});

// enterprise.litigation_hold_released — MSA §11.6.6 (DEC-080, 2026-06-23)
// Severity: HIGH · Retention: 7 yr · Hold release; deletion must complete within 10 business days
export const LitigationHoldReleasedPayload = z.object({
  tenant_id:              z.string().uuid(),
  hold_reason_category:   LitigationHoldReasonEnum,
  release_reason:         z.enum([
    'dispute_resolved', 'legal_claim_concluded', 'regulatory_obligation_expired',
    'court_order_vacated', 'max_duration_reached',
  ]),
  activation_date:        z.string().date(),   // from the corresponding declared event
  release_date:           z.string().date(),
  held_data_categories:   z.array(HeldDataCategoryEnum).min(1),
  deletion_target_date:   z.string().date(),   // 10 business days from release_date
  compliance_officer_id:  z.string().uuid(),
  // Privacy floor: no employee user_id, health values, coaching content, or Art. 9 data.
});

// system.deletion_sla_monitor_stale_declared — HIGH · 7 yr (R-41 monitoring-control)
// DELETION-SLA-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 prerequisite — must precede restored for same incident_id
export const DeletionSlaMonitorStaleDeclaredPayload = z.object({
  incident_id:                    z.string().uuid(),
  confirmed_stale_since:          z.string().datetime(),   // ISO 8601 UTC — last successful run
  stale_minutes:                  z.number().positive(),
  missed_runs:                    z.number().int().nonneg(),
  trigger:                        z.enum([
    'pagerduty_alert',
    'manual_discovery',
    'co_active_r03',     // H2 shared pg_cron failure co-activation
  ]),
  initial_severity:               z.enum(['P1', 'P0']),   // P0 if danger_window_tenants_affected > 0
  danger_window_tenants_affected: z.number().int().nonneg(),  // count from R-41-C2; 0 = no active danger
  // Privacy floor: integer count only — no tenant_id, no employee user_id, no health data, no Art. 9 data.
});

// system.deletion_sla_monitor_restored — STANDARD · 3 yr (R-41 monitoring-control)
// DELETION-SLA-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 terminal event — requires prior stale_declared for same incident_id
export const DeletionSlaMonitorRestoredPayload = z.object({
  incident_id:                    z.string().uuid(),
  restored_at:                    z.string().datetime(),
  root_cause:                     z.enum(['H1', 'H2', 'H3', 'H4']),
  fix_deployed_at:                z.string().datetime(),
  stale_window_hours:             z.number().positive(),
  danger_window_tenants_affected: z.number().int().nonneg(),
  art17_sla_breached:             z.boolean(),   // true = R-09 activated; false = no Art. 17 breach
  // Privacy floor: art17_sla_breached is boolean; no tenant_id, no employee data, no health values.
});

// enterprise.offboard_chain_sla_breach — OBSERVABILITY §53.7.2
// Severity: HIGH · Retention: 7 yr · pg_cron job 44 offboard_chain_monitor
// OFFL-CHAIN-01: this event must be emitted + HTTP 200 confirmed BEFORE AL-OFFL-01 PagerDuty fires.
export const OffboardChainSlaBreachPayload = z.object({
  tenant_id:          z.string().uuid(),                           // FORM-internal UUID — no employee PII
  churn_date:         z.string().regex(/^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/),    // ISO 8601 date of confirmed churn
  hours_since_churn:  z.number().int().nonnegative(),              // integer hours elapsed since churn_date
  check_run_at:       z.string().datetime(),                       // job 44 run timestamp (ISO 8601 UTC)
  alert_dedup_key:    z.string(),                                  // "offboard-chain-breach-{tenant_id}"
  // Privacy floor: no employee user_id, name, email, health value, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category data.
});

// system.offboard_chain_check_passed — OBSERVABILITY §53.7.2
// Severity: LOW · Retention: 1 yr · pg_cron job 44 all-clear path (breach_count === 0)
// Privacy floor: no tenant_id — emitted only when no breach detected; zero-breach signal reveals no per-tenant data.
export const OffboardChainCheckPassedPayload = z.object({
  tenants_checked:  z.number().int().nonnegative(), // total enterprise_churn_events rows checked; no individual identifiers
  check_run_at:     z.string().datetime(),           // job 44 run timestamp (ISO 8601 UTC)
});

// system.offboard_chain_monitor_stale_declared — INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-44 §R-44.7
// Severity: HIGH · Retention: 7 yr · IC-emitted at R-44 T+0 (compliance-officer, PAM-elevated)
// OFFBOARD-CHAIN-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 prerequisite.
// Privacy floor: offboard_chain_breaches_at_declared is integer count — no tenant_id, no employee data.
export const OffboardChainMonitorStaleDeclaredPayload = z.object({
  incident_id:                        z.string().uuid(),
  confirmed_stale_since:              z.string().datetime(),   // ISO 8601 UTC — last successful job 44 run
  stale_minutes:                      z.number().int().positive(),
  missed_runs:                        z.number().int().nonnegative(),
  trigger:                            z.enum(['pagerduty_alert', 'manual_discovery', 'co_active_r03']),
  initial_severity:                   z.enum(['P1', 'P0']),   // P0 if offboard_chain_breaches_at_declared > 0
  offboard_chain_breaches_at_declared: z.number().int().nonnegative(),
});

// system.offboard_chain_monitor_restored — INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-44 §R-44.7
// Severity: STANDARD · Retention: 3 yr · IC-emitted at R-44 Step 6 (compliance-officer, PAM-elevated)
// OFFBOARD-CHAIN-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 terminal event.
// Privacy floor: integer counts only — no tenant_id, no employee data.
export const OffboardChainMonitorRestoredPayload = z.object({
  incident_id:                         z.string().uuid(),      // must match stale_declared incident_id
  restored_at:                         z.string().datetime(),
  root_cause:                          z.enum(['H1', 'H2', 'H3', 'H4']),
  fix_deployed_at:                     z.string().datetime(),
  stale_window_hours:                  z.number().positive(),
  offboard_chain_breaches_at_declared: z.number().int().nonnegative(),
  offboard_chain_breaches_at_restored: z.number().int().nonnegative(),
});
```

**Emitter assignments:**
- `enterprise.winback_initiated` — CSM (manual, `form_admin` role, PAM-elevated) via Admin Console "Winback Pipeline" (§43.10 item 6, P1/M12 — pending build). One emission per outreach sequence initiation per `tenant_id`.
- `enterprise.winback_converted` — compliance-officer (manual, PAM-elevated) via Admin Console "Winback Pipeline" counter-sign step. `emit-audit-event` Worker validates WINBACK-CHAIN-01 predecessor before appending; HTTP 422 `WINBACK_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` on absent.
- `enterprise.deletion_certificate_issued` — compliance-officer (manual, PAM-elevated) via Admin Console "Offboarding Workflow" Step 3 (§43.10 item 4, P0/M11 — pending build). `emit-audit-event` Worker validates `days_since_trigger ≤ 35`; HTTP 422 `DELETION_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` on violation.
- `enterprise.deletion_sla_warning` — `form_system` (`service_role`), automated, pg_cron job 43 (`0 */6 * * *`). `form_api` REVOKED from `enterprise_churn_events`; `service_role` elevation required for DELETION-SLA-01 SQL.
- `enterprise.deletion_sla_critical` — `form_system` (`service_role`), automated, pg_cron job 43 (`0 */6 * * *`). Emitted on same cycle as `deletion_sla_warning` when `days_since_deletion_requested > 29`; both events may be emitted in the same job run when day counter crosses 29 (warning event first, then critical).
- `enterprise.litigation_hold_declared` — compliance-officer (manual, PAM-elevated) via Admin Console "Litigation Hold" workflow. Outside counsel countersign required before emission (verified by compliance-officer; `outside_counsel_ref` non-null enforced by Zod). No automated path; no `form_api` or `form_system` emission.
- `enterprise.litigation_hold_released` — compliance-officer (manual, PAM-elevated) via Admin Console "Litigation Hold" workflow. Emitted when hold is released, withdrawn, or expired. `deletion_target_date` field triggers compliance-officer calendar obligation for deletion certification (no automated deletion path; human-verified per MSA §11.6.6).
- `system.deletion_sla_monitor_stale_declared` — compliance-officer (manual, PAM-elevated) via `emit-audit-event` Worker at R-41 T+0. Emitted once per R-41 activation on receipt of `system.cron_job_stale` PagerDuty page for `job_name = 'deletion_sla_monitor'`, or on manual discovery. Not automated — requires IC human judgment on P0 vs P1 severity classification (R-41.2 decision table).
- `system.deletion_sla_monitor_restored` — compliance-officer (manual, PAM-elevated) via `emit-audit-event` Worker at R-41 Step 6 after job 43 confirmed healthy. Carries `art17_sla_breached` boolean self-attestation (human-determined after R-41 Step 5 stale-window assessment). No automated path.
- `enterprise.offboard_chain_sla_breach` — `form_system` (`service_role`), automated, pg_cron job 44 (`0 * * * *`). `form_api` REVOKED from `enterprise_churn_events`; `service_role` elevation required for OFFBOARD-CHAIN-01 SQL. OFFL-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant: audit event emission HTTP 200 confirmed before AL-OFFL-01 PagerDuty call within each loop iteration (see §53.5.2 enforcement SQL). One emission per flagged tenant per hourly run; DEC-030 trail continues every run while breach persists.
- `system.offboard_chain_check_passed` — `form_system` (`service_role`), automated, pg_cron job 44 (`0 * * * *`), all-clear path only (emitted when `breach_count = 0`). No `tenant_id` in payload — privacy floor (fleet-clean state carries no per-tenant disclosure).
- `system.offboard_chain_monitor_stale_declared` — compliance-officer (manual, PAM-elevated) via `emit-audit-event` Worker at R-44 T+0. Emitted once per R-44 activation on receipt of `system.cron_job_stale` PagerDuty page for `job_name = 'offboard_chain_monitor'`, or on manual discovery. Not automated — requires IC human judgment on P0 vs P1 severity classification (R-44.2 decision table). Registered per R-44.11 item 1 (INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-44 v1.0, 2026-06-25).
- `system.offboard_chain_monitor_restored` — compliance-officer (manual, PAM-elevated) via `emit-audit-event` Worker at R-44 Step 6 after job 44 confirmed healthy. Carries `offboard_chain_breaches_at_restored` integer self-attestation (human-verified after R-44 Step 5 breach sweep). No automated path. Registered per R-44.11 item 1 (INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-44 v1.0, 2026-06-25).

**Alert routing:**
- `enterprise.winback_initiated` → no alert (CSM-authored governance anchor; compliance dashboard surface only).
- `enterprise.winback_converted` → no alert (compliance-officer authored positive completion event); customer-success team notified via Admin Console workflow notification.
- `enterprise.deletion_certificate_issued` → no alert on normal issuance; if `trigger_type = 'gdpr_art17_request'` and `days_since_trigger > 25`, DEL-CERT-01 advisory fires `#compliance-ops` Slack (non-blocking — certificate was issued within the 30-day GDPR SLA but within the 5-day warning band).
- `enterprise.deletion_sla_warning` → AL-DEL-01 P1 PagerDuty `form-enterprise` → compliance-officer + enterprise-architect; dedup key `deletion-sla-warning-{tenant_id}` 6h cooldown; 5-day SLA to confirm deletion completion before GDPR Art. 17 breach. Auto-resolves in PagerDuty when `deletion_completed_at` is populated and next 6h job run emits no warning event. DEC-030 trail continues every 6h cycle regardless of PagerDuty dedup.
- `enterprise.deletion_sla_critical` → AL-DEL-01 P0 escalation PagerDuty `form-enterprise` → founder + compliance-officer; same 6h dedup key `deletion-sla-warning-{tenant_id}`; if `deletion_completed_at` remains NULL at day 30, GDPR Art. 17 SLA is breached — trigger INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-09 (GDPR breach assessment). `ece_deletion_sla_respected` CHECK in `enterprise_churn_events` DDL (DATA_MODEL §44) provides the 35-day hard backstop.
- `enterprise.litigation_hold_declared` → no automated PagerDuty alert (compliance-officer authored governance event). Visible in Admin Console compliance dashboard. Compliance-officer calendar obligation: 6-month review (`target_review_date` field) to re-confirm hold necessity per MSA §11.6.3.
- `enterprise.litigation_hold_released` → no automated PagerDuty alert (compliance-officer authored). Compliance-officer calendar obligation: deletion of held data within 10 business days of `release_date` (`deletion_target_date` field); written certification to Customer within 5 business days of deletion completion per MSA §11.6.6.
- `system.deletion_sla_monitor_stale_declared` → no additional PagerDuty alert on emission (the `system.cron_job_stale` PagerDuty page already fired from `pg-cron-health-monitor`); DEC-030 event serves as the tamper-evident IC response anchor in the HMAC chain.
- `system.deletion_sla_monitor_restored` → resolves PagerDuty P1/P0 `deletion-sla-monitor-stale` alert; no separate alert on restoration. If `art17_sla_breached = true`: INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-09 is activated immediately — R-09 carries its own PagerDuty routing and Art. 33 72h notification workflow.
- `enterprise.offboard_chain_sla_breach` → AL-OFFL-01 P1 PagerDuty `form-enterprise` → compliance-officer + enterprise-architect; dedup key `offboard-chain-breach-{tenant_id}` 4h cooldown; auto-resolves in PagerDuty when `offboarding_initiated_at` is populated and next hourly job 44 run detects no breach for that tenant. DEC-030 trail continues per run while breach persists — full audit chain preserved without PagerDuty storm. If breach persists beyond working-hours first response, INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-44 (job 44 stale recovery runbook, §53.10 item 6 — authored 2026-06-25) covers forced manual offboarding initiation.
- `system.offboard_chain_check_passed` → no alert (fleet-level all-clear; surfaced in §53.9 Metabase `Enterprise Post-Churn Lifecycle Health` dashboard, "AL-OFFL-01 activation log" panel rolling 30d). Provides continuous auditor-inspectable attestation that OFFL-SLO-01 was met in each hourly sweep.
- `system.offboard_chain_monitor_stale_declared` → no additional PagerDuty alert on emission (the `system.cron_job_stale` PagerDuty P1 `form-enterprise` page already fired from `pg-cron-health-monitor`); DEC-030 event serves as the tamper-evident IC response anchor in the HMAC chain; opens the OFFBOARD-CHAIN-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 chain.
- `system.offboard_chain_monitor_restored` → resolves PagerDuty P1/P0 `offboard-chain-monitor-stale` alert; no separate alert on restoration; closes the OFFBOARD-CHAIN-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 chain. If `offboard_chain_breaches_at_declared > 0`: compliance-officer must confirm all affected tenants have `offboarding_initiated_at` populated and notify enterprise-architect per R-44 Step 5 post-incident controls.

**SOC 2 evidence artefacts:**

| Artefact | SOC 2 criteria | Description | Cadence | Retention | Path |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **DEL-E-001** | C1.2 / CC4.1 | Annual deletion certificate archive: all `deletion_certificate.pdf` files + corresponding `enterprise.deletion_certificate_issued` DEC-030 events for the observation year; `days_since_trigger ≤ 30` attestation for all `trigger_type = 'gdpr_art17_request'` rows; cross-check: every churned tenant with `deletion_requested_at` in the year has a certificate on file. Defined in `docs/COST_MODEL.md §43.9`; registered in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §102` (v3.27.0, 2026-06-23) | Annual | 7 yr | `compliance/evidence/deletions/DEL-E-001_<YYYY>.zip` |
| **WBK-E-001** | CC4.1 / CC5.2 | Annual winback programme report: `enterprise_churn_events` aggregate by `churn_reason` × `wau_band_at_churn`; outreach attempts; `winback_converted` count; ACV premium realised vs. §43.6.1 model. Fleet-level aggregate only — no tenant names or individual user data. Defined in `docs/COST_MODEL.md §43.9`; registered as WBK-E-001 (not WIN-E-001) in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §102` to avoid ID collision with existing §87.2 `WIN-E-001` (deal-close governance) | Annual | 3 yr | `compliance/evidence/winback/WBK-E-001_<YYYY>.csv` |
| **CHN-E-001** | CC6.1 / CC7.1 | Quarterly OFFBOARD-CHAIN-01 compliance report: all `enterprise.account_churned` events in the quarter; elapsed time to `enterprise.offboarding_initiated` for the same `tenant_id`; any elapsed time > 24h flagged with compliance-officer explanation. Zero-breach quarters filed as affirmative attestation. Defined in `docs/COST_MODEL.md §43.9`; registered in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §102` (v3.27.0, 2026-06-23) | Quarterly | 3 yr | `compliance/evidence/offboarding/CHN-E-001_<YYYY-QN>.csv` |

**DELETION-SLA-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 — Ordering invariant (R-41 monitoring-control chain):** `system.deletion_sla_monitor_restored` is blocked (HTTP 422 `DELETION_SLA_MONITOR_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION`) if no prior `system.deletion_sla_monitor_stale_declared` exists for the same `incident_id`; violation → R-05 activated. Privacy floor (both R-41 events): `danger_window_tenants_affected` and `art17_sla_breached` are integer/boolean operational metadata — no `tenant_id`, no employee `user_id`, no health values, no GDPR Art. 9 data in either chain event. Affected tenant identities are preserved only in the stale-window exception note at `compliance/evidence/deletions/deletion-sla-stale/r41-<incident_id>/stale-window-note.md` (access: compliance_reviewer; `form_api` REVOKED per §80.3). Registered per R-41.11 item 1 (P0/M10 — [x] Done 2026-06-25, AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.41).

**C1.2 auditor narrative:** C1.2 requires FORM to dispose of confidential information per contractual data protection obligations. `enterprise.deletion_certificate_issued` CRITICAL/7yr provides an HMAC-chained record of every enterprise tenant deletion: which data classes were erased, the GDPR Art. 17(3)(e) legal basis for retained classes (`audit_log_chain`, `financial_records`, `contract_records`, `aggregate_metrics`), and confirmation the certificate was issued within the SLA (`days_since_trigger ≤ 35`; ≤ 30 for GDPR Art. 17 requests). DEL-E-001 (annual archive) aggregates these events for the observation year. DELETION-CHAIN-01 makes late issuance architecturally impossible; the `ece_deletion_sla_respected` CHECK constraint in `enterprise_churn_events` (DATA_MODEL §44) provides an additional DDL-layer backstop at 35 days — a SLA violation becomes a schema-enforced blocker, not a controls failure.

**CC4.1 auditor narrative:** CC4.1 requires FORM to evaluate and communicate performance against defined thresholds. `enterprise.deletion_sla_warning` HIGH/7yr and `enterprise.deletion_sla_critical` CRITICAL/7yr provide a continuous audit trail of every pre-breach detection cycle. The 7h freshness window on pg_cron job 43 means any monitoring gap > 7h triggers `system.cron_job_stale` HIGH (OBSERVABILITY §12.6) — proving the monitoring control was either running or detected as stale within the freshness window.

**CC5.2 auditor narrative:** CC5.2 requires FORM to enforce commitments and accountabilities. WINBACK-CHAIN-01 enforces that no winback conversion can be recorded without a prior CSM-authorised outreach event for the same tenant within 365 days. `enterprise.winback_converted` CRITICAL/7yr proves the commercial recovery programme operates under chain-enforced accountability — no retroactive winback recognition is possible even with `form_admin` role. WBK-E-001 (annual aggregate) demonstrates the programme is monitored at fleet level without exposing individual employee data.

**CC6.1 auditor narrative:** CC6.1 requires FORM to implement logical access controls including deprovisioning when no longer needed. `enterprise.deletion_certificate_issued` closes the full post-churn chain: OFFBOARD-CHAIN-01 (`enterprise.account_churned` → `enterprise.offboarding_initiated` within 24h) ensures SSO teardown and SCIM deprovisioning begin immediately; DELETION-CHAIN-01 ensures full data deletion completes within 35 days. CHN-E-001 (quarterly OFFBOARD-CHAIN-01 report) proves deprovisioning was initiated within 24 hours of churn confirmation for every churned tenant in the observation quarter.

**OFFL-CHAIN-01 — Ordering invariant (job 44 monitoring-detection chain):** `enterprise.offboard_chain_sla_breach` must be emitted and confirmed (HTTP 200 from `emit-audit-event`) BEFORE the AL-OFFL-01 PagerDuty alert fires within each loop iteration of pg_cron job 44. The §53.5.2 enforcement SQL places the audit event `PERFORM` call before the PagerDuty `PERFORM` call; if the audit event pg_net call returns non-200, DEC-030 is the authoritative source of truth for the breach detection — re-fire PagerDuty manually using the `audit_log_events` row `id` as the incident reference. This invariant is analogous to SCA-CHAIN-01 (OBSERVABILITY §52.7), BAM-STALE-CHAIN-01 (OBSERVABILITY §39), and the full family of pg_cron monitoring-detection chains. INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-44 (job 44 stale recovery runbook — §53.10 item 6, authored 2026-06-25, INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md v1.0) covers the stale-monitor companion case where job 44 itself stops running. Privacy floor (`enterprise.offboard_chain_sla_breach`): `tenant_id` is FORM-internal UUID; payload carries only `hours_since_churn` (integer) and timestamps — no employee `user_id`, name, email, health value, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category data. Registered per §53.10 item 1 (P0/M10 — [x] Done 2026-06-25, AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.43).

**OFFBOARD-CHAIN-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 — Ordering invariant (R-44 monitoring-control chain):** `system.offboard_chain_monitor_restored` is blocked (HTTP 422 `OFFBOARD_CHAIN_MONITOR_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION`) if no prior `system.offboard_chain_monitor_stale_declared` exists for the same `incident_id`; violation → R-05 activated. Privacy floor (both R-44 events): `offboard_chain_breaches_at_declared` and `offboard_chain_breaches_at_restored` are integer counts only — no `tenant_id`, no employee `user_id`, no health values, no GDPR Art. 9 data in either chain event. Affected tenant identities are preserved only in the stale-window exception note at `compliance/evidence/offboarding/offboard-chain-monitor-stale/r44-<incident_id>/stale-window-note.md` (access: compliance_reviewer; `form_api` REVOKED per §80.3). Registered per R-44.11 item 1 (INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-44 v1.0, 2026-06-25 — [x] Done 2026-06-25, AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.44).

**CC7.2 auditor narrative:** CC7.2 requires FORM to detect, monitor, and respond to deviations from expected operating conditions. `enterprise.offboard_chain_sla_breach` and `system.offboard_chain_check_passed` together provide a continuous, HMAC-chained record of the OFFBOARD-CHAIN-01 monitoring cadence: every hourly pg_cron job 44 run either emits a per-tenant breach event or a fleet-level all-clear attestation. Combined with the `pg-cron-health-monitor` 2h freshness window on job 44 (OBSERVABILITY §12.6), this yields an auditor-verifiable monitoring record with no gap longer than 2h.
