CHN-OBS-E-001 (quarterly, 3yr — `compliance/evidence/offboarding/CHN-OBS-E-001_<YYYY-QN>.md`, defined in OBSERVABILITY §53.8) aggregates OFFL-SLO-01 performance and AL-OFFL-01 activation log for the observation quarter; registration in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4` master evidence table per §53.10 item 5 (P1/M11 — pending).

---

### Enterprise Litigation Hold Monitoring events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-45 · CC5.3/C1.1/C1.2/CC7.1/CC7.2)

> Defined in `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-45.10` (INCIDENT_RESPONSE v1.0, 2026-06-25 — R-45 Litigation Hold Compliance Monitor Stale). Two DEC-030 HMAC-chained events covering the R-45 stale-activation lifecycle for pg_cron job 45 (`litigation_hold_compliance_monitor`, `0 8 * * *`, 26h freshness window). Job 45 monitors three MSA §11.6 obligations simultaneously: LITH-SLO-01 (6-month review, CC5.3/MSA §11.6.3), LITH-SLO-02 (36-month cap, C1.1/MSA §11.6.4), LITH-SLO-03 (10-business-day deletion, C1.2/MSA §11.6.6). Unlike peer stale runbooks (R-41 for job 43; R-44 for job 44 — each single-obligation), R-45 staleness creates a simultaneous triple-obligation detection blind spot. **LITH-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 (blocking):** `system.litigation_hold_monitor_restored` is blocked (HTTP 422 `LITH_MONITOR_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION`) unless a prior `system.litigation_hold_monitor_stale_declared` exists for the same `incident_id` → R-05 activated on violation. **Privacy floor (both events):** Integer counts and timestamps only — no `tenant_id`, no employee `user_id`, no health data, no GDPR Art. 9 special-category data. Affected hold details live only in per-path stale-window exception notes under `compliance/evidence/litigation-hold/*/r45-<incident_id>/` (access: compliance_reviewer; `form_api` REVOKED per §80.3). **Closes `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-45.14` item 1 (P0/M6 — register both events + LITH-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 + Zod schemas; 🟢 Done 2026-06-25, AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.46).** Cross-ref: `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-45` (full runbook — five scope queries R-45-C1/C2a/C2b/C2c/C3; H1–H4 root cause classification; three escalation paths §R-45.5/R-45.6/R-45.7); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §12.6` (job 45 `litigation_hold_compliance_monitor` registry); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §54` (AL-LITH-01/02/03 alert rules); `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §46` (`litigation_hold_records` table); DEC-030.

| Event type | Severity | Retention | Trigger | Key payload fields |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `system.litigation_hold_monitor_stale_declared` | HIGH | 7 yr | IC-emitted at R-45 T+0 when `pg-cron-health-monitor` pages with `job_name = 'litigation_hold_compliance_monitor'` or manual discovery confirms job 45 exceeds 26h freshness; emitter: compliance-officer (manual, PAM-elevated, `emit-audit-event` Worker); LITH-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 prerequisite — must precede `system.litigation_hold_monitor_restored` for same `incident_id`; `max_duration_breach_count_at_declared > 0` triggers immediate P0 escalation (MSA §11.6.4 breach — unconditional P0 per §R-45.6); registered per R-45.14 item 1 (INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-45 v1.0, 2026-06-25) | `incident_id` (UUID — LITH-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 anchor), `confirmed_stale_since` (ISO 8601 UTC — last successful job 45 run), `stale_hours` (positive float), `missed_runs` (nonneg int), `trigger` (enum: `pagerduty_alert`\|`manual_discovery`\|`co_active_r03`), `initial_severity` (enum: `P1`\|`P0` — P0 if `max_duration_breach_count_at_declared > 0`), `review_overdue_count_at_declared` (nonneg int — LITH-SLO-01; 0 = no breach), `max_duration_breach_count_at_declared` (nonneg int — LITH-SLO-02; 0 = no C1.1 breach), `deletion_overdue_count_at_declared` (nonneg int — LITH-SLO-03; 0 = no C1.2 breach) |
| `system.litigation_hold_monitor_restored` | STANDARD | 3 yr | IC-emitted at R-45 Step 6 when job 45 confirmed running after restoration and C2a/C2b/C2c re-run passes (all breach counts zero or all affected holds actioned); LITH-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 terminal event; emitter: compliance-officer (manual, PAM-elevated, `emit-audit-event` Worker); PagerDuty P1/P0 `litigation-hold-monitor-stale` resolved on emission; registered per R-45.14 item 1 (INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-45 v1.0, 2026-06-25) | `incident_id` (UUID — must match `stale_declared`), `restored_at` (ISO 8601 UTC), `root_cause` (enum: `H1`\|`H2`\|`H3`\|`H4`), `fix_deployed_at` (ISO 8601 UTC), `stale_window_hours` (positive float), `review_overdue_count_at_declared` (nonneg int), `max_duration_breach_count_at_declared` (nonneg int), `deletion_overdue_count_at_declared` (nonneg int), `review_overdue_count_at_restored` (nonneg int — 0 required before PagerDuty resolve if C2a was clean), `max_duration_breach_count_at_restored` (nonneg int), `deletion_overdue_count_at_restored` (nonneg int) |

**Zod v2 schemas (canonical source: `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-45.10` — update INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md first on any schema change):**

```typescript
import { z } from 'zod/v4';

// system.litigation_hold_monitor_stale_declared — INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-45 §R-45.10
// Severity: HIGH · Retention: 7 yr · IC-emitted at R-45 T+0 (compliance-officer, PAM-elevated)
// LITH-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 prerequisite.
// Privacy floor: three breach counts are integers — no tenant_id, no employee data, no GDPR Art. 9 data.
export const LitigationHoldMonitorStaleDeclaredPayload = z.object({
  incident_id:                          z.string().uuid(),
  confirmed_stale_since:                z.string().datetime(),      // ISO 8601 UTC — last successful job 45 run
  stale_hours:                          z.number().positive(),
  missed_runs:                          z.number().int().nonnegative(),
  trigger:                              z.enum(['pagerduty_alert', 'manual_discovery', 'co_active_r03']),
  initial_severity:                     z.enum(['P1', 'P0']),        // P0 if max_duration_breach_count_at_declared > 0
  review_overdue_count_at_declared:      z.number().int().nonnegative(),  // LITH-SLO-01; 0 = no review breach
  max_duration_breach_count_at_declared: z.number().int().nonnegative(),  // LITH-SLO-02; 0 = no C1.1 breach
  deletion_overdue_count_at_declared:    z.number().int().nonnegative(),  // LITH-SLO-03; 0 = no C1.2 breach
});

// system.litigation_hold_monitor_restored — INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-45 §R-45.10
// Severity: STANDARD · Retention: 3 yr · IC-emitted at R-45 Step 6 (compliance-officer, PAM-elevated)
// LITH-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 terminal event.
// Privacy floor: six breach counts are integers — no tenant_id, no employee data, no GDPR Art. 9 data.
export const LitigationHoldMonitorRestoredPayload = z.object({
  incident_id:                           z.string().uuid(),          // must match stale_declared incident_id
  restored_at:                           z.string().datetime(),
  root_cause:                            z.enum(['H1', 'H2', 'H3', 'H4']),
  fix_deployed_at:                       z.string().datetime(),
  stale_window_hours:                    z.number().positive(),
  review_overdue_count_at_declared:       z.number().int().nonnegative(),
  max_duration_breach_count_at_declared:  z.number().int().nonnegative(),
  deletion_overdue_count_at_declared:     z.number().int().nonnegative(),
  review_overdue_count_at_restored:       z.number().int().nonnegative(),
  max_duration_breach_count_at_restored:  z.number().int().nonnegative(),
  deletion_overdue_count_at_restored:     z.number().int().nonnegative(),
});
```

**Emitter assignments:**
- `system.litigation_hold_monitor_stale_declared` — compliance-officer (manual, PAM-elevated) via `emit-audit-event` Worker at R-45 T+0. Emitted once per R-45 activation on receipt of `system.cron_job_stale` PagerDuty page for `job_name = 'litigation_hold_compliance_monitor'`, or on manual discovery. Not automated — requires IC human judgment on P0 vs P1 severity classification (§R-45.2: P0 if `max_duration_breach_count_at_declared > 0`; P1 otherwise). Registered per R-45.14 item 1 (INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-45 v1.0, 2026-06-25).
- `system.litigation_hold_monitor_restored` — compliance-officer (manual, PAM-elevated) via `emit-audit-event` Worker at R-45 Step 6 after job 45 confirmed healthy and C2a/C2b/C2c re-run passes. Carries six breach-count integer self-attestations (human-verified after R-45 Steps 5–6 breach sweep and escalation paths). No automated path. Registered per R-45.14 item 1 (INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-45 v1.0, 2026-06-25).

**Alert routing:**
- `system.litigation_hold_monitor_stale_declared` → no additional PagerDuty alert on emission (the `system.cron_job_stale` PagerDuty P1 `form-compliance` page already fired from `pg-cron-health-monitor` with dedup key `litigation-hold-monitor-stale`); DEC-030 event serves as the tamper-evident IC response anchor in the HMAC chain; opens the LITH-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 chain.
- `system.litigation_hold_monitor_restored` → resolves PagerDuty P1/P0 `litigation-hold-monitor-stale` alert; no separate alert on restoration; closes the LITH-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 chain. If any `*_count_at_declared > 0`: compliance-officer must confirm all affected holds have been actioned via §R-45.5/R-45.6/R-45.7 escalation paths per R-45 Step 5 post-incident controls.

**LITH-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 — Ordering invariant (R-45 monitoring-control chain):** `system.litigation_hold_monitor_restored` is blocked (HTTP 422 `LITH_MONITOR_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION`) if no prior `system.litigation_hold_monitor_stale_declared` exists for the same `incident_id`; violation → R-05 activated. Privacy floor (both R-45 events): all payload fields are integer counts, timestamps, and enum values — no `tenant_id`, no employee `user_id`, no health values, no GDPR Art. 9 special-category data in either chain event. The three zero-values at declaration (`review_overdue_count_at_declared = 0`, `max_duration_breach_count_at_declared = 0`, `deletion_overdue_count_at_declared = 0`) together constitute the auditor-inspectable attestation that no CC5.3/C1.1/C1.2 obligation breach occurred undetected during the stale window. Affected hold details live only in per-path stale-window exception notes under `compliance/evidence/litigation-hold/*/r45-<incident_id>/` (access: compliance_reviewer; `form_api` REVOKED per §80.3). Registered per R-45.14 item 1 (INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-45 v1.0, 2026-06-25 — [x] Done 2026-06-25, AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.46).

**CC5.3 auditor narrative:** CC5.3 requires FORM to deploy controls addressing monitored risks. R-45 demonstrates that FORM's three-layer structural controls for litigation hold compliance (DDL CHECK constraints via DATA_MODEL §46, automated pg_cron monitoring via job 45, compliance-officer Admin Console PAM workflow) include an IC protocol for when the automated layer fails. The `system.litigation_hold_monitor_stale_declared` payload with three zero-values (`review_overdue_count_at_declared = 0`, `max_duration_breach_count_at_declared = 0`, `deletion_overdue_count_at_declared = 0`) provides an auditor-inspectable declaration that no CC5.3 obligation breach occurred undetected during the stale window.

**C1.1 auditor narrative:** C1.1 requires FORM to collect data consistent with its commitments. R-45 §R-45.6 (LITH-SLO-02) is the IC response to the MSA §11.6.4 36-month cap. `max_duration_breach_count_at_declared = 0` in the `stale_declared` event is the auditor-inspectable attestation that no hold has exceeded 36 months undetected during the stale window. If `max_duration_breach_count_at_declared > 0`, the unconditional P0 escalation path (§R-45.6 — founder page, CSM notification, legal determination) provides the compensating control evidence.

**C1.2 auditor narrative:** C1.2 requires FORM to dispose of confidential information per contractual obligations. R-45 §R-45.7 (LITH-SLO-03) is the IC response to the MSA §11.6.6 10-business-day deletion obligation. `deletion_overdue_count_at_declared = 0` confirms no C1.2 breach occurred undetected during the stale window. Combined with LITH-E-001 (DATA_MODEL §46.7), auditors have two independent C1.2 evidence paths for the litigation hold lifecycle.

**CC7.2 auditor narrative:** `pg-cron-health-monitor` (§12.7) detects any gap exceeding 26h in job 45 runs and pages compliance-officer via PagerDuty P1 `form-compliance` with dedup key `litigation-hold-monitor-stale`. The DEC-030 HMAC-chained event pair (`stale_declared` HIGH/7yr → `restored` STANDARD/3yr) anchors the stale window in the tamper-evident audit record with all six breach counts (at declaration and restoration) preserved in both event payloads — independently verifiable per HMAC-VERIFY-ALGO-001. Annual artefact LITH-OBS-E-001 (CC5.3/CC4.1/C1.2, annual, 3yr — `compliance/evidence/litigation-hold/LITH-OBS-E-001_<YYYY>.md`) includes R-45 activation sections per OBSERVABILITY §54.8.

---

### Enterprise Pricing Exception Monitoring events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · OBSERVABILITY §55 · INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-46 · CC5.2/CC1.4/CC4.1/CC7.1/CC7.2)

> Defined in `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §55.7` (OBSERVABILITY v5.2.3, 2026-06-25 — §55 Pricing Exception Compliance Observability). Three DEC-030 HMAC-chained events covering the pg_cron job 46 (`pricing_exception_compliance_monitor`, `0 9 * * *`, 26h freshness window) monitoring-layer lifecycle. Job 46 is the **monitoring complement** to the write-path REENTRY-CHAIN-01 Worker enforcement (COST_MODEL §44.5; HTTP 422 `REENTRY_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` on violation): it sweeps `audit_log_events` daily to verify no REENTRY-CHAIN-01 bypass occurred undetected, and triggers the quarterly pricing exception audit obligation formalised in COST_MODEL §44.7. **REENTRY-MONITOR-CHAIN-01 (monitoring-layer ordering invariant):** `enterprise.reentry_chain_integrity_violation` CRITICAL DEC-030 event must receive HTTP 200 from `emit-audit-event` Worker **before** AL-PRICE-01 PagerDuty P0 fires for the same sweep run; if non-200, PagerDuty is suppressed and `system.cron_job_stale` HIGH from `pg-cron-health-monitor` (§12.6) handles monitoring degradation independently. No HTTP 422 is emitted by this invariant — 422 is the write-path REENTRY-CHAIN-01 enforcement per COST_MODEL §44.5. **Privacy floor (all three events):** `enterprise.reentry_chain_integrity_violation` carries `tenant_id` UUID (FORM-internal) + `renewed_event_id` UUID + `renewal_date` DATE + `check_run_at` timestamp only — no employee `user_id`, name, email, health value, GDPR Art. 9 special-category data; `system.pricing_exception_quarterly_audit_triggered` and `system.pricing_exception_check_passed` carry aggregate fleet counts only — no `tenant_id`, `deal_id`, or `approver_user_id`. **Closes `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §55.10` item 1 (P0/M9 — register three events + REENTRY-MONITOR-CHAIN-01 + Zod schemas; 🟢 Done 2026-06-25, AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.47).** Cross-ref: `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §55` (AL-PRICE-01/02/03 alert rules; PRICE-SLO-01/02/03; PRICE-OBS-E-001/002 evidence artefacts; job 46 full spec §55.5; Zod schemas §55.7.2; REENTRY-MONITOR-CHAIN-01 invariant §55.5.5); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §44.5` (REENTRY-CHAIN-01 write-path Worker enforcement — job 46 is the monitoring-layer complement); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §44.7` (quarterly pricing exception audit obligation — formalised by job 46 sweep 2); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §31.8` (four pricing governance DEC-030 events including `enterprise.pricing_exception_approved`); `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-46` (job 46 stale recovery runbook — §R-46.5 REENTRY-CHAIN-01 violation escalation; §R-46.6 quarterly trigger miss path); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §12.6` (job 46 registry entry — v1.9 patch, 2026-06-25); DEC-030.

| Event type | Severity | Retention | Trigger | Key payload fields |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `enterprise.reentry_chain_integrity_violation` | CRITICAL | 7 yr | job 46 sweep 1 via `emit-audit-event` Worker when `enterprise.contract_renewed` with `contract_discount_type = 'loyalty_reentry'` found in last 25h without a matching `enterprise.pricing_exception_approved` (`exception_type = 'loyalty_reentry'`, same `tenant_id`) within prior 30 days — REENTRY-CHAIN-01 bypass detected (COST_MODEL §44.5); REENTRY-MONITOR-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant: DEC-030 HTTP 200 confirmed before AL-PRICE-01 PagerDuty P0 fires; no auto-resolve; IC response: INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-46 §R-46.5; dedup `reentry-chain-violation-{tenant_id}-{renewal_date_iso}` no-expiry; registered per §55.10 item 1 (OBSERVABILITY v5.2.3, 2026-06-25) | `tenant_id` (UUID — FORM-internal only; **no employee user_id, name, email, or GDPR Art. 9 data**), `renewed_event_id` (UUID — FK to the `enterprise.contract_renewed` event in `audit_log_events`), `renewal_date` (string `YYYY-MM-DD`), `check_run_at` (ISO 8601 UTC — job 46 sweep 1 run timestamp) |
| `system.pricing_exception_quarterly_audit_triggered` | STANDARD | 3 yr | job 46 sweep 2 via `emit-audit-event` Worker during first 7 days of Jan/Apr/Jul/Oct — aggregate count of `enterprise.pricing_exception_approved` events for the prior calendar quarter by `exception_type`; emits Slack `#compliance` advisory (P3, no PagerDuty) triggering compliance-officer PRICE-OBS-E-001 filing obligation within 5 business days (OBSERVABILITY §55.8); **no `tenant_id`** — aggregate fleet counts only; registered per §55.10 item 1 (OBSERVABILITY v5.2.3, 2026-06-25) | `quarter` (string regex `^\d{4}-Q[1-4]$` — e.g. `"2026-Q1"`), `event_count` (nonneg int — total `enterprise.pricing_exception_approved` events in the prior quarter), `loyalty_reentry_count` (nonneg int), `standard_discount_count` (nonneg int), `floor_exception_count` (nonneg int), `pilot_credit_count` (nonneg int), `check_run_at` (ISO 8601 UTC) |
| `system.pricing_exception_check_passed` | LOW | 1 yr | job 46 all-clear after both sweeps complete without violation — positive baseline signal for AL-PRICE-03 26h freshness monitoring; emitted unconditionally on every successful run (regardless of whether sweep 2 triggered); suppressed only on `emit-audit-event` Worker non-200 response (REENTRY-MONITOR-CHAIN-01 violation path); **no `tenant_id`** — aggregate only; registered per §55.10 item 1 (OBSERVABILITY v5.2.3, 2026-06-25) | `loyalty_renewals_checked` (nonneg int — count of `enterprise.contract_renewed` events with `loyalty_reentry` discount examined by sweep 1 in the 25h window), `violations_found` (nonneg int — **must be 0** when emitted; if sweep 1 found ≥ 1 violation, `enterprise.reentry_chain_integrity_violation` CRITICAL is emitted first and check-passed is suppressed for that run), `quarterly_trigger_fired` (boolean — `true` if sweep 2 fired `system.pricing_exception_quarterly_audit_triggered` this run), `check_run_at` (ISO 8601 UTC) |

**Zod v2 schemas (canonical source: `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §55.7.2` — update OBSERVABILITY.md first on any schema change):**

```typescript
import { z } from 'zod/v4';

// enterprise.reentry_chain_integrity_violation — CRITICAL · 7 yr (registered v2.47)
// job 46 sweep 1 monitoring-layer sentinel — NOT a write-path enforcement event
// REENTRY-MONITOR-CHAIN-01: DEC-030 HTTP 200 confirmed before AL-PRICE-01 PagerDuty P0
// Privacy floor: tenant_id UUID only — no employee user_id, name, email, GDPR Art. 9 data
export const ReentryChainIntegrityViolationPayload = z.object({
  tenant_id:        z.string().uuid(),     // FORM-internal UUID only — no PII
  renewed_event_id: z.string().uuid(),     // audit_log_events FK for the offending contract_renewed event
  renewal_date:     z.string().date(),     // YYYY-MM-DD
  check_run_at:     z.string().datetime(), // ISO 8601 UTC — job 46 sweep 1 run timestamp
});

// system.pricing_exception_quarterly_audit_triggered — STANDARD · 3 yr (registered v2.47)
// job 46 sweep 2 — first 7 days of Jan/Apr/Jul/Oct only
// Privacy floor: aggregate fleet counts only — no tenant_id, deal_id, approver_user_id
export const PricingExceptionQuarterlyAuditTriggeredPayload = z.object({
  quarter:                 z.string().regex(/^\d{4}-Q[1-4]$/),  // e.g. "2026-Q1"
  event_count:             z.number().int().nonnegative(),       // total enterprise.pricing_exception_approved in quarter
  loyalty_reentry_count:   z.number().int().nonnegative(),
  standard_discount_count: z.number().int().nonnegative(),
  floor_exception_count:   z.number().int().nonnegative(),
  pilot_credit_count:      z.number().int().nonnegative(),
  check_run_at:            z.string().datetime(),
});

// system.pricing_exception_check_passed — LOW · 1 yr (registered v2.47)
// job 46 all-clear — emitted after both sweeps complete without violation
// Privacy floor: aggregate counts only — no tenant_id
export const PricingExceptionCheckPassedPayload = z.object({
  loyalty_renewals_checked: z.number().int().nonnegative(), // contract_renewed loyalty_reentry events examined in last 25h
  violations_found:         z.number().int().nonnegative(), // must be 0 when this event is emitted
  quarterly_trigger_fired:  z.boolean(),                    // true if sweep 2 fired this run
  check_run_at:             z.string().datetime(),
});
```

**Emitter assignments:**
- `enterprise.reentry_chain_integrity_violation` — `form_system` role via pg_cron job 46 (`pricing_exception_compliance_monitor`) → `emit-audit-event` Worker (pg_net HTTP POST). Emitted at most once per violation per run; dedup key `reentry-chain-violation-{tenant_id}-{renewal_date_iso}` ensures idempotency on job retry. No human emission path — monitoring-layer automation only. `form_api` REVOKED from `audit_log_events`.
- `system.pricing_exception_quarterly_audit_triggered` — `form_system` role via job 46 sweep 2 on the first 7 days of each calendar quarter. Triggers compliance-officer PRICE-OBS-E-001 filing obligation (OBSERVABILITY §55.8). Not emitted outside the quarter-boundary window. `form_api` REVOKED from `audit_log_events`.
- `system.pricing_exception_check_passed` — `form_system` role via job 46 on every successful run after both sweeps complete. Emitted unconditionally when no violation found — positive baseline signal that powers AL-PRICE-03 26h dead-man's switch freshness check (OBSERVABILITY §55.4). Suppressed only on Worker emit failure (REENTRY-MONITOR-CHAIN-01 non-200 path). `form_api` REVOKED from `audit_log_events`.

**Alert routing:**
- `enterprise.reentry_chain_integrity_violation` → AL-PRICE-01 PagerDuty P0 `form-compliance` (founder + compliance-officer + enterprise-architect; no dedup; no auto-resolve); per REENTRY-MONITOR-CHAIN-01, PagerDuty dispatch is **gated** on DEC-030 HTTP 200 from `emit-audit-event` Worker — if non-200, PagerDuty is suppressed for this run and `system.cron_job_stale` HIGH from `pg-cron-health-monitor` handles monitoring degradation. IC response: INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-46 §R-46.5 (forensic chain investigation; retroactive exception event issuance process).
- `system.pricing_exception_quarterly_audit_triggered` → Slack `#compliance` advisory (P3; no PagerDuty). Advisory-only signal that triggers compliance-officer PRICE-OBS-E-001 filing obligation. Not an incident.
- `system.pricing_exception_check_passed` → no alert on emission. Gap of > 26h between consecutive emissions triggers AL-PRICE-03 PagerDuty P1 `form-devops` → devops-lead (dedup `pricing-exception-check-stale`; per OBSERVABILITY §55.4). Recovery runbook: INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-46.

**REENTRY-MONITOR-CHAIN-01 — Ordering invariant (job 46 monitoring-control layer):** Per OBSERVABILITY §55.5.5, for each sweep 1 REENTRY-CHAIN-01 violation detected by job 46: (1) `enterprise.reentry_chain_integrity_violation` CRITICAL/7yr is emitted via `emit-audit-event` Worker and HTTP 200 must be confirmed; (2) only after HTTP 200 is received does job 46 dispatch AL-PRICE-01 PagerDuty P0. If the Worker returns non-200: PagerDuty P0 is suppressed for that run cycle; `system.cron_job_stale` HIGH from `pg-cron-health-monitor` (§12.6) handles the resulting monitoring-layer degradation independently. **No HTTP 422 is emitted by this invariant** — HTTP 422 (`REENTRY_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION`) is the write-path enforcement per COST_MODEL §44.5 and DATA_MODEL §45.6; this invariant governs the monitoring-layer path only. Privacy floor (all three events): `enterprise.reentry_chain_integrity_violation` payload carries `tenant_id` UUID + `renewed_event_id` UUID + `renewal_date` DATE + `check_run_at` only — no employee name, email, coaching content, or GDPR Art. 9 data; `system.pricing_exception_quarterly_audit_triggered` and `system.pricing_exception_check_passed` carry only aggregate fleet-level integer counts and timestamps. Registered per §55.10 item 1 (OBSERVABILITY v5.2.3, 2026-06-25 — [x] Done 2026-06-25, AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.47).

**CC5.2 auditor narrative:** CC5.2 requires FORM to enforce its pricing commitments and hold management accountable. `enterprise.reentry_chain_integrity_violation` CRITICAL/7yr is the DEC-030 evidence that FORM's daily monitoring of REENTRY-CHAIN-01 (COST_MODEL §44.5) detected a pricing commitment breach — a loyalty re-entry contract renewal that lacked the required prior `enterprise.pricing_exception_approved` authorisation. Every detected violation creates a HMAC-chained, tamper-evident record with the specific `tenant_id` UUID and `renewed_event_id` — independently verifiable per HMAC-VERIFY-ALGO-001. PRICE-OBS-E-001 (quarterly, 7yr — OBSERVABILITY §55.8) attests that no violations occurred during the observation window, or documents resolution of any that did. Auditors have two independent CC5.2 evidence paths: the per-event DEC-030 chain record and the quarterly compliance-officer attestation.

**CC1.4 auditor narrative:** CC1.4 requires FORM to communicate its pricing commitments clearly and hold operations accountable. `system.pricing_exception_quarterly_audit_triggered` STANDARD/3yr is the DEC-030 record that the quarterly pricing exception audit obligation (COST_MODEL §44.7) was triggered on schedule — within the first 7 days of each calendar quarter. PRICE-SLO-02 (100% quarterly trigger cadence — OBSERVABILITY §55.3) and PRICE-OBS-E-001 (compliance-officer attestation within 5 business days) together provide the evidence chain. Auditors can verify quarterly trigger cadence from `audit_log_events WHERE event_type = 'system.pricing_exception_quarterly_audit_triggered' AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM created_at) = <YYYY>` — four rows required per year.

**CC4.1 auditor narrative:** CC4.1 requires FORM to implement monitoring activities to evaluate controls and exceptions. `system.pricing_exception_check_passed` LOW/1yr is the operational all-clear signal that job 46 completed both sweeps without finding a REENTRY-CHAIN-01 violation. The 26h freshness window enforced by `pg-cron-health-monitor` (PRICE-SLO-01 — OBSERVABILITY §55.3) ensures any gap in monitoring coverage triggers AL-PRICE-03 PagerDuty P1 detection. PRICE-OBS-E-002 (annual, 3yr — OBSERVABILITY §55.8) documents the monitoring infrastructure run history for the year. Auditors have two independent CC4.1 evidence paths: continuous monitoring health (PRICE-OBS-E-002) and compliance outcomes (PRICE-OBS-E-001).

---

**R-46 monitoring-control events (PRICING-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01):** Two additional events anchor the R-46 incident lifecycle when job 46 itself goes stale — distinct from `enterprise.reentry_chain_integrity_violation` (which job 46 emits WHEN RUNNING to signal active REENTRY-CHAIN-01 breaches). These events are emitted by the IC (Incident Commander), not by automated processes. **PRICING-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant:** `system.pricing_exception_monitor_restored` MUST follow `system.pricing_exception_monitor_stale_declared` for the same `incident_id`; the `emit-audit-event` Worker returns HTTP 422 `PRICING_MONITOR_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` on ordering inversion → R-05 activated. **Privacy floor (both events):** Integer counts, booleans, timestamps, and enum values only — no `tenant_id`, no employee `user_id`, no health data, no GDPR Art. 9 special-category data. Cross-ref: `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-46` (full runbook — §R-46.9 canonical Zod schemas; §R-46.13 item 1 closes this registration); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §55.5` (job 46 spec — REENTRY-MONITOR-CHAIN-01 invariant; AL-PRICE-03 26h dead-man's switch freshness window); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §12.6` (job 46 `pricing_exception_compliance_monitor` pg_cron registry); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §44.5` (REENTRY-CHAIN-01 write-path enforcement — job 46 is the monitoring-layer complement); DEC-030. **Closes `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-46.13` item 1 (P0/M9 — register both events + PRICING-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 + Zod schemas; 🟢 Done 2026-06-26, AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.48).**

| Event type | Severity | Retention | Trigger | Key payload fields |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `system.pricing_exception_monitor_stale_declared` | HIGH | 7 yr | IC-emitted at R-46 T+0 when `pg-cron-health-monitor` (§12.6) pages with `job_name = 'pricing_exception_compliance_monitor'` or manual discovery confirms job 46 exceeds 26h freshness window; emitter: devops-lead (manual, PAM-elevated, `emit-audit-event` Worker); PRICING-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 prerequisite — must precede `system.pricing_exception_monitor_restored` for same `incident_id`; `initial_severity = 'P0'` immediately if `reentry_violations_found_during_stale ≥ 1` (R-46-C2 sweep confirms active REENTRY-CHAIN-01 breach during stale window); registered per R-46.13 item 1 (INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-46 v1.0, 2026-06-25) | `incident_id` (UUID — PRICING-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 anchor), `confirmed_stale_since` (ISO 8601 UTC — last successful job 46 run), `stale_hours` (positive float), `missed_runs` (nonneg int), `trigger` (enum: `pagerduty_alert`\|`manual_discovery`\|`co_active_r03`), `initial_severity` (enum: `P1`\|`P0` — P0 if `reentry_violations_found_during_stale ≥ 1`), `stale_window_overlaps_quarter_start` (bool — `true` if stale window intersects first 7 days of Jan/Apr/Jul/Oct — PRICE-SLO-02 gap flag), `reentry_violations_found_during_stale` (nonneg int — R-46-C2 count at declaration; **0 = auditor-inspectable attestation that no REENTRY-CHAIN-01 breach occurred undetected during stale window**) |
| `system.pricing_exception_monitor_restored` | STANDARD | 3 yr | IC-emitted at R-46 Step 6 when job 46 confirmed running after restoration and R-46-C2 re-run passes (zero active REENTRY-CHAIN-01 violations or all violations actioned per §R-46.5 stale-window note); PRICING-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 terminal event; emitter: devops-lead (manual, PAM-elevated, `emit-audit-event` Worker); PagerDuty P1/P0 `pricing-exception-check-stale` resolved on emission; registered per R-46.13 item 1 (INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-46 v1.0, 2026-06-25) | `incident_id` (UUID — must match `stale_declared`), `restored_at` (ISO 8601 UTC), `root_cause` (enum: `H1_job_deleted`\|`H2_form_system_permission_revoked`\|`H3_pg_net_degraded`\|`H4_supabase_outage`), `fix_deployed_at` (ISO 8601 UTC), `stale_window_hours` (positive float), `stale_window_overlaps_quarter_start` (bool — must match `stale_declared` value for same `incident_id`), `reentry_violations_found_during_stale` (nonneg int — must match `stale_declared` value), `reentry_violations_at_restored` (nonneg int — R-46-C2 re-run count at restoration; **must be 0 or all violations documented as actioned in §R-46.5 stale-window note before PagerDuty P0 resolution**), `quarterly_trigger_manually_fired` (bool — `true` if §R-46.6 compensating control was activated because stale window overlapped quarter-start trigger window) |

**Zod v2 schemas (canonical source: `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-46.9` — update INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md first on any schema change):**

```typescript
// system.pricing_exception_monitor_stale_declared — HIGH · 7 yr · CC5.2/CC1.4/CC4.1/CC7.1/CC7.2 · PRICING-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 anchor
const PricingExceptionMonitorStaleDeclaredSchema = z.object({
  incident_id:                           z.string().uuid(),
  confirmed_stale_since:                 z.string().datetime(),
  stale_hours:                           z.number().positive(),
  missed_runs:                           z.number().int().nonnegative(),
  trigger:                               z.enum(['pagerduty_alert', 'manual_discovery', 'co_active_r03']),
  initial_severity:                      z.enum(['P1', 'P0']),
  stale_window_overlaps_quarter_start:   z.boolean(),
  reentry_violations_found_during_stale: z.number().int().nonnegative(),
});

// system.pricing_exception_monitor_restored — STANDARD · 3 yr · CC7.2 · PRICING-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 terminal
const PricingExceptionMonitorRestoredSchema = z.object({
  incident_id:                           z.string().uuid(),
  restored_at:                           z.string().datetime(),
  root_cause:                            z.enum([
    'H1_job_deleted',
    'H2_form_system_permission_revoked',
    'H3_pg_net_degraded',
    'H4_supabase_outage',
  ]),
  fix_deployed_at:                       z.string().datetime(),
  stale_window_hours:                    z.number().positive(),
  stale_window_overlaps_quarter_start:   z.boolean(),
  reentry_violations_found_during_stale: z.number().int().nonnegative(),
  reentry_violations_at_restored:        z.number().int().nonnegative(),
  quarterly_trigger_manually_fired:      z.boolean(),
});
```

**Emitter assignments:**
- `system.pricing_exception_monitor_stale_declared` — devops-lead (IC, manual, PAM-elevated) via `emit-audit-event` Worker at R-46 T+0. Emitted once per R-46 activation on receipt of `system.cron_job_stale` PagerDuty page for `job_name = 'pricing_exception_compliance_monitor'`, or on manual discovery. Not automated — requires IC human judgment on P0 vs P1 severity classification (P0 if R-46-C2 sweep detects `reentry_violations_found_during_stale ≥ 1`; P1 otherwise per R-46.2). Registered per R-46.13 item 1 (INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-46 v1.0, 2026-06-25).
- `system.pricing_exception_monitor_restored` — devops-lead (IC, manual, PAM-elevated) at R-46 Step 6 after job 46 confirmed healthy in `pg_cron.job_run_details` and R-46-C2 re-run returns zero violations (or all violations actioned per §R-46.5 stale-window note). Registered per R-46.13 item 1 (INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-46 v1.0, 2026-06-25).

**Alert routing:**
- `system.pricing_exception_monitor_stale_declared` → no additional PagerDuty alert on emission (the `system.cron_job_stale` PagerDuty P1 `form-devops` alert already fired from `pg-cron-health-monitor` with dedup key `pricing-exception-check-stale`; AL-PRICE-03 per OBSERVABILITY §55.4); DEC-030 event serves as the tamper-evident IC response anchor in the HMAC chain; opens the PRICING-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 chain.
- `system.pricing_exception_monitor_restored` → resolves PagerDuty P1/P0 `pricing-exception-check-stale` alert; no separate alert on restoration; closes the PRICING-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 chain. If `reentry_violations_found_during_stale > 0` at declaration: devops-lead must confirm all violations have been investigated and actioned per §R-46.5 before PagerDuty P0 resolution (`reentry_violations_at_restored = 0` or all violations documented in stale-window note required).

**PRICING-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 — Ordering invariant (R-46 monitoring-control chain):** `system.pricing_exception_monitor_restored` is blocked (HTTP 422 `PRICING_MONITOR_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION`) if no prior `system.pricing_exception_monitor_stale_declared` exists for the same `incident_id`; violation → R-05 activated. Privacy floor (both R-46 events): all payload fields are integer counts, booleans, timestamps, and enum values — no `tenant_id`, no employee `user_id`, no health values, no GDPR Art. 9 special-category data in either chain event. `reentry_violations_found_during_stale = 0` in `stale_declared` is the auditor-inspectable attestation that no REENTRY-CHAIN-01 breach occurred undetected during the job 46 stale window (CC5.2/CC1.4). `reentry_violations_at_restored = 0` (or all positives documented as actioned in the §R-46.5 stale-window note) is required before resolving PagerDuty P0. `quarterly_trigger_manually_fired` is the CC4.1 attestation that PRICE-SLO-02 was satisfied via compensating control when the automated quarterly trigger missed during the stale window (§R-46.6). Registered per R-46.13 item 1 (INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-46 v1.0, 2026-06-25 — [x] Done 2026-06-26, AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.48).

**CC5.2 + CC1.4 auditor narrative (R-46 stale events):** CC5.2 requires FORM to enforce its pricing commitments. CC1.4 requires accountability for pricing exception approvals. `system.pricing_exception_monitor_stale_declared` with `reentry_violations_found_during_stale = 0` provides the auditor-inspectable DEC-030 declaration that no REENTRY-CHAIN-01 pricing commitment bypass occurred undetected during the job 46 stale window. The `stale_window_overlaps_quarter_start` flag documents whether PRICE-SLO-02 (quarterly pricing exception audit trigger — COST_MODEL §44.7) was at risk of being missed; `quarterly_trigger_manually_fired = true` in the paired `stale_restored` event confirms the CC1.4 compensating control per §R-46.6 was activated. Together with PRICE-OBS-E-001 (quarterly compliance outcome — OBSERVABILITY §55.8, 7yr) and PRICE-STALE-E-001 (annual stale-incident artefact — INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-46.13 item 4, pending), auditors have three independent CC5.2/CC1.4 evidence pathways.

**CC7.1 + CC7.2 auditor narrative (R-46 stale events):** CC7.2 requires FORM to monitor its system components. `pg-cron-health-monitor` (OBSERVABILITY §12.6) detects job 46 staleness and emits `system.cron_job_stale` HIGH (AL-PRICE-03), which fires PagerDuty P1 `form-devops`. R-46 activates. CC7.1 requires defined procedures for responding to monitoring system failures — the five-query structured IC response (R-46-C1 staleness confirmation, R-46-C2 REENTRY-CHAIN-01 gate, R-46-C3 quarterly trigger gate, R-46-C4 H4 peer discriminator, R-46-C5 H1 job registration) demonstrates monitoring-failure response independent of the breaches that system monitors. The DEC-030 HMAC chain (`stale_declared` → `stale_restored`) provides the tamper-evident detection-to-restoration timeline independently verifiable per HMAC-VERIFY-ALGO-001 (`compliance/docs/hmac-chain-verification-algorithm.md`).

---

### SCIM Provisioning Compliance Monitoring events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · OBSERVABILITY §56 · CC6.4/CC4.1/CC7.2/A1.1)

> Defined in `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §56.7` (OBSERVABILITY v5.3.0, 2026-06-26 — §56 SCIM Provisioning Compliance Observability). Three DEC-030 HMAC-chained events covering the pg_cron job 47 (`scim_provisioning_compliance_monitor`, `0 6 * * *`, 26h freshness window) monitoring-layer lifecycle. Job 47 is the **proactive daily baseline sentinel** complementing the reactive burst-detection alert AL-SCIM-01 (SSO_SCIM §26.7b): it sweeps `audit_log_events` daily for any `scim.rejected_sensitive_attribute` event (GDPR Art. 9 sensitive health-data attribute blocked at the SCIM write boundary per SSO_SCIM §14), and triggers the quarterly SCIM-PROV-E-003 zero-count assertion obligation formalised in SOC2_READINESS §119. **SCIM-ATTR-CHAIN-01 (monitoring-layer ordering invariant):** `security.scim_sensitive_attr_violation_detected` CRITICAL DEC-030 event must receive HTTP 200 from `emit-audit-event` Worker **before** AL-SCIM-PROV-01 PagerDuty P0 fires for the same sweep run; if non-200, PagerDuty is suppressed and `system.cron_job_stale` HIGH from `pg-cron-health-monitor` (§12.6) handles monitoring degradation independently. **Privacy floor (all three events):** `security.scim_sensitive_attr_violation_detected` carries only aggregate `violation_count` int + three timestamps — no `tenant_id`, no rejected attribute VALUE, no `user_id`, no employee name, email, health value, or GDPR Art. 9 data; `system.scim_provisioning_quarterly_check_triggered` and `system.scim_provisioning_check_passed` carry aggregate counts only — no `tenant_id`. **Closes `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §56.10` item 1 (P0/M5 — register three events + SCIM-ATTR-CHAIN-01 + Zod schemas; 🟢 Done 2026-06-26, AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.51).** Cross-ref: `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §56` (AL-SCIM-PROV-01/02 alert rules; SCIM-PROV-SLO-01/02; SCIM-PROV-MON-E-001 evidence artefact; job 47 full spec §56.5; Zod schemas §56.7.2; SCIM-ATTR-CHAIN-01 invariant §56.5.5); `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §27.12` (SCIM-PROV-E-001..004 lifecycle evidence artefacts; `scim.rejected_sensitive_attribute` source events); `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §26.7b` (AL-SCIM-01 reactive burst complement — detects > 3 events per tenant per 1h; job 47 detects any single event fleet-wide in 26h); `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §119` (SCIM-PROV-E-001..004 lifecycle evidence registration); `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §120` (SCIM-PROV-MON-E-001 monitoring infrastructure evidence — pending §56.10 item 5 P1/M6); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §12.6` (job 47 pg_cron registry — v2.1 patch, 2026-06-26); `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-47` (job 47 stale recovery runbook — v1.0, 2026-06-26); DEC-030.

| Event type | Severity | Retention | Trigger | Key payload fields |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `security.scim_sensitive_attr_violation_detected` | CRITICAL | 7 yr | job 47 sweep 1 via `emit-audit-event` Worker when `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM audit_log_events WHERE event_type = 'scim.rejected_sensitive_attribute' AND created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '26 hours'` returns count > 0 — any GDPR Art. 9 sensitive attribute reached the SCIM write boundary (SSO_SCIM §14 `BLOCKED_ATTRIBUTE_PATTERNS` blocklist fired); SCIM-ATTR-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant: DEC-030 HTTP 200 confirmed before AL-SCIM-PROV-01 PagerDuty P0 fires; dedup `scim-prov-sensitive-attr-{YYYY-MM-DD}` 24h; no auto-resolve — IC forensic investigation + IdP attribute mapping remediation required (INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-47 §R-47.5 — pending §56.10 item 6 P1/M7); SCIM-PROV-SLO-01 breach (zero-tolerance); registered v2.51 | `violation_count` (positive int — `COUNT(*)` of `scim.rejected_sensitive_attribute` events in 26h window; **no `tenant_id`, no rejected attribute VALUE, no `user_id` or employee PII**), `window_start` (ISO 8601 UTC — `NOW() - INTERVAL '26 hours'` at sweep 1 run), `window_end` (ISO 8601 UTC — `NOW()` at sweep 1 run), `check_run_at` (ISO 8601 UTC — job 47 sweep 1 run timestamp) |
| `system.scim_provisioning_quarterly_check_triggered` | STANDARD | 3 yr | job 47 sweep 2 via `emit-audit-event` Worker during first 7 days of Jan/Apr/Jul/Oct — aggregate count of `scim.rejected_sensitive_attribute` events for the prior calendar quarter; emits Slack `#compliance` advisory (P3, no PagerDuty) triggering compliance-officer SCIM-PROV-E-003 quarterly zero-count assertion filing obligation (SOC2_READINESS §119); **no `tenant_id`** — aggregate fleet counts only; SCIM-PROV-SLO-02 trigger confirmation; registered v2.51 | `quarter_label` (string regex `^\d{4}-Q[1-4]$` — e.g. `"2026-Q1"`), `quarterly_rejection_count` (nonneg int — aggregate `scim.rejected_sensitive_attribute` count for prior quarter; **zero = CC6.4 clean; > 0 requires linked incident report per SCIM-PROV-E-003**), `quarter_start` (ISO 8601 date string — `YYYY-MM-DD`), `quarter_end` (ISO 8601 date string — `YYYY-MM-DD`), `check_run_at` (ISO 8601 UTC) |
| `system.scim_provisioning_check_passed` | LOW | 1 yr | job 47 all-clear after all sweeps complete without violation — positive baseline signal for AL-SCIM-PROV-02 26h freshness dead-man's switch monitoring (SCIM-PROV-SLO-02); emitted unconditionally on every successful job 47 run (regardless of whether sweep 2 triggered); suppressed only on `emit-audit-event` Worker non-200 response (SCIM-ATTR-CHAIN-01 violation path — PagerDuty also suppressed in that path); **no `tenant_id`** — aggregate only; registered v2.51 | `violations_found` (nonneg int — **must be 0** when emitted; if sweep 1 found ≥ 1 violation, `security.scim_sensitive_attr_violation_detected` CRITICAL is emitted first and check-passed is suppressed for that run), `quarterly_trigger_fired` (boolean — `true` if sweep 2 fired `system.scim_provisioning_quarterly_check_triggered` this run), `check_run_at` (ISO 8601 UTC) |

**Zod v2 schemas (canonical source: `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §56.7.2` — update OBSERVABILITY.md first on any schema change):**

```typescript
import { z } from 'zod/v4';

// security.scim_sensitive_attr_violation_detected — CRITICAL · 7 yr (registered v2.51)
// job 47 sweep 1 monitoring-layer sentinel — NOT the write-path rejection event (scim.rejected_sensitive_attribute)
// SCIM-ATTR-CHAIN-01: DEC-030 HTTP 200 confirmed before AL-SCIM-PROV-01 PagerDuty P0
// Privacy floor: COUNT(*) + timestamps only — no tenant_id, no rejected VALUE, no user_id, no employee PII
export const ScimSensitiveAttrViolationDetectedPayload = z.object({
  violation_count: z.number().int().positive(),      // COUNT(*) — any positive value is a SCIM-PROV-SLO-01 breach
  window_start:    z.string().datetime(),            // NOW() - INTERVAL '26 hours' at sweep 1 run
  window_end:      z.string().datetime(),            // NOW() at sweep 1 run
  check_run_at:    z.string().datetime(),            // job 47 sweep 1 execution timestamp
});

// system.scim_provisioning_quarterly_check_triggered — STANDARD · 3 yr (registered v2.51)
// job 47 sweep 2 — fires only in first 7 days of Jan/Apr/Jul/Oct
// Privacy floor: aggregate quarterly count only — no tenant_id, no user_id, no GDPR Art. 9 data
export const ScimProvisioningQuarterlyCheckTriggeredPayload = z.object({
  quarter_label:             z.string().regex(/^\d{4}-Q[1-4]$/), // e.g. "2026-Q1"
  quarterly_rejection_count: z.number().int().nonnegative(),     // 0 = CC6.4 clean; > 0 requires SCIM-PROV-E-003 incident report
  quarter_start:             z.string().date(),                  // YYYY-MM-DD
  quarter_end:               z.string().date(),                  // YYYY-MM-DD
  check_run_at:              z.string().datetime(),
});

// system.scim_provisioning_check_passed — LOW · 1 yr (registered v2.51)
// job 47 all-clear — emitted after all sweeps complete without violation
// Privacy floor: aggregate counts only — no tenant_id, no user_id
export const ScimProvisioningCheckPassedPayload = z.object({
  violations_found:        z.number().int().nonnegative(), // must be 0 when emitted; > 0 suppresses this event
  quarterly_trigger_fired: z.boolean(),                    // true if sweep 2 fired this run
  check_run_at:            z.string().datetime(),
});
```

**Emitter assignments:**
- `security.scim_sensitive_attr_violation_detected` — `form_system` role via pg_cron job 47 (`scim_provisioning_compliance_monitor`) → `emit-audit-event` Worker (pg_net HTTP POST). Emitted at most once per sweep 1 run with count > 0; dedup key `scim-prov-sensitive-attr-{YYYY-MM-DD}` (24h) prevents duplicate P0 alerts on retry. No human emission path — monitoring-layer automation only. SCIM-ATTR-CHAIN-01 requires HTTP 200 before PagerDuty dispatch. `form_api` REVOKED from `audit_log_events`; job runs via `form_system` role.
- `system.scim_provisioning_quarterly_check_triggered` — `form_system` role via job 47 sweep 2 on the first 7 days of each calendar quarter. Triggers compliance-officer SCIM-PROV-E-003 quarterly zero-count assertion filing obligation (SOC2_READINESS §119). Not emitted outside the quarter-boundary window. `form_api` REVOKED from `audit_log_events`.
- `system.scim_provisioning_check_passed` — `form_system` role via job 47 on every successful run after all sweeps complete without violation. Emitted unconditionally when sweep 1 finds zero violations — positive baseline signal powering AL-SCIM-PROV-02 26h dead-man's switch freshness check (OBSERVABILITY §56.4). Suppressed only on Worker emit failure in the sweep 1 violation path (SCIM-ATTR-CHAIN-01 non-200). `form_api` REVOKED from `audit_log_events`.

**Alert routing:**
- `security.scim_sensitive_attr_violation_detected` → AL-SCIM-PROV-01 PagerDuty **P0** `form-security` (security-engineer + compliance-officer; no auto-resolve; dedup `scim-prov-sensitive-attr-{YYYY-MM-DD}` 24h); per SCIM-ATTR-CHAIN-01, PagerDuty dispatch is **gated** on DEC-030 HTTP 200 from `emit-audit-event` Worker — if non-200, PagerDuty is suppressed for this run and `system.cron_job_stale` HIGH from `pg-cron-health-monitor` (§12.6) handles monitoring degradation. IC response: INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-47 §R-47.5 (pending §56.10 item 6 P1/M7).
- `system.scim_provisioning_quarterly_check_triggered` → Slack `#compliance` advisory (P3; no PagerDuty). Advisory-only signal that triggers compliance-officer SCIM-PROV-E-003 quarterly filing obligation within 5 business days (SOC2_READINESS §119). Not an incident.
- `system.scim_provisioning_check_passed` → no alert on emission. Gap of > 26h between consecutive emissions triggers AL-SCIM-PROV-02 PagerDuty **P1** `form-devops` → devops-lead (dedup `scim-prov-check-stale`; auto-resolves on next successful `system.scim_provisioning_check_passed` emission; per OBSERVABILITY §56.4). Recovery runbook: INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-47 (pending §56.10 item 6 P1/M7).

**SCIM-ATTR-CHAIN-01 — Ordering invariant (job 47 monitoring-control layer):** Per OBSERVABILITY §56.5.5, for each sweep 1 violation detected by job 47: (1) `security.scim_sensitive_attr_violation_detected` CRITICAL/7yr is emitted via `emit-audit-event` Worker and HTTP 200 must be confirmed; (2) only after HTTP 200 is received does job 47 dispatch AL-SCIM-PROV-01 PagerDuty P0. If the Worker returns non-200: PagerDuty P0 is suppressed for that run cycle; `system.cron_job_stale` HIGH from `pg-cron-health-monitor` (§12.6) handles the resulting monitoring-layer degradation independently. **No HTTP 422 is emitted by this invariant** — HTTP 422 is reserved for write-path SCIM Worker enforcement at the SSO_SCIM §14 `BLOCKED_ATTRIBUTE_PATTERNS` boundary; this invariant governs the monitoring-layer path only. Privacy floor: `security.scim_sensitive_attr_violation_detected` payload carries aggregate `violation_count` int + `window_start`/`window_end`/`check_run_at` timestamps only — no `tenant_id`, no rejected attribute VALUE, no `user_id`, no employee name, email, or GDPR Art. 9 health data. `system.scim_provisioning_quarterly_check_triggered` and `system.scim_provisioning_check_passed` carry only aggregate fleet-level integer counts and timestamps. Registered per §56.10 item 1 (OBSERVABILITY v5.3.0, 2026-06-26 — [x] Done 2026-06-26, AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.51).

**R-47 monitoring-control events (SCIM-PROV-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01):** Two additional events anchor the R-47 incident lifecycle when job 47 itself goes stale — distinct from `security.scim_sensitive_attr_violation_detected` (which job 47 emits WHEN RUNNING to report active violations). These events are emitted by the IC (Incident Commander), not by automated processes. **SCIM-PROV-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant:** `system.scim_provisioning_monitor_restored` MUST follow `system.scim_provisioning_monitor_stale_declared` for the same `incident_id`; the `emit-audit-event` Worker returns HTTP 422 `SCIM_PROV_MONITOR_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` on ordering inversion → R-05 activated. **Privacy floor (both events):** Integer counts, booleans, timestamps, and enum values only — no `tenant_id`, no employee `user_id`, no rejected attribute VALUE, no GDPR Art. 9 special-category data. Cross-ref: `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-47` (full runbook — §R-47.9 canonical Zod schemas; §R-47.12 item 1 closes this registration); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §56` (job 47 spec — SCIM-ATTR-CHAIN-01 §56.5.5; AL-SCIM-PROV-02 26h freshness window §56.4); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §12.6` (job 47 registry); DEC-030. **Closes `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-47.12` item 1 (P0/M7 — register both events + SCIM-PROV-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 + Zod schemas; 🟢 Done 2026-06-26, AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.52). Structural peers: PRICING-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 (§Enterprise Pricing Exception Monitoring events — registered v2.48); LITH-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 (v2.46); OFFBOARD-CHAIN-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 (v2.44).**

| Event type | Severity | Retention | Trigger | Key payload fields |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `system.scim_provisioning_monitor_stale_declared` | HIGH | 7 yr | IC-emitted at R-47 T+0 when `pg-cron-health-monitor` (§12.6) pages with `job_name = 'scim_provisioning_compliance_monitor'` (AL-SCIM-PROV-02 dedup `scim-prov-check-stale`) or manual discovery confirms job 47 exceeds 26h freshness window; emitter: devops-lead (IC, manual, PAM-elevated, `emit-audit-event` Worker); SCIM-PROV-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 prerequisite — must precede `system.scim_provisioning_monitor_restored` for same `incident_id`; `initial_severity = 'P0'` immediately if `violations_found_during_stale ≥ 1` (R-47-C2 confirms `scim.rejected_sensitive_attribute` events during stale window — §R-47.5); registered v2.52 | `incident_id` (UUID — SCIM-PROV-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 anchor), `confirmed_stale_since` (ISO 8601 UTC), `stale_hours` (positive float), `missed_runs` (nonneg int), `trigger` (enum: `pagerduty_alert`\|`manual_discovery`\|`co_active_r03`), `initial_severity` (enum: `P1`\|`P0` — P0 if `violations_found_during_stale ≥ 1`), `stale_window_overlaps_quarter_start` (bool — SCIM-PROV-SLO-02 gap flag), `violations_found_during_stale` (nonneg int — **0 = CC6.4 auditor-inspectable attestation that no `scim.rejected_sensitive_attribute` events occurred undetected during stale window; > 0 → §R-47.5 forensic path required**) |
| `system.scim_provisioning_monitor_restored` | STANDARD | 3 yr | IC-emitted at R-47 Step 6 when job 47 confirmed running and R-47-C2 re-run returns zero violations (or all violations actioned per §R-47.5); SCIM-PROV-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 terminal event; emitter: devops-lead (IC, manual, PAM-elevated, `emit-audit-event` Worker); resolves PagerDuty P1/P0 `scim-prov-check-stale`; registered v2.52 | `incident_id` (UUID — must match `stale_declared`), `restored_at` (ISO 8601 UTC), `root_cause` (enum: `H1_job_deleted`\|`H2_form_system_permission_revoked`\|`H3_pg_net_degraded`\|`H4_supabase_outage`), `fix_deployed_at` (ISO 8601 UTC), `stale_window_hours` (positive float), `stale_window_overlaps_quarter_start` (bool — must match `stale_declared` value), `violations_found_during_stale` (nonneg int — must match `stale_declared` value), `violations_at_restored` (nonneg int — R-47-C2 re-run count at restoration; **0 required or all violations documented as actioned per §R-47.5 before P0 resolution**), `quarterly_trigger_manually_fired` (bool — `true` if §R-47.6 compensating control activated because stale window overlapped quarter-start trigger window) |

**Zod v2 schemas (canonical source: `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-47.9` — update INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md first on any schema change):**

```typescript
// system.scim_provisioning_monitor_stale_declared — HIGH · 7 yr · CC6.4/CC4.1/CC7.1/CC7.2 · SCIM-PROV-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 anchor
const ScimProvisioningMonitorStaleDeclaredSchema = z.object({
  incident_id:                         z.string().uuid(),
  confirmed_stale_since:               z.string().datetime(),
  stale_hours:                         z.number().positive(),
  missed_runs:                         z.number().int().nonnegative(),
  trigger:                             z.enum(['pagerduty_alert', 'manual_discovery', 'co_active_r03']),
  initial_severity:                    z.enum(['P1', 'P0']),
  stale_window_overlaps_quarter_start: z.boolean(),
  violations_found_during_stale:       z.number().int().nonnegative(),
  // 0 = CC6.4 clean attestation; > 0 → R-47-A forensic + R-47-C retroactive CRITICAL event required
});

// system.scim_provisioning_monitor_restored — STANDARD · 3 yr · CC7.2 · SCIM-PROV-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 terminal
const ScimProvisioningMonitorRestoredSchema = z.object({
  incident_id:                         z.string().uuid(), // must match stale_declared
  restored_at:                         z.string().datetime(),
  root_cause:                          z.enum([
    'H1_job_deleted',
    'H2_form_system_permission_revoked',
    'H3_pg_net_degraded',
    'H4_supabase_outage',
  ]),
  fix_deployed_at:                     z.string().datetime(),
  stale_window_hours:                  z.number().positive(),
  stale_window_overlaps_quarter_start: z.boolean(),        // must match stale_declared value
  violations_found_during_stale:       z.number().int().nonnegative(), // must match stale_declared value
  violations_at_restored:              z.number().int().nonnegative(), // 0 or all violations actioned per §R-47.5 before P0 resolve
  quarterly_trigger_manually_fired:    z.boolean(),        // CC4.1 compensating-control record for §R-47.6
});
```

**Emitter assignments:**
- `system.scim_provisioning_monitor_stale_declared` — devops-lead (IC, manual, PAM-elevated) via `emit-audit-event` Worker at R-47 T+0. Emitted once per R-47 activation. Not automated — requires IC human judgment on P0 vs P1 severity classification. `initial_severity = 'P0'` if R-47-C2 returns `violations_found_during_stale ≥ 1`; P1 otherwise. Registered v2.52.
- `system.scim_provisioning_monitor_restored` — devops-lead (IC, manual, PAM-elevated) at R-47 Step 6 after job 47 confirmed healthy in `pg_cron.job_run_details` and R-47-C2 re-run returns zero violations (or all violations actioned per §R-47.5 before P0 resolution). Registered v2.52.

**Alert routing:**
- `system.scim_provisioning_monitor_stale_declared` → no additional PagerDuty alert on emission (the `system.cron_job_stale` PagerDuty P1 `form-devops` alert already fired from `pg-cron-health-monitor` §12.6 with dedup key `scim-prov-check-stale`; AL-SCIM-PROV-02 per OBSERVABILITY §56.4); DEC-030 event serves as the tamper-evident IC response anchor in the HMAC chain; opens the SCIM-PROV-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 chain.
- `system.scim_provisioning_monitor_restored` → resolves PagerDuty P1/P0 `scim-prov-check-stale`; no separate alert on restoration; closes the SCIM-PROV-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 chain. If `violations_found_during_stale > 0` at declaration: devops-lead must confirm all violations have been investigated and actioned per §R-47.5 before PagerDuty P0 resolution (`violations_at_restored = 0` or all violations documented required).

**SCIM-PROV-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 — Ordering invariant (R-47 monitoring-control chain):** `system.scim_provisioning_monitor_restored` is blocked (HTTP 422 `SCIM_PROV_MONITOR_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION`) if no prior `system.scim_provisioning_monitor_stale_declared` exists for the same `incident_id`; violation → R-05 activated. Privacy floor (both R-47 events): all payload fields are integer counts, booleans, timestamps, and enum values — no `tenant_id`, no employee `user_id`, no rejected attribute VALUE, no GDPR Art. 9 special-category data. `violations_found_during_stale = 0` in `stale_declared` is the CC6.4 auditor-inspectable attestation that no `scim.rejected_sensitive_attribute` events occurred undetected during the job 47 stale window; write-boundary enforcement (SSO_SCIM §14 HTTP 422 `BLOCKED_ATTRIBUTE_PATTERNS`) was operational throughout. `violations_at_restored = 0` (or all positives documented as actioned per §R-47.5) is required before resolving PagerDuty P0. `quarterly_trigger_manually_fired` is the CC4.1 attestation that SCIM-PROV-SLO-02 was satisfied via compensating control when the automated quarterly trigger missed (§R-47.6). Structural peers: PRICING-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 (R-46); LITH-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 (R-45); OFFBOARD-CHAIN-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 (R-44). Registered v2.52 (2026-06-26 — [x] Done, AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.52, this commit).

**CC6.4 auditor narrative (R-47 stale events):** CC6.4 requires FORM to restrict access to sensitive data. `system.scim_provisioning_monitor_stale_declared` with `violations_found_during_stale = 0` provides the DEC-030 HMAC-chained IC declaration that no GDPR Art. 9 sensitive attribute violations occurred undetected during the job 47 monitoring blind-spot window. The write-boundary enforcement (SSO_SCIM §14 `BLOCKED_ATTRIBUTE_PATTERNS` HTTP 422) operated independently of the monitoring layer throughout the stale window — this is the primary CC6.4 preventive control. Job 47 monitoring is a detective control; `stale_declared` with `violations_found_during_stale = 0` attests that detection would have been available retroactively had any violations occurred.

**CC4.1 + CC7.1 + CC7.2 auditor narrative (R-47 stale events):** CC7.2 requires FORM to monitor system components. `pg-cron-health-monitor` §12.6 detects job 47 staleness and emits `system.cron_job_stale` HIGH (AL-SCIM-PROV-02), firing PagerDuty P1 `form-devops`. R-47 activates. CC7.1 requires defined procedures for responding to monitoring system failures — the five-query structured IC response (R-47-C1 through R-47-C5) demonstrates monitoring-failure response independent of the violations that system monitors. CC4.1 — `quarterly_trigger_manually_fired = true` in `stale_restored` is the auditor-inspectable attestation that the SCIM-PROV-E-003 quarterly trigger obligation (SOC2_READINESS §119) was satisfied via compensating control per §R-47.6 when the automated quarterly trigger missed. The DEC-030 HMAC chain (`stale_declared` → `stale_restored`) provides tamper-evident detection-to-restoration timeline verifiable per HMAC-VERIFY-ALGO-001.

**CC6.4 auditor narrative:** CC6.4 requires FORM to restrict logical access to sensitive data to authorised individuals. `security.scim_sensitive_attr_violation_detected` CRITICAL/7yr is the DEC-030 evidence that FORM's daily monitoring of the SCIM sensitive-attribute boundary detected a GDPR Art. 9 health attribute reaching the SCIM provisioning endpoint. Each emission creates a HMAC-chained, tamper-evident record with `violation_count` and the 26h observation window — independently verifiable per HMAC-VERIFY-ALGO-001. SCIM-PROV-E-003 (quarterly, 7yr — SOC2_READINESS §119) attests that the zero-count CC6.4 target was met during the observation quarter, or documents resolution of any violation. The two-track evidence structure (per-event CRITICAL chain + quarterly compliance-officer attestation) gives auditors independent CC6.4 verification paths.

**CC4.1 auditor narrative:** CC4.1 requires FORM to implement monitoring activities to evaluate controls and exceptions. `system.scim_provisioning_check_passed` LOW/1yr is the operational all-clear signal that job 47 completed all sweeps without detecting a sensitive attribute violation. The 26h freshness window enforced by `pg-cron-health-monitor` (SCIM-PROV-SLO-02 — OBSERVABILITY §56.3) ensures any gap in monitoring coverage triggers AL-SCIM-PROV-02 PagerDuty P1 detection. SCIM-PROV-MON-E-001 (annual, 3yr — OBSERVABILITY §56.8; SOC2_READINESS §120 pending §56.10 item 5 P1/M6) documents the monitoring infrastructure run history for the year. `system.scim_provisioning_quarterly_check_triggered` STANDARD/3yr demonstrates that the quarterly SCIM-PROV-E-003 evidence collection trigger (CC6.4 zero-count assertion) fired on schedule — four rows per year required. Auditors have three independent CC4.1 evidence paths: continuous monitoring health (`scim_provisioning_check_passed` daily cadence), quarterly trigger compliance (`scim_provisioning_quarterly_check_triggered` four-per-year), and annual monitoring infrastructure export (SCIM-PROV-MON-E-001).

**CC7.2 auditor narrative:** CC7.2 requires FORM to monitor system components for anomalies. `security.scim_sensitive_attr_violation_detected` CRITICAL/7yr demonstrates that FORM's proactive daily baseline sweep detected a SCIM sensitive attribute anomaly (GDPR Art. 9 health data reaching the provisioning boundary) and triggered immediate P0 incident response. This complements the reactive AL-SCIM-01 burst-detection mechanism (SSO_SCIM §26.7b) — a single isolated violation from one misconfigured IdP yields `violation_count = 1`, below AL-SCIM-01's per-tenant 1h burst threshold, yet is detected and escalated by AL-SCIM-PROV-01. SCIM-PROV-MON-E-001 (annual, 3yr) provides the year-level auditor-verifiable record that job 47 ran daily throughout the SOC 2 observation period. See OBSERVABILITY §56.11 OQ-SCIM-PROV-01 for the complementary-tracks rationale.

---

### Contract Amendment Rate Compliance Monitoring events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · OBSERVABILITY §57 · CC5.2/CC6.1/A1.1)

> Defined in `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §57.7` (OBSERVABILITY v5.4.0, 2026-06-27 — §57 Amendment Rate Change Compliance Observability). Three DEC-030 HMAC-chained events covering the pg_cron job 48 (`amendment_rate_compliance_monitor`, `0 7 1 * *`, 33-day freshness window) monitoring-layer lifecycle. Job 48 is the **monthly belt-and-suspenders forensic cross-check** complementing the real-time TU-CHAIN-01 enforcement at the Worker layer (`amend_contract_tier()` SECURITY DEFINER RPC HTTP 422 — COST_MODEL §45.5) and the DDL-layer price floor CHECK constraint on `enterprise_contracts` (DATA_MODEL §47 migration 0088). Sweep 1 verifies no `billing.rate_updated` event exists without a matching `enterprise.contract_amended` chain partner within ±24h for the same `tenant_id` (TU-CHAIN-01 chain integrity). Sweep 2 verifies no `enterprise_contracts` row with `amendment_type IS NOT NULL` carries `rate_per_seat_usd` below the §31.5 tier floor (price floor forensic verification). **AMEND-MONITOR-CHAIN-01 (monitoring-layer ordering invariant):** For each CRITICAL event emitted by job 48: the DEC-030 HTTP 200 must be confirmed from `emit-audit-event` Worker **before** the corresponding PagerDuty P0 alert fires (AL-AMEND-01 for chain gap; AL-AMEND-02 for floor breach); `system.amendment_rate_check_passed` LOW/1yr is **suppressed** when any CRITICAL is emitted this run. **Privacy floor (all three events):** `security.amendment_chain_gap_detected` carries `chain_gap_count` (positive int), `billing_event_ids` (UUID[] — FORM-internal `audit_log_events` PKs only), `check_run_at` — no `tenant_id`, no employee `user_id`, name, email, rate value, or GDPR Art. 9 data; `security.amendment_floor_breach_detected` carries `breach_count` (positive int), `breaching_contract_ids` (UUID[] — FORM-internal `enterprise_contracts` PKs only), `check_run_at` — no rate value, no tenant name, no employee identity; `system.amendment_rate_check_passed` carries aggregate counts only (`chain_gap_count: 0`, `floor_breach_count: 0`, `contracts_checked`, `events_checked`, `check_run_at`). `form_api` REVOKED from `audit_log_events` and `enterprise_contracts`; job 48 runs via `form_system` role. **Closes `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §57.10` item 1 (P0/M5 — register three events + AMEND-MONITOR-CHAIN-01 + Zod v2 schemas; 🟢 Done 2026-06-27, AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.53).** Cross-ref: `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §57` (AL-AMEND-01/02/03 alert rules §57.4; job 48 full spec §57.5; AMEND-MONITOR-CHAIN-01 §57.5.5; Zod schemas §57.7; AMEND-OBS-E-001 evidence artefact §57.8; §57.10 item 1 — this registration); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §45.5` (TU-CHAIN-01 source definition — `amend_contract_tier()` SECURITY DEFINER RPC; `billing.rate_updated` → `enterprise.contract_amended` two-event pair); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §31.5` (price floor constants — Starter $6.00, Growth $4.50, Enterprise $4.00 1yr; $4.50/$4.50/$4.00 3yr); `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §47` (migration 0088 — `amendment_type`, `amendment_signed_at`, `rate_per_seat_usd` columns + `ec_amendment_rate_floor_check` DDL CHECK constraint on `enterprise_contracts`); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §45.7` (ADM-E-001/ADM-E-002 annual SOC 2 evidence artefacts); `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §118` (ADM-E-001/ADM-E-002 evidence registration); `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-48` (job 48 stale recovery runbook; §R-48.5 TU-CHAIN-01 chain gap escalation; §R-48.6 price floor breach escalation; v1.0, 2026-06-27); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §12.6` (job 48 pg_cron registry — v2.3 patch, 2026-06-27); DEC-030.

| Event type | Severity | Retention | Trigger | Key payload fields |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `security.amendment_chain_gap_detected` | CRITICAL | 7 yr | job 48 sweep 1 via `emit-audit-event` Worker when correlated subquery finds ≥ 1 `billing.rate_updated` event in last 32 days without matching `enterprise.contract_amended` for same `tenant_id` within ±24h (TU-CHAIN-01 per COST_MODEL §45.5); AMEND-MONITOR-CHAIN-01: DEC-030 HTTP 200 confirmed before AL-AMEND-01 PagerDuty P0 dispatched; dedup `amend-chain-gap-{YYYY-MM}` no-expiry; no auto-resolve — IC forensic investigation per INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-48 §R-48.5 required; `system.amendment_rate_check_passed` suppressed this run; registered v2.53 | `chain_gap_count` (positive int — count of `billing.rate_updated` events in last 32 days without chain partner; **no `tenant_id`, no rate value, no employee PII**), `billing_event_ids` (UUID[] nonempty — FORM-internal `audit_log_events` PKs for the gap events), `check_run_at` (ISO 8601 UTC — job 48 sweep 1 run timestamp) |
| `security.amendment_floor_breach_detected` | CRITICAL | 7 yr | job 48 sweep 2 via `emit-audit-event` Worker when `SELECT id FROM enterprise_contracts WHERE amendment_type IS NOT NULL AND rate_per_seat_usd < CASE tier WHEN 'starter' THEN 6.00 WHEN 'growth' THEN 4.50 WHEN 'enterprise' THEN 4.00 END` returns ≥ 1 row (unknown-tier rows excluded by NULL bypass → `system.amendment_unknown_tier_detected` STANDARD/3yr); AMEND-MONITOR-CHAIN-01: DEC-030 HTTP 200 confirmed before AL-AMEND-02 PagerDuty P0 dispatched; dedup `amend-floor-breach-{YYYY-MM}` no-expiry; no auto-resolve — IC forensic investigation per INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-48 §R-48.6 required; registered v2.53 | `breach_count` (positive int — count of `enterprise_contracts` rows with `amendment_type IS NOT NULL` and `rate_per_seat_usd` below §31.5 floor; **no rate value, no tenant name, no employee identity**), `breaching_contract_ids` (UUID[] nonempty — FORM-internal `enterprise_contracts` PKs for breaching rows), `check_run_at` (ISO 8601 UTC — job 48 sweep 2 run timestamp) |
| `system.amendment_rate_check_passed` | LOW | 1 yr | job 48 all-clear after both sweeps complete with zero detections; AMEND-MONITOR-CHAIN-01 satisfied (no CRITICAL emitted this run); powers AL-AMEND-03 33-day dead-man's switch freshness monitoring (auto-resolves on next emission); **no `tenant_id`** — aggregate counts only; registered v2.53 | `chain_gap_count` (`z.literal(0)` — must be 0 when emitted; any gap suppresses this event and emits CRITICAL instead), `floor_breach_count` (`z.literal(0)` — must be 0 when emitted), `contracts_checked` (nonneg int — `COUNT(enterprise_contracts WHERE amendment_type IS NOT NULL)` at sweep 2 time), `events_checked` (nonneg int — `COUNT(billing.rate_updated events in last 32 days)` at sweep 1 time), `check_run_at` (ISO 8601 UTC) |

**Zod v2 schemas (canonical source: `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §57.7` — update OBSERVABILITY.md first on any schema change):**

```typescript
import { z } from 'zod/v4';

// security.amendment_chain_gap_detected — CRITICAL · 7 yr (registered v2.53)
// job 48 sweep 1 monitoring-layer sentinel — TU-CHAIN-01 bypass indicator
// AMEND-MONITOR-CHAIN-01: DEC-030 HTTP 200 confirmed before AL-AMEND-01 PagerDuty P0
// Privacy floor: chain_gap_count + billing_event_ids (FORM-internal PKs) + timestamp only
// — no tenant_id, no rate value, no employee identity, no GDPR Art. 9 data
export const AmendmentChainGapDetectedPayload = z.object({
  chain_gap_count:   z.number().int().positive(),           // count of billing.rate_updated without chain partner
  billing_event_ids: z.array(z.string().uuid()).nonempty(), // FORM-internal audit_log_events PKs
  check_run_at:      z.string().datetime(),
});

// security.amendment_floor_breach_detected — CRITICAL · 7 yr (registered v2.53)
// job 48 sweep 2 price floor forensic cross-check
// AMEND-MONITOR-CHAIN-01: DEC-030 HTTP 200 confirmed before AL-AMEND-02 PagerDuty P0
// Privacy floor: breach_count + breaching_contract_ids (FORM-internal PKs) + timestamp only
// — no rate value, no tenant name, no employee identity, no GDPR Art. 9 data
export const AmendmentFloorBreachDetectedPayload = z.object({
  breach_count:           z.number().int().positive(),           // count of enterprise_contracts rows below §31.5 floor
  breaching_contract_ids: z.array(z.string().uuid()).nonempty(), // FORM-internal enterprise_contracts PKs
  check_run_at:           z.string().datetime(),
});

// system.amendment_rate_check_passed — LOW · 1 yr (registered v2.53)
// job 48 all-clear — emitted after both sweeps complete with zero detections
// AMEND-MONITOR-CHAIN-01: suppressed when any CRITICAL event emitted this run
// Privacy floor: aggregate counts only — no tenant_id, no rate value, no employee identity
export const AmendmentRateCheckPassedPayload = z.object({
  chain_gap_count:    z.literal(0),                       // must be 0 — AMEND-MONITOR-CHAIN-01
  floor_breach_count: z.literal(0),                       // must be 0 — AMEND-MONITOR-CHAIN-01
  contracts_checked:  z.number().int().nonnegative(),     // COUNT(enterprise_contracts WHERE amendment_type IS NOT NULL)
  events_checked:     z.number().int().nonnegative(),     // COUNT(billing.rate_updated events in last 32 days)
  check_run_at:       z.string().datetime(),
});
```

**Emitter assignments:**
- `security.amendment_chain_gap_detected` — `form_system` role via pg_cron job 48 (`amendment_rate_compliance_monitor`) → `emit-audit-event` Worker (pg_net HTTP POST). Emitted at most once per sweep 1 run with gap_count > 0; dedup key `amend-chain-gap-{YYYY-MM}` (no-expiry) prevents duplicate P0 alerts on retry. No human emission path — monitoring-layer automation only. AMEND-MONITOR-CHAIN-01 requires HTTP 200 before AL-AMEND-01 PagerDuty P0 dispatch. `form_api` REVOKED from `audit_log_events` and `enterprise_contracts`.
- `security.amendment_floor_breach_detected` — `form_system` role via job 48 sweep 2 → `emit-audit-event` Worker (pg_net HTTP POST). Emitted at most once per sweep 2 run with breach_count > 0; dedup key `amend-floor-breach-{YYYY-MM}` (no-expiry). No human emission path — monitoring-layer automation only. AMEND-MONITOR-CHAIN-01 requires HTTP 200 before AL-AMEND-02 PagerDuty P0 dispatch.
- `system.amendment_rate_check_passed` — `form_system` role via job 48 after both sweeps complete with zero detections. Emitted unconditionally on every successful clean run — positive baseline signal powering AL-AMEND-03 33-day dead-man's switch freshness check (OBSERVABILITY §57.4). Suppressed (AMEND-MONITOR-CHAIN-01) on any run where a CRITICAL event was emitted. `form_api` REVOKED from `audit_log_events`.

**Alert routing:**
- `security.amendment_chain_gap_detected` → AL-AMEND-01 PagerDuty **P0** `form-security` + `form-compliance` (security-engineer + compliance-officer + enterprise-architect; no auto-resolve; dedup `amend-chain-gap-{YYYY-MM}` no-expiry); AMEND-MONITOR-CHAIN-01: PagerDuty dispatch gated on DEC-030 HTTP 200 from `emit-audit-event` Worker. IC response: INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-48 §R-48.5 (TU-CHAIN-01 chain gap forensic investigation).
- `security.amendment_floor_breach_detected` → AL-AMEND-02 PagerDuty **P0** `form-security` + `form-compliance` (security-engineer + compliance-officer; no auto-resolve; dedup `amend-floor-breach-{YYYY-MM}` no-expiry); AMEND-MONITOR-CHAIN-01: PagerDuty dispatch gated on DEC-030 HTTP 200. IC response: INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-48 §R-48.6 (price floor breach forensic investigation).
- `system.amendment_rate_check_passed` → no alert on emission. Gap of > 33 days between consecutive emissions triggers AL-AMEND-03 PagerDuty **P1** `form-devops` → devops-lead (dedup `amend-check-stale` 33-day cooldown; auto-resolves on next successful emission; per OBSERVABILITY §57.4). Recovery runbook: INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-48.

**AMEND-MONITOR-CHAIN-01 — Ordering invariant (job 48 monitoring-control layer):** Per OBSERVABILITY §57.5.5, job 48 executes in strict sequence: (1) sweep 1 (TU-CHAIN-01 chain integrity — 32-day correlated subquery on `audit_log_events`); (2) if gap_count > 0: emit `security.amendment_chain_gap_detected` CRITICAL/7yr, confirm HTTP 200, then dispatch AL-AMEND-01 PagerDuty P0, set `critical_emitted = true`; (3) sweep 2 (price floor forensic verification — `enterprise_contracts` CASE comparison); (4) if breach_count > 0: emit `security.amendment_floor_breach_detected` CRITICAL/7yr, confirm HTTP 200, then dispatch AL-AMEND-02 PagerDuty P0, set `critical_emitted = true`; (5) handle unknown-tier rows → emit `system.amendment_unknown_tier_detected` STANDARD/3yr if any (NULL bypass protection); (6) if `critical_emitted = false`: emit `system.amendment_rate_check_passed` LOW/1yr all-clear; (7) if `critical_emitted = true`: suppress `system.amendment_rate_check_passed` for this run. **Primary enforcement gates operate independently of job 48:** TU-CHAIN-01 is enforced in real time at the Worker layer via HTTP 422 in `amend_contract_tier()` SECURITY DEFINER RPC (COST_MODEL §45.5); price floor is enforced at the DDL layer via `ec_amendment_rate_floor_check` CHECK constraint on `enterprise_contracts` (DATA_MODEL §47 migration 0088). Job 48 is the belt-and-suspenders monthly forensic cross-check — not the primary enforcement gate. **Privacy floor (all three events):** all event payloads carry FORM-internal UUIDs or aggregate counts only — no employee `user_id`, name, email, health value, rate value, tenant name, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category data. `form_api` REVOKED from `audit_log_events` and `enterprise_contracts`; `enterprise_admin`, `tenant_admin`, `tenant_manager` excluded from Metabase dashboard visibility (OBSERVABILITY §57.9). Structural peers: SCIM-ATTR-CHAIN-01 (§56 / job 47); REENTRY-MONITOR-CHAIN-01 (§55 / job 45); PRICING-MONITOR-CHAIN-01 (§55 / job 46). Registered v2.53 (2026-06-27 — [x] Done, AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.53, this commit).

**CC5.2 auditor narrative:** CC5.2 requires FORM to demonstrate commitment and accountability for pricing integrity in commercial commitments. `security.amendment_chain_gap_detected` CRITICAL/7yr is the DEC-030 HMAC-chained evidence that job 48's monthly sweep detected a `billing.rate_updated` event without the required companion `enterprise.contract_amended` chain partner (TU-CHAIN-01 per COST_MODEL §45.5). The primary CC5.2 enforcement occurs at the Worker layer (HTTP 422 in `amend_contract_tier()` SECURITY DEFINER); job 48 provides the monthly forensic cross-check evidence between ADM-E-001/ADM-E-002 annual filings (SOC2_READINESS §118). AMEND-OBS-E-001 (annual run history, 3yr — OBSERVABILITY §57.8; SOC2_READINESS §124 pending §57.10 item 5 P1/M6) provides the full-year view for auditors to verify job 48 was operational and found zero gaps throughout the SOC 2 observation period.

**CC6.1 auditor narrative:** CC6.1 requires FORM to restrict write paths to authorised processes. `security.amendment_floor_breach_detected` CRITICAL/7yr provides the DEC-030 HMAC-chained evidence that job 48's monthly sweep detected an `enterprise_contracts` row with `amendment_type IS NOT NULL` and `rate_per_seat_usd` below the §31.5 floor — indicating a possible bypass of the `amend_contract_tier()` SECURITY DEFINER write path (which enforces `floor_respected: z.literal(true)` as an HTTP 422 gate) or the DDL-layer `ec_amendment_rate_floor_check` CHECK constraint (DATA_MODEL §47). `form_api` REVOKED from `enterprise_contracts` amendment columns and `audit_log_events` INSERT (enforced by `amend_contract_tier()` SECURITY DEFINER exclusivity). The dual-evidence track — real-time enforcement (HTTP 422 + DDL CHECK) + monthly forensic cross-check (job 48 + CRITICAL events) — satisfies CC6.1 multi-layer authorised-write-path control requirements.

**A1.1 auditor narrative:** A1.1 requires FORM to maintain processing availability per commitments. `system.amendment_rate_check_passed` LOW/1yr provides the DEC-030 monthly all-clear signal confirming job 48 completed both sweeps with zero detections. AL-AMEND-03 (OBSERVABILITY §57.4) enforces a 33-day freshness window: a gap of > 33 days between consecutive `system.amendment_rate_check_passed` emissions fires a PagerDuty P1 `form-devops` alert → devops-lead, ensuring job 48 monitoring availability is itself monitored.
