Two DEC-030 HMAC-chained stale-recovery events: (1) `system.invite_expiry_sweep_stale_declared` HIGH/7yr — IC-declared jobs 10 (`invite_expiry_sweep`) and 12 (`invite_email_expiry_cleanup`) stale incident anchor; INVITE-SWEEP-STALE-CHAIN-01 anchor; P0 upgrade if `pii_at_risk_count > 0` (GDPR Art. 17 active breach — Art. 33 72h window starts); 8-field Zod v2 `InviteExpirySweepStaleDeclaredSchema` (privacy floor: aggregate counts only — no `tenant_id`, `user_id`, `invited_email`, or GDPR Art. 9 data). (2) `system.invite_expiry_sweep_restored` LOW/3yr — IC-confirmed jobs 10 + 12 restoration terminal event; INVITE-SWEEP-STALE-CHAIN-01 terminal; HTTP 422 `INVITE_SWEEP_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` if no prior `stale_declared` for same `incident_id` → R-05; 6-field Zod v2 `InviteExpirySweepRestoredSchema`. INVITE-SWEEP-STALE-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant table. CC6.3/P5.1/P5.2 SOC 2 auditor narratives provided. Retention table: +2 rows (`system.invite_expiry_sweep_stale_declared` HIGH 7yr CC6.3/P5.1/P5.2; `system.invite_expiry_sweep_restored` LOW 3yr CC6.3/P5.1/P5.2). SOC 2 evidence artefact INVITE-STALE-E-001 (CC6.3/P5.1/P5.2, per-activation, 7yr; `compliance/evidence/invite/invite-stale-e-001-<YYYY-MM-DD>/`; registered `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §148`, v3.74.0, 2026-07-03). **Closes `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-60.12` item 1 (P0 — 🟢 2026-07-03).** Document header v2.74 → v2.75. Owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer.

*v2.74 (2026-07-03): §R-57 PKJWT Key Expiry Sweep Stale events — new section `### R-57 PKJWT Key Expiry Sweep Stale events` inserted between `### SSO PKJWT Key Lifecycle events` and `### Enterprise Adoption Monitoring events`. Two DEC-030 HMAC-chained stale-recovery events: (1) `system.pkjwt_expiry_sweep_stale_declared` HIGH/7yr — IC-declared job 58 stale incident anchor; PKJWT-SWEEP-STALE-CHAIN-01 anchor; payload: `incident_id` UUID, `confirmed_stale_since`, `stale_hours`, `missed_runs`, `at_risk_tenant_count` aggregate int, `trigger` enum, `initial_severity` enum; privacy floor: no `tenant_id`, `kid`, user data, or GDPR Art. 9 data. (2) `system.pkjwt_expiry_sweep_restored` LOW/3yr — IC-confirmed job 58 restoration terminal event; PKJWT-SWEEP-STALE-CHAIN-01 terminal; HTTP 422 `PKJWT_SWEEP_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` if no prior `stale_declared` for same `incident_id`; payload: `incident_id`, `restored_at`, `root_cause` H1–H4 enum, `tenants_warned_during_recovery` aggregate int. Zod v2 schemas: `PkjwtExpirySweepStaleDeclaredPayload` + `PkjwtExpirySweepRestoredPayload` (canonical source: `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-57.7`). PKJWT-SWEEP-STALE-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant table with HTTP 422 error code. SOC 2 evidence artefact PKJWT-SWEEP-STALE-E-001 (CC6.6, per-activation, 7yr; registered `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §147`, v3.73.0, 2026-07-03). Retention table: +2 rows (`system.pkjwt_expiry_sweep_stale_declared` HIGH 7yr CC6.6; `system.pkjwt_expiry_sweep_restored` LOW 3yr CC6.6). Companion edits: `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md` v5.14.1 (§12.6 job 58 companion runbook cross-ref added); `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md` v3.73.0 (§147 PKJWT-SWEEP-STALE-E-001 registered); `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md` v3.22.1 (§R-57.11 items 1–3 closed). **Closes `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-57.11` item 1 (P0 — 🟢 2026-07-03).** Document header v2.73 → v2.74. *v2.73 (2026-07-02): §SSO-PKJ-Lifecycle — three DEC-030 HMAC-chained SSO PKJWT Key Lifecycle events registered (SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §42 · DEC-097 · SOC 2 CC6.6). New section `### SSO PKJWT Key Lifecycle events` inserted between `### SSO Fleet Health Monitoring events` and `### Enterprise Adoption Monitoring events`. Three events: (1) `sso.pkjwt_key_generated` HIGH/7yr — per-tenant OIDC `private_key_jwt` asymmetric key pair generation record; PAM-gated `POST /internal/tenant/{tenant_id}/sso/pkjwt/generate`; payload: `tenant_id` UUID, `kid` UUID, `algorithm` enum (RS256|ES256), `key_generated_at`, `key_expires_at` (+ 1 year per §42.9); private key absent from payload. (2) `sso.pkjwt_key_rotated` HIGH/7yr — annual rotation record; payload: `tenant_id` UUID, `old_kid` UUID, `new_kid` UUID, `rotation_reason` enum (scheduled|incident|customer_request), `rotated_at`. (3) `sso.pkjwt_key_expiry_warning` STANDARD/3yr — pg_cron job 58 (`pkjwt_key_expiry_sweep`, `0 9 * * *`) daily sweep; emitted per tenant where `pkjwt_key_expires_at < NOW() + INTERVAL '30 days'`; payload: `tenant_id` UUID, `kid` UUID, `key_expires_at`, `days_until_expiry` non-negative int; P3 Slack-only `#alerts-enterprise`; non-blocking. Zod v2 schemas: `SsoPkjwtKeyGeneratedPayload`, `SsoPkjwtKeyRotatedPayload`, `SsoPkjwtKeyExpiryWarningPayload`. SOC 2 evidence artefact PKJWT-E-001 (CC6.6, quarterly + per-incident, 7yr) registered in section. CC6.6 auditor narrative: confirms private key is never in transit or at IdP — only public key published via JWKS endpoint; PKJWT-E-001 demonstrates annual rotation compliance and key payload privacy invariant (no `private_key` field in any Zod schema). Privacy floor: all three events contain only FORM-internal UUIDs (`tenant_id`, `kid`) and timestamps — no employee `user_id`, name, email, health value, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category data; private key material is absent from all payloads by Zod schema constraint. Retention table: +2 rows (`sso.pkjwt_key_generated`/`sso.pkjwt_key_rotated` 7yr CC6.6; `sso.pkjwt_key_expiry_warning` 3yr CC6.6 advisory). Companion edits: `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md` v5.14.0 (§12.6 job 58 `pkjwt_key_expiry_sweep`); `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md` v2.16 (§42.11 items 4 + 5 closed). Document header v2.72 → v2.73. Owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer + platform-engineer.*

*v2.72 (2026-07-02): +1 `geo.contract_currency_set` HIGH/7yr event in new `### Enterprise Geo & Billing Currency events` section — closes `docs/COST_MODEL.md §56.12` item 2 (P0 — register `geo.contract_currency_set` before first UA enterprise contract execution; DEC-096). `geo.contract_currency_set` (HIGH, 7yr, CC1.3/CC3.1): emitted once per contract execution when billing currency is confirmed; PAM-gated `POST /internal/enterprise/contract/currency-set`, `form_system` role; GEO-CHAIN-01 invariant: `emit-audit-event` Worker returns HTTP 422 `GEO_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` if `enterprise.tenant_created` arrives for the same `tenant_id` with no prior `contract_currency_set` in chain (blocks tenant provisioning until currency record filed). Payload: `tenant_id` UUID, `contract_id` UUID, `billing_currency` enum (`USD`|`EUR`|`GBP`), `reference_currency` string nullable (e.g. `"UAH"` for UA addendum per MSA §4.7.2), `reference_rate_at_signing` positive number nullable (USD/UAH spot rate at Order Form Effective Date), `floor_protection_pct` number 0–100 nullable (10.0 for ±10% UAH floor per §4.7.3), `contract_effective_date` ISO 8601 datetime, `signed_by` UUID (FORM-internal auth UUID — **never customer employee identifier**). Privacy floor: no customer employee `user_id`, name, email, health value, coaching content, or GDPR Art. 9 data; `signed_by` is FORM-internal only. Retention: 7yr (ASC 606 + GDPR DPA evidence). Evidence artefact GEO-E-001 (CC1.3/CC3.1, annual, 7yr): annual export confirming `billing_currency: 'USD'` on all records and GEO-CHAIN-01 predecessor coverage for every `enterprise.tenant_created` in the period; `compliance/evidence/geo/GEO-E-001_<YYYY>.csv`. SOC 2 auditor narratives: CC1.3 — immutable record that management communicated and operationalised USD billing policy (DEC-096) at contract execution; CC3.1 — FX risk documented at the contract level (billing_currency, reference_rate_at_signing, floor_protection_pct) for each customer relationship, satisfying risk-identification-at-appropriate-operating-units requirement. Document header v2.71 → v2.72. Cross-ref: `docs/COST_MODEL.md §56.5` (canonical Zod v2 schema); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §56.3` (OQ-GEO-01 decision — USD standard); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §56.12` item 2 (→ 🟢 Done 2026-07-02); `docs/MSA_TEMPLATE.md §4.7` (Currency & FX clause — P0 fulfilled this pass); `docs/ENTERPRISE.md §Pricing` (billing currency note — P0 fulfilled this pass); `docs/DECISION_LOG.md DEC-096` (decision authority). Owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer.*

*v2.71 (2026-07-02): +2 `admin.*` events — `admin.key_rotation_incident_opened` (HIGH/7yr) + `admin.key_rotation_incident_closed` (HIGH/7yr) — closes `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md §R-54.7` item 1 (P0/M7 — register both DEC-030 event types in AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md before R-21 can be used in a live production incident). Added to `### Admin (key management)` section after `admin.key_rotation_overdue`. `admin.key_rotation_incident_opened` (HIGH, 7yr, CC6.8/CC7.2/CC7.3): IC opened before any R-21 rotation action; KEY-IC-CHAIN-01 anchor; payload — `incident_id` UUID (correlation key bridging PagerDuty + DEC-030 + IR-KEY-E-001..E-006 artefacts), `key_type` enum (8-value production inventory), `trigger_alert_id` (AL-KEY-01..04 or manual), `key_age_days` int ≥ 0, `rotation_due_by` ISO 8601, `overdue` bool, `two_person_auth_required` bool (true for HMAC_AUDIT_CHAIN_KEY + KEYPOINTS_ENC_KEY), `ic_user_id` FORM-staff UUID. `admin.key_rotation_incident_closed` (HIGH, 7yr, CC6.8/CC5.3/CC7.5): IC closed after rotation + verification (or deferred/false_positive); KEY-IC-CHAIN-01 terminal; HTTP 422 `KEY_IC_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` if no preceding opened event for same `incident_id`; max window 48h (HMAC_AUDIT_CHAIN_KEY/KEYPOINTS_ENC_KEY) or 72h (other keys); payload — `incident_id` UUID (same as opened), `key_type` enum (redundant for query convenience), `resolution` enum (rotated_on_schedule|rotated_emergency|deferred_approved|false_positive), `rotation_completed_at` ISO 8601|null, `verification_completed` bool, `deferred_reason` string|null (max 200 chars; operational text only — no employee name/health/Art. 9 data), `pir_committed` bool (true if severity ≥ P1). Zod v2 schemas added inline (cross-ref INCIDENT_RESPONSE §R-54.5 as canonical source). HMAC chain requirement note: five-event chain diagram (IC-opened → encryption_key_rotation_initiated → encryption_key_rotated → encryption_key_rotation_verified → IC-closed); auditor CC6.8 SQL query; stale-IC monitoring windows. *v2.68 (2026-07-01): +1 `admin.*` event — `admin.programme_health_label_viewed` LOW/1yr — closes `docs/COST_MODEL.md §54.10` item 3 (P1/M7 — register DEC-030 event before M7 Admin Dashboard v2 deploy). New section `### Enterprise Admin Programme Health events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · COST_MODEL §54 · C1.2 / CC2.2)` inserted after `### Admin (dashboard sessions)`. One event: `admin.programme_health_label_viewed` (LOW, 1yr) — emitted by Admin Dashboard backend (`emit-audit-event` Worker) on HTTP 200 from `GET /api/admin/tenants/:tenantId/programme-health` with `phi_label ≠ 'insufficient_data'`; skipped entirely for `insufficient_data` state (k < 5 — QBR-K-ANON-01 Tier 1 floor per §51). `ProgrammeHealthLabelViewedSchema` Zod v2 (four fields: `phi_label` enum healthy/needs_attention/at_risk, `snapshot_date` ISO 8601 date, `trend` improved/stable/declined/null, `admin_user_id` UUID — FORM-internal auth UUID per §29.12 pattern). **PHI-VIEW-01 ordering invariant:** `admin.programme_health_label_viewed` must only fire after `enterprise.health_score_updated` for the same `tenant_id` + `snapshot_date` — guaranteed by read-after-write model (Worker reads from `tenant_engagement_snapshots` populated by `fehs_compute` pg_cron job that emits `enterprise.health_score_updated`); NOT a Worker HTTP 422 invariant (model-guaranteed, not chain-enforced). **Deduplication key:** `phi-label-viewed-{tenant_id}-{admin_user_id}-{snapshot_date}` — `emit-audit-event` Worker returns HTTP 200 `{"deduplicated": true}` on KV hit; prevents event flooding on page refresh within the same monthly snapshot period. **SOC 2 C1.2:** `phi_label` string in the chain event confirms what was disclosed to the tenant admin; absence of `fehs_score`, `activation_rate_pct`, `wau_rate_pct` in the Zod schema confirms what was withheld — satisfies C1.2 (confidential information management). **SOC 2 CC2.2:** non-null `trend` field constitutes a chain-anchored record of external PHI communication and its content (3-band label + trend direction). Retention table: +1 row (`admin.programme_health_label_viewed` LOW 1yr C1.2/CC2.2). Privacy floor: no raw FEHS score; no `activation_rate_pct` or `wau_rate_pct`; no individual employee `user_id`, name, email, health value, coaching content, or GDPR Art. 9 data; `admin_user_id` is FORM-internal auth UUID only (never employer HR identifier); `form_api` REVOKED from `tenant_engagement_snapshots`. ---

## Export & delivery

Enterprise tenants can:
- Stream live audit log via **webhook** (`POST` to their endpoint)
- Daily **S3 bucket sync** (their bucket, our role assumes)
- Pull historical via **REST API** (`GET /v1/audit/logs?since=...&until=...`)
- SIEM integrations: **Datadog, Splunk, Sumo Logic** (native exporters)

Default format: JSON Lines (NDJSON). Optional CEF for SIEM.

---

## Privacy guarantees

- `actor_ip` — masked to /24 (IPv4) or /48 (IPv6) before storage. Full IP only retained 30 days for security investigation, then truncated.
- `actor_ua` — full string acceptable (not PII).
- `changes` JSONB redacted for sensitive fields. Specifically: `password`, `mfa_secret`, `api_key`, `health_data_blob`. Replaced with `"[REDACTED]"`.
- Individual user health/workout data **never** logged in `changes` — only metadata about access (who, when, scope).

---

## Implementation notes for `platform-engineer`

- Write path: every privileged action goes through `auditLog()` middleware. Failure to log = transaction abort (fail-closed).
- Async path NOT acceptable for `auth.*`, `tenant.*`, `support.*` (must be synchronous w/ transaction).
- Async path acceptable for `data.read_aggregate`, `integration.api_call` (high volume).
- Partition rotation cron daily at 02:00 UTC.
- HMAC validation cron weekly — alerts if chain broken.

---

## SLO

- Log durability: **99.999%** (5 nines — backed up 3 regions)
- Log latency: **P95 < 50ms** added to request
- Chain integrity verification: **weekly batch**, no in-band cost
- Export latency: webhook delivery **P95 < 5s** after event

---

**v2.35 · 2026-06-22 · owner: devops-lead + compliance-officer + security-engineer**
*v2.35 (2026-06-22): +2 events — White-Label Cert Check Stale incident chain (INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-35 · WL-CERT-CHAIN-01 · CC7.2/A1.2 · P0/M10). Two new DEC-030 HMAC-chained events added to `### System` section after `system.renewal_notice_check_passed`, closing INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-35.11 item 1 (P0/M10). (1) `system.wl_cert_check_failure_declared` (HIGH, 7yr): devops-lead (IC) declares a `white_label_cert_check` (job 40, `0 2 * * *`, 26h freshness window) stale incident at T+0 of every R-35 activation; gap leaves WL-SLO-02 (Cloudflare Custom Hostnames cert expiry monitoring) and WL-SLO-03 (cert renewal trigger) in a detection blind spot — active white-label certificate expirations may go undetected during the stale window; Zod v2 payload: `incident_id` UUID, `confirmed_stale_since` datetime, `stale_hours` positive, `missed_runs` nonneg int, `at_risk_domain_count` nonneg int (0 at P1 before R-35-C1 sweep; > 0 at P0 when R-35-C1 confirms ≥ 1 domain expiring within 7 days), `earliest_expiry_at` datetime|null (null when `at_risk_domain_count = 0`; earliest cert expiry among at-risk domains when > 0), `trigger` enum['pagerduty_alert','manual_discovery'], `initial_severity` enum['P1','P0']; WL-CERT-CHAIN-01 anchor; HTTP 422 `WL_CERT_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` on ordering inversion → R-05; privacy floor: no `user_id`, no `tenant_id`, no domain names, no hostname values — `at_risk_domain_count` is aggregate only, no employee name/email, no health data, no GDPR Art. 9 special-category data. (2) `system.wl_cert_check_restored` (STANDARD, 3yr): devops-lead confirms job 40 re-enabled and first successful post-fix run confirmed; Zod v2 payload: `incident_id` UUID, `restored_at` datetime, `root_cause` enum['H1_job_deleted','H2_cf_token_invalid','H3_worker_proxy_failure','H4_cloudflare_api_outage','H5_supabase_outage'], `fix_deployed_at` datetime, `domains_verified` nonneg int (count of white-label domains confirmed active on restored run — 0 if no white-label tenants provisioned); WL-CERT-CHAIN-01 terminal event; privacy floor: no `user_id`, no `tenant_id`, no domain names, no hostname values — `domains_verified` is aggregate count. Retention table: +2 rows (`system.wl_cert_check_failure_declared` HIGH 7yr CC7.2/A1.2; `system.wl_cert_check_restored` STANDARD 3yr CC7.2/A1.2). WL-CERT-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant: `system.wl_cert_check_failure_declared` must precede `system.wl_cert_check_restored` for the same `incident_id`; `emit-audit-event` Worker returns HTTP 422 `WL_CERT_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` on inversion → R-05. SOC 2 evidence artefact WL-CERT-E-001 (CC7.2/A1.2, quarterly, 3yr, devops-lead, `compliance/evidence/wl/wl-cert-e-001-YYYY-QN.md`) registered in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4` (v3.25.6 — closes R-35.11 item 3). Cross-references: INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-35 §R-35.7 (canonical Zod schemas and WL-CERT-CHAIN-01 full spec); OBSERVABILITY §12.6 job 40 (`white_label_cert_check` — `0 2 * * *`, 26h freshness window, `wl-cert-check-stale` PagerDuty dedup, `form-devops` P1 routing); OBSERVABILITY §29 (WL-SLO-02/WL-SLO-03 alert coverage — detection gap scope); SOC2_READINESS §79.4 (WL-CERT-E-001 quarterly evidence artefact); R-05 (WL-CERT-CHAIN-01 violation response); DEC-030. **Closes INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-35.11 item 1 (P0/M10 — 🟢 2026-06-22) and INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-35.11 item 3 (P1/M11 — 🟢 2026-06-22).** Document header v2.34 → v2.35. (1) `admin.pentest_initiated` (HIGH, 7yr): engagement anchor — signed engagement letter; `authorised_by_compliance_officer: true` + `authorised_by_founder: true` dual-sign-off literals; `staging_only_flag` boolean; `supabase_notification_filed: true` + `supabase_notification_date` fields (PT-E-007 chain-of-custody per §62.2, OQ-PT-02 resolution); PENTEST-CHAIN-02 anchor; CC9.2/CC4.1. (2) `admin.pentest_finding_logged` (HIGH, 7yr): per-finding immutable record; `finding_id` UUID PENTEST-CHAIN-01 anchor; `severity_tier` enum P0/P1/P2/P3; `affected_surface` 12-value enum; `cwe_id` CWE-NNN regex; `cvss_score` float 0–10; `sla_deadline` computed per §61.5 SLA table; verbal notification SLA clock starts on P0 emission; CC7.1/CC4.1. (3) `admin.pentest_finding_remediated` (HIGH, 7yr): remediation closure; `remediation_pr` URL; `retest_outcome` enum (confirmed_fixed / partial_fix_further_retest_required); PENTEST-CHAIN-01 successor (HTTP 422 on inversion → R-05); CC7.2/A1.2. (4) `admin.pentest_report_filed` (HIGH, 7yr): final report chain record; `report_sha256` hex-64 tamper-evident hash of PT-E-001; `finding_count_p0/p1/p2/p3` aggregate summary (no per-finding detail in chain); `r2_object_key` path; PENTEST-CHAIN-02 terminal event (HTTP 422 on inversion → R-05); CC4.1/CC7.1. (5) `admin.pentest_retest_completed` (MEDIUM, 7yr): per-finding re-test attestation; `retest_method` enum (vendor / self — self only for P2/P3); PENTEST-CHAIN-01 successor; CC7.2/A1.2; 7yr matches primary `admin.pentest_finding_logged` record (re-test is admissible only while finding record is retained). (6) `admin.pentest_risk_accepted` (HIGH, 7yr): dual sign-off risk acceptance for P2/P3 findings; `approved_by_compliance_officer: true` + `approved_by_founder: true` literals; `compensating_control` max 500 chars (CC5.3 evidence); `decision_log_ref` required (mirrors PIPE-CHAIN-02 pattern); Worker HTTP 422 `PENTEST_RISK_ACCEPTANCE_P0_P1_VIOLATION` if `severity_tier ∈ {'P0','P1'}` — no P0/P1 risk acceptance permitted; CC5.3/CC4.1. (7) `admin.pentest_chain_enumeration_authorised` (HIGH, 7yr) — NEW from §62.4.3: Layer B production chain enumeration gate; `enumeration_scope: z.literal('audit_log_events SELECT')` hard invariant; `production_flag: z.literal(true)` + `staging_only_destructive_tests_confirmed: z.literal(true)` dual invariant; `credential_expiry` datetime (48h max); must precede any vendor Layer B query (enforced by Worker); CC6.1/CC9.2; OQ-PT-04 resolution. Retention table: +7 rows for all `admin.pentest_*` events and `admin.pentest_chain_enumeration_authorised`. PENTEST-CHAIN-01/02/03 invariant specs. PENTEST_RISK_ACCEPTANCE_P0_P1_VIOLATION severity gate. Full Zod v2 schemas for all 7 events. SOC 2 auditor narratives for CC4.1/CC7.1/CC7.2/CC6.1/CC6.6/CC9.2/A1.2. PT-E-001 through PT-E-007 artefact register. Aggregate PT-E-006 SQL query (privacy-safe, no finding detail, no Art. 9 data). **Closes `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md` PT-P1-02 (M6 — all six §61.8 event types now registered), PT-P1-06 (M6 — `admin.pentest_chain_enumeration_authorised` now registered), and 62.8-01 (M5 — full DEC-030 spec with HIGH severity, 7-year retention). Document header v2.33 → v2.34. Owner: security-engineer + compliance-officer.**

**v2.32 · 2026-06-22 · owner: security-engineer + compliance-officer + devops-lead**
*v2.32 (2026-06-22): +2 events — Google Directory Alert Check Stale incident chain (INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-38 · GDIR-ALERT-CHAIN-01 · CC7.2/CC7.3 · P0/M4). Two new DEC-030 HMAC-chained events added to `### System` section after `system.sso_fleet_check_restored`, closing INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-38.11 item 1 (P0/M4). (1) `system.gdir_alert_stale_declared` (HIGH, 7yr): devops-lead (IC) declares a `google_directory_alert_check` (job 35, `*/5 * * * *`, 6-min freshness window) stale incident at T+0 of every R-38 activation; constitutes a CC7.2 monitoring control degradation — AL-SSO-GDIR-01 and AL-SSO-GDIR-02 detection blind spot during stale window; Zod v2 payload: `incident_id` UUID, `confirmed_stale_since` datetime, `stale_minutes` positive, `missed_runs` nonneg int, `active_sync_error_tenant_count` nonneg int (0 at P1; > 0 at P0 when R-38-C1 confirms ≥ 1 tenant with ≥ 3 `sso.google_directory_sync_error` events in any 15-min window during stale period), `trigger` enum['pagerduty_alert','manual_discovery','co_active_r32','co_active_r37'] (co_active_r32 = R-32 job 37 `caep_reregister_sweep` co-stale; co_active_r37 = R-37 job 38 `sso_fleet_health_check` co-stale), `initial_severity` enum['P1','P0'], `peer_jobs_stale` boolean (true when co-stale with job 37 or job 38 — shared pg_cron infrastructure failure indicator H2/H4); emitting at T+0 with `active_sync_error_tenant_count = 0` and `initial_severity = 'P1'` is permitted; GDIR-ALERT-CHAIN-01 anchor; HTTP 422 `GDIR_ALERT_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` on ordering inversion → R-05; privacy floor: no `user_id`, no `tenant_id` (aggregate count only), no employee name/email, no health data, no GDPR Art. 9 special-category data. (2) `system.gdir_alert_restored` (STANDARD, 3yr): devops-lead confirms job 35 re-enabled and first successful post-fix run confirmed; Zod v2 payload: `incident_id` UUID, `restored_at` datetime, `root_cause` enum['H1','H2','H3','H4'] (H1 = job deleted/disabled; H2 = shared pg_cron infrastructure co-stale with jobs 37/38; H3 = `audit_log_events` query failure/timeout; H4 = Supabase platform outage), `fix_deployed_at` datetime, `al_sso_gdir_01_window_assessment_performed` bool (true on all normal activations; false only if emergency restoration was required before R-38-C1 could be run, requiring deferred query); GDIR-ALERT-CHAIN-01 terminal event; privacy floor: no `user_id`, no `tenant_id`, no employee name/email, no health data. Retention table: +2 rows (`system.gdir_alert_stale_declared` HIGH 7yr CC7.2; `system.gdir_alert_restored` STANDARD 3yr CC7.2). GDIR-ALERT-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant: `system.gdir_alert_restored` MUST follow `system.gdir_alert_stale_declared` for same `incident_id`; `emit-audit-event` Worker returns HTTP 422 `GDIR_ALERT_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` on inversion → R-05. Key distinction from `system.gdir_alert_check_stale` (LOW/1yr — registered v2.20, 2026-06-19): that LOW advisory event is emitted by job 35 itself as a self-monitoring signal (from `pg_cron.job_run_details`) and does not page; `system.gdir_alert_stale_declared` HIGH/7yr is the IC-declared incident record emitted at T+0 of R-38 activation after PagerDuty pages. Both events coexist in the chain for any given stale incident: LOW advisory from job 35 self-monitoring; HIGH declaration from IC. Cross-references: INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-38 §R-38.7 (canonical Zod schemas and GDIR-ALERT-CHAIN-01 full spec); OBSERVABILITY §12.6 job 35 (`gdir-alert-check-stale` dedup; `form-devops` P1 routing; R-38 cross-ref added v1.1 patch 2026-06-22); OBSERVABILITY §48 (AL-SSO-GDIR-01/AL-SSO-GDIR-02 canonical alert definitions; job 35 SQL spec §48.4); SOC2_READINESS §92 (SSO-OBS-E-007 R-38 activation addendum — updated v3.25.5 2026-06-22); AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA v2.20 (prior registration of `system.gdir_alert_check_stale` LOW/1yr advisory); R-05 (GDIR-ALERT-CHAIN-01 violation response); R-32 (job 37 peer — co-stale trigger); R-37 (job 38 peer — co-stale trigger; R-37 T+5 and H2 hypothesis cross-referenced "job 35 stale alerts" establishing R-38's necessity); DEC-030. **Closes INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-38.11 item 1 (P0/M4 — 🟢 2026-06-22).** Document header v2.31 → v2.32. Owner: security-engineer + compliance-officer + devops-lead.*

*v2.31 (2026-06-22): +2 events — SSO Fleet Health Monitoring (OBSERVABILITY §49 · DEC-070 · CC7.2/CC7.3 · P0/M5). One new event registered in `### SSO Fleet Health Monitoring events` section (new section inserted after `### SIEM Consent & Export Governance events`): (1) `siem.sso_fleet_health_breach` (HIGH, 3yr): pg_cron job 38 (`sso_fleet_health_check`, `*/5 * * * *`, `form_audit` role) emits when ≥ 3 distinct tenants simultaneously breach SSO-SLO-01 (login success rate < 99%, ≥ 5 attempts per tenant, 15-min rolling window); Zod v2 schema `SiemSsoFleetHealthBreachPayload`: `failing_tenant_count` nonneg int (≥ 3 at emission), `fleet_success_rate_pct` numeric 0–100, `window_minutes` literal(15), `breach_threshold` literal(3), `sla_credit_impact` literal('none') (DEC-070 hard invariant — emit-audit-event Worker HTTP 422 if absent), `dec_reference` literal('DEC-070'); no `tenant_id`, no user identity, no Art. 9 data — aggregate counts only; privacy invariant: `tenant_id` stays inside the SQL GROUP BY, never exits the pg_cron execution context; Part B of SSO-FLEET-E-001 quarterly evidence artefact (CC7.2/CC7.3); CC7.2 auditor narrative: alert-triggering evidence and SOC 2 artefact share the same HMAC chain; CC7.3 auditor narrative: Part C PagerDuty cross-reference ±5 min of chain event proves immutable-record-triggered response. One new event registered in `### System` section (after `system.sso_fleet_check_restored`, before `system.scim_guard_cfg_cache_stale`): (2) `system.fleet_sso_check_stale` (LOW, 1yr): §12.7 `pg-cron-health-monitor` advisory when job 38 exceeds 6-minute freshness window (3-run tolerance of 5-min cadence); Zod v2 payload: `job_name` literal('sso_fleet_health_check'), `stale_since` datetime, `stale_minutes` positive, `missed_runs` nonneg int; no PagerDuty (advisory only; high-severity complement is `system.sso_fleet_check_failure_declared` HIGH/7yr from R-37 activation); consumed by SSO-FLEET-E-001 Part A Step 2 freshness gap cross-check (LAG window query over `pg_cron.job_run_details`; non-zero rows require devops-lead remediation note); no chain ordering invariant; privacy invariant: operational metadata only — no `tenant_id`, no user data, no Art. 9 data; 1yr retention sufficient (evidential value carried by 3yr SSO-FLEET-E-001 and 7yr R-37 record). Retention table: +2 rows (`system.fleet_sso_check_stale` LOW 1yr CC7.2; `siem.sso_fleet_health_breach` HIGH 3yr CC7.2/CC7.3). Privacy floor (both events): no `user_id`, no `tenant_id` (group-by internal only), no employee name, no email, no health value, no GDPR Art. 9 special category; `failing_tenant_count` and `missed_runs` are aggregate integers; `fleet_success_rate_pct` is a numeric fleet-wide average; `stale_minutes` is a positive scalar; `sla_credit_impact: 'none'` is a DEC-070 hard invariant confirmed by Zod literal. Cross-references: OBSERVABILITY §49 (SSO-SLO-01b / AL-SSO-FLEET-01 / job 38 full SQL spec / SSO-FLEET-E-001 definition); OBSERVABILITY §49.9 item 3 (P0/M5 — 🟢 closed by v2.31 — both events now registered); OBSERVABILITY §12.6 job 38 (`sso_fleet_health_check` — primary emitter); OBSERVABILITY §12.7 (health supervisor — freshness advisory pattern; `system.fleet_sso_check_stale` 6-min window same as jobs 24/35); SOC2_READINESS §95 (SSO-FLEET-E-001 evidence artefact — Part A/B/C/D collection protocol; §95.6 item 3 P0/M5 — 🟢 closed by v2.31); SOC2_READINESS §95.2 Part A Step 2 (freshness gap cross-check SQL consuming `system.fleet_sso_check_stale` advisory count); SOC2_READINESS §95.2 Part B (quarterly `siem.sso_fleet_health_breach` chain export; `sla_credit_impact: 'none'` post-export assertion); INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-37 (job 38 stale recovery runbook — activated when PagerDuty fires on `system.cron_job_stale` for job 38, not on `system.fleet_sso_check_stale` LOW advisory directly); DEC-070 (formal adoption — five grounds; `sla_credit_impact: 'none'` hard invariant rationale; SSO-SLO-01b breach threshold ≥ 3). **Closes `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §49.9` item 3 (P0/M5 — 🟢 2026-06-22) and `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §95.6` item 3 (P0/M5 — 🟢 2026-06-22).** Document header v2.30 → v2.31. *v2.28 (2026-06-21): +2 events — Enterprise Contract Renewal Monitoring (OBSERVABILITY §51 · CC4.1/CC2.2/A1.1 · P0/M11). New section `### Enterprise Contract Renewal Monitoring events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · OBSERVABILITY §51 · CC4.1/CC2.2/A1.1)` inserted before `## Export & delivery`. (1) `enterprise.renewal_notice_overdue` (HIGH, 7yr): per-tenant alert event emitted by `renewal_notice_check` pg_cron job 39 (daily 09:00 UTC) when RENEW-NOTICE-01 SQL detects an active `enterprise_contracts` row with `renewal_date BETWEEN CURRENT_DATE + 85 AND CURRENT_DATE + 95` and no `enterprise.renewal_notice_sent` event on file within 100 days; fires AL-RENEW-01 P1 PagerDuty `form-enterprise` → compliance-officer; dedup key `renew-notice-missing-{tenant_id}` 72h (dedup suppresses PagerDuty storm but NOT the DEC-030 event — full audit trail preserved); 5-day compliance-officer SLA; auto-resolves when notice filed; Zod v2 `RenewalNoticeOverduePayload`: `tenant_id`, `renewal_date` (date-regex), `days_until_renewal` (int 85–95), `acv_usd` (positive — aggregate contract value), `check_run_at` (datetime), `alert_dedup_key` (string). (2) `system.renewal_notice_check_passed` (LOW, 1yr): operational all-clear health signal emitted by `renewal_notice_check` job 39 when RENEW-NOTICE-01 SQL returns zero rows; no `tenant_id` — privacy floor; aggregate `contracts_checked` count only; absence for > 26 h triggers `system.cron_job_stale` HIGH + PagerDuty P1 `form-compliance` → INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-28; Zod v2 `RenewalNoticeCheckPassedPayload`: `contracts_checked` (nonneg int), `check_run_at` (datetime). Retention table: +2 rows (`enterprise.renewal_notice_overdue` HIGH 7yr CC4.1/CC2.2; `system.renewal_notice_check_passed` LOW 1yr CC4.1/A1.1 operational all-clear). Section cross-references: canonical event specs in OBSERVABILITY §51.4; pg_cron job 39 spec in OBSERVABILITY §51.2; SOC 2 evidence REN-E-002 and REN-OBS-E-001 in OBSERVABILITY §51.5 and SOC2_READINESS §79.4; INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-28 (job 39 stale recovery); COST_MODEL §42.3.2 (RENEW-NOTICE-01 invariant SQL). Privacy floor (both events): no `user_id`, no employee name, no email, no health value, no GDPR Art. 9 special category; `enterprise.renewal_notice_overdue` carries only FORM-internal `tenant_id` UUID + contract-level aggregate fields; `system.renewal_notice_check_passed` carries no `tenant_id`. **Closes `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §51.7` item 1 (P0/M11 — 🟢 2026-06-21).** Document header v2.27 → v2.28. **v2.27 · 2026-06-21 · owner: compliance-officer + platform-engineer + security-engineer**
*v2.27 (2026-06-21): +1 system.scim_guard_cfg_cache_stale (LOW, 1yr) — closes SOC2_READINESS.md §98.5 item 1 (P1/M13). Advisory event emitted by `billing-event-relay` Cloudflare Worker on `SCIM_KV.delete` failure during BDG KV cache invalidation; 3600s TTL safety-net self-clears within 1h. Payload: `tenant_id` (UUID), `kv_key` string (`scim:guard_cfg:<tenantId>`), `error_message` (truncated — no full stack trace), `emitted_at` (ISO-8601). No PagerDuty alert; compliance-officer reviews in GUARD-E-001 quarterly stale-advisory cross-check (§98.3 SQL). Privacy invariant: no `user_id`, no employee name/email, no health data, no GDPR Art. 9 special category. Advisory only (no HTTP 422 blocking gate; no chain ordering invariant). SOC 2 CC7.2 — converts silent KV invalidation failure into an observable DEC-030 advisory in the audit chain. Retention table: +1 row (`system.scim_guard_cfg_cache_stale` 1yr, SOC 2 CC7.2 advisory monitoring). Cross-ref: `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §35.5` (canonical emitter spec), `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §98.3` (CC7.2 monitoring rationale + stale-advisory cross-check SQL for GUARD-E-001), `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §91.2` (GUARD-E-001 collection SQL extended with stale-advisory cross-check per v3.22.1), `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §42.6` (A1.2 monitoring signal), DEC-069 (Option A KV caching decision), DEC-030. Owner: compliance-officer + platform-engineer.*

**v2.23 · 2026-06-20 · owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer + enterprise-architect**
*v2.23 (2026-06-20): +3 events — Enterprise Contract Renewal (COST_MODEL §42.7 · CC5.2/CC1.4/CC4.1/CC2.2/A1.1 · P0/M12). New section `### Enterprise Contract Renewal events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · COST_MODEL §42.7 · CC5.2/CC1.4/CC4.1/CC2.2/A1.1)` inserted before `## Export & delivery`. (1) `enterprise.renewal_notice_sent` (STANDARD, 7yr): RENEW-CHAIN-01 anchor event; compliance-officer emits ≥ 90 days before `renewal_date` via Admin Console Renewal Workflow Step 1; `days_until_renewal` Zod range (85–100) enforces the 90±5-day contractual window; `notice_type` enum (standard / price_escalation / non_renewal); `escalation_preview_pct` optional field for price_escalation notices; `sent_by` is a FORM-internal compliance-officer UUID (never a tenant employee). (2) `enterprise.renewal_escalation_calculated` (HIGH, 7yr): ESCALATION-CHAIN-01 prerequisite for `enterprise.contract_renewed` with `rate_basis = 'escalation'` within 150 days → HTTP 422 `ESCALATION_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` if absent; emitted at Admin Console Step 2 when CPI escalation applies; `prior_rate_per_seat_usd`, `cpi_rate_pct` (0–0.15), `applied_escalation_pct` (capped at 0.05), `cap_triggered` bool, `new_rate_per_seat_usd`, `floor_respected: z.literal(true)` (HTTP 422 if absent), `bls_report_date` date-only (never raw URL); `calculated_by` is a FORM-internal UUID. (3) `enterprise.contract_renewed` (STANDARD, 7yr): terminal event emitted at Admin Console Step 3 on DocuSign countersignature; triggers `enterprise_contracts` UPDATE + `enterprise_renewals` INSERT (migration 0084) in a single SERIALIZABLE transaction; RENEW-CHAIN-01 enforced (HTTP 422 `RENEW_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` if no prior `enterprise.renewal_notice_sent` within 120 days); ESCALATION-CHAIN-01 enforced when `rate_basis = 'escalation'`; `floor_respected: z.literal(true)` (HTTP 422 if absent — Zod Worker-layer + DDL-layer `CONSTRAINT chk_floor_always_respected CHECK (floor_respected = true)` per DATA_MODEL §43); `notice_event_id` UUID FK (RENEW-CHAIN-01 durable anchor); `pricing_exception_event_id` required when `rate_basis = 'retention_discount'`. Three Zod v2 schemas registered: `RenewalNoticeSentPayload`, `RenewalEscalationCalculatedPayload`, `ContractRenewedPayload` — canonical source `docs/COST_MODEL.md §42.7`. RENEW-NOTICE-01 monitoring invariant: `evidence-collection-cron` fires P1 PagerDuty `form-enterprise` when no `enterprise.renewal_notice_sent` found 85–95 days before `enterprise_contracts.renewal_date`; compliance-officer 5-day SLA. Retention table: +3 rows (`enterprise.renewal_notice_sent` STANDARD 7yr CC4.1/CC2.2; `enterprise.renewal_escalation_calculated` HIGH 7yr ESCALATION-CHAIN-01 prerequisite; `enterprise.contract_renewed` STANDARD 7yr CC5.2/CC1.4 REN-E-001 primary source). Three SOC 2 evidence artefacts: REN-E-001 (CC5.2/CC1.4 — annual `enterprise.contract_renewed` chain export, `floor_respected: true` attestation, RENEW-CHAIN-01 compliance cross-reference, 7yr); REN-E-002 (CC4.1/CC2.2 — quarterly `enterprise.renewal_notice_sent` compliance export, `days_until_renewal` distribution, 3yr); REN-E-003 (CC4.1/A1.1 — annual renewal conversion rate analysis vs. §42.8.2 model, fleet-aggregate ARR waterfall, 3yr). Privacy floor (all three events): no `user_id`, no employee name, no email, no health metric, no Art. 9 special category; all identifiers are FORM-internal team UUIDs or contract-level aggregates; `bls_report_date` date-only; `enterprise_renewals.form_api` REVOKED. Document title: v2.21 → v2.23. Cross-ref: `docs/COST_MODEL.md §42` (canonical event specs, Zod schemas, renewal economics); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §42.9` (REN-E-001/002/003 evidence artefact definitions); `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §43` (`enterprise_renewals` DDL + RLS — migration 0084; DDL-layer floor CHECK backstop); `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §100` (three-document chain cross-reference closure); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §31.5` (price floor rates); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §32` (Pricing Exception Approval — retention_discount path); `docs/MSA_TEMPLATE.md §6.1` + `§8.6` (auto-renewal + price escalation clause). Closes `docs/COST_MODEL.md §42.10` checklist item 1 (P0/M12 — register all three §42.7 DEC-030 events in `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §Enterprise` — 🟢 2026-06-20). Owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer + enterprise-architect.*

**v2.22 · 2026-06-20 · owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer + enterprise-architect**
*v2.22 (2026-06-20): +4 events — Enterprise Seat Expansion & Tier Upgrade (COST_MODEL §41.7 · CC5.2/CC1.4/A1.1 · P0/M10). New section `### Enterprise Seat Expansion & Tier Upgrade events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · COST_MODEL §41.7 · CC5.2/CC1.4/A1.1)` inserted before `## Export & delivery`. (1) `enterprise.expansion_initiated` (STANDARD, 7yr): anchor event — CSM or `tenant_admin` counter-signs expansion amendment; `amendment_id` UUID correlates the full expansion cluster; `trigger_type` enum (csm_initiated / tenant_initiated / milestone_driven); financial fields: `seats_before/after`, `seats_added`, `rate_per_seat_usd`, `expansion_invoice_usd`, `tier_crossing_credit_usd`, `net_invoice_usd`, `billing_variant` (immediate / anniversary_rollup), `discount_pct`, `floor_respected: z.literal(true)` (HTTP 422 if absent — EXP-CHAIN invariant); `approved_by` enum (csm / cs_lead / founder / founder_board); `decision_log_ref` required when `discount_pct > 15`. (2) `billing.seats_expanded` (STANDARD, 7yr): first full AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA registration of this event — DATA_MODEL §24.5.1 baseline fields (`tenant_id`, `previous_seats`, `new_seats`, `price_per_seat_usd_cents`) + COST_MODEL §41.7.2 financial extension (`expansion_arr_usd`, `net_invoice_usd`, `billing_variant`, `amendment_id`, `floor_respected: z.literal(true)`); emitted by `billing-worker` after `enterprise_seat_allocations` INSERT; EXP-CHAIN-01 successor (HTTP 422 if `enterprise.expansion_initiated` absent for same `amendment_id`); post-event hook triggers Cloudflare KV DELETE `bDG:${tenant_id}:guardConfig` (BDG cache invalidation — SSO_SCIM §35.7). (3) `enterprise.tier_upgraded` (STANDARD, 7yr): emitted only when `tier_crossing: true` (EXP-CHAIN-02); fields: `tier_before/after`, `seats_at_upgrade`, `old_rate_per_seat_usd`, `new_rate_per_seat_usd`, `tier_crossing_credit_usd`, `upgrade_effective_date`, `multi_year_commitment` (bool), `floor_respected: z.literal(true)`. (4) `enterprise.expansion_floor_enforced` (HIGH, 7yr): emitted synchronously when Admin Console pricing preview detects `proposed_rate < floor`; FLOOR-CHAIN-01: must precede `enterprise.expansion_initiated` for same `amendment_id`; fields: `requested_rate_usd`, `floor_rate_usd`, `applied_rate_usd` (= floor), `floor_source: literal('COST_MODEL_§31.5')`, `csm_notified`, `founder_approval_required`. Three chain invariants: EXP-CHAIN-01 (`billing.seats_expanded` must follow `expansion_initiated`, same `amendment_id`, HTTP 422 on absence); EXP-CHAIN-02 (`tier_upgraded` must follow when `tier_crossing: true`, HTTP 422 if initiating event absent); FLOOR-CHAIN-01 (`expansion_floor_enforced` must precede `expansion_initiated` for same `amendment_id` when floor is applied). Four Zod v2 schemas: `ExpansionInitiatedPayload`, `SeatsExpandedPayload`, `TierUpgradedPayload`, `ExpansionFloorEnforcedPayload` — canonical source `docs/COST_MODEL.md §41.7`; registered here for `emit-audit-event` Worker validation. Retention table: +4 rows (`enterprise.expansion_initiated` 7yr CC5.2/CC1.4/A1.1; `billing.seats_expanded` 7yr CC5.2/CC1.4 EXP-E-001 source; `enterprise.tier_upgraded` 7yr CC5.2/CC1.4; `enterprise.expansion_floor_enforced` 7yr HIGH CC5.2/CC1.4). Two SOC 2 evidence artefacts: EXP-E-001 (CC5.2/CC1.4 — annual `billing.seats_expanded` DEC-030 export, `floor_respected: true` on every row, 7yr, `compliance/evidence/expansion/EXP-E-001_<YYYY>.csv`); EXP-E-002 (CC4.1 — quarterly expansion pipeline vs. adoption health band cross-reference, 3yr, `compliance/evidence/expansion/EXP-E-002_<YYYY>-Q<N>.csv`); registration of EXP-E-001/002 in SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4/§80.3 pending P1/M11 (COST_MODEL §41.10 item 7). Auditor narratives: CC5.2 (two-layer floor enforcement: system auto-cap + chain invariant); CC1.4 (immutable floor rejection record + `approved_by` authority chain + `decision_log_ref` linkage); A1.1 (`seats_after` + `tier_after` + `rate_per_seat_usd` as contracted capacity evidence). Privacy floor (all four events): no `user_id`, no employee name/email, no health data, no Art. 9 special category; aggregate contract-level integers and floats only; `enterprise_contracts` financial rate columns: `form_api` REVOKED. Cross-ref: `docs/COST_MODEL.md §41.7` (canonical event specs and Zod schemas); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §41.9` (EXP-E-001/002 evidence artefact definitions); `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §24.5.1` (`billing.seats_expanded` baseline schema origin); `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §35` (BDG `contracted_seats` — KV cache invalidation obligation on `billing.seats_expanded` emission); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §31.5` (price floors — `floor_rate_usd` source); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §41.5` (discount authority matrix — `approved_by` authority levels). Closes `docs/COST_MODEL.md §41.10` checklist item 1 (P0/M10 — register all four §41.7 DEC-030 events in AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §Enterprise — 🟢 2026-06-20). **v2.21 · 2026-06-20 · owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer + enterprise-architect**
*v2.21 (2026-06-20): +7 events — CAEP / SSF (SSO_SCIM §23 + §36 · DEC-072 · CC6.3/CC6.6/CC7.2/CC7.3 · P0/M4 + P0/M5). New section `### CAEP / SSF events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · SSO §23 + §36 · DEC-072 · CC6.3/CC6.6/CC7.2/CC7.3)` inserted after `### Google Directory Sync events`. Six original events from §23 (v1.5, 2026-06-01): (1) `sso.caep_event_received` (STANDARD, 7yr) — primary CC6.3 evidence record; every successfully processed CAEP/RISC SET; payload: `tenant_id`, `event_type` (URI), `action_taken` (6-value enum), `set_jti_hash` SHA-256, `idp_subject_hash` SHA-256. (2) `sso.caep_session_revoked` (HIGH, 7yr) — session/credential-change/token-claims-change event response; cross-refs `caep_event_received` via `set_jti_hash`; payload: `tenant_id`, `user_id_hash`, `session_id_hash` (nullable), `caep_event_type`, `set_jti_hash`. (3) `sso.caep_user_suspended` (HIGH, 7yr) — `account-disabled` response; `account_suspended:{tenant_id}:{user_id}` KV TTL 72h; payload: `tenant_id`, `user_id_hash`, `idp_subject_hash`, `caep_event_type`. (4) `sso.caep_user_purged` (CRITICAL, 7yr) — `account-purged` response; GDPR Art. 17 deletion SLA start-of-clock; **chain commit before KV write** (ordering invariant §23.8 / R-05 on violation); two-person rule on manual override; payload: `tenant_id`, `user_id_hash`, `idp_subject_hash`, `gdpr_deletion_queue_id`. (5) `sso.caep_stream_error` (HIGH, 7yr) — SET validation failure; 7-value controlled `error_type` enum (hmac_invalid / jwks_kid_not_found / set_signature_invalid / set_replay_detected / tenant_mismatch / rate_limit_exceeded / reregistration_failed_post_cert_rotation — last value added by DEC-072 §36); no free-form messages; primary AL-CAEP-01 signal; payload: `tenant_id`, `error_type`, `set_jti_hash` (nullable). (6) `sso.caep_stream_registered` (HIGH, 7yr) — initial provisioning or `caep_reregister_sweep` job 37 re-registration; `admin_user_id_hash` sentinel for sweep-triggered events; payload: `tenant_id`, `idp_type` (3-value enum), `caep_delivery_mode`, `admin_user_id_hash`. One new event from §36 (DEC-072, v2.8, 2026-06-19): (7) `sso.caep_reregistration_queued` (STANDARD, 7yr) — `cert-expiry-check` Worker hook on `cert_rotation_state → 'complete'` for CAEP-active/error tenants; sets `caep_reregistration_required = TRUE` (migration 0082); payload: `tenant_id`, `trigger` (4-value enum: cert_rotation/stream_error/manual/initial), `caep_status_at_queue_time` (active/error). Vocabulary extension: `sso.google_directory_sync_error` `error_code` controlled vocabulary (registered in `### Google Directory Sync events`) extended by `'cache_eviction_failed_on_risc_hijacking'` (DEC-072 §36.4 — RISC hijacking cache eviction failure path; non-fatal advisory; no PagerDuty). CAEP ordering constraint (§23.8): `sso.caep_user_purged` chain commit must precede `tenant_users.status` mutation and KV write — retroactive insertion → R-05. Cert-rotation chain ordering: `sso.caep_reregistration_queued` (T1) must precede `sso.caep_stream_registered` (T2) for same `tenant_id`. Four SOC 2 evidence artefacts CC6-E-CAEP-001 (CC6.3 — `caep_events` export), CC6-E-CAEP-002 (CC6.3/CC6.6 — `audit_log` `sso.caep_*` chain export), CC6-E-CAEP-003 (CC6.3 — stream registration records sanitised), CC6-E-CAEP-004 (CC7.2/CC7.3 — AL-CAEP-01/AL-CAEP-03 PagerDuty history). Auditor narrative for CC6.3: `caep_event_received` + `caep_events` SET `iat` delta demonstrates < 30 s IdP-to-revocation latency; `caep_user_purged` CRITICAL chain event is immutable GDPR Art. 17 deletion SLA start-of-clock. `sso.google_directory_sync_error` vocabulary extension note added to `### Google Directory Sync events` (above table). Retention table: +7 rows (caep_event_received STANDARD 7yr CC6.3; caep_session_revoked HIGH 7yr CC6.3/CC6.6; caep_user_suspended HIGH 7yr CC6.6; caep_user_purged CRITICAL 7yr CC6.3/P1.2; caep_stream_error HIGH 7yr CC7.2/CC7.3; caep_stream_registered HIGH 7yr CC6.3; caep_reregistration_queued STANDARD 7yr CC6.3). Closes `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §23.13` checklist item 9 (P0/M4 — register all 6 original DEC-030 CAEP event types) and `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §36.6` checklist item 4 (P0/M5 — register `sso.caep_reregistration_queued` + `sso.google_directory_sync_error` vocabulary extension). Privacy floor (all 7 events): no plaintext IdP `sub`, email, FORM `user_id`, health data, or Art. 9 special category in any payload; `set_jti_hash`, `idp_subject_hash`, `user_id_hash`, `admin_user_id_hash` are SHA-256 digests; `caep_events` table `form_api` ZERO-ROWS RLS; `gdpr_deletion_queue_id` is a Cloudflare Queue message ID (infrastructure reference — no personal data). Cross-ref: `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §23` (canonical CAEP/SSF architecture — 10-step pipeline, action dispatch, caep_events table DDL, 5 alerting rules AL-CAEP-01..05, 4 evidence artefacts CC6-E-CAEP-001..004); `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §23.8` (ordering invariant — chain commit before state mutation for `caep_user_purged`); `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §23.11` (CC6-E-CAEP-001..004 artefact definitions + SOC 2 mapping); `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §23.13` (P0/M4 implementation checklist — item 9 closed); `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §36.2.4` (`cert-expiry-check` hook — `sso.caep_reregistration_queued` emitter); `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §36.4.3` (RISC hijacking cache eviction — `sso.google_directory_sync_error` vocabulary extension); `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §36.6` (P0/M5 implementation checklist — item 4 closed); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §12.6` (pg_cron job 37 `caep_reregister_sweep` — registered v0.4 2026-06-19); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §6.2` (AL-CAEP-01..05 — to be registered per §23.13 item 10 P0/M4); `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-05` (chain integrity violation — CAEP ordering invariant violation path); `docs/DECISION_LOG.md DEC-072` (OQ-SSO-23.1/23.3/23.4 resolution — `sso.caep_reregistration_queued` introduced). Owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer + enterprise-architect.*

**v2.20 · 2026-06-19 · owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer + enterprise-architect**
*v2.20 (2026-06-19): +1 `system.gdir_alert_check_stale` event (OBSERVABILITY §12.6 job 35 · CC7.2/CC7.3 · P1/M4). Registers the DEC-030 HMAC-chained monitoring gap event noted in OBSERVABILITY.md v4.7.1 (2026-06-19) pg_cron job 35 registration — obligation flagged in CHANGELOG v6.37.1 as pending `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §System` registration. Event added to `### System` section after `system.quarterly_perf_reference_completed`. Specification: pg_cron job 35 (`google_directory_alert_check`, `*/5 * * * *`) is the 5-min CC7.2/CC7.3 monitoring job that fires AL-SSO-GDIR-01 (PagerDuty P2 `form-platform` when ≥ 3 `sso.google_directory_sync_error` DEC-030 events within a rolling 15-min window for a single tenant) and AL-SSO-GDIR-02 (Slack `#alerts-sso` on per-tenant P95 fetch latency > 3,000 ms sustained for 5 consecutive 5-min windows). When the health supervisor detects any gap > 6 min (3-run tolerance of 5-min cadence) via `pg_cron.job_run_details`, it emits `system.gdir_alert_check_stale` (LOW severity, 1yr retention); PagerDuty P1 `form-devops` → devops-lead + platform-engineer; dedup key `gdir-alert-check-stale`. Payload: `{job_name: 'google_directory_alert_check', schedule: '*/5 * * * *', last_successful_run (ISO 8601), gap_minutes (positive number — actual gap since last successful run), freshness_window_minutes: 6 (literal)}`. Privacy invariant: no `user_id`, no `tenant_id`, no health data — infrastructure monitoring signal only; stale gap is a per-job aggregate (not per-tenant). SOC 2 quarterly evidence artefact: SSO-OBS-E-007 Part B (zero-gap confirmation throughout the quarter; absence of `system.gdir_alert_check_stale` events in the evidence window is the affirmative attestation). DEC-067 signal-source coherence: the `sso.google_directory_sync_error` records that serve as AL-SSO-GDIR-01/02 alerting signal are themselves HMAC-chained — a gap in the monitoring job does not affect chain integrity but does create a CC7.2 control degradation window. Retention table: +1 row (`system.gdir_alert_check_stale` — 1yr, CC7.2 operational signal). Closes `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §48.8` item 3 (P1/M4 — `system.gdir_alert_check_stale` registration obligation — 🟢). Cross-ref: `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §12.6` (job 35 canonical registration with DEC-067 signal-source coherence note), `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §48` (AL-SSO-GDIR-01/02 canonical alert definitions + SSO-OBS-E-007 Part A/B), `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §92` (SSO-OBS-E-007 quarterly evidence artefact registration), DEC-067, DEC-030. Owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer + enterprise-architect.*

**v2.19 · 2026-06-19 · owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer + enterprise-architect + customer-success**
*v2.19 (2026-06-19): +5 events — SCIM Bulk Deprovision Guard (SSO_SCIM §34 · DEC-066 · OQ-R24-01 · CC6.3/A1.2/CC7.2/CC9.2 · P0/M13). New section `### SCIM Bulk Deprovision Guard events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · SSO §34 · DEC-066 · CC6.3/A1.2/CC7.2/CC9.2)` inserted before `### Security & tenant isolation events`. (1) `scim.bulk_deprovision_blocked` (HIGH, 7yr): SCIM Worker synchronous preventive gate; emitted when incoming SCIM push would deprovision ≥ `bdg_threshold_pct`% of contracted seats in 5-minute rolling window; HTTP 422 `BULK_DEPROVISION_THRESHOLD_EXCEEDED`; payload: `tenant_id`, `deprovisioned_count`, `contracted_seats`, `threshold_pct` (int 5–100, default 20), `window_key` (5-min epoch bucket), `scim_source`. (2) `scim.bulk_deprovision_override_issued` (HIGH, 7yr): CSM-via-PAM issues time-limited override token; payload: `tenant_id`, `override_token_hash` (SHA-256), `expires_at_iso` (+1h), `override_issued_by_email_hash` (SHA-256(lowercase(email))), `pam_session_id`, `tenant_owner_request_event_id`. (3) `scim.bulk_deprovision_override_used` (HIGH, 7yr): override consumed; auto-revoked after first use; payload: `tenant_id`, `override_token_hash`, `deprovisioned_count`, `contracted_seats`, `threshold_pct`, `override_used_by_scim_source`, `auto_revoked: true`. (4) `scim.bulk_deprovision_threshold_updated` (STANDARD, 7yr): `tenant_owner` updates threshold via Admin Dashboard; `update_bdg_threshold()` SECURITY DEFINER RPC; payload: `tenant_id`, `old_threshold_pct`, `new_threshold_pct`, `updated_by_email_hash`, `updated_by_role`. (5) `system.scim_guard_repeated_trigger` (STANDARD, 1yr): advisory; pg_cron job 33 daily at 02:00 UTC; ≥ 3 blocked events in 7-day window per tenant; GUARD-CHAIN-01 advisory invariant; payload: `tenant_id`, `trigger_count`, `window_start_iso`, `recommended_action: 'review_idp_config'`; PagerDuty P2 `form-customer-success`. SOC 2 evidence artefact GUARD-E-001 (CC6.3/A1.2/CC7.2/CC9.2) registered in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §91` (v3.16.0, 2026-06-19). Retention table: +5 rows (blocked/override_issued/override_used/threshold_updated 7yr; repeated_trigger 1yr). Closes `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §34.11` checklist item 5 (P0/M13 — register all five DEC-030 events before enterprise GA). Cross-ref: `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §34` (guard design — `enforceDeprovisionGuard()`, `getGuardConfig()`, `revokeActiveOverride()`); `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §34.7` (CSM override protocol — PAM §24, two-person approval); migration `0079_bulk_deprovision_guard.sql` (three new columns, two constraints, pg_cron job 33); `docs/DECISION_LOG.md DEC-066` (OQ-R24-01 resolution); `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-24.15` item 10 (P1 OQ-R24-01 — closed 2026-06-19); `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §91` (GUARD-E-001 evidence cross-reference patch). *v2.18 (2026-06-19): +5 events — Enterprise Adoption Monitoring (COST_MODEL §40.8 · CC4.1/CC2.2/CC7.3/CC4.2/A1.1 · P0/M10). New section `### Enterprise Adoption Monitoring events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · COST_MODEL §40.8 · CC4.1/CC2.2/CC7.3/CC4.2/A1.1)` inserted before `## Export & delivery`. (1) `enterprise.adoption_snapshot_filed` (STANDARD, 3yr): monthly `enterprise_adoption_snapshots` INSERT record (migration 0078); emitted by `evidence-collection-cron` or CSM manual trigger; payload: `tenant_id`, `snapshot_month` (`/^\d{4}-\d{2}-01$/`), `contracted_seats`, `activated_seats`, `activation_rate_pct`, `wau_count`, `wau_rate_pct`, `habitual_seat_count`, `habitual_rate_pct`, `wau_health_band` (green/amber/red), `coaching_sessions_total`, `filed_by` (evidence-collection-cron|csm-manual); no `user_id`, no per-user data, `coaching_engaged_seats < 5` suppressed at API layer. (2) `enterprise.adoption_milestone_reached` (STANDARD, 3yr): emitted once per milestone per tenant on first threshold crossing; milestone enum: `s1_activation_40pct`/`s2_wau_30pct`/`s3_habitual_20pct`/`s2_wau_40pct_green`; idempotency enforced. (3) `enterprise.adoption_health_downgraded` (HIGH, 3yr): emitted by monthly snapshot run when `wau_health_band` worsens (Green→Amber, Green→Red, Amber→Red); payload: `tenant_id`, `snapshot_month`, `prior_band`, `current_band`, `wau_rate_pct`, `contracted_seats`, `acv_usd` (aggregate contract value); ADO-CHAIN-01 monitoring invariant: `evidence-collection-cron` weekly sweep checks for CSM follow-up within 10 business days; emits `system.csm_followup_overdue` (LOW advisory) if absent. (4) `enterprise.qbr_completed` (STANDARD, 3yr): CSM manual trigger via Admin Console "Complete QBR" modal (cannot be back-dated > 7 days); QBR-PRIV-01 chain invariant: `privacy_floor_verified: z.literal(true)` required — HTTP 422 if absent or false; payload: `tenant_id`, `qbr_date`, `tier`, `contracted_seats`, `wau_rate_pct_at_qbr`, `wau_health_band_at_qbr`, `renewal_date` (date only), `expansion_discussed` (bool), `privacy_floor_verified` (literal true); no attendee names, no per-user metrics. (5) `system.csm_followup_overdue` (LOW, 1yr): ADO-CHAIN-01 advisory; emitted by weekly `evidence-collection-cron` sweep; payload: `tenant_id`, `downgrade_event_id` (UUID FK to adoption_health_downgraded chain record), `days_since_downgrade` (int ≥ 10), `current_band` (amber|red), `acv_usd`; non-blocking — no HTTP 422 gate; routes to compliance-officer SIEM review, not PagerDuty. Zod v2 schemas: `AdoptionSnapshotFiledPayload`, `AdoptionMilestoneReachedPayload`, `AdoptionHealthDowngradedPayload`, `QbrCompletedPayload`, `CsmFollowupOverduePayload` — canonical source `docs/COST_MODEL.md §40.8`; registered here for `emit-audit-event` Worker validation. Retention table: +6 rows (enterprise.adoption_snapshot_filed 3yr CC4.1/A1.1; enterprise.adoption_milestone_reached 3yr CC4.1; enterprise.adoption_health_downgraded 3yr CC7.3/CC4.2; enterprise.qbr_completed 3yr CC2.2/CC4.1; system.csm_followup_overdue 1yr advisory). SOC 2 auditor narratives for CC4.1, CC2.2, CC7.3, CC4.2, A1.1. Evidence artefacts: ADO-E-001/002/003 (already registered in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §90`, v3.15.0, 2026-06-19). Closes `docs/COST_MODEL.md §40.10` checklist item 1 (P0/M10 — register all four §40.8 DEC-030 events + `system.csm_followup_overdue` advisory in AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §Enterprise + §System before first enterprise pilot go-live). Cross-ref: `docs/COST_MODEL.md §40.8` (Zod schema source of truth); `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §90` (ADO-E-001/002/003 master evidence table); `docs/ENTERPRISE.md` (privacy floor — no individual employee data to HR). Owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer + enterprise-architect + customer-success.*

**v2.17 · 2026-06-19 · owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer + enterprise-architect**
*v2.17 (2026-06-19): +2 `siem.consent_addendum_signed` / `siem.consent_addendum_revoked` DEC-030 HMAC-chained events (SIEM Consent & Export Governance · OBSERVABILITY §47 · DEC-065 · P0/M5). Closes `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §47.11` checklist item 2 (P0/M5 — register both events in AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §SIEM namespace before first enterprise SIEM export goes live). New section `### SIEM Consent & Export Governance events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · OBSERVABILITY §47 · DEC-065 · SOC 2 CC9.2/CC1.1)` inserted before `## Export & delivery`. (1) `siem.consent_addendum_signed` (HIGH, 7yr): SIEM-CONSENT-01 chain prerequisite; emitted synchronously by `emit-audit-event` Worker on MSA Addendum 4 in-product acceptance (Admin Dashboard → Integrations → SIEM Export → Sign and Activate); payload (Zod v2 — `SiemConsentAddendumSignedPayload`): `tenant_id` (string max 64), `addendum_version` (regex `/^\d+\.\d+$/`), `siem_destination_type` (5-value enum: splunk/sentinel/datadog/qradar/webhook), `endpoint_url_hash` (SHA-256[:32] — 32 hex chars; URL never in chain), `signed_by_email_hash` (SHA-256(lowercase(email)) — 64 hex chars; plaintext never stored by FORM), `signed_at_iso` (ISO 8601 UTC), `docusign_envelope_id` (UUID optional — set async when DocuSign envelope created; export not blocked pending DocuSign). (2) `siem.consent_addendum_revoked` (HIGH, 7yr): revocation record; emitted synchronously on "Revoke Addendum" admin confirmation; payload (Zod v2 — `SiemConsentAddendumRevokedPayload`): `tenant_id`, `addendum_version`, `siem_destination_type`, `endpoint_url_hash`, `revoked_by_email_hash` (SHA-256(lowercase(email)) — 64 hex chars), `revoked_at_iso` (ISO 8601), `revocation_reason` (5-value enum: tenant_requested/compliance_officer_required/contract_termination/addendum_version_update/security_incident). SIEM-CONSENT-01 formal invariant: for any `tenant_id` T, if `siem.tenant_export_enabled` at chain position N, then `siem.consent_addendum_signed` at position M < N for same T; `emit-audit-event` Worker returns HTTP 422 `SIEM_CONSENT_01_NO_ADDENDUM` on violation → P1 PagerDuty `form-security`. Revocation does not reset invariant; new re-acceptance (new `siem.consent_addendum_signed`) re-satisfies it before re-activation. Fail-closed: if Worker returns non-2xx, Admin Dashboard action is rolled back. SOC 2 evidence artefact SIEM-CONSENT-E-001 (CC9.2/CC1.1): quarterly export of both event types; cross-check query (`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tenant_siem_configs WHERE export_enabled = true AND addendum_signed_at IS NULL` must return zero); zero-event quarters filed as affirmative SIEM-CONSENT-01 attestation JSON; 7yr retention; path `compliance/evidence/siem-consent/SIEM-CONSENT-E-001_<YYYY-QN>.csv` (or `.json` zero-event attestation); registered in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §88` (v3.9.2, 2026-06-18). Retention table: +2 rows (`siem.consent_addendum_signed` 7yr CC9.2/CC1.1; `siem.consent_addendum_revoked` 7yr CC9.2/CC1.1). Privacy floor (both events): `endpoint_url_hash` SHA-256[:32] (32 hex chars); `signed_by_email_hash` / `revoked_by_email_hash` SHA-256(lowercase(email)) (64 hex chars); no `user_id`, no Art. 9 health data, no body-composition values, no coaching content; `tenant_id` = org slug; `form_api` REVOKED from `tenant_siem_configs`. Cross-ref: `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §47.7` (SIEM-CONSENT-01 invariant + Worker enforcement pseudo-code); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §47.8` (canonical Zod v2 schemas — source of truth; this section registers them in the DEC-030 SIEM namespace); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §47.9` (SIEM-CONSENT-E-001 SOC 2 evidence artefact definition); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §47.11 item 2` (P0/M5 — 🟢 closed by this patch); `docs/MSA_TEMPLATE.md §Addendum 4` (five-clause SIEM DPA template v1.0, DEC-065, 2026-06-18 — the instrument these events record acceptance/revocation of); `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §40.3` (migration 0076 — `addendum_signed_at TIMESTAMPTZ` + `addendum_version TEXT` + `idx_tsc_addendum_signed` partial index on `tenant_siem_configs`); `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §88` (SIEM-CONSENT-E-001 registered); `docs/DECISION_LOG.md DEC-065` (decision rationale). Owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer + enterprise-architect.*

**v2.15 · 2026-06-17 · owner: compliance-officer + platform-engineer + security-engineer**
*v2.16 (2026-06-17): +1 `scim.ip_kv_invalidation_error` advisory event (SSO §33.3 · DEC-062 OQ-SSO-32.2 resolution). Closes SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §33.7 checklist item 3 (P0/M8 — register `scim.ip_kv_invalidation_error` in AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §SCIM namespace). New section `### SCIM IP KV Cache Invalidation Error events` inserted after `### SCIM IP Enforcement Misconfiguration events`. One new event: `scim.ip_kv_invalidation_error` (STANDARD, 3yr, non-blocking advisory). Trigger: `env.SSO_KV.delete('scim_ip_cfg:' + tenantId)` throws inside `PATCH /v1/admin/scim/ip-enforcement` handler after a successful `update_scim_ip_enforcement_flag()` Supabase RPC write; HTTP 200 returned regardless. Payload: `tenant_id` (UUID), `kv_error_class` (string max 64 — mirrors `scim.session_revocation_kv_fallback` pattern), `new_enforcement_value` (bool — toggle direction; disable path is the higher-risk window: enforcement stays active ≤ 5 min), `fallback_propagation_ttl_s: 300` (literal int — confirms eventual-consistency window). Emitter: `form_system` — Admin Dashboard API `apps/admin-dashboard-api/src/routes/scim-ip-enforcement.ts` via `AUDIT_QUEUE` binding (management-plane event, distinct from SCIM Worker emitters). No HMAC chain ordering invariant — standalone advisory (same non-blocking pattern as `scim.ip_enforcement_misconfigured`). No PagerDuty alert — compliance-officer weekly SIEM review only. Retention table: +1 row (`scim.ip_kv_invalidation_error` — 3yr, SOC 2 CC7.2 infrastructure fault detection). Privacy floor: `tenant_id` and error metadata only — no `user_id`, no employee PII, no CIDR values, no health data; `form_api` REVOKED from emitter path; `kv_error_class` is a structural error category tag. Cross-ref: `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §33.3` (OQ-SSO-32.2 resolution — KV delete handler design and failure semantics); `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §33.4` (DEC-030 changes — `scim.ip_kv_invalidation_error` canonical definition); `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §33.7 item 3` (P0/M8 — closed by this patch); `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §32.3.4` (`scim_ip_cfg:{tenant_id}` KV key + 5-min base TTL — unchanged; active invalidation is additive per §33.3.3); `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §32.5` (`scim.ip_enforcement_misconfigured` — sister advisory event; same STANDARD/3yr/non-blocking registration pattern); migration `0077_scim_ip_enforcement_toggle_rpc.sql` (Postgres RPC that precedes the KV delete call); `docs/DECISION_LOG.md DEC-062`. Owner: compliance-officer + platform-engineer.*
*v2.15 (2026-06-17): +2 `mobile.replay_*` Mobile Session Replay events — closes `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §46.11` item 3 (P0, M5 — register both DEC-030 events in AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §Mobile namespace before first enterprise debug session using replay) and `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §86.7` item 3 (P0, M5). New section `### Mobile Session Replay events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · OBSERVABILITY §46 · DEC-063 · SOC 2 CC6.7/CC7.2/P1.1)` inserted after `### Mobile SSO Browser Mode events`. (1) `mobile.replay_config_updated` (STANDARD, 3yr): emitted by feature-flag Worker when `REPLAY_ENTERPRISE_ENABLED` or `REPLAY_SESSION_RATE_10PCT` flag changes for an enterprise tenant; payload: `tenant_id` (org slug), `action` (enabled/disabled/rate_changed), `new_session_rate_pct` (int 0–100), `new_error_rate_pct` (int 0–100), `changed_by` (devops-lead UUID — never end-user or HR), `linear_ticket_ref` (≤ 20 chars), `duration_hours_requested` (nullable — null on disable), `clinical_safety_sign_off_at` (ISO datetime, required on first enable for a new screen scope); audit gate for §46.5 on-demand replay protocol; REPLAY-CHAIN-01 invariant anchor. (2) `mobile.replay_tier_violation` (HIGH, 7yr): emitted by React Native `beforeSend` route gate in `apps/mobile/src/observability/sentry-replay-config.ts` when a replay frame for a non-Tier-S route is intercepted and dropped before Sentry egress; payload: `route_name_hash` (SHA-256 — plaintext route name never stored), `tenant_id` (org slug), `frame_dropped: true`, `violation_type` (tier_r | tier_h); **zero-fire expected in production** — any emission triggers P1 PagerDuty `form-compliance` + `form-devops` (NOT R-05 — process-compliance signal, not chain tamper) and §46.7 inadvertent capture escalation protocol. REPLAY-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant: `mobile.replay_config_updated` with `action: 'enabled'` must precede Sentry session ingestion for the affected tenant; Sentry creation timestamp predating the chain record by > 5 min → P1 PagerDuty `form-compliance` + `form-devops`. Zod v2 schemas provided for both events (canonical source: `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §46.6`). Retention table: +2 rows (`mobile.replay_config_updated` 3yr CC6.7/REPLAY-E-001; `mobile.replay_tier_violation` 7yr CC7.2+P1.1/REPLAY-E-003). SOC 2 evidence: REPLAY-E-001 (CC6.7 — on-demand per enterprise debug window; `mobile.replay_config_updated` chain records confirm §46.5 protocol followed); REPLAY-E-003 (CC7.2 + P1.1 — quarterly `mobile.replay_tier_violation` export; zero-event quarters affirm Art. 9 data was not captured). Both artefacts registered in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §86`. Privacy floor (both events): no `user_id`, no employee name, no Art. 9 health or body-composition data; `route_name_hash` is SHA-256 — plaintext route name never stored; `tenant_id` is always org slug; consumer-tier replay permanently 0/0; `form_api` NO ACCESS to `compliance/evidence/replay/`. Cross-ref: `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §46.6` (canonical payload specs and REPLAY-CHAIN-01 invariant); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §46.5` (enterprise on-demand protocol — `mobile.replay_config_updated` is audit gate for step 3); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §46.7` (inadvertent capture escalation — triggered by non-zero `mobile.replay_tier_violation`); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §46.8` (clinical-safety gate for `REPLAY_ALLOWED_ROUTES`); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §46.9` (REPLAY-E-001/002/003 definitions); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §46.11 item 3` (🟢 closed by this patch); `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §86` (REPLAY-E-001, REPLAY-E-003 registered); `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §86.7 item 3` (🟢 closed by this patch); `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-22` (privacy floor breach — co-activated on non-zero violation + health-adjacent content confirmed); `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-01` (data breach — co-activated on Art. 9 confirmation); `docs/DECISION_LOG.md DEC-063`. Owner: compliance-officer + platform-engineer + security-engineer.*

*v2.13 (2026-06-16): +6 `tenant.invite_*` / `tenant.bulk_invite_*` Enterprise seat invitation events — closes `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §27.15` checklist item 7 (P0, M5 — register all six DEC-030 events in AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md §6 before invite feature ships to any tenant). New section `### Enterprise seat invitation events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · DATA_MODEL §27 · CC6.1/CC6.2/CC6.3/P4.2/P5.2)` inserted before `### Enterprise DSAR deletion certificate events`. Six events registered: (1) `tenant.invite_sent` (STANDARD, 7yr — `email_hash` HMAC-SHA256 only; `invited_by` UUID per CC6.2; `bulk_import_job_id` nullable; emitted on create and resend); (2) `tenant.invite_accepted` (STANDARD, 7yr — `accepted_by` new user UUID; `linked_via` enum `registration`\|`scim` distinguishes auto-link path); (3) `tenant.invite_expired` (STANDARD, 3yr — pg_cron `invite_expiry_sweep` daily 02:30 UTC; `bulk_import_job_id` nullable); (4) `tenant.invite_revoked` (HIGH, 7yr — `revoked_by` nullable for automated offboarding revocation; `reason` 3-value enum: `admin_action`\|`offboarding`\|`seat_reduction`; CC6.3 irreversible compliance action); (5) `tenant.bulk_invite_started` (STANDARD, 3yr — INVITE-BULK-CHAIN-01 window open; `total_rows` positive int); (6) `tenant.bulk_invite_completed` (STANDARD, 3yr — INVITE-BULK-CHAIN-01 window close; 3-count summary + `status` enum). INVITE-BULK-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant: for any `bulk_import_job_id`, `tenant.bulk_invite_started` must precede all `tenant.invite_sent` events for that ID, and `tenant.bulk_invite_completed` must follow all of them; `emit-audit-event` Worker returns HTTP 422 on violation; P1 PagerDuty `form-platform` (not R-05 — compliance-signal ordering, not chain tamper). Six Zod v2 schemas provided (canonical source: `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §27.11`). Five SOC 2 evidence artefacts: INV-E-001 (CC6.1 — seat ceiling enforcement; semi-annual seat assignment export), INV-E-002 (CC6.2 — annual `tenant.invite_sent` chain export confirming `invited_by` for every access grant), INV-E-003 (CC6.3 — annual `tenant.invite_revoked` chain export + `revoked_at` cross-check ≤ 60 seconds), INV-E-004 (P4.2 — version-controlled invite email template with Privacy Policy link before CTA), INV-E-005 (P5.2 — pg_cron `invite_email_expiry_cleanup` execution log confirming 30-day post-expiry `invited_email` nullification). Retention table: +2 rows (7yr for `tenant.invite_sent` / `tenant.invite_accepted` / `tenant.invite_revoked`; 3yr for `tenant.invite_expired` / `tenant.bulk_invite_started` / `tenant.bulk_invite_completed`). Privacy floor (all six events): `invited_email` never in any payload; `email_hash` = HMAC-SHA256 with `INVITE_HASH_SALT`; HR (`tenant_manager`) blocked from invitation data; no health data, no coaching data. Cross-ref: `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §27.11` (canonical event spec and payload fields), `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §27.12` (INV-E-001–005 SOC 2 evidence artefact definitions), `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §27.15` item 7 (P0 checklist — 🟢 closed by this patch), `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §25` (§25 offboarding pipeline emits `tenant.invite_revoked` with `reason: 'offboarding'`), `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §12.6` (pg_cron job 10 `invite_expiry_sweep` registry), `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §CC6.2` (INV-E-002 evidence row), `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §CC6.3` (INV-E-003 evidence row), `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-05` (INVITE-BULK-CHAIN-01 invariant violations — chain integrity escalation).
