**v2.89 · 2026-07-04 · owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer + platform-engineer**
*v2.90 (2026-07-04): +4 BCL monitoring + admin revocation events — new sections `### BCL Chain Integrity Monitor events` and `### BCL Admin Session Bulk Revocation events` inserted between `### OIDC Back-Channel Logout events` and `### Enterprise Adoption Monitoring events`. Closes `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-73.12` item 1 (P1/M8 — `enterprise.admin_sessions_bulk_revoked` HIGH/7yr), `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-74.12` item 1 (P1/M8 — `security.bcl_chain_01_violation` CRITICAL/7yr + `security.bcl_chain_01_violation_closed` HIGH/7yr), and `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §70.9` item 11 (P1/M8 — `system.bcl_chain_check_passed` LOW/1yr + `security.bcl_chain_01_violation` companion). **BCL Chain Integrity Monitor (§BCL-Chain-Monitor-Events):** (1) `security.bcl_chain_01_violation` (CRITICAL, 7yr): hourly BCL-CHAIN-01 check job (§70.9 item 4, OQ-BCL-OBS-01 open for pg_cron vs. CF Workers Cron deployment) emits per orphaned `{tenant_id, bcl_request_id}` pair detected by R-74-C2 retrospective SQL; BCL-VIO-CHAIN-01 anchor; P0 PagerDuty `form-security`; no auto-resolve; payload: `tenant_id` UUID, `bcl_request_id` UUID, `incident_id` UUID (BCL-VIO-CHAIN-01 anchor), `violation_type` literal `retrospective_sql`, `detected_at` datetime, `revoked_event_at` datetime; self-referential note: H4/H5 fallback to `compliance_reviewer` PAM direct INSERT per R-74.7. (2) `security.bcl_chain_01_violation_closed` (HIGH, 7yr): IC PAM-elevated terminal event; compliance-officer sign-off required; `r74_c2_zero_row_confirmed: true` literal gate prevents emission before SQL confirms clean; BCL-VIO-CHAIN-01 terminal — `emit-audit-event` Worker HTTP 422 `BCL_VIO_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` on inversion; payload: `tenant_id` UUID, `bcl_request_id` UUID, `incident_id` UUID (terminal), `pam_request_id` UUID, `root_cause_code` enum H1–H5 per R-74.5, `incident_reference` string, `artefact_path` string (R2 BCL-CHN-E-001 path), `r74_c2_zero_row_confirmed` literal true. (3) `system.bcl_chain_check_passed` (LOW, 1yr): all-clear path — hourly job R-74-C2 SQL returns 0 rows; fleet-wide one per run; no per-tenant disclosure; `bcl_pairs_checked` int ≥ 0, `check_run_at` datetime; surfaces in BCL-OBS-E-001 quarterly report (§70.8). BCL-VIO-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant table. BCL-CHN-E-001 evidence artefact (CC7.2/CC7.3/CC8.1, per-activation, 7yr WORM, `compliance/evidence/oidc-bcl/chain-violations/bcl-chn-e-001-{incident_id}-{YYYY-MM-DD}/`); compliance-officer sign-off required; R2 subfolder provision pending R-74.12 item 3 (P1/M8); registration in SOC2_READINESS §79.4 pending R-74.12 item 4 (P1/M8). SOC 2 auditor narratives for CC7.2 (continuous detection via hourly sweep with `bcl_pairs_checked > 0` attestation), CC7.3 (P0 IC-declare ≤ 15 min; `violation_closed` + BCL-CHN-E-001 close the audit trail), CC8.1 (H4/H5 root cause requires change-management remediation + tabletop per R-74.12 item 5). **BCL Admin Session Bulk Revocation (§BCL-Admin-Revocation-Events):** (4) `enterprise.admin_sessions_bulk_revoked` (HIGH, 7yr): IC PAM-elevated (two-person auth §19.4) via `POST /internal/tenant/{slug}/revoke-all-sessions` Admin API during R-73 resolution; bulk `enterprise_sessions` UPDATE (`revoked_at = NOW()` for all active sessions for tenant); not BCL-CHAIN-01 chained (post-incident remediation, not BCL lifecycle); payload: `tenant_id` UUID, `pam_request_id` UUID, `bcl_request_ids` string[] UUIDs (from R-73-C1; may be `[]`), `sessions_revoked_count` int ≥ 0 (aggregate; no `user_id`), `incident_reference` string. BCL-REV-E-001 evidence artefact (CC6.3/CC7.3, per-activation, 7yr WORM, `compliance/evidence/oidc-bcl/bcl-rev-e-001-{YYYY-MM-DD}/`); filed within T+60 min; compliance-officer reviews within 24h; R2 subfolder provision pending R-73.12 item 2 (P1/M8); registration in SOC2_READINESS §79.4 pending R-73.12 item 3 (P1/M8). SOC 2 auditor narratives for CC6.3 (`sessions_revoked_count > 0` proves gap was closed; `incident_reference` links to IR record), CC7.3 (BCL-REV-E-001 `csm-notification.txt` proves §4.3 SLA notification within 15 min; `incident-timeline.md` documents T+0..T+60 response). **Retention table additions:** +4 rows: `security.bcl_chain_01_violation` CRITICAL 7yr CC7.2/CC7.3/CC8.1; `security.bcl_chain_01_violation_closed` HIGH 7yr CC7.2/CC7.3/CC8.1; `system.bcl_chain_check_passed` LOW 1yr CC7.2; `enterprise.admin_sessions_bulk_revoked` HIGH 7yr CC6.3/CC7.3. Privacy floor (all four events): no `user_id`, no `oidc_sub_hash`, no employee name, email, health value, coaching content, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category data. `oidc_sub_hash` in underlying `audit_log_events` rows for BCL violations is never surfaced in violation event payloads; `chain-segment.json` in BCL-CHN-E-001 must be redacted of `oidc_sub_hash` before auditor sharing. Document header v2.89 → v2.90. Owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer + devops-lead.*

*v2.89 (2026-07-04): +4 `backchannel_logout.*` OIDC Back-Channel Logout events — new section `### OIDC Back-Channel Logout events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · SSO §46 · SOC 2 CC6.1/CC6.3)` inserted between `### SAML SLO (Single Logout) events` and `### Enterprise Adoption Monitoring events`. Closes `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §46.8` item 6 (P0/M8 — register four DEC-030 event types with Zod v2 schemas, payload tables, BCL-CHAIN-01 invariant, and CC6.1/CC6.3 auditor narratives). Four events: (1) `backchannel_logout.received` (STANDARD, 7yr): `POST /auth/oidc/backchannel-logout` receives `logout_token`; BCL-CHAIN-01 anchor; emitted before JWT validation; `tenant_id` nullable (null when `iss` unrecognised); payload: `tenant_id` UUID nullable, `iss` URL, `bcl_request_id` UUID, `received_at` datetime. (2) `backchannel_logout.validated` (STANDARD, 7yr): `logout_token` passed all OIDC BCL checks (signature, `events` claim, `nonce` absent, `sub`/`sid` present); revocation initiated; payload: `tenant_id` UUID, `bcl_request_id` UUID, `iss` URL, `sub_hash` 64-char SHA-256 hex nullable (null if only `sid`; raw `sub` never stored), `sid` string nullable, `revocation_method` enum (sid|sub). (3) `backchannel_logout.revoked` (STANDARD, 7yr): sessions revoked in `enterprise_sessions`; `sessions_revoked_count: 0` is valid (no-match); BCL-CHAIN-01 terminal; `async: true` for deferred-to-queue path; payload: `tenant_id` UUID, `bcl_request_id` UUID, `sessions_revoked_count` nonneg int, `revocation_method` enum (sid|sub), `sub_hash` 64-char nullable, `async` bool. (4) `backchannel_logout.failed` (HIGH, 7yr): validation failure or DB error; P3 Slack `#alerts-enterprise`; **BCL-CHAIN-01 exempt** (JWT decode crash before received emit); payload: `tenant_id` UUID nullable, `bcl_request_id` UUID, `reason` enum (6 values: jwt_verification_failed|missing_events_claim|nonce_present|neither_sub_nor_sid|db_error_deferred_to_queue|max_retries_exceeded), `error` string optional. BCL-CHAIN-01: `emit-audit-event` Worker returns HTTP 422 `BCL_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` when `backchannel_logout.revoked` arrives without prior `backchannel_logout.received` anchor for same `{tenant_id, bcl_request_id}` — 60s TTL KV anchor window; implementation pending §46.8 item 7 (P0/M8); co-activates R-05 on violation. Three SOC 2 evidence artefacts registered in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §163` (count 132 → 135, per §46.8 item 10 P1): BCL-E-001 (CC6.1/CC6.3 — 30-day BCL chain export, 7yr); BCL-E-002 (CC6.1 — Okta + Azure AD integration test results, 7yr); BCL-E-003 (CC8.1 — Migration 0101 log, 7yr). Privacy floor (all four events): `sub_hash` is SHA-256 hex of OIDC `sub` — raw `sub` never stored in `enterprise_sessions` or emitted in any payload; `sid` is non-PII opaque IdP session reference (OIDC Core §2); `iss` is IdP issuer URL — not a user identity attribute; no `user_id`, no health data, no coaching content, no GDPR Art. 9 special-category data; `form_api` REVOKED from emission path — `oidc-backchannel-logout.ts` Worker via `form_system` role. Retention table: +2 rows (`backchannel_logout.received`/`.validated`/`.revoked` STANDARD 7yr CC6.1/CC6.3; `backchannel_logout.failed` HIGH 7yr CC6.1). Document header v2.88 → v2.89. Owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer + platform-engineer.*

**v2.88 · 2026-07-04 · owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer + platform-engineer**
*v2.88 (2026-07-04): +5 `slo.*` SAML Single Logout events — new section `### SAML SLO (Single Logout) events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · SSO §45 · SOC 2 CC6.1/CC6.3)` inserted between `### R-57 PKJWT Key Expiry Sweep Stale events` and `### Enterprise Adoption Monitoring events`. Closes `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §45.8` item 6 (P0/M7 — register five DEC-030 event types with Zod v2 schemas, payload tables, SLO-CHAIN-01 invariant, and CC6.1/CC6.3 auditor narratives). Five events: (1) `slo.sp_initiated` (HIGH, 7yr): FORM SP dispatches SAML `LogoutRequest` to IdP; SLO-CHAIN-01 anchor; payload: `tenant_id` UUID, `slo_request_id` UUID, `idp_name_id` string nullable, `session_id` UUID nullable, `binding` enum (http-post|http-redirect), `return_url` URL nullable. (2) `slo.idp_initiated` (HIGH, 7yr): IdP sends SAML `LogoutRequest` to FORM `POST /auth/saml/slo`; SLO-CHAIN-01 anchor; payload: `tenant_id` UUID, `slo_request_id` UUID, `session_index` string nullable, `idp_name_id` string nullable, `sessions_revoked_count` nonneg int. (3) `slo.completed` (HIGH, 7yr): SLO round-trip succeeded — `StatusCode: Success` confirmed; SLO-CHAIN-01 terminal success; payload: `tenant_id` UUID, `slo_request_id` UUID, `session_id` UUID nullable, `idp_response_status` enum (success|partial_logout|no_action), `round_trip_ms` nonneg int. (4) `slo.failed` (HIGH, 7yr): SLO failed (timeout / invalid_signature / malformed_response / unknown); **SLO-CHAIN-01 exempt** (must be writable even without prior initiation event — covers network failure pre-write); `invalid_signature` routes P2 PagerDuty `#alerts-enterprise` (potential replay); payload: `tenant_id` UUID, `slo_request_id` UUID, `reason` enum (4 values), `fallback_applied` bool. (5) `slo.fallback_local_only` (STANDARD, 3yr): SLO not attempted; local-only session revocation applied; SLO-CHAIN-01 terminal (alongside `slo.completed`); three trigger paths (`slo_not_configured`, `idp_timeout`, `no_name_id`); payload: `session_id` UUID nullable, `tenant_id` UUID, `user_id` UUID nullable, `reason` enum (3 values). SLO-CHAIN-01 invariant: `emit-audit-event` Worker returns HTTP 422 `SLO_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` when `slo.completed` or `slo.fallback_local_only` is emitted without prior `slo.sp_initiated` or `slo.idp_initiated` for same `{tenant_id, slo_request_id}` — implementation pending §45.8 item 7 (P0/M7); co-activates R-05. Four SOC 2 evidence artefacts registered in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §160` (SLO-E-001…SLO-E-004; count 128 → 132 per §45.8 item 11): SLO-E-001 (CC6.1/CC6.3 — SP-initiated chain export, 7yr); SLO-E-002 (CC6.1/CC6.3 — IdP-initiated export, 7yr); SLO-E-003 (CC6.1 — integration test matrix SLO-I-001…SLO-I-008 results, 7yr); SLO-E-004 (CC8.1 — migration 0100 log, 7yr). Privacy floor (all five events): `idp_name_id` is a federation-layer pseudonym (SAML `NameID`) — not a GDPR Art. 9 identifier; `user_id` present only in `slo.fallback_local_only` (nullable); `session_id` nullable in `slo.sp_initiated` / `slo.completed` / `slo.fallback_local_only`; no health data, no coaching content; `form_api` REVOKED from emission path — `saml-slo.ts` Worker via `form_system` role. Retention table: +2 rows (`slo.sp_initiated`/`slo.idp_initiated`/`slo.completed`/`slo.failed` 7yr CC6.1/CC6.3; `slo.fallback_local_only` 3yr CC6.3). Document header v2.87 → v2.88. Owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer + platform-engineer.*

**v2.87 · 2026-07-04 · owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer**
*v2.87 (2026-07-04): +2 events — `system.audit_event_flush_stale_declared` (HIGH/7yr) and `system.audit_event_flush_restored` (LOW/3yr) — in new section `### Audit Event Flush Stale events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-72 · A1.2 / CC7.2)`. Closes `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-72.11` item 1 (P0 — register both events with Zod v2 schemas, payload tables, AEF-FLUSH-STALE-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant, and A1.2/CC7.2 auditor narrative). AEF-FLUSH-STALE-CHAIN-01: `system.audit_event_flush_restored` blocked (HTTP 422 `AEF_FLUSH_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION`) by `emit-audit-event` Worker without prior `stale_declared` for same `incident_id`; co-activates R-05 on violation; implementation pending (platform-engineer — R-72.11 item 2). Six-field Zod v2 `AuditEventFlushStaleDeclaredSchema`: `incident_id` (UUID), `confirmed_stale_since` (datetime — last confirmed successful run from R-72-C1), `stale_hours` (int≥0), `missed_flush_cycles` (int≥0 — stale_hours×2), `kv_buffer_events_at_risk` (int≥−1; −1 = KV scan not yet run; from R-72-C2 Cloudflare KV API `prefix=pending:` count), `p0_escalated` (bool — true if stale > 4h or kv_buffer_events_at_risk > 0). Six-field Zod v2 `AuditEventFlushRestoredSchema`: `incident_id` (UUID), `restored_at` (datetime), `root_cause` (enum h1_deleted/h2_disabled/h3_infra/h4_edge_function_broken — 4 values; consistent with R-67/R-68/R-69/R-70/R-71 4-value pattern), `stale_hours_total` (int≥0), `kv_events_flushed` (int≥0 — count of `pending:` KV keys committed to Postgres HMAC chain during recovery; 0 if buffer was empty at restoration), `manual_flush_required` (bool — true if IC triggered manual flush rather than awaiting next 30-min automated cycle). A1.2 significance: `audit-event-flush` (pg_cron `*/30 * * * *`, 2h freshness) drains `AUDIT_EVENTS_KV` buffer into `audit_log_events` Postgres HMAC chain; stale window = potential irreversible KV event loss if pending key TTL expires before recovery; `kv_events_flushed` captures the magnitude of chain completeness risk; 7yr HIGH retention of `stale_declared` ensures complete audit trail for any retrospective DEC-030 chain gap investigation. CC7.2 significance: stale `audit-event-flush` is a scheduled-job anomaly detected by pg-cron-health-monitor within 1h of 2h freshness breach; detection → IC DEC-030 emission → recovery is the monitored control chain. P0 default (consistent with R-68 P0 chain integrity sentinel and R-71 P0 data-integrity sentinel): stale `audit-event-flush` directly threatens DEC-030 chain completeness. Privacy floor (both schemas): `kv_buffer_events_at_risk` and `kv_events_flushed` are aggregate integer counts of KV buffer key-count queries — not event payloads; no `user_id`, `tenant_id`, coaching content, health values, PII, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category data; `form_api` REVOKED from emission path; IC PAM-elevated emission only. Retention table: +2 rows (`system.audit_event_flush_stale_declared` HIGH 7yr A1.2/CC7.2; `system.audit_event_flush_restored` LOW 3yr A1.2/CC7.2). Document header v2.86 → v2.87. Owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer.*

**v2.86 · 2026-07-04 · owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer**
*v2.86 (2026-07-04): +2 events — `system.row_count_monitor_stale_declared` (HIGH/7yr) and `system.row_count_monitor_restored` (LOW/3yr) — in new section `### Row Count Monitor Stale events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-71 · CC7.1 / A1.2)`. Closes `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-71.12` item 1 (P0 — register both events with Zod v2 schemas, payload tables, ROWCOUNT-MON-STALE-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant, and CC7.1/A1.2 auditor narrative). ROWCOUNT-MON-STALE-CHAIN-01: `system.row_count_monitor_restored` blocked (HTTP 422 `ROWCOUNT_MON_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION`) by `emit-audit-event` Worker without prior `stale_declared` for same `incident_id`; co-activates R-05 on violation; implementation pending (platform-engineer — R-71.12 item 2). Six-field Zod v2 `RowCountMonitorStaleDeclaredSchema`: `incident_id` (UUID), `confirmed_stale_since` (datetime — last confirmed successful run from R-71-C1), `stale_hours` (int≥0), `missed_runs_estimate` (int≥0 — stale_hours×4), `manual_deviation_found` (bool — R-71-C2 found deviation_pct>5%), `max_deviation_pct` (float≥−1; −1 = scan not yet run). Six-field Zod v2 `RowCountMonitorRestoredSchema`: `incident_id` (UUID), `restored_at` (datetime), `root_cause` (enum h1_deleted/h2_disabled/h3_infra/h4_edge_function_broken — 4 values; consistent with R-67/R-69/R-70 4-value pattern), `stale_hours_total` (int≥0), `manual_deviation_found` (bool), `max_deviation_pct` (float≥0 — final scan; 0 if no deviation). CC7.1 significance: `row-count-monitor` (pg_cron `*/15 * * * *`, 1h freshness) compares current table row counts against `monitoring_baselines` 1-hour rolling averages for 7 critical tables; stale window is a detection gap bridged by R-71-C2 compensating SQL — P0 default (distinct from R-68 P0 chain integrity sentinel and R-70 P2 PAM sync gap). A1.2 significance: stale `row-count-monitor` directly impacts ability to detect availability-threatening data anomalies; 7yr HIGH retention ensures complete audit chain for retrospective investigation. Privacy floor (both schemas): `monitoring_baselines.row_count_avg` is aggregate-only (total table counts — not per-user/per-tenant); `manual_deviation_found` is boolean; `max_deviation_pct` is aggregate scalar; no `user_id`, `tenant_id`, coaching content, health values, or GDPR Art. 9 data; `form_api` REVOKED; IC PAM-elevated emission only. Retention table: +2 rows (`system.row_count_monitor_stale_declared` HIGH 7yr CC7.1/A1.2; `system.row_count_monitor_restored` LOW 3yr CC7.1/A1.2). Document header v2.85 → v2.86. Owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer.*

**v2.84 · 2026-07-03 · owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer**
*v2.85 (2026-07-03): +1 `tenant.*` event — `tenant.k_floor_updated` HIGH/7yr — closes `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §51.9` item 3 (P0 — register DEC-030 event before M12 enterprise deploy). New section `### Tenant k-Anonymity Floor events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · DATA_MODEL §51 · Migration 0092 · SOC 2 P4.1/CC2.2/CC3.2)` inserted after `### Mobile OTA Change Window events`. `KFloorUpdatedSchema` Zod v2 (eight fields: `event`, `tenant_id` slug, `previous_k_floor` enum `5|10|15`, `new_k_floor` enum `5|10|15`, `change_reason` string ≤ 200 chars, `approved_by_user_id` UUID FORM enterprise-architect, `compliance_officer_cosign_ref` nullable string, `changed_at` ISO 8601). **KANON-CHAIN-01 two HTTP 422 invariants** (enforced at `emit-audit-event` Worker layer): `KANON_CHAIN_01_CLINICAL_COSIGN_REQUIRED` (`new_k_floor = 15` but `compliance_officer_cosign_ref = null`); `KANON_CHAIN_01_FLOOR_REDUCTION_COSIGN_REQUIRED` (`new_k_floor < previous_k_floor` but `compliance_officer_cosign_ref = null`). Emitter: FORM staff via `PATCH /internal/tenant/{slug}/k-floor` (enterprise-architect Cloudflare Access only) — not automated, not tenant-initiated. SOC 2 evidence: KANON-E-001 (P4.1/CC2.2, per-change + annual nil attestation, 7yr, `compliance/evidence/k-anonymity/`); zero-change years filed as affirmative nil attestation; DDL snapshot of `chk_reporting_k_floor_tier` constraint. Three SOC 2 auditor narratives: P4.1 (`chk_reporting_k_floor_tier` DDL-layer enforcement makes values < 5 structurally impossible — PRV-21 DDL closure); CC2.2 (tamper-evident HIGH-severity HMAC chain records every floor change with `approved_by_user_id`); CC3.2 (enterprise-architect approval + `compliance_officer_cosign_ref` for clinical-tier or floor reduction constitute formal risk assessment). Taxonomy update: `tenant.k_floor_updated` added to `### Tenant administration` bullet list (§Action taxonomy). Retention table: +1 row (`tenant.k_floor_updated` HIGH 7yr P4.1/CC2.2/CC3.2). Privacy floor: `tenant_id` is org slug only — never UUID or employee PII; `approved_by_user_id` is FORM enterprise-architect internal UUID — not an enterprise employee identifier; `previous_k_floor` and `new_k_floor` are integer thresholds (5, 10, 15) — not personal data; no employee name, email, health data, coaching content, or individual aggregate metrics in payload; `form_api` REVOKED from `tenants.reporting_k_floor` (migration 0092 selective GRANT). Document header v2.84 → v2.85. Cross-references: `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §51` (canonical spec — migration 0092; KANON-CHAIN-01 formal definition; §51.9 item 3 [x] Done 2026-07-03); `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §17.4.4` (informal DDL sketch — formalised by §51); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §51` (QBR-K-ANON-01 — k-floor Tier 1/Tier 2 framework; `reporting_k_floor` is the persistent DB form of QBR-K-ANON-01); `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §159` (KANON-E-001 — pending P1/M13 first collection). Owner: compliance-officer + enterprise-architect.*

*v2.84 (2026-07-03): +2 events — `security.pam_postgres_sync_stale_declared` (HIGH/7yr) and `security.pam_postgres_sync_restored` (LOW/3yr) — in new section `### PAM Postgres Sync Stale events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-70 · CC6.2 / CC6.6)`. Closes `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-70.12` item 1 (P0 — register both events with Zod v2 schemas, payload tables, PAM-SYNC-STALE-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant, and CC6.2/CC6.6 auditor narrative). PAM-SYNC-STALE-CHAIN-01: `security.pam_postgres_sync_restored` blocked (HTTP 422 `PAM_SYNC_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION`) by `emit-audit-event` Worker without prior `stale_declared` for same `incident_id`; co-activates R-05 on violation; implementation pending (platform-engineer — R-70.12 item 2). Six-field Zod v2 `PamPostgresSyncStaleDeclaredSchema`: `incident_id` (UUID), `confirmed_stale_since` (datetime — last confirmed successful run from R-70-C2), `stale_hours` (int≥0 — 35h freshness window, every-30-min cadence), `missed_runs_estimate` (int≥0 — stale_hours×2 rounded down), `manual_gap_count` (int≥−1, −1 = gate not yet run; final count in `restored`), `initial_severity` (enum p2/p1_escalate/p0_escalate — p0_escalate if break-glass gap confirmed OR stale > 72h; p1_escalate if non-break-glass gap confirmed). Six-field Zod v2 `PamPostgresSyncRestoredSchema`: `incident_id` (UUID), `restored_at` (datetime), `root_cause` (enum h1_deleted/h2_disabled/h3_infra/h4_pg_net_degraded — 4 values; consistent with R-67/R-69 4-value pattern), `stale_hours_total` (int≥0), `manual_gap_count` (int≥0 — final; 0 if no gap during stale window), `gap_remediated` (bool — true if R-70.5 step 4 manual sync reconciliation executed). CC6.2 significance: job 20 (`pam_postgres_sync`, every 30 min, 35h freshness) verifies every `pam.elevation_approved` DEC-030 event has a matching `admin_jit_escalations` row (KV-to-Postgres PAM sync integrity); stale window is a detection gap remediated by R-70-C1 compensating SQL — P2 default (distinct from R-68 P0 chain integrity; distinct from R-69 P1 SLO measurement gap). CC6.6 significance: if R-70-C1 finds a break-glass session without `admin_jit_escalations` row, escalates to P0 + R-20 co-activation. Privacy floor (both schemas): `manual_gap_count` is an aggregate integer count; `gap_remediated` is a boolean — no `admin_user_id` (UUID pseudonym), no plaintext name, email, justification text, or target tenant data in either event payload; R-70-C1 scope query output restricted to security-engineer + IC (not Slack); `form_api` REVOKED from emission path; IC PAM-elevated emission only. Retention table: +2 rows (`security.pam_postgres_sync_stale_declared` HIGH 7yr CC6.2/CC6.6; `security.pam_postgres_sync_restored` LOW 3yr CC6.2/CC6.6). Document header v2.83 → v2.84. Owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer.*

Seven-field Zod v2 `VictorBaselineRefreshStaleDeclaredSchema`: `incident_id` (UUID), `job_id` (literal 14), `stale_declared_at` (datetime), `confirmed_stale_since` (datetime|null — null on H1/deleted or purged run history), `stale_hours` (int≥0), `missing_categories` (string[] — R-62-C2 KV probe result; empty = all categories have fresh baseline), `safety_incident_count_at_declared` (int≥−1, −1 = not yet run). Seven-field Zod v2 `VictorBaselineRefreshRestoredSchema`: `incident_id` (UUID), `job_id` (literal 14), `restored_at` (datetime), `root_cause` (enum deleted/disabled/infra/function_broken/kv_write_failure — 5 values, H1–H5; H5 `kv_write_failure` unique to job 14: pg_cron shows `succeeded` but KV write to `system.victor_safety_baselines` failed silently — discriminated by R-62-C2 Wrangler probe, not R-62-C1 pg_cron history), `compensating_computation_executed` (bool — true if §Step 5 manual KV backfill executed via PAM-elevated Wrangler write), `categories_restored` (string[]), `clinical_safety_review_required` (bool — true if `safety_incident_count_at_declared > 0`; victor-safety-lead review attestation required before P1 closure). Privacy floor (both schemas): no `user_id`, `session_id`, coaching session content, body composition metrics, GDPR Art. 9 data; no Victor safety flag text or session identifiers; aggregate INT counts and trigger-category strings only; `form_api` REVOKED. Retention table: +2 rows (`system.victor_baseline_refresh_stale_declared` 7yr CC7.2/A1.2; `system.victor_baseline_refresh_restored` 3yr CC7.2/A1.2 advisory). Clinical safety note: FORM-VICTOR-001 (P0, threshold-independent — any VT-03/04/05/06) unaffected by baseline staleness; staleness risk is specific to VICTOR-002/003/004 proportional tiers. Owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer.*

**v2.60 · 2026-06-29 · owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer**
*v2.60 (2026-06-29): +1 `system.admin_mv_refreshed` event in new section `### Admin Reporting Pipeline MV Refresh events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · OBSERVABILITY §62 · C1.1/P4.1/CC7.2)` — closes `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §62.9` item 2 (P0/M7 — register `system.admin_mv_refreshed` LOW/1yr with Zod v2 schema, ADMIN-MV-CHAIN-01 advisory ordering invariant, and privacy invariant annotations). Four-field Zod v2 `AdminMvRefreshedSchema`: `view_name` (enum of 4 MV names), `tenant_row_count` (int ≥ 0), `suppressed_cell_count` (int ≥ 0 — ADMIN-RPT-SLO-02 health signal; non-null on every emission), `refresh_duration_ms` (int ≥ 0). ADMIN-MV-CHAIN-01 advisory invariant: within-night ordering for 4 views (02:15→02:30→02:45→03:00 UTC); enforced by staggered schedule, not by `emit-audit-event` Worker HTTP 422 — structural distinction from all STALE-CHAIN-01 pairs (R-41 through R-51) which are HTTP-422-enforced IC lifecycle events. This event is an automated pg_cron completion signal (`form_system` emitter), not an IC-emitted incident anchor — no PAM elevation; no R-05 co-activation on ordering anomaly. Retention table: +1 row (`system.admin_mv_refreshed` LOW 1yr C1.1/P4.1/CC7.2). Three SOC 2 auditor narratives: C1.1 (pipeline confirms prohibited-metric registry enforcement — no per-employee health data visible to HR; ~1,460 events/year evidence; ADMIN-RPT-E-001 annual artefact); P4.1 (`suppressed_cell_count` is observable proof k-anonymity guard ran per cycle; non-zero values confirm privacy floor was active); CC7.2 (HMAC chain tamper-evidence for absence-based AL-ADMIN-RPT-01..04 alerting). Privacy floor: no employee `user_id`, name, email, coaching content, health value, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category data; no `tenant_id` UUID or tenant name — `view_name` is the sole discriminant (MV type, not tenant identity); `suppressed_cell_count` is an aggregate integer, not derivable to individual identity; `form_api` REVOKED from all four MV relations and `audit_log_events` INSERT. Cross-references: `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §62.6` (canonical Zod v2 schema + ADMIN-MV-CHAIN-01 invariant — primary spec source; §62.9 item 2 → [x] Done 2026-06-29); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §62.4` (AL-ADMIN-RPT-01..04 alert rules — auto-resolve on `system.admin_mv_refreshed` per `view_name`); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §62.2` (ADMIN-RPT-SLO-01/02 — freshness ≤ 26h and `suppressed_cell_count` non-null per emission); `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §130` (ADMIN-RPT-E-001 annual artefact registration — C1.1/P4.1/CC7.2, annual, 3yr); `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-52` (Admin MV refresh stale recovery runbook); DEC-030. Owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer.*

**v2.58 · 2026-06-29 · owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer**
*v2.58 (2026-06-29): +1 `admin.dashboard_session_started` event — formal version registration for the `### Admin (dashboard sessions)` section added during the OBSERVABILITY §61 authoring pass (v5.8.0, 2026-06-29). The section content (event schema, two-layer de-dup invariant, Zod v2 schema, SOC 2 CC3.2 auditor narrative, cross-references) was written inline by that pass with a forward reference to "canonical source: this section, v2.58"; this entry formally increments the document version, adds the `admin.dashboard_session_started` row to the §Retention table, and closes the header lag. **Event:** `admin.dashboard_session_started` (LOW severity, 1yr retention, DEC-030 HMAC-chained). Emitted by Admin Dashboard backend (`emit-audit-event` Worker) on initial session start for `enterprise_admin` and `tenant_manager` roles. **Two-layer de-duplication invariant (OQ-CHAMP-OBS-01 resolution — OBSERVABILITY §61.4):** (1) client-side `sessionStorage.getItem('form-admin-sess-emitted')` gate prevents re-emission within the same browser tab session; (2) server-side KV key `admin-sess-start-{actor_id}:{tenant_id}` with 4 h TTL — `emit-audit-event` Worker returns HTTP 200 `{"deduplicated": true}` on KV hit with no HMAC chain entry; a genuine second login after ≥ 4 h emits a fresh event (consistent with COST_MODEL §46.2 T0-Beta "≥ 2 admin logins" criterion). **Payload fields:** `tenant_id_slug` (string max 64 — opaque slug, not UUID), `actor_role` (enum: `enterprise_admin`|`tenant_manager`), `pilot_day` (INT|NULL — day within pilot window; null if not on pilot), `is_pilot_active` (BOOL). **Privacy invariant:** no actor `user_id`, `actor_name`, or `actor_email`; no coaching content or GDPR Art. 9 special-category health data. `tenant_id_slug` is an opaque human-safe identifier — not a UUID. **SOC 2:** CC3.2 (champion engagement monitoring control — T0-Beta signal source for `champion_login_monitor` job 50, OBSERVABILITY §59). **Retention table:** +1 row (`admin.dashboard_session_started` LOW 1yr CC3.2). Document header v2.57 → v2.58. Closes `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §59.10` item 1 documentation portion (`[x] Done — AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.58, 2026-06-29`) and `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §61.4` forward reference ("AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md v2.58 registers event — closes §59.10 item 1 documentation portion"). Privacy floor: `admin.dashboard_session_started` carries no employee `user_id`, name, email, coaching session content, or GDPR Art. 9 data; `form_api` role cannot emit this event (Admin Dashboard auth context required); `tenant_manager` events are session-level signals only — `hr_never_sees_individual_data` invariant (ENTERPRISE.md) upheld. Cross-references: `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §59` (job 50 `champion_login_monitor` — primary consumer; §59.4 AL-SAVE-02 / AL-CHAMP-ACT-01; §59.5 canonical SQL); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §61.4` (OQ-CHAMP-OBS-01 resolution — de-dup mechanism definition, forward reference closed); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §46.2` (T0-Beta trigger: "Day-45 champion logins < 2"); `docs/SSO_SCIM_IMPLEMENTATION.md §17` (enterprise pilot runbook — SSO setup timing may affect when admin champion first logs in); DEC-030. Owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer.*

(2) `system.etf_cron_manual_check_completed` (HIGH, 7yr): IC emits per missed Monday window during stale period after manual CHS detection query (R-29-C1); `all_clear` boolean; `tenants_flagged[]` UUID-array for R-29-C3 scope; ETF-CRON-CHAIN-01 intermediate event (blocked if `restored` already emitted for same `incident_id` — terminal enforcement). *v2.54 (2026-06-27): +2 R-48 DEC-030 HMAC-chained monitoring-control events in `§Contract Amendment Rate Compliance Monitoring events` — closes `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-48.12` item 1 (P0/M7 — register `system.amendment_rate_monitor_stale_declared` HIGH/7yr and `system.amendment_rate_monitor_restored` STANDARD/3yr with AMEND-STALE-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant + Zod v2 schemas + CC5.2/CC6.1/CC7.2 auditor narratives). Both events anchor the R-48 incident lifecycle for pg_cron job 48 (`amendment_rate_compliance_monitor`) staleness — distinct from the three AMEND-MONITOR-CHAIN-01 events registered in v2.53 (which job 48 emits WHEN RUNNING). (1) `system.amendment_rate_monitor_stale_declared` (HIGH, 7yr): devops-lead (IC, PAM-elevated) at R-48 Step 1 after R-48-C1/C2/C3 gates; AMEND-STALE-CHAIN-01 anchor; `chain_gap_found = false` is CC5.2 IC attestation that no TU-CHAIN-01 gaps occurred undetected (primary `amend_contract_tier()` HTTP 422 enforcement was operational); `floor_breach_found = false` is CC6.1 attestation that no below-floor `enterprise_contracts` rows existed; `initial_severity = 'P0'` if either gate positive; no additional PagerDuty on emission (parent AL-AMEND-03 P1 `amend-check-stale` already fired); 7yr retention — CC5.2/CC6.1 multi-cycle SOC 2 observation evidence. (2) `system.amendment_rate_monitor_restored` (STANDARD, 3yr): devops-lead (IC, PAM-elevated) at R-48 Step 6; AMEND-STALE-CHAIN-01 terminal event; `chain_gaps_at_restoration = 0` + `floor_breaches_at_restoration = 0` required before P0/P1 closure; `root_cause` H1–H4 enum; resolves PagerDuty `amend-check-stale`; 3yr retention (primary attestations in 7yr `stale_declared`). AMEND-STALE-CHAIN-01: `restored` blocked HTTP 422 `AMEND_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` without prior `declared` for same `incident_id` → R-05. Retention table: +2 rows (`system.amendment_rate_monitor_stale_declared` HIGH 7yr CC5.2/CC6.1/A1.1/CC4.1; `system.amendment_rate_monitor_restored` STANDARD 3yr CC7.2). Privacy floor (both events): counts, booleans, timestamps, enums only — no `tenant_id`, no employee `user_id`, name, email, rate value, tenant name, or GDPR Art. 9 data; rate values from R-48-C3 floor forensic stay in `#security-restricted` only. Document header v2.53 → v2.54. Structural peers: SCIM-PROV-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 (v2.52); PRICING-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 (v2.48); LITH-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 (v2.46); OFFBOARD-CHAIN-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 (v2.44). One event: `enterprise.annual_nrr_bridge_filed` (LOW, 3yr) — annual retention ARR bridge filing event; NRR-BRIDGE-INV-01 arithmetic invariant enforced at `emit-audit-event` Worker (HTTP 422 `NRR_BRIDGE_INV_01_RETENTION_CHECK` when `|retained + expansion − contraction − churned − closing| > $1.00`; no chain-predecessor requirement — arithmetic consistency only); Zod v2 `AnnualNrrBridgeFiledSchema` (14 fields: `reporting_year` INT 2025–2050, `cohort_tenant_count` positive INT, six NUMERIC ARR components nonneg, `grr_pct` 0–1, `nrr_pct` 0–2, `logo_retention_rate_pct` 0–1, `green_fleet_pct_at_m12` 0–1, `filed_by` 3-value enum, `bridge_hash` 64-char hex); emitter: compliance-officer / data-engineer / founder (PAM-elevated, human review required — not automated by `form_system`); SOC 2 evidence: NRR-BRIDGE-E-001 (CC4.1/A1.1, annual Q1, 3yr, `compliance/evidence/nrr/NRR-BRIDGE-E-001_<YYYY>.csv`) registered in `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §131` (v3.56.0, 2026-06-30). CC4.1 auditor narrative: NRR-BRIDGE-INV-01 Worker enforcement + `bridge_hash` SHA-256 create a tamper-evident, arithmetic-verified annual retention record without requiring auditor access to source tables; zero-renewal years filed as affirmative attestation. A1.1 auditor narrative: `logo_retention_rate_pct` + `nrr_pct` are fleet-level availability-outcome metrics proving service commitments translated to observable retention. Retention table: +1 row (`enterprise.annual_nrr_bridge_filed` LOW 3yr CC4.1/A1.1). **v2.51 · 2026-06-26 · owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer**
*v2.53 (2026-06-27): +3 DEC-030 HMAC-chained events in new `§Contract Amendment Rate Compliance Monitoring events` section — closes `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §57.10` item 1 (P0/M5 — register `security.amendment_chain_gap_detected` CRITICAL/7yr, `security.amendment_floor_breach_detected` CRITICAL/7yr, and `system.amendment_rate_check_passed` LOW/1yr with AMEND-MONITOR-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant + Zod v2 schemas + CC5.2/CC6.1/A1.1 SOC 2 auditor narratives). Section inserted after `§SCIM Provisioning Compliance Monitoring events` and before `§Rate limiting and abuse events`, consistent with OBSERVABILITY §57.7 spec (canonical source). Three events cover the pg_cron job 48 (`amendment_rate_compliance_monitor`, `0 7 1 * *`, 33-day freshness window) monthly belt-and-suspenders forensic cross-check: (1) `security.amendment_chain_gap_detected` (CRITICAL, 7yr, CC5.2/CC6.1): job 48 sweep 1 emits when correlated subquery finds ≥ 1 `billing.rate_updated` event in last 32 days without matching `enterprise.contract_amended` for same `tenant_id` within ±24h (TU-CHAIN-01 bypass indicator per COST_MODEL §45.5); AMEND-MONITOR-CHAIN-01: DEC-030 HTTP 200 confirmed before AL-AMEND-01 PagerDuty P0; dedup `amend-chain-gap-{YYYY-MM}` no-expiry; no auto-resolve; IC response: INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-48 §R-48.5; Zod: `{ chain_gap_count: z.number().int().positive(), billing_event_ids: z.array(z.string().uuid()).nonempty(), check_run_at: z.string().datetime() }` — no `tenant_id`, no rate value, no employee PII. (2) `security.amendment_floor_breach_detected` (CRITICAL, 7yr, CC5.2/CC6.1): job 48 sweep 2 emits when `SELECT id FROM enterprise_contracts WHERE amendment_type IS NOT NULL AND rate_per_seat_usd < CASE tier WHEN 'starter' THEN 6.00 WHEN 'growth' THEN 4.50 WHEN 'enterprise' THEN 4.00 END` returns ≥ 1 row; unknown-tier rows excluded by NULL bypass → `system.amendment_unknown_tier_detected` STANDARD/3yr; AMEND-MONITOR-CHAIN-01: DEC-030 HTTP 200 confirmed before AL-AMEND-02 PagerDuty P0; IC response: INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-48 §R-48.6; Zod: `{ breach_count: z.number().int().positive(), breaching_contract_ids: z.array(z.string().uuid()).nonempty(), check_run_at: z.string().datetime() }` — no rate value, no tenant name, no employee identity. (3) `system.amendment_rate_check_passed` (LOW, 1yr, A1.1/CC4.1): job 48 all-clear after both sweeps complete with zero detections; AMEND-MONITOR-CHAIN-01: suppressed when any CRITICAL emitted this run; powers AL-AMEND-03 33-day freshness dead-man's switch (auto-resolves on emission); Zod: `{ chain_gap_count: z.literal(0), floor_breach_count: z.literal(0), contracts_checked: z.number().int().nonnegative(), events_checked: z.number().int().nonnegative(), check_run_at: z.string().datetime() }` — aggregate counts only; no `tenant_id`, no rate value. Retention table: +3 rows (`security.amendment_chain_gap_detected` CRITICAL 7yr CC5.2/CC6.1; `security.amendment_floor_breach_detected` CRITICAL 7yr CC5.2/CC6.1; `system.amendment_rate_check_passed` LOW 1yr A1.1/CC4.1). Privacy floor (all three events): all payloads carry FORM-internal UUIDs or aggregate counts only — no employee `user_id`, name, email, health value, rate value, tenant name, or GDPR Art. 9 special-category data; `form_api` REVOKED from `audit_log_events` and `enterprise_contracts`; `enterprise_admin`, `tenant_admin`, `tenant_manager` excluded from Metabase dashboard (OBSERVABILITY §57.9). **v2.50 · 2026-06-26 · owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer**
*v2.51 (2026-06-26): +3 DEC-030 HMAC-chained events in new `§SCIM Provisioning Compliance Monitoring events` section — closes `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §56.10` item 1 (P0/M5 — register `security.scim_sensitive_attr_violation_detected` CRITICAL/7yr, `system.scim_provisioning_quarterly_check_triggered` STANDARD/3yr, `system.scim_provisioning_check_passed` LOW/1yr). Section inserted after `§Enterprise Pricing Exception Monitoring events` and before `§Rate limiting and abuse events`. Three events cover the pg_cron job 47 (`scim_provisioning_compliance_monitor`, `0 6 * * *`, 26h freshness window) monitoring-layer lifecycle — the proactive daily baseline complement to reactive AL-SCIM-01 burst-detection (SSO_SCIM §26.7b). (1) `security.scim_sensitive_attr_violation_detected` (CRITICAL, 7yr, CC6.4/CC7.2): job 47 sweep 1 emits when `COUNT(*) FROM audit_log_events WHERE event_type = 'scim.rejected_sensitive_attribute' AND created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '26 hours'` > 0; SCIM-ATTR-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant: DEC-030 HTTP 200 confirmed before AL-SCIM-PROV-01 PagerDuty P0 fires; dedup `scim-prov-sensitive-attr-{YYYY-MM-DD}` 24h; no auto-resolve; IC response INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-47 §R-47.5 (pending §56.10 item 6 P1/M7); Zod: `{ violation_count: z.number().int().positive(), window_start: z.string().datetime(), window_end: z.string().datetime(), check_run_at: z.string().datetime() }` — no `tenant_id`, no rejected attribute VALUE, no `user_id`, no GDPR Art. 9 data; SCIM-PROV-SLO-01 zero-tolerance breach on any single emission. (2) `system.scim_provisioning_quarterly_check_triggered` (STANDARD, 3yr, CC4.1): job 47 sweep 2 fires in first 7 days of Jan/Apr/Jul/Oct; aggregate `scim.rejected_sensitive_attribute` count for prior quarter; triggers compliance-officer SCIM-PROV-E-003 quarterly zero-count assertion (SOC2_READINESS §119); Slack `#compliance` advisory (no PagerDuty); Zod: `{ quarter_label: z.string().regex(/^\d{4}-Q[1-4]$/), quarterly_rejection_count: z.number().int().nonnegative(), quarter_start: z.string().date(), quarter_end: z.string().date(), check_run_at: z.string().datetime() }` — no `tenant_id`; quarterly_rejection_count > 0 requires linked incident report per SCIM-PROV-E-003. (3) `system.scim_provisioning_check_passed` (LOW, 1yr, CC4.1/A1.1): job 47 all-clear after all sweeps complete without violation; positive baseline signal for AL-SCIM-PROV-02 26h dead-man's switch freshness monitoring (SCIM-PROV-SLO-02); suppressed when sweep 1 finds violation (SCIM-ATTR-CHAIN-01 path); Zod: `{ violations_found: z.number().int().nonnegative(), quarterly_trigger_fired: z.boolean(), check_run_at: z.string().datetime() }` — violations_found must be 0 when emitted. SCIM-ATTR-CHAIN-01 structural peer to REENTRY-MONITOR-CHAIN-01 (§55 v2.47), LITH-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 (v2.46), OFFBOARD-CHAIN-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 (v2.44). Retention table: +3 rows (`security.scim_sensitive_attr_violation_detected` 7yr CC6.4/CC7.2; `system.scim_provisioning_quarterly_check_triggered` 3yr CC4.1; `system.scim_provisioning_check_passed` 1yr CC4.1/A1.1). *v2.50 (2026-06-26): +1 `billing.rate_updated` event in new `### Enterprise Pure Tier Upgrade events` section — closes `docs/COST_MODEL.md §45.8` item 5 (P0/M12 — register `billing.rate_updated` HIGH/7yr in `docs/AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md` alongside `billing.seats_expanded`, OQ-EXP-03 Resolution, DEC-082). `billing.rate_updated` (HIGH, 7yr, CC5.2/CC6.1): emitted by `amend_contract_tier()` Supabase RPC (SECURITY DEFINER, `form_system` role) as the terminal event in the two-event pure tier upgrade pair (`enterprise.contract_amended` §45.5.1 → `billing.rate_updated` §45.5.2); TU-CHAIN-01 invariant: `emit-audit-event` Worker returns HTTP 422 `TU_CHAIN_01_FLOOR_VIOLATION` if `new_rate_per_seat_usd` < §31.5 floor for target tier (Starter $6.00 / Growth $4.50 / Enterprise $4.00 — 1yr; $4.50/$4.50/$4.00 — 3yr); `floor_respected: z.literal(true)` chain invariant (HTTP 422 if absent or false); Zod v2 `RateUpdatedPayload`: `tenant_id` UUID, `contract_id` UUID (same as pre-amendment — Option A invariant per DEC-082 / DEC-079 compatibility), `prior_rate_per_seat_usd` positive number (four decimal places), `new_rate_per_seat_usd` positive number (four decimal places; TU-CHAIN-01 enforced), `effective_date` ISO 8601 date-only, `amendment_event_id` UUID (soft-ref to `enterprise.contract_amended` event UUID), `floor_respected` literal true. Post-emit: RPC invalidates BDG KV cache key `scim:guard_cfg:{tenant_id}` within 60s per §45.5.3 advisory (seat count unchanged; tier field updated). Retention table: +1 row (`billing.rate_updated` HIGH 7yr CC5.2/CC6.1). SOC 2 auditor narratives: CC5.2 — two-path evidence via ADM-E-001 (`enterprise.contract_amended` annual chain export with `floor_respected: true` + `asc_606_treatment`) and ADM-E-002 (annual `billing.rate_updated` cross-reference confirming no unauthorized rate reduction); CC6.1 — SECURITY DEFINER exclusivity + dual countersign + `floor_respected` invariant + `amendment_event_id` anchor. TU-CHAIN-01 structural peers: REENTRY-CHAIN-01 (§44.5) and RENEW-CHAIN-01 (§42.7) — all three are write-path HTTP 422 floor gates. Privacy floor: no employee `user_id`, name, email, health value, coaching content, or GDPR Art. 9 data; `tenant_id` and `contract_id` are FORM-internal UUIDs; rate figures are commercial-metadata-only aggregate contract-level values; `form_api` REVOKED from `enterprise_contracts` amendment columns and `audit_log_events` INSERT. (1) `security.quota_hard_block` (HIGH, 3yr, CC6.1): `quota-check.ts` Cloudflare Worker (`form_system`) emits when `hard_blocked_at` is SET on `api_quota_usage` (DATA_MODEL §28.4); payload: `api_key_id` UUID, `tenant_id` UUID nullable, `period_month` YYYY-MM, `endpoint_category` enum (coaching|cv|analytics|all), `overage_count` nonneg int; RL-E-001 primary source (annual CC6.1 quota enforcement evidence). (2) `security.abuse_flag_raised` (HIGH, 7yr, CC6.6/CC7.2): `abuse-detection` Cloudflare Worker (`form_system`) emits on `abuse_flags` INSERT; `form_api` has zero INSERT grant on `abuse_flags` (DATA_MODEL §28.3 RLS); payload: `flag_id` UUID, `flag_type` string, `severity` enum (low|medium|high|critical), `flagged_entity_type` enum (api_key|tenant|user), `flagged_entity_id` UUID, `auto_actioned` bool; RL-E-002 90-day rolling CC6.6 evidence source. (3) `security.abuse_action_taken` (HIGH, 7yr, CC6.6/CC7.2): automated via `abuse-detection` Worker or human via `security_reviewer` (PAM-elevated); emits on `abuse_flags` UPDATE where `action_taken` changes; payload: `flag_id` UUID, `previous_action` string, `new_action` string, `reviewed_by` UUID nullable (null = automated), `auto_actioned` bool; RL-E-003 quarterly CC7.2 evidence source. (4) `data.quota_records_archived` (STANDARD, 1yr, CC4.1): `form_system` via pg_cron job 17 `api_quota_usage_archive` (monthly 00:30 UTC) emits before DELETE batch; payload: `period_months_deleted` array of YYYY-MM strings, `rows_deleted` nonneg int, `archive_run_at` ISO 8601 UTC. Three SOC 2 evidence artefacts defined: RL-E-001 (CC6.1 — annual `security.quota_hard_block` export), RL-E-002 (CC6.6 — 90-day rolling `security.abuse_flag_raised` export), RL-E-003 (CC7.2 — quarterly `security.abuse_action_taken` export; `security_reviewer`-exclusive access). All four Zod v2 schemas provided (canonical source: `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §28.6`; `form_api` REVOKED from `abuse_flags`). Privacy floor (all four events): no employee `user_id`, no health values, no coaching content, no GDPR Art. 9 special-category data; `api_key_id` is FORM-internal UUID — plaintext API key never in chain. **v2.48 · 2026-06-26 · owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer**
*v2.48 (2026-06-26): +2 R-46 DEC-030 HMAC-chained monitoring-control events in `§Enterprise Pricing Exception Monitoring events` — closes `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-46.13` item 1 (P0/M9 — register `system.pricing_exception_monitor_stale_declared` HIGH/7yr and `system.pricing_exception_monitor_restored` STANDARD/3yr with PRICING-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant + Zod v2 schemas + CC auditor narratives). Both events anchor the R-46 incident lifecycle for pg_cron job 46 (`pricing_exception_compliance_monitor`, `0 9 * * *`, 26h freshness) staleness — distinct from the three OBSERVABILITY §55 events registered in v2.47 (which job 46 emits WHEN RUNNING). (1) `system.pricing_exception_monitor_stale_declared` (HIGH, 7yr): devops-lead (IC, PAM-elevated) at R-46 T+0; PRICING-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 prerequisite; Zod schema `PricingExceptionMonitorStaleDeclaredSchema`: `incident_id` UUID, `confirmed_stale_since` datetime, `stale_hours` positive, `missed_runs` nonneg int, `trigger` enum pagerduty_alert|manual_discovery|co_active_r03, `initial_severity` enum P1|P0 (P0 if `reentry_violations_found_during_stale ≥ 1`), `stale_window_overlaps_quarter_start` bool (PRICE-SLO-02 gap flag), `reentry_violations_found_during_stale` nonneg int (`0 = auditor-inspectable CC5.2/CC1.4 attestation that no REENTRY-CHAIN-01 breach occurred during stale window`); no additional PagerDuty on emission (parent `system.cron_job_stale` P1 `form-devops` AL-PRICE-03 already fired; dedup `pricing-exception-check-stale`); 7yr retention (CC7.2 — tamper-evident IC response anchor; `reentry_violations_found_during_stale = 0` constitutes CC5.2/CC1.4 auditor-inspectable attestation). (2) `system.pricing_exception_monitor_restored` (STANDARD, 3yr): devops-lead (IC, PAM-elevated) at R-46 Step 6 after job 46 confirmed healthy and R-46-C2 re-run passes; PRICING-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 terminal event; Zod schema `PricingExceptionMonitorRestoredSchema`: `incident_id` UUID, `restored_at` datetime, `root_cause` enum H1_job_deleted|H2_form_system_permission_revoked|H3_pg_net_degraded|H4_supabase_outage, `fix_deployed_at` datetime, `stale_window_hours` positive, `stale_window_overlaps_quarter_start` bool (must match `stale_declared`), `reentry_violations_found_during_stale` nonneg int (must match `stale_declared`), `reentry_violations_at_restored` nonneg int (0 required or all violations actioned before PagerDuty P0 resolve), `quarterly_trigger_manually_fired` bool (CC4.1 compensating-control record for §R-46.6 quarterly trigger miss); resolves PagerDuty `pricing-exception-check-stale`; 3yr retention (CC7.2 — operational restoration record). PRICING-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01: `restored` blocked HTTP 422 `PRICING_MONITOR_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` without prior `declared` for same `incident_id` → R-05. Section header updated: `CC7.1/CC7.2` added (covered by both R-46 events); `INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-46` added. Retention table: +2 rows (`system.pricing_exception_monitor_stale_declared` HIGH 7yr CC5.2/CC1.4/CC7.1/CC7.2; `system.pricing_exception_monitor_restored` STANDARD 3yr CC7.2). Privacy floor (both R-46 events): integer counts, booleans, timestamps, enum values only — no `tenant_id`, no employee `user_id`, no health data, no GDPR Art. 9 data (structural peer to LITH-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 v2.46 / OFFBOARD-CHAIN-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 v2.44 privacy pattern). **v2.47 · 2026-06-25 · owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer**
*v2.47 (2026-06-25): +3 job 46 DEC-030 HMAC-chained events in new `§Enterprise Pricing Exception Monitoring events` subsection after `§Enterprise Litigation Hold Monitoring events` — closes `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §55.10` item 1 (P0/M9 — register `enterprise.reentry_chain_integrity_violation` CRITICAL/7yr, `system.pricing_exception_quarterly_audit_triggered` STANDARD/3yr, `system.pricing_exception_check_passed` LOW/1yr with REENTRY-MONITOR-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant + Zod v2 schemas + SOC 2 auditor narratives). All three events are pg_cron job 46 (`pricing_exception_compliance_monitor`, `0 9 * * *`, 26h freshness) monitoring-layer signals — they complement, not replace, the write-path REENTRY-CHAIN-01 HTTP 422 enforcement in the `emit-audit-event` Worker (COST_MODEL §44.5; DATA_MODEL §45.6). (1) `enterprise.reentry_chain_integrity_violation` (CRITICAL, 7yr): `form_system` via job 46 sweep 1 when REENTRY-CHAIN-01 bypass detected; REENTRY-MONITOR-CHAIN-01: DEC-030 HTTP 200 confirmed before AL-PRICE-01 PagerDuty P0 fires; dedup `reentry-chain-violation-{tenant_id}-{renewal_date_iso}` no-expiry; Zod `ReentryChainIntegrityViolationPayload`: `tenant_id` UUID, `renewed_event_id` UUID, `renewal_date` date string, `check_run_at` datetime. (2) `system.pricing_exception_quarterly_audit_triggered` (STANDARD, 3yr): `form_system` via job 46 sweep 2 on first 7 days of each calendar quarter; aggregate prior-quarter `enterprise.pricing_exception_approved` counts by `exception_type`; triggers PRICE-OBS-E-001 filing obligation via Slack `#compliance` advisory; Zod `PricingExceptionQuarterlyAuditTriggeredPayload`: `quarter` regex `\d{4}-Q[1-4]`, `event_count` + four `exception_type` breakdown counts, `check_run_at`; no `tenant_id`. (3) `system.pricing_exception_check_passed` (LOW, 1yr): `form_system` via job 46 all-clear; positive baseline for AL-PRICE-03 26h dead-man's switch; suppressed when REENTRY-MONITOR-CHAIN-01 non-200 path fires; Zod `PricingExceptionCheckPassedPayload`: `loyalty_renewals_checked` nonneg int, `violations_found` nonneg int (must be 0 on emission), `quarterly_trigger_fired` boolean, `check_run_at`; no `tenant_id`. REENTRY-MONITOR-CHAIN-01 invariant block added (pattern: OFFL-CHAIN-01 §53, LITH-REVIEW-CHAIN-01 §54). SOC 2 auditor narratives: CC5.2 (`reentry_chain_integrity_violation` — daily monitoring evidence that REENTRY-CHAIN-01 commitment enforcement operated without bypass); CC1.4 (`pricing_exception_quarterly_audit_triggered` — quarterly trigger cadence PRICE-SLO-02 evidence); CC4.1 (`pricing_exception_check_passed` — continuous daily monitoring health evidence, PRICE-OBS-E-002 run-history source). Emitter assignments: all three `form_system` (automated job 46 path); `form_api` REVOKED from `audit_log_events`. Alert routing: `enterprise.reentry_chain_integrity_violation` → AL-PRICE-01 P0 PagerDuty (gated per REENTRY-MONITOR-CHAIN-01); `system.pricing_exception_quarterly_audit_triggered` → Slack `#compliance` advisory only; `system.pricing_exception_check_passed` → no alert on emission (26h gap triggers AL-PRICE-03 P1 PagerDuty `form-devops` → devops-lead). Retention table: +3 rows (`enterprise.reentry_chain_integrity_violation` CRITICAL 7yr CC5.2/CC1.4; `system.pricing_exception_quarterly_audit_triggered` STANDARD 3yr CC1.4/CC4.1; `system.pricing_exception_check_passed` LOW 1yr CC4.1). Privacy floor (all three events): `enterprise.reentry_chain_integrity_violation` — `tenant_id` UUID + `renewed_event_id` UUID + `renewal_date` DATE + `check_run_at` only; `system.pricing_exception_quarterly_audit_triggered` — aggregate counts by `exception_type` only (no `tenant_id`, `deal_id`, `approver_user_id`); `system.pricing_exception_check_passed` — aggregate counts only; no PII, no employee data, no GDPR Art. 9 data in any payload. Document header v2.46 → v2.47. Cross-ref: `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §55` (REENTRY-MONITOR-CHAIN-01 invariant §55.5.5; Zod schemas §55.7.2; AL-PRICE-01/02/03 §55.4; PRICE-OBS-E-001/002 §55.8; §55.10 item 1 — now [x] Done 2026-06-25); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §44.5` (REENTRY-CHAIN-01 write-path Worker enforcement); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §44.7` (quarterly pricing exception audit obligation); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §31.8` (four pricing governance DEC-030 events); `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-46` (job 46 stale recovery runbook — §R-46.5 REENTRY-CHAIN-01 violation escalation; §R-46.6 quarterly trigger miss); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §12.6` (job 46 pg_cron registry — v1.9 patch, 2026-06-25); DEC-030. **v2.46 · 2026-06-25 · owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer**
*v2.46 (2026-06-25): +2 R-45 DEC-030 HMAC-chained events in new `§Enterprise Litigation Hold Monitoring events` — closes `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-45.14` item 1 (P0/M6 — register `system.litigation_hold_monitor_stale_declared` HIGH/7yr and `system.litigation_hold_monitor_restored` STANDARD/3yr with LITH-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 + Zod schemas). Both events are IC-emitted monitoring-control chain events for pg_cron job 45 `litigation_hold_compliance_monitor` stale recovery (INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-45 v1.0, 2026-06-25). Unlike structural peers R-41 (single GDPR deletion obligation) and R-44 (single offboarding obligation), R-45 monitors three simultaneous MSA §11.6 obligations — LITH-SLO-01 (6-month review/CC5.3), LITH-SLO-02 (36-month cap/C1.1), LITH-SLO-03 (10-business-day deletion/C1.2) — requiring three independent breach-count fields in each payload. (1) `system.litigation_hold_monitor_stale_declared` (HIGH, 7yr): compliance-officer (IC, PAM-elevated) at R-45 T+0; LITH-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 prerequisite; Zod schema `LitigationHoldMonitorStaleDeclaredPayload`: `incident_id` UUID, `confirmed_stale_since` datetime, `stale_hours` positive, `missed_runs` nonneg int, `trigger` enum pagerduty_alert|manual_discovery|co_active_r03, `initial_severity` enum P1|P0 (P0 if `max_duration_breach_count_at_declared > 0`), three `*_count_at_declared` nonneg int fields (review_overdue/max_duration_breach/deletion_overdue); no PagerDuty on emission (parent `system.cron_job_stale` P1 `form-compliance` alert already fired; dedup `litigation-hold-monitor-stale`); 7yr retention (CC7.2 — tamper-evident IC response anchor; three zero-values constitute auditor-inspectable CC5.3/C1.1/C1.2 attestation). (2) `system.litigation_hold_monitor_restored` (STANDARD, 3yr): compliance-officer (IC, PAM-elevated) at R-45 Step 6 after job 45 confirmed healthy and C2a/C2b/C2c re-run passes; LITH-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 terminal event; Zod schema `LitigationHoldMonitorRestoredPayload`: `incident_id` UUID, `restored_at` datetime, `root_cause` enum H1–H4, `fix_deployed_at` datetime, `stale_window_hours` positive, six `*_count_at_declared/_at_restored` nonneg int fields; resolves PagerDuty `litigation-hold-monitor-stale`; 3yr retention (CC7.2). LITH-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01: `restored` blocked HTTP 422 `LITH_MONITOR_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` without prior `declared` for same `incident_id` → R-05. Privacy floor both events: integer counts, timestamps, enum values only — no `tenant_id`, no employee `user_id`, no health data, no GDPR Art. 9 data (structural peer to OFFBOARD-CHAIN-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 / DELETION-SLA-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 privacy pattern). Document header v2.45 → v2.46. Cross-ref: `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-45` (full runbook — §R-45.10 canonical Zod schemas; §R-45.14 item 1 closes this registration); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §12.6` (job 45 `litigation_hold_compliance_monitor` — 26h freshness window); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §54.4` (AL-LITH-01/02/03 runbook rows); `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §46` (`litigation_hold_records` table — LITH-E-001 §46.7); R-05 (LITH-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 violation escalation); DEC-030. **v2.45 · 2026-06-25 · owner: compliance-officer + enterprise-architect**
*v2.45 (2026-06-25): `enterprise.pricing_exception_approved` payload extended — closes `docs/COST_MODEL.md §44.8 item 2` (P0, M9 — register `exception_type` enum in AUDIT_LOG_SCHEMA.md before first winback outreach). DEC-081 (2026-06-23, OQ-WIN-02 resolution) introduced the loyalty re-entry discount; REENTRY-CHAIN-01 (COST_MODEL §44.5) requires that `enterprise.contract_renewed` with `contract_discount_type = 'loyalty_reentry'` be preceded by `enterprise.pricing_exception_approved` with `exception_type = 'loyalty_reentry'` within 30 days for the same `tenant_id`. Payload change: `exception_type` field added to `enterprise.pricing_exception_approved` key payload fields — enum `standard_discount`\|`loyalty_reentry`\|`pilot_credit`\|`floor_exception`; optional for pre-DEC-081 events (back-fills to `'standard_discount'` at schema migration); **required** and must equal `'loyalty_reentry'` when the associated `enterprise_contracts` row carries `contract_discount_type = 'loyalty_reentry'` (REENTRY-CHAIN-01). Zod schema: `exception_type: z.enum(['standard_discount','loyalty_reentry','pilot_credit','floor_exception']).optional().default('standard_discount')` — additive field, no existing chain integrity impact. No new events; no retention changes; no new sections. Document header v2.44 → v2.45. Privacy floor unchanged: no PII, no company name, no employee data in any `enterprise.pricing_exception_approved` payload field. Cross-ref: `docs/COST_MODEL.md §44` (DEC-081 — loyalty re-entry discount full spec); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §44.5` (REENTRY-CHAIN-01 invariant — authoritative enforcement gate); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §44.8 item 2` (this obligation — now [x] Done); `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §45` (migration 0086 `contract_discount_type` — DATA_MODEL canonical registration, v1.30, 2026-06-25; §45.9 cross-reference closure); `docs/DECISION_LOG.md §DEC-081` (formal decision record); `docs/COST_MODEL.md §31.8` (canonical `enterprise.pricing_exception_approved` definition — `exception_type` extends existing payload without changing other fields). Both DEC-030 HMAC-chained. New section `### SCA stale-monitor incident events (DEC-030 HMAC-chained · INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-42 · CC6.8/CC7.1/CC7.2)` inserted after `### SCA monitoring sentinel events`. Closes `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-42.11` item 1 (P0/M9). (1) `system.sca_monitor_stale_declared`: IC (security-engineer + devops-lead) emits at T+0 of every R-42 activation when `pg-cron-health-monitor` (§12.7) confirms `sca_sla_monitor` (job 42, `*/15 * * * *`, 20-min freshness window) stale; Zod payload: `incident_id` UUID, `confirmed_stale_since` datetime, `stale_minutes` positive, `missed_runs` nonneg int, `trigger` enum['pagerduty_alert','manual_discovery'], `initial_severity` enum['P1','P0'], `open_critical_sla_breached_at_declared` nonneg int (R-42-C1 count — `0` = auditor-inspectable attestation that no SCA-SLO-01 breach occurred during stale window; ≥ 1 = immediate P0 escalation); SCA-STALE-CHAIN-01 anchor; HTTP 422 `SCA_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` on ordering inversion → R-05. (2) `system.sca_monitor_stale_restored`: IC emits at R-42 Step 6 after job 42 first successful post-fix run confirmed in `cron.job_run_details`; Zod payload: `incident_id` UUID, `restored_at` datetime, `root_cause` enum(H1_job_deleted/H2_ddl_change/H3_pg_net_degraded/H4_form_system_permission_revoked/H5_supabase_outage), `fix_deployed_at` datetime, `stale_window_hours` positive, `open_critical_sla_breached_at_declared` nonneg int (must match `stale_declared` value for same `incident_id`), `open_critical_remaining_at_restored` nonneg int (R-42-C1 re-run at restoration; must be 0 or accompanied by active Dependabot PR references before PagerDuty P0 resolution); SCA-STALE-CHAIN-01 terminal event; 3yr sufficient (primary CC7.2/CC6.8 evidence carried by 7yr `stale_declared`). SCA-STALE-CHAIN-01 invariant: `restored` requires prior `declared` for same `incident_id`; HTTP 422 on violation → R-05. Privacy floor both events: no `user_id`, no `tenant_id`, no employee name, no health data, no GDPR Art. 9 special category; `open_critical_sla_breached_at_declared` is integer count only (CVE IDs in separately emitted `security.sca_sla_breach` events). Retention table: +2 rows (`system.sca_monitor_stale_declared` 7yr CC6.8/CC7.1/CC7.2; `system.sca_monitor_stale_restored` 3yr CC7.2). SOC 2 criteria: CC6.8 (SCA-SLO-01 blind-spot coverage; `open_critical_sla_breached_at_declared = 0` auditor-inspectable attestation); CC7.1 (structured IC response to monitoring system failure); CC7.2 (`pg-cron-health-monitor` detection + DEC-030 HMAC chain = tamper-evident detection-to-restoration timeline independently verifiable per HMAC-VERIFY-ALGO-001). Cross-ref: `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-42` (full runbook — §R-42.7 canonical Zod schemas; §R-42.11 item 1 closed by this registration); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §12.6` (job 42 `sca_sla_monitor` registry — stale-consequence cross-ref updated per v1.6 patch 2026-06-25); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §52` (companion `security.sca_sla_breach` + `system.sca_sla_check_passed` runtime events); `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §54.5` (SCA-SLO-01 source); `docs/SOC2_READINESS.md §79.4` (SCA-STALE-E-001 evidence — pending R-42.11 item 4); R-03 (H5 co-activation); R-05 (SCA-STALE-CHAIN-01 violation response); DEC-030. Document header v2.40 → v2.42 (v2.41 patch 2026-06-25 registered R-41 deletion SLA monitor events but the document title was not updated at that time; this patch corrects the header gap and adds v2.42 content). **v2.41 · 2026-06-25 · owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer**
*v2.41 (2026-06-25): +2 `system.*` R-41 GDPR Deletion SLA Monitor monitoring-control events — `system.deletion_sla_monitor_stale_declared` (HIGH, 7yr) and `system.deletion_sla_monitor_restored` (STANDARD, 3yr). Both DEC-030 HMAC-chained. Registered in `§Enterprise Post-Churn & Deletion SLA events` per R-41.11 item 1 (P0/M10 — closes checklist obligation). (1) `system.deletion_sla_monitor_stale_declared`: IC-emitted at R-41 T+0 when `pg-cron-health-monitor` (§12.7) pages with `job_name = 'deletion_sla_monitor'` or manual discovery confirms job 43 exceeds 7h freshness; `danger_window_tenants_affected` (nonneg int from R-41-C2) drives P1 vs P0 severity; `initial_severity = 'P0'` immediately if any tenant in danger window. (2) `system.deletion_sla_monitor_restored`: IC-emitted at R-41 Step 6 after job 43 confirmed healthy; `art17_sla_breached` (boolean) self-attests whether GDPR Art. 17 30-day SLA was breached during the stale window; `true` → INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-09 activated and 72h Art. 33 window starts from `confirmed_stale_since`. DELETION-SLA-MONITOR-STALE-CHAIN-01 ordering invariant: `restored` requires prior `stale_declared` for same `incident_id`; HTTP 422 `DELETION_SLA_MONITOR_STALE_CHAIN_01_VIOLATION` on breach → R-05. Privacy floor both events: `danger_window_tenants_affected` is integer count only; `art17_sla_breached` is boolean — no `tenant_id`, no employee `user_id`, no health data, no GDPR Art. 9 data in either event payload. Affected tenant details in stale-window exception note at `compliance/evidence/deletions/deletion-sla-stale/r41-<incident_id>/stale-window-note.md` (compliance_reviewer access; `form_api` REVOKED). Retention table: +2 rows (`system.deletion_sla_monitor_stale_declared` 7yr C1.2/CC4.1/CC7.2; `system.deletion_sla_monitor_restored` 3yr C1.2/CC4.1/CC7.2). Cross-ref: `docs/INCIDENT_RESPONSE.md R-41` (full runbook — §R-41.7 canonical Zod schemas; §R-41.11 checklist item 1 closes this registration); `docs/OBSERVABILITY.md §12.6` (job 43 `deletion_sla_monitor` — v1.5 patch 2026-06-25 adds INCIDENT_RESPONSE R-41 cross-ref to stale-consequence column); `docs/DATA_MODEL.md §44` (`enterprise_churn_events` — `ece_deletion_sla_respected` CHECK DDL backstop at 35 days; `form_api` REVOKED; `tenant_manager` excluded); DEC-030. **v2.5 · 2026-06-13 · owner: compliance-officer + security-engineer**
*v2.5 (2026-06-13): +1 `system.*` event — `system.compliance_tool_connected` (STANDARD severity, 3yr retention, HMAC-chained). Registered per SOC2_READINESS §78.7. DPA-first activation record for continuous compliance tooling (Vanta or Drata). Emitter: `compliance-officer` (human, once per activation — not `form_system`). Payload: `{tool_name: 'vanta'|'drata', integrations_active: string[], dpa_executed_date: 'YYYY-MM-DD', dpa_file_ref: string, vanta_soc2_report_date?: 'YYYY-MM-DD', activated_by: uuid}`. Privacy invariant: no `user_id`, no `tenant_id`, no health data — tool name and integration list only. DPA must be executed before this event is emitted (DPA-first discipline enforced by §78.6 pre-activation procedure). Zod v2 schema canonical in SOC2_READINESS §78.7; filing path `compliance/evidence/ctool/activation-event.json` (CTOOL-E-006). Retention table: +1 row (`system.compliance_tool_connected` 3yr, SOC 2 CC4.1/CC9.2). Cross-ref: SOC2_READINESS §78, docs/VENDOR_REGISTRY.md (Vanta High-tier vendor), DEC-030.
